See “RATING” herein.
In the opinion of Orrick, Herrington  & Sutcliffe llp, Bond Counsel to the Bond Issuer (“Bond Counsel”), based upon an analysis of existing laws, 
regulations, rulings and court decisions, and assuming, among other matters, the accuracy of certain representations and compliance with certain covenants, 
interest on the Series 2022A Bonds is excluded from gross income for federal income tax purposes under Section 103 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 
(the “Code”), except that no opinion is expressed as to the status of interest on any Series 2022A Bond for any period that such Series 2022A Bond is held by a 
“substantial user” of the facilities financed or refinanced by the Series 2022A Bonds or by a “related person” within the meaning of Section 147(a) of the Code.  
Bond Counsel observes, however, that interest on the Series 2022A Bonds is a specific preference item for purposes of the federal alternative minimum tax.  
Bond Counsel further observes that interest on the Series 2022B Bonds is not excluded from gross income for federal income tax purposes under Section 103 of 
the Code.  In the further opinion of Bond Counsel, interest on the Series 2022 Bonds is exempt from District of Columbia taxation, except estate, inheritance 
and gift taxes.  Bond Counsel expresses no opinion regarding any other federal or state tax consequences related to the ownership or disposition of, or the 
amount, accrual or receipt of interest on, the Series 2022 Bonds.  See “TAX MATTERS.”

This 
cover page contains certain information for quick reference only. It is not a summary of this Official Statement, and investors must read the entire 
Official Statement to obtain the information essential to the making of an informed investment decision.
The above-reference Private Activity Revenue Bonds (DC Smart Street Lighting Project), Series 2022A (Green Bonds) (the “Series 2022A Bonds”) and Revenue 
Bonds (DC Smart Street Lighting Project), Series 2022B (Federally Taxable) (Green Bonds) (the “Series 2022B Bonds” and together with the Series 2022A Bonds, 
the “Series 2022 Bonds”) , are being issued by the District of Columbia (the “Bond Issuer”), pursuant to a Trust Indenture, to be dated as of May 1, 2022 (the “Bond 
Indenture”), between the Bond Issuer and U.S. Bank Trust Company, National Association, as Bond Trustee (the “Bond Trustee”). The proceeds of the Series 
2022 Bonds are being loaned to Plenary Infrastructure DC LLC, a special and single purpose limited liability company formed under the laws of Delaware (the 
“Borrower”) pursuant to a Loan Agreement dated as of May 1, 2022 (the “Loan Agreement”) and will be made available to: (a) pay a portion of the costs of designing 
and constructing the Project described herein; (b) to fund a portion of the interest during construction on the Series 2022 Bonds; and (c) to pay certain costs of 
issuing the Series 2022 Bonds. Project Costs not funded with proceeds of the Series 2022 Bonds, including part of the interest to accrue on the Series 2022 Bonds 
during construction, are to be funded from (a) the Milestone Payments and Availability Payments (each as defined herein) to be made by the District of Columbia 
(in its capacity as party to the Project Agreement (as defined herein), the “District”) to the Borrower under the Project Agreement, dated as of March 7, 2022 (the 
“Project Agreement”) between the Borrower and the District, (b) Equity Contributions (as defined herein) from the Holdco (as defined herein) and (c) interest 
earnings on all amounts held in the Project Accounts (as defined herein) other than the HRA (as defined herein). See “FINANCING FOR THE PROJECT.” 
The project encompasses the upgrade of the entirety of the District’s street light network that currently consists of approximately 75,000 primarily street and 
alley lights (as well as a limited number of other District owned exterior lights. and the installation of 239 Wireless Access Points,(as more fully defined herein, 
the “Project”). The Project is being developed pursuant to the Project Agreement under which the District has granted to the Borrower an exclusive right to, and 
the Borrower has agreed to (a) develop, design, construct, and finance the Project and (b) undertake Asset Management Services (as defined herein) with respect 
to the Project in return for payments by the District to the Borrower primarily in the form of Milestone Payments and Availability Payments. The source of funds 
for payment of the Milestone Payments, Availability Payments and other amounts due to the Borrower under the Project Agreement is ultimately subject to the 
appropriation by the Council of the District of Columbia (the “Council”), the inclusion of such appropriations in the budget approved by the Council and submitted to 
Congress by the President, and appropriation by Congress of such funds (as required under applicable Law). See “THE DC SMART STREET LIGHTING PROJECT.”   
The Series 2022 Bonds and any Additional Parity Bonds (as defined herein) will be payable solely from the Trust Estate established under the Bond Indenture 
and from certain collateral held by U.S. Bank Trust Company, National Association, as Collateral Agent (the “Collateral Agent”) on behalf of the Secured Parties, 
pursuant to a Collateral Agency Agreement, to be dated on or about May 5, 2022 (the “Collateral Agency Agreement”), among the Borrower, the Bond Trustee, the 
Collateral Agent and the Securities Intermediary. The Borrower will pledge and grant to the Collateral Agent a security interest in and lien on the collateral, which 
is comprised of (a) all of the Borrower’s personal property, including all rights to Milestone Payments, Availability Payment and other revenues generated from the 
Project, and the Borrower’s rights under the Project Agreement; (b) all moneys held by the Collateral Agent in certain accounts created under the Collateral Agency 
Agreement (including all Equity Contributions); and (c) a pledge by the Holdco of its membership interests in the Borrower. See “SECURITY AND SOURCES OF 
REPAYMENT FOR THE SERIES 2022 BONDS.”
Interest on the Series 2022 Bonds from their date of delivery is payable semi-annually on the last day of each February and each August 31, commencing 
on August 31, 2022, at the rates shown on the inside cover page. The Series 2022 Bonds are subject to extraordinary mandatory redemption prior to maturity as 
described herein. See “THE SERIES 2022 BONDS—General.”
The Series 2022 Bonds are being issued as fully registered bonds in denominations of $5,000 and integral multiples thereof and, when issued, the Series 2022 
Bonds will be registered in the name of Cede & Co., as nominee for The Depository Trust Company of New York (“DTC”). DTC will act as securities depository for 
the Series 2022 Bonds. Purchases of beneficial interests in the Series 2022 Bonds will be made in book-entry form only, and purchasers will not receive certificates 
representing their interests in the Series 2022 Bonds except as described herein. See “THE SERIES 2022 BONDS—Payment of the Series 2022 Bonds.”
THE SERIES 2022 BONDS SHALL BE SPECIAL OBLIGATIONS OF THE BOND ISSUER.  THE SERIES 2022 BONDS ARE WITHOUT RECOURSE TO THE 
BOND ISSUER.  THE SERIES 2022 BONDS SHALL NOT BE GENERAL OBLIGATIONS OF THE BOND ISSUER, SHALL NOT BE A PLEDGE OF OR INVOLVE 
THE FAITH AND CREDIT OR THE TAXING POWER OF THE BOND ISSUER, SHALL NOT CONSTITUTE A DEBT OF THE BOND ISSUER AND SHALL NOT 
CONSTITUTE LENDING OF THE PUBLIC CREDIT FOR PRIVATE UNDERTAKINGS AS PROHIBITED BY THE HOME RULE ACT (AS DEFINED HEREIN).  THE 
SERIES 2022 BONDS SHALL NOT GIVE RISE TO ANY PECUNIARY LIABILITY OF THE BOND ISSUER AND THE BOND ISSUER SHALL HAVE NO OBLIGATION 
WITH RESPECT TO THE PURCHASE OF THE SERIES 2022 BONDS.  PRINCIPAL OF, AND INTEREST AND PREMIUM, IF ANY, ON THE SERIES 2022 BONDS 
WILL BE PAYABLE SOLELY FROM THE RECEIPTS AND REVENUES OF THE BOND ISSUER FROM THE LOAN AGREEMENT AND THE NOTES.
The Series 2022 Bonds are offered when, as and if issued and delivered and accepted by the Underwriters (as defined herein) and subject to receipt of the 
approving legal opinion of Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP, Washington, D.C., as Bond Counsel. Certain legal matters will be passed upon for the District by 
the Office of the Attorney General for the District of Columbia, for the Borrower by its counsel, Fasken Martineau DuMoulin LLP, Toronto, Canada; and for the 
Underwriters by their counsel, Ashurst LLP, New York, New York. It is expected that delivery of the Series 2022 Bonds will be made through the facilities of DTC 
on or about May 5, 2022.

SUMMARY 
This Summary is not a complete description of the Series 2022 Bonds or the Project and does not contain all of the 
information you should consider before making an in vestment decision with respect to the Series 2022 Bonds.  You 
should read the more detailed information appearing or incorporated by reference in this Official Statement and the 
documents summarized or des cribed herein in their  entirety for a m ore complete understanding about the Project, 
the offering and the terms of and se curity and sources of payment for the Series 2022 Bonds.  Terms u sed in this 
Summary and not defined in the Summary are defined in "APPENDIX A— DEFINITIONS OF TERMS." 
THE SERIES 2022 BONDS 
Bonds Offered ..............................................T he District o f Columbia Private  Activity Revenue Bond s (DC S mart 
Street Lighting Project), Series 2022A  (Green Bonds) in the aggregate 
principal am ount of $ 144,485,000 and Revenue Bonds (DC Smart 
Street Lightin g Project), Se ries 2022B  ( Federally Taxable) (Green 
Bonds) in the aggregate principal amount of $15,210,000 (the "Series 
2022 B onds").  The Series 20 22 Bonds are b eing iss ued as ful ly 
registered bonds in denom inations of $5,000 and integral multiple s 
thereof.  See "T HE SERIES 2022 BONDS."  A s a g eneral matter, the 
Series 2022 Bonds will be repaid fro m sources including in respect of 
Milestone Payments and Availability Payments made under the Pr oject 
Agreement and other available amounts , all as more fully d escribed 
herein.  
Interest ..........................................................The Series 20 22 Bonds will b ear i nterest at t he ra tes shown on the 
inside cover page of this Official Statement.  Interest on the Series 2022 
Bonds will be calculated on the basis of a 360- day year cons isting of 
twelve 30-day months.

February and August 31 each year, commencing on August 31, 20 22, 
until maturity or prior redemption.

cover page of this Official Statement.

redemption as d escribed under  " THE SERIES 2022 BONDS—

Bonds Milestone 2 Maturities .....................Each Bonds Milestone 2 Maturity will be repaid upon receipt by the 
Borrower of the Miles tone 2 P ayment. The Borrower is entitled to 
invoice the District for the Milestone 2 Payment on the final day in the 
Calendar Quarter in which Project Final Completion is achieve d. Based 
on the expected Project Final Completion Date of May 4, 2024, the 
Borrower expects to invoice the  District for Milestone 2 on June 30, 
2024 and exp ects to receive the Mile stone 2 Payment on or around 
August 31, 2024.  If the Bonds Milestone 2 Maturities are redeemed in 
part, such Bonds Milestone 2  Maturities will be redeemed in t he 
manner described in "THE SERIES 2022 BONDS—Selection of Series 
2022 Bonds for Redemption" below (provided that a portion of a Series 
2022 Bond may be redeemed only in Authori zed Denominations) and 
such extraordinary mandatory redemption shall not be sub ject to any 
premium or make -whole amount.  The Bonds Milestone 2 Mat urities 
will only be rede emed pursuant to this sectio n if there are sufficient 
funds to redeem both Bonds Milestone 2 Maturities.

The Bond Issuer ...........................................District of Columb ia. 
Special Obligations ......................................The Series 2022 Bonds  shall be special obliga tions of the Bond Iss uer.  
The Series 2022 Bonds  are without recourse to the Bond Issuer.  The 
Series 2022 Bonds shall not be general obligations  of the Bo nd Issuer, 
shall not be a pledge of or  involve the faith and credit or the taxing 
power of the Bond Issuer, shall not constitute a debt of the Bond Issuer, 
and shall not constitute lending of the public credit for private 
undertakings as prohibited by section 6.02(a)(2) of the Home Rule Act.  
The Series 2022 Bon ds are payable solely from the loan payments 
received by the Bond Tru stee from the Borrower and are secured by a 
pledge of the Trust Estate, all of wh ich shall, except as  may be 
otherwise expressly authorized in the Bond In denture, be us ed for n o 
other purpose than to pay the principal of, redemption  premium, if any, 
on, and interest on, the Series 2022 Bonds .  The Series 2022 Bonds  
shall not give rise to any pecuniary liability of the Bond Issuer and  the 
Bond Issuer shall have no obligation with respect to the purchase of the 
Series 2022 Bonds, upon issuance, sale and delivery of the Series 2022 
Bonds. 
Rating  ..........................................................Moody's has assigned an initial rating of "A3" (with a stable outlook) to 
the Series 2022 Bonds. This rating is not a recommendation to buy, sell 
or hold the Seri es 2022 Bonds and is subject to revi sion or withdrawal 
at any time by Moody's. The delivery of an equivalent final rating from 
such rating agency is a condition to the issuance of the Series 2022 
Bonds. For more information, see "RATING." 
THE PROJECT  
The Project ...................................................The Project encompasses: (a) if such term is us ed in connection with  
the Loan Agreement or the Bond Indenture, the (i) upgrade of the entire 
streetlight network within and throughout the District of Columbia that 
currently consists of approximately 75 ,000 primarily street and alle y 
lights, as well as s elect overhead guide s igns, internal and ex ternal 
lighting for approximately 30 "Welcome to Washington, D.C." signs 
located throughout the District of Columbia, and navigation, underpass, 
bike trail, and tunnel lights, which upgrades shall include: (A) 
converting existing non-L ED and LED Di strict-owned l ights to LED 
technology; (B) designing and installing s ystems providi ng for the 
remote monitoring and contr ol of the street light network; (C) 
upgrading po les and associated i nfrastructure, including limited  
underground infrastr ucture where  appropri ate or  required; (D) 
delivering long-term asset management of  the streetlight network; and 
(E) installing smart city technology, consisting of 239 wireless acces s 
points, to leverag e the streetlight network and the District’s signific ant 
fiber opti c networ k, DC -Net, for ancillary uses and to deliver 
broadband Wi -Fi coverage that ad vances t he Dist rict’s goal of city -  
 3 
wide broadband access; (ii) funding  of any credit  enhancement costs, 
liquidity costs or debt service reserve fund relating  to the Bonds; (iii) 
payment of any costs of issuance and other related costs t o the extent 
permissible; and (b) if such term is  used in connection with  any other 
Transaction Documen t, means the design , building, installation, 
financing, and asset management of upgrades to the existing street light 
network in the District of Columbia , as more fully des cribed in the 
Project Agreement (the " Project"), s ee "THE DC SMART STREET 
LIGHTING PROJECT."  
Project Costs ................................................Total costs of the Project are currently est imated t o be a pproximately 
$225 million. Project Costs with respect to the D&C Wor k are 
estimated to be approximately $145.8 million. Project Costs are to b e 
funded from proceeds of the Series 2022 Bonds, Equity Contributions 
from the H oldco, Availability Pay ments and Milestone Payments 
(which includes the Smart City Milestone Payments)  received by  the 
Borrower f rom the District under the Project Agreement  and i nterest 
earnings on all amounts in the  Project Accounts.  See "ESTIMATED 
SOURCES AND USES OF FUNDS , P ROJECTED CASH FLOW 
AND DEBT SERVICE COVERAGE." 
The District ...................................................The District of Columbia  (the "District") a cting by and through t he 
District of Columbia Office of Public-Private Partnerships ("OP3"), for 
and on behalf of the District of Columbia Department of Transportation 
("DDOT") and t he Office of the Chief Technology Officer ("OCTO"), 
is the counterpart y to the Project Agreement  with the  Borrower. 
DDOT, which oversees the Di strict's street light network,  was created 
in 2002. DDOT is a n a gency within t he exe cutive branch of the  
government of th e District and  works to improve the District' s 
economic competitiveness and quality of life by planning, coordinating, 
and operating the transportation system, and managing and maintaining 
the transportation infrastructure, to ensure the safe, efficient movement 
of people, goods, and information along public -rights-of-way. OP3, 
which was established b y the Dis trict's Public -Private Partne rship Act 
of 2014, i s charged w ith buildin g collaborations be tween the private  
sector a nd D istrict government to c omplete major infrast ructure 
projects and other programs through long-t erm, performanc e-based 
procurements. OCTO is the central technology org anization of the 
District of C olumbia Government. OCTO develo ps, implements, a nd 
maintains the D istrict's technology infr astructure. See " PROJECT 
PARTICIPANTS—District" and "SOURCE OF  F UNDS FOR 
PAYMENTS BY THE  DISTRICT, DISTRICT BUDGETING AND 
APPROPRIATIONS PROCESS UNDER DISTRICT LAW."

membership i nterest); and  Phoenix Sponsor (10% membership 
interest).

follows: (a) $21,970,000 the final day of the Calendar Quarter in which 
the Borrower has achieved Street Light Bundle  Substantial Completion 
for more than 50% of the total number of Lighting Units expected to be 
in the Improved  Street Light Network upon Project Final Completion 
and (b) $2 2,220,000 th e fina l day of th e Cal endar Quarter in which 
Project Final Completion is  achieved. The t wo Milestone Payments for 
the Smart City Work are payable in th e amounts and at the times as 
follows: (a) $2,07 5,000 the final day of the Cale ndar Quarter in which 
the percentage of Smart Ci ty Bun dles that h ave reached Smart City 
Bundle Sub stantial Completion first e xceeds 50% and (b) $2,075,000 
the final d ay of the Calendar Quarter in wh ich all Sm art City Bundles 
have reac hed Sma rty City Bundle  Substantial C ompletion. T he 
Milestone Paym ents are su bject to adjustment under the Project 
Agreement. See " FINANCING FOR THE PROJECT —Milestone  
Payments a nd Availab ility Payments " and "THE PROJECT 
AGREEMENT—Paym ents to the Borrower."   
Availability  Payments ..................................In accordance with th e terms of the Project Agreemen t, a nd 
commencing with the achievement of Pr oject Final Completion and 
continuing for the  term of the Project Ag reement, the District will pay 
to the Borrowe r the Availability Payments, the proceeds of which are 
expected to be u sed to, among  other things, pay project capital co sts 
and satisfy principal and interest payments owed in respect to the Series 
2022 Bonds. The calculation o f the gross Availability Payment in each 
Calendar Quarter pursuant to the Project Agreement is b ased on a 
number of v ariables. The ne t Ava ilability Payment is calculated f or 
each Calendar Quar ter pursuant to the  Project Agreement and may b e 
adjusted for cer tain Unavaila bility Events wher e roadway s and oth er 
public spaces adjacent to a Project Site do not meet availability and use 
requirements, and the accu mulation of No ncompliance Points, which 
are assessed if the Borrower causes a Noncompliance Event (essentially 
a fail ure to meet certai n perform ance s tandards in the Pro ject 
Agreement). The Borrower is entitled to submit an invoice  to the 
District each Calendar Quarter sett ing out  the net amount of the 
Availability Payment due for such Calendar Quarter. The  Availability 
Payments constitute the pri mary s ource of  revenues f rom the Project 
and such payments  are e xpected to exceed amoun ts require d to pay 
scheduled debt ser vice on the Series 2022 Bonds  and maintenance and   
 7 
renewal costs of the Pro ject during the T erm. See "FINANCING FOR 
THE PROJECT—M ilestone Payments and Availability Payments" and 
"THE PROJECT AGREEMENT—Pay ments to the Borrower."   
Compensation Events ..................................Pursuant to  t he Project Agreement, the Borrower may be entitled to 
schedule relief and compensatio n as a resu lt of Compensation Events. 
On the occurrence of a Compensation Event, the Borrower is, in certain 
circumstances, entit led to claim ( a) a n extension to  the Guarante ed 
Project Final Completion Date or, followin g the Guaranteed  Project 
Final Com pletion D ate, the Long Stop De adline, ( b) re lief from  
compliance with i ts oblig ations under the Projec t Ag reement, ( c) 
compensation for any C hange in Cost s that the Borrower will suffer as 
a direct result of the  Compensation Event; and/or ( d) compensation for 
any additiona l finance cos ts that th e Borrower will incur  as a direct 
result of any Milestone Paym ent being received later  than it o therwise 
would have been h ad it not be en for the occurrence of such 
Compensation Event. In the c ase of schedule relief, provided that the  
Borrower has complied with its oblig ations, the Guaranteed Pr oject 
Final Completion Date or (following the Gu aranteed Pr oject Final 
Completion Date), the Lo ng Stop Deadline, shall be extended by such 
time as shall be reas onable for such a  Compensation Event, but only to 
the ext ent that the Borrower demonstrates to the District by way of 
Time Impact Analy sis that th e Comp ensation Event will result in an 
identifiable and measurable disruption to the Work, which will impact a 
critical path ac tivity; and in  the case of com pensation, the 
compensation is contemplated t o be  generally equal to the  amount 
necessary to resto re the Borrower to the  same econ omic position it 
would have been in if the Co mpensation Event  had not occ urred. 
Schedule relief and m onetary r elief is sub ject to certai n time and 
monetary deductibles to be borne by the Borrower. T he source of funds 
for the payment of c ompensation amounts is subject to  appropriation. 
See "THE PROJECT AGREEMENT—Co mpensation Events ," 
"APPENDIX B—SUMMARY O F CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF T HE 
PROJECT AGREEMENT" and " SOURCE OF FUNDS FOR 
PAYMENTS BY THE DISTRICT, DIST RICT BUDGETING AND 
APPROPRIATIONS PROCESS UNDER DISTRICT LAW." 
Relief Event s .................................................Pu rsuant to the Project Agreement, th e Borrower may be enti tled to

Project Agreement as a result of a Relie f Event that ha s c aused or is 
likely to directly cause, the Borrower to (a) fail to achieve Project Final 
Completion by the Guaranteed Project Final Completio n Date or , 
following the Guara nteed Project Fi nal Completion Date, the Long 
Stop Deadl ine, ( b) fai l to  receive any Milestone Payment on t he date 
shown in the Base Case Fi nancial Model, or (c) comply with any of its 
obligations under the P roject Agreement. Provided the Borrower 
follows the relevant procedures and provides the requisite evidence, the 
Guaranteed Project Final Completion Date o r the Long Stop  Deadline, 
as applicable, shall be extended by such time as shall be reasonable for 
such Relief Event, but only to th e extent that the Borro wer 
demonstrates by way of Time Impact Analysis that the relev ant Relief 
Event will result in an iden tifiable and measurable disruptio n to th e 
Work, which will impact a c ritical path  activity. A Relief Event shall 
affect the accrual of any Unavailability Event or Noncompliance Event, 
as applicable, during the period in which the Relief Event is subsisting. 
The Borrower may be enti tled to  compensation for  any additional   
 8 
finance costs t hat the Borrower will incur as a  direct result o f a ny 
Milestone Payment being receive d later th an it otherwis e would have

INTRODUCTION 
The purpose of this O fficial Sta tement, including the cover page, ins ide cover pa ge and app endices, is to  
provide information in connection with the issuance by the Bon d Issuer of the Serie s 2022A Bonds in an aggregate 
principal amount of $144,485,000 and the Series 2022B Bonds in an aggregate principal amount of $15,210,000.  The 
Series 2022 Bonds will be issued pursuant to a Trust Indenture, to be dated as of May 1 , 2022 (the "Bond Indenture"), 
between the Bond I ssuer and the Bond Trustee.  C apitalized terms u sed but not defin ed in the front portion  of this 
Official Statement have the meanings set forth in "APPENDIX A—D EFINITIONS OF TERMS" attached hereto. 
 
Th
e Series 2022 Bonds are being issued by the Bond Issuer to fund a loan to Plenary Infrastructure DC LLC, 
a special and single purpose limited liability company formed under the laws of Delaware (the "Borrower") to, among 
other things, finance a portion of the costs of designing and constructing the Project. 
The Project is being developed pursuant to the Project Agreement under which the District has granted to the 
Borrower an exclusive right to, and the  Bo rrower has agreed to, subject to the terms of the Proje ct Agreement, (a) 
develop, design, construct and finance the Project, and (b) undertake Asset Management Services with respect to the 
Project in re turn f or pa yments by t he District to th e Borrower p rimarily in the form of Milest one Paymen ts and  
Availability Payments. The source of  funds for payment of the Milestone Pay ments, Availability Payments and other 
amounts due to the Borrower under the Proj ect Agreement are ultimately subject to the appro priation by the Council, 
the inclu sion of such appropriati ons in the budget approved by the Cou ncil and sub mitted to Congress by the 
President, and app ropriation by Congress of suc h funds (as required under applicable Law ). See "SOURCE OF 
FUNDS FOR PAYMENTS BY TH E DISTRICT, D ISTRICT BUDGETING AND APPROPRIATIONS PROCE SS 
UNDER DISTRICT LAW " and "RISK FACTORS—Risks Relating to the D istrict and the Borrower—Appropriation 
Risk."

THE BOND ISSUER 
The Bond Issuer  was created in 1 791 by a ct of the United  States Congres s ( "Congress") a nd Pre sidential 
proclamation and has served as the capital of the United States of America since 1800.  Under Article I, Section 8  of 
the Unit ed States Constitution, Congress  has excl usive legisl ative authority over the Bond Issuer as the N ation’s 
Capital.  Since January 2, 1975, the Bond Issuer has been governed in accordance with the District of Columbia Home 
Rule Act, Pub. L. 93 -198, an Act of Congress signed by the Preside nt of the United States on December 24, 1973, as 
amended (the "Home Rule Act").   
 Under the Home Rule Act, the Bond Issuer  is gove rned by an elected Mayor (the "Mayor") exercising 
executive powers and the elected 13-member Council of the District of Columbia (the "Council") exercising legislative 
powers.  With limited exceptions, in cluding the payment of deb t service, the Bon d Issuer may not obligate or exp end 
funds absent annual Congressional appropriation.  The Council discharges the powers delegated in the Home Rule Act 
by enacting acts or adopting resolu tions approved by a majori ty of its members present and voting.  The Series 2022 
Bonds have been duly authorized for issuance, sale, and delivery p ursuant to Section 490 of t he Home Rule Act and 
the Plenary Infrastructure DC, LLC Revenue Bonds Project (DC Smart Lighting) Approval Resolution of 2022, R24-
375, adopted by the Council on February 1, 2022. 
THE SERIES 2022 BONDS SHALL BE SPECIAL OBLIGATION S OF THE BOND ISSUER .  T HE 
SERIES 2022 BONDS  ARE WITHOUT  RECOURSE TO THE BOND ISS UER.  THE SERIES 2022 BOND S 
SHALL NOT BE GENERAL OBLIGATI ONS OF THE BOND ISSUER , SHALL N OT BE A PL EDGE OF OR 
INVOLVE THE  FAITH AND  CREDIT OR THE TAXING POWER OF THE BOND ISSUE R, SHALL NOT 
CONSTITUTE A DEBT OF THE BOND ISSUER AND SHALL NOT CONSTITUTE LENDING OF THE PUBLIC 
CREDIT FOR PRIVATE UNDERTAKINGS AS PROHIB ITED BY THE HOME RULE AC T.  THE SERIES 2022 
BONDS SHALL NOT GIVE RISE TO ANY PECUNIARY LIABILITY OF THE BOND ISSUER AND THE BOND 
ISSUER SHALL HAVE NO OBLIGATION WITH RESPECT TO THE PUR CHASE OF TH E SERIES 2022 
BONDS.  PRINCIPAL OF, AND INTEREST AND PREMIUM, IF ANY, ON THE SERIES 2022 BONDS WILL BE 
PAYABLE SOLELY FROM THE RECEIPT S AND REVENUES OF THE BOND ISSUER  FROM THE LOAN 
AGREEMENT AND THE NOTES.   
13 
 
THE SERIES 2022 BONDS 
General 
The Series 20 22A Bon ds are being  issued in the a ggregate princi pal amount of  $1 44,485,000 and Series 
2022B Bonds are being issued in the aggregate principa l amount of $15,210,000. T he Series 2022 Bonds will mature, 
subject to prior redemp tion, on the d ates shown on the insi de cover page of this Official Statement.  The Series 20 22 
Bonds will be subject to  redemption prior to maturity as described below.  The Series  2022 Bonds are being issued as 
fully registered b onds in denominations of $ 5,000 and int egral multip les there of.  The S eries 20 22 Bonds wil l be 
issued in book -entry form pursuant to the book -entry-only system described herein. Bondholders of the Series 20 22 
Bonds will not receive physical delivery of any Bond certificates. 
The Series 20 22 Bonds will be d ated the date of their original issuance and delivery and will b ear interest 
from their date, or from the most recent Bonds Payment Date to whi ch interest has been paid  or duly provided for, at 
the per annum rate set forth on the inside cover page of this Official Statement. 
Interest on the Series 2022 Bonds is payable semi-annually on the last day of February and August 31 of each 
year, commencing on August 31, 2022, until maturity or prior re demption.  Interest on the Series 2022 Bonds will be 
calculated on the basis of a 360-day year consisting of twelve 30-day months. 
Payment of the Series 2022 Bonds 
The principal of and interest on the Series 2022 Bonds will be payabl e only to the Bond holder appearing on 
the registration books or, upon the written order o f the Bondholder thereof, his legal representative. So l ong as the  
Bonds remain in a "book-entry only" system, the Bond Trustee will make such payments only to DTC or its nomine e, 
which in t urn, is oblig ated t o r emit such princi pal and interest to th e Participants for disbur sement to  Beneficial 
Bondholders of the Bonds. 
Pursuant to t he Bond Indenture, the principal of and redemption premium, if any on the Series 2022 Bonds 
will be payable by check or draft or by such other method as mutually agreed in writing between the Bondholder of the 
Bond and the Bond Trustee at ma turity or upon earlier redemption to the Bon dholder thereof as  shown on the 
registration records of the Bond Trustee  upon maturity or prior redemption thereof in accordance with the terms of the 
Bond Indenture and upon presentation and surrender of the Series 2022 Bonds at the designated payment office of the 
Bond Trustee.  Interest on the Series 2022 Bonds is payable to the Bondholder whose name appears in the regi stration 
books at th e clo se of busi ness on the R ecord Dat e and will be paid (a ) by check or d raft s ent on o r prior to the 
appropriate date of payment, by the Bond Trustee to the address of the Bondholder appearing in the registration books 
on the Record Dat e or (b) by such other method as m utually agreed in writing between the Bondholder of the Se ries 
2022 Bond and the Bond T rustee.  The "Record Date" for the Series 20 22 Bonds is t he close o f business on each 
February 15 and August 15, regardless of whether or not such date is a Business Day.   
The Bond I ndenture provides that  any interest not timely paid will cease to be p ayable to the Bondholder 
thereof at the close of business on the Record Date and will be payable to the Person who is the Bondholder thereof at 
the clo se of bus iness on  a "Special R ecord Date." S uch Spe cial R ecord Dat e will be fixed by th e Bond T rustee 
whenever moneys become available for payment of the d efaulted interest, and notice of th e Special Recor d Date will 
be given by the Bond Tr ustee to the Bondholder s of the Series 2022 Bonds, not less than 10 days prior to  the Special 
Record Date, by c ertified o r first -class ma il to each su ch Bondholder as show n on the Bond Trustee 's regi stration 
records on a date selected by the Bond Trustee, stating the date of the Special Record Date and the date fixed for the 
payment of such defaulted interest.  Alternative means of payment of  interest may be used if mutuall y agreed to in 
writing between the Bondholder and the Bond Trustee. 
So long  as the Series 2022 Bonds or any portion thereof are registered in the name of DTC or any nominee 
thereof, all payments of the principal or redemption price of or interest on such Series 2022 Bonds shall be made to 
DTC, or its nominee in same day funds on the dates provided for such payments under the Bond Indenture.  Each such 
payment to DTC or its nominee shall be valid and effectiv e to fully discharge all liability of the Bond Issuer or the 
Bond Trustee with respect to the principal or redemption price of or interest on the Series 2022 Bonds to the extent of 
the sum or sums so paid.

Redemption 
The Series 2022 Bonds are subject to redemption prior to th eir stated maturity, in accord ance with the terms 
and provisions of the Bond Indenture, as follows: 
Optional Redemption. The Series 2022 Bonds are not subject to optional redemption.  
Extraordinary Mandatory Redemption 
The Series 2022 Bonds are subject to extra ordinary mandatory redemption prior to their stated m aturity, in 
accordance with the terms and provisions of t he Bond Indenture and subject to the Redemption Price and amounts as 
set out below. The Redemption Price for Series 2022 Bonds subject to extraordinary mandatory redemption will be (a) 
in respect of an extraordinary mandatory redemption described at "—Unspent Series 2022A Bond Pro ceeds" and "—
Mandatory Redemption Upon Receipt of Milestone 2 Payment " below, at the redemption price described therein, (b) in 
respect of any other extraordinary mandatory redemption described below  (i) one hundred percent ( 100%) of the 
principal amo unt of the Bonds to be r edeemed plus a ccrued and unpaid inter est to the r edemption da te, witho ut 
redemption premium (and with respect to such "—Mandatory Redempt ion Upon Receipt of Milestone 2 Payme nt," no 
make-whole amount), or (ii) if such Bonds were sold at a premium, the Amortized Redemption Price.  
Unspent Series 2022A Bo nd Proceeds.  The Se ries 2022A Bonds are subject to ex traordinary manda tory 
redemption, i n whol e or in part ( selected in the  manner described under "—Selection of Series 2022 Bonds fo r 
Redemption" b elow) at the Redemption Price on the  r edemption da te (which will be se t by the Bond Tr ustee on a 
Business Day that is no earlier than the date  that is, the earlier of (a) the date that is ninety (90) days after the five (5) 
year anniversary of Financial Closing Date or (b) 60 da ys after the Project Final Completion Date) at a red emption 
price equal to par plus accrued interest thereon up to but not including the redemption date, in the principal amount of 
(rounded upward to a multiple of $ 5,000) such Series 2022A Bonds being redeemed and to t he extent any remaining 
unspent proceeds from Series 2022A Bonds on such dat e, unless as of such date, at least 95% of the net proceed s of 
the Series 2022A Bonds are expended for the P roject; provided that no such redemption will be required if Borrower 
has obtained an opinion of Bond C ounsel st ating that the fa ilure to redeem any such Seri es 2022A Bonds will not 
adversely affect th e exclusion o f intere st on such Seri es 2022A Bonds from gr oss income for f ederal i ncome tax 
purposes and furnished such opinion to the Bond Issuer and the Bond Trustee. 
Termination Unde r P roject Agr eement.  The Series 2022 Bonds are su bject to extraordinar y m andatory 
redemption, in whole (but not in part) at the Redemption Price on the redemption date upon termination of the Project 
Agreement by eith er the Borrower or the District ( with such re demption da te to be set by the Bon d Trustee  on a 
Business Day that is within three (3) Business Days after the Bond Tr ustee receives notice  from the Borrower of the 
receipt by the Borrower of any "Termination Compensation" payable by the Dis trict pursuant to the Project 
Agreement).  Upon the termin ation of the Project Agreement, the Borrower will provide notice of such termination to 
the Bond Trustee within three (3) Business Days of such termination. See "PROJECT AGREEMENT—Compen sation 
on Termination." 
Insurance Proceeds.  The Series 2022 Bonds are subject to extraordinary mandatory redemption, in whole or 
in part, at the Redemption Price on the redemption date (which will be set by the Bond Trustee on a Business Day that 
is no la ter than sixty ( 60) da ys af ter the re ceipt by the Borrower of net amounts  of insurance or l oss proceeds  
(excluding delayed start-up, soft c osts, business inte rruption i nsurance p roceeds and ot her a nticipated l osses of 
revenue) or condemnation proceeds) (a) to the extent that the affected property cannot be restored or is not required to 
be restored pursuant to the terms of the Project Agreement and the Finance Documents and the Borrower elects not to 
do so and to the ex tent o therwise permitted und er the Project A greement), in the pri ncipal a mount of (rounded 
downward to a multip le of $5,000) and (b) to the e xtent of a ny suc h insurance  or loss proceeds or condemna tion 
proceeds transferred from th e Loss Proceeds Account to the Mandatory Prepayment Account on such d ate, sufficient 
to effectuate such redemption.  If the Series 2022 Bonds are redeemed in part, the Series 2022 Bonds will be redeemed 
in the manner described under "—Se lection of Series 2022 Bonds for Redemption" below (provided that a portion of a 
Series 2022 Bond may be redeemed only in Authorized Denominations).

not later than a hundred and twenty (120) days after a Determination of Taxability.  Such redemption will be in whole,   
15 
 
or in part to the ex tent that an Opinion of Bond Counsel is  delivered to the effect that interest on the Series 2022A

Collateral Agent shall, pursuant to the Collateral Agency Agreemen t, promptly and automatically (withou t further 
direction from Borrower) transfer such funds into the  Mandatory Prepayment Accoun t to pay the Redemption Price 
due on the Bonds Mile stone 2 Maturities with any remaining balance of the Milestone 2 Payment after suc h transfer is

assuming that Project Final Completion is achieved no later than June 30, 2024. If such maturity of Series 2022 Bonds 
are redeemed in part, the Series 2022 Bonds will be redeemed in the manner described in "—Selection  of Series 2022

amount.

any of their Affiliates (for purposes of this paragraph,  the "Optionee") have the option to a cquire all (but not less than

District CA Termination Notice to the Collateral Agent in respect to  clause (p) of  the definition of Borrower Default,

Contract in the ev ent of a termination of the D&C Contract for Project Agreement termination, an d (c) the  Optionee 
has obtained any consent of the District required under the Project Agreement to the acquisition by the Optionee of the 
Secured Parties' interest in the Bonds.  Upon three (3) Business Days' notice from the Optionee of its intent to exe rcise 
this option, the  Bonds will be  subject to e xtraordinary mandatory redempti on, in wh ole (but no t in part), at the 
Amortized Redemption Price (with the redemption date to be set by the Bond Trustee on a Business Day that is within 
three (3) Business Da ys after the Bond Trus tee receives notice from the Collateral Agent that the conditions set forth 
above have been satisfied.

Selection of Series 2022 Bonds for Redemption 
Selection of Series 2022A Bonds for Redem ption. The Series 2022A Bonds will be redeem ed only in whole 
multiples of $5,000.  If less than all the Series 2022A  Bonds are called for redemption, the Series 2022A Bonds to be 
so redeemed will be called for redemption in the man ner set forth in an Offic er’s Certificate file d with the Bond 
Trustee.  If less than all of the Series 2022A Bonds of an y one maturity are to be called for redem ption, the Bond 
Registrar will select the Series 2022A Bonds to be redeemed by lot, each $5,000 portion of principal being counted as 
one Series 2022A Bond for this purpose; provided, however, that so long as the only Bondholder of the Series 2022A 
Bonds is DTC, such selection will be made by DTC in accordance with its rules and procedures. 
Selection of Series 2022B Bonds f or Redemption. If less than all of the Series 2022 Bonds of any series and 
maturity are called for prior redemption as described above in "—Ex traordinary Mandatory Redemption" above, the 
particular Series 2022 Bonds or portions thereof to be re deemed will be selected by t he Bond Trustee on a p ro rata 
pass-through distribution of principal basis in accordance with the procedures of the Securities Dep ository, provided 
that, so lo ng as the Series 2022 Bonds are held in book -entry-only form, the selection for redemptio n of su ch Series 
2022 Bonds of the applicable series and maturity will be made in accordance wit h the operational arrangements of the 
Securities Depository then in eff ect.  Neither the Bond Issuer nor the Bo nd Trustee provides any assurance that the 
Security Depository, the Security Depository's direct and ind irect participants or any other in termediary will allocate 
the redemption of the Series 2022 Bonds on such basis .  If  the operational arrangements of th e Securities Depository 
do not allow for red emption on a pr o rata pass-through distribution of principal basis, the Seri es 2022 Bonds of the 
applicable series and maturity will be selecte d fo r redemption, in accordan ce wi th the Se curities Depository's 
procedures, b y lot. The foregoing will not ap ply if all of th e Series 2022 Bonds Milestone Maturities are redeemed 
pursuant to "—Mandatory Redemp tion Upon Receipt of Milestone 2 Payment or Proceeds" above. 
Book-Entry Form. I f the Series 2022 Bonds are not  in book-entry-only form and less t han all of th e Series 
2022 Bonds are to be redeemed as described above in "—Extraord inary Mandatory R edemption," the Series 2022 
Bonds to be re deemed will be selected by the Bond Trustee  on a pro rata pass-through distribution of principal basis 
among all of the Bondholders of the Series 2022 Bonds based on the principal amount of Series 2022 Bonds owned by 
such Bondholders, pro rata a cross all m aturities, and if less than all of the Seri es 2022 Bonds of a maturity are to be 
redeemed, t he Bond  Trustee will select by lot or othe r cu stomary mann er such Serie s 2022 Bonds to be re deemed 
within a maturity. 
Additional Parity Bond s. Additional Parity Bond s shall be subject to redemption as set f orth under the 
applicable Supplemental Bond Indenture.

redemption notice by United States first-class mail, at least thirty (30) days and not more than six ty (60) days prior to 
the date fixed for redemption, to the B ondholder of each Series 2022 Bond to be re deemed at the address as it last 
appears on the registration records of the Bond Trustee ; provided, however, that fa ilure t o g ive any such notice by 
mailing, or any defect therein, will not affect the validity of any proceedings of any Series 2022 Bonds as to which no

such failure has occurred.  The Bond Trustee will call the Series 2022 Bonds for redemption and payment as provided 
in the Bond Indenture upon receipt by the Bond Trustee at least thirty-five (35) days prior to the redemption date of a 
written request of  the Borrower.  Such request must specify the principal amount of the Ser ies 2022 Bonds and their 
maturities to be called for redemption, the applicable redemption price or prices, the date fixed for redemption and the 
provision or provisions of the B ond Indenture pursuant to which Serie s 2022 Bonds are to be called for redempt ion.  
Any notice m ailed a s described herein wi ll be conc lusively de emed to hav e been duly given,  whether or not the 
Bondholder receives the notice. 
So long as DTC is effecting book-entry transfers of the Series 2022 Bonds, the Bond Trustee will provide the 
notices specified in the Bond Indenture  to DTC.  I t is e xpected that DTC shall, in turn, notify its Direct Participants 
and that th e Direct Participants, in turn, wi ll notify or cause to be notified the Beneficial Bondholders of the Se ries 
2022 Bonds.  Any failure on the part of DTC or a Direct Participant, or failure on the part of a nominee of a Beneficial 
Bondholder of a Series 2022 Bond (having been mailed notice from the Bond Trustee, DTC, a Direct Participant or 
otherwise) to notify the Beneficial Bondholder of the Series 2022 Bond so affected, shall not af fect the validity of the 
redemption of such Series 2022 Bond.

Events of Default under the Bond Indenture 
Any of the following will constitute an "Event of Default" under the Bond Indenture with respect to all of the 
Outstanding Bonds:  
(a) Failure to pay any portion of the principal or pre mium, if any, o f any Outstanding Bond w hen due 
and payable;   
19 
 
(b) F ailure to pay a ny portion of i nterest on any O utstanding Bond when such interest payment is due 
and payable; 
(c) Failure by the Bond Issuer to comply with the provisions of the Home Rule Act relating to the Bonds 
or the Pr oject or  to observe or perf orm any other cov enant, agreement or obligation on its part to be obser ved or 
performed and whic h is contained in t he Bond Indenture or in t he Bonds, which failure shall have continued for a 
period of sixty (60) days after written notice, by registered or certified mail, to t he Bond Issuer, the Collateral Agent 
and the Borrower specifying the failu re and requiring t hat it be remedied, which notice may be given by the  Bond 
Trustee in its discretion a nd will be give n by the Bond Trustee at the w ritten request of the Bondholders of not less 
than a ma jority in ag gregate principal amo unt of B onds then out standing, or such longer period as is reasonably 
necessary under the circumstances to remedy such failure not to exceed ninety (90) days; or 
(d) A Loan Agreement Default has occurred and is continuing. 
In the case of clauses (a ) and (b) described above, such non-payment will be an Event of Default if it remains 
unremedied for a period of three (3 ) Business Days, provided t hat it will not constitute an Ev ent of Default if such  
failure re sults from a t echnical error or omissio n, caused by a party  o ther than Borrower, in conne ction w ith the 
administration of the  accounts from which such p ayments are to be made and continues for a period not greater than 
two (2) Business Days after notice of such non-payment is received by Borrower from the Bond Trustee. 
Remedies Following  and During the Continuance of an Event of Default 
Upon the occurrence and during the conti nuance of an Even t of Default under t he Bond Indenture, any  
Bondholder or the Bond Issuer may deliver to the  Bond Trustee a written notice, with a copy to the Bond Issuer, the 
Collateral Agent and the Borrower that an Event of Default has occurred and is continuing.  The Bond Trustee will not 
be deemed to have any knowledge of the occurrence of an Event of Default, except with respect to an Event of Default 
as descr ibed in c lause (a) or (b ) in "—Events of  Default un der the Bond Indenture " above, unles s and until a 
Responsible Officer has received such a notice from the relevant party. 
At any t ime duri ng w hich an Event of Default under the Bond I ndenture has occu rred and is co ntinuing, 
commencing on the date of delivery to the Bond Trustee of the notice described above (except with respect to an Event 
of Default described in cla uses (a) or (b) in "—Even ts of Default un der the Bond Indenture " above, which does not 
require notice), the Bondholders of a ma jority of the aggregate p rincipal amount of Outstanding B onds will have the 
right to give the Bond Trustee one or more enforcement directions directing the Bond Trustee to take on behalf of the 
Bondholders, subject to the Collater al Agency Agreement and the immediately succeeding paragraph, whatever action 
at law or in equity may appear necessary or desirable to enforce the rights of the Bondholders.   
Upon the occurrence and during the continuance of  an Event of Default, if so ins tructed by the Bondholders 
of not less than a majori ty of the  aggregate principal am ount of the th en Out standing Bonds (or 100% of the 
Bondholders in the c ase of an Event of Default under clause (c) in "—Events of Default unde r the Bond Indenture " 
above), the Bond Trustee, subject to the immediately following provisos, will (a) declare all Bonds, all interest accrued 
and unpaid thereon, and all other amounts payable in respect of the Bonds to be due and payable whereupo n the same 
shall become immediately du e and payable without presentment, demand, protest or further  notice of any kind, al l of 
which are waived by the Bond Issuer; provided that the Outstanding Bonds may be accelerated pursuant to the Bond 
Indenture only to the extent the underlying loan under  t he Loan Agreement (or any other loan pursua nt to any 
Supplemental Loan Agreement ) will have been or is b eing a ccelerated and/o r (b) instruct the  Collateral Agent t o 
foreclose on any or all of the Collatera l or proceed  to en force all remedies availab le to t he Bond Trustee or the 
Collateral Agent pursuant to the Finance Documents.  For further details of remedies available to the Collateral Agent 
see "SECURITY AND SOURCES OF REPAYMENT FOR THE SERIES 2022 BONDS" herein. 
The Majo rity Bondholders may, by written notice to the Bond Trustee, on behalf of all o f the Bo ndholders, 
rescind any acceleration and i ts consequences, if the rescission would not conflict with any judgment or de cree and if 
all existing Events of Default ( except nonpayment of principal, inte rest or  premium t hat has become due solely 
because of the acceleration) have been cured or waived and the Bond Issuer has paid or deposited, or caused to be paid 
or deposited, with the Bond Trustee a sum sufficient to pay all sums paid or advanced by the Bond Trustee under the 
Bond Indenture and the reasonable compensation, expenses, disbursements and advances of the Bond Trustee, and the

Collateral Agent, as applicable, their respective agents and counsel.  In case of an y such rescission, then and in every  
such case the Bond Issuer, the Bond Trustee, the Collateral Agent and the Bondholders will be restored to their former 
positions and rights. 
The Bond Trustee will waive any Event of Default upon the written direction of Bondholders owning not less 
than a majority of the a ggregate principal amount of the Outstanding Bonds; provided, howeve r, that any Ev ent o f 
Default in the payment of the principal of or interest on, or the Redemption Price of, any Bond when due shall not  be

Bond Tru stee a nd the Bondholders shall be resto red to  their former positions and rig hts, b ut no such waiver shall

Outstanding Bonds and receipt of indem nity to its satisf action will, in i ts own name (a) by mandamus, or other suit,

of the Bond Indenture for the benefit of the Bondholders and to perform its duties under the Home Rule Act; (b) bring

an ex press t rust f or the Bondholders; and ( d) by a ction or suit in equity enj oin a ny a cts or  things w hich may b e 
unlawful or in violation of the rights of the Bondholders. 
All rights and actions and cla ims under th e Bond Inde nture may be prosecuted an d enforced by the Bond 
Trustee on beha lf of the Bondholders.  In t he cas e of  pendency of any receivership, inso lvency, liquidation,

deliverable on any such cla ims and to distribute the same in accordance with the terms of the Bond Inden ture and of 
the Collateral Agency Agreement.

DESIGNATION AS GREEN BONDS 
The Borrower h as se lf-designated the  2022 Bon ds a s "Green Bonds" b ased on the Green Bond P rinciples 
("GBP"), updat ed as o f June 2021, established as a financ ial bo nd i nstrument where the proceed s or an equivalent 
amount will be exclusively appli ed to finance or  re-finance, i n part or in full , ne w and/or existing eligible Green 
Projects in  accordance with the Sustainability B ond Guidelines and the S ustainability-Linked B ond Pri nciples. The 
Borrower believes the Project constitute s an " eligible Green Project " under the GBP  because it has the following key 
environmental and social purposes:  
• To improve the District’s sustainability efforts b y reducing streetlight ene rgy consumption by m ore 
than 50%, eliminating 38,000 tons of greenhouse gas emissions each year;  
• Reduction of glare and light pollu tion that could affect wild life or other light sensitive acti vities in 
the District;  
• Promote more efficient management systems with remote monitoring and control capabilities; and 
• Adding 239 Wirel ess Access Points, expanding the District’s b roadband Wi -Fi network to more 
areas of n eed a nd integrating smart c ity tec hnology to  track and improve the net works and the 
networks' energy usage.  
The GBP a re voluntary process guidelines that recommend transp arency and disclosure and are intended to 
promote integrity by clarifying the approac h for issuance of a Green B ond. The GBP are comprised of the following 
four components:  
• Use of Proceeds;  
• Process for Project Evaluation and Selection;  
• Management of Proceeds; and  
• Reporting.  
In accordance with the 2021 edition of the GBP, the following  sections set forth information relating to the 
Project and the use of proceeds of the 2022 Bonds within the framework of the four components of the GBP.  
Use of Proceeds 
The green bond utilization of the pr oceeds tow ards a green  p roject must provide clear  environmentally 
sustainable benefits which, where feasible, will be qu antified or assessed by the Bond Issuer. The Borrower, not the 
Bond Issuer, has take n all act ion and will undertake all future action co ncerning the el igibility of the Project using 
green bond proceeds and the utilization of the proceeds of the 2022 Bonds for an eligible green project.  
The Project i s expected to generate certain environmental and other benefits as further outlined below a nd in 
the Technical Advisor Report.  
Process for Project Evaluation and Selection  
The GBP e ncourages a high level of transparency in the evaluatio n and selectio n process and recommends 
that a green bon ds issuer outline the decision-making process it f ollows to determine the el igibility of a project usin g 
green bond proceeds.   
   
23 
 
While the D istrict, not the Borrower, d eveloped and obta ined environmental approvals for the Pro ject and 
assessed the environmen tal, social and economic effects of the Project, based on the recor ds of su ch app rovals, the 
Borrower has concluded that the Project constitutes an "eligible Green Project" under the GBP.  
Per the GBP, "Project Evaluation and Selection", the development, bidding, and award of the Project has been 
carried out with a high level of diligence and transparency at each stage.  
Management of Proceeds 
Proceeds of the Series 2022 Bonds will be tracked by the Borrower.  So long as the Series 2022 Bonds remain 
outstanding, the balance of the proceeds will be reduced by amounts disbursed for the Pr oject.  Provided the bonds  are 
still outstanding, the balance of the fun ds generated through the bond  issue will be red uced through disburseme nt to 
the Project.  
Reporting  
Until the Serie s 20 22 bond proceeds have been fu lly alloc ated, the Borrower w ill pu blish annual updates 
regarding the amount of net  proceeds expended on th e Project.  Disclosures will be made through the Electronic 
Municipal Market A ccess system o f the Municipal Securit ies Rulemaking Board, accessible at www.e mma.msrb.org.  
Once all net proceeds of the Series 2022 Bonds are expended a nd disclosure regarding such expenditures is m ade, no 
further disclosures as to expenditure of proceeds will be provided.  The Borrower has committed to a number of design 
and oper ational characteristics of t he Project in order to ad vance key environmental  goals of the Project.  The 
Borrower may provide reports and updates to this end, however, such reports and updates are provided on a vol untary 
basis and are not included as part of the Borrower’s Continuing Disclosure Agreement for the Series 2022 Bonds 
A key element of the Proj ect is the energy savin gs target of over 50% against the existing baseline usa ge 
which pr imarily utilizes  inefficient in candescent and high i ntensity discharge a rc tubes ( "HPS") bulbs. LED lights 
generally last between 35,000 to 50,000 hours which is  a 51% improvement from HPS and 96% improvement from 
incandescent bulbs. This will drastically reduce both energ y consumption, but also outages and the t ime and effor t 
required to repl ace street lights. Furth ermore, the Borrower is held to performance metri cs with financial penalties to 
ensure energy performance is upheld throughout the P roject term. The Borrower will maintain a continuous log of the 
energy performance throughout the Project term.  
Per Green Bond Principles, "Reporting", the Project has numerous quantitative key performance indicators to 
help measure and just ify its expected en vironmental perfo rmance, and he nce ju stify the expectation  of the Green 
Bonds investors. The requirements of the PA ensure the Project conforms to this principle. 
Technical Ad visor Review  
Based on its review, the LTA makes the following conclusions 
• The Project conforms to t he Green B ond Cat egories of Ene rgy efficiency, and to a le sser extent, 
Pollution Prevention and Control; and 
• The Project meets the Core Green Bond princip les of Use of Proceeds, Process for Project 
Evaluation and Selection, Management of Proceeds, and Reporting. 
Based on the above, the Borrower considers the Project to be appropriate for green financing. 
For further information, see "APPENDIX L—GREEN BOND REPORT."

THE DC SMART STREET LIGHTING PROJECT 
Overview 
The scope of the District of Columbia Smart Street Lighting Project encompasses the Project. 
The OP3, in conjunction with DDOT and OCTO, i ssued th e RFP for t he Project in accordance with the 
District of Col umbia Public-Private Partnership Act of 2014 (" P3 Act"), and Ti tle 27, Cha pter 48, of the  District of 
Columbia Municipal Regulations, to seek competitive proposals for a public-private partnership ("P3") for the Project.   
The specific components of the Project are set for th in, and governed by the terms of, the Project Agreement, and 
consist of the following as described in the Technical Provisions:  
(a) Designing and converting the exi sting non-LED luminaires in the Existing Street Light Network to 
LED technology, as well as replacing or upgrad ing t he e xisting LED lumina ires to meet the requirements  of the 
Technical Provisions, providing for color te mperatures and wattage appropriate to  their setting, inc luding repairing, 
renewing, rehabilitating , replacing, an d upgrading e xisting wirin g and electrical sys tems necess ary to suppor t the 
conversion to LED technology;  
(b) Designing, pr ocuring, installing, m aintaining, upgrading fro m time to tim e, manag ing, monit oring 
and using a R emote Monitoring and Control System ( "RMCS") to pro vide the District with real-time, fully scalable, 
reliable, accurate, monitori ng and c ontrol of t he Stre et L ight N etwork that relies o n open stan dards and includes 
dynamic and environmentally responsive dimming capability; 
(c) Upgrading, repairing or replacing  D istrict-owned: l uminaires; the mounting system for  l uminaires 
(including the poles, arms, bases, and foundation); the complete electrical system above and below groun d connecting 
the luminaire to the ele ctrical power  supply; the conduits and cabinets housing the electrical system and the access 
points to the electrical system and any othe r asse t necessary to p rovide i llumination of the District's Street Light 
Network w here appropriate or required based on  their c ondition and a  p re-determined process and cr iteria fo r 
establishing when such assets must be replaced; 
(d) Managi ng, mo nitoring, m aintaining, renewing, and  rehabilitating the S treet L ight Network and 
performing any other Work necessary to meet the Performance Requirements; 
(e) Expanding the Street Light Network occasionally as requested by the District; 
(f) Maintaining an accurate inventory of streetlight assets at all times;  
(g) Performing Make Safe W ork a nd Administrative Red irects promptly when so requested by t he 
District;  
(h) Performing demonstration testing, and c ommissioning te sting to satisfy the Distr ict's r equirements 
and demonstrating that the LED Luminaires meet the requirements of the Technical Provisions;  
(i) Performing some trimming of trees that may obstr uct or impede th e proper illum inance of the 
Luminaires; and  
(j) Installing the Smart City Improvements. 
The District will retain ownership of each e lement of the St reet Light Network and the associated rights of  
way. The payment obligations of the District to the Borrower pursuant to the Project Agreement are based solely upon 
the Borrower's performance of its obligations and are not  dependent upon or contingent in any way on toll or other 
project revenues that the District may or may not receive over the Term.    
25 
 
The District has identified six key goals for the Project. Th ese goals have been developed j ointly by DDOT, 
OP3, and OCTO. 
(a) Public and Traffic Safety: Reduce traffic-related accidents and injuries in keeping with the District's 
Vision Zero goals and enhan ce cr ime prev ention a nd detection by providing more uniform and  reliable lig hting 
throughout all of the District's Wards through the use of LED street lights. 
(b) Design and Historic Preservation: Design and implement a modernized street l ight system, including 
dimming, shielding and other adjustments to minimize impacts on wildlife and eliminate light pollution. Having given 
due consideration to the nature of historic districts, protected landmarks, and the District's status as the nation's capital, 
there should be no change to the structure of the street light system or style following the conversion to LED fixtures. 
(c) Smart City Technology: Introduce functionality for the street light system to s erve as a plat form for 
ancillary uses, including Wi-Fi technology, that will help close the digital divide, increase effectiveness and efficiency 
of government services, and promote economic development. 
(d) Energy Efficiency and Performance-Based Delivery: Reduce energy consumption by more than 50% 
per year, resulting in a reduction of approximately 38,000 ton s of greenhouse gases annually throughout the District, 
by installing energy ef ficient LED L uminaires th at ha ve a useful life that is t hree to fou r times  longer than t he 
technology cu rrently in us e, and i mplement an improved pay -for-performance mechanis m t hat maintains the s treet 
lighting system at a higher state of repair. 
(e) Risk Transfer: Transfer project in tegration and other commercial risks to a private  entity across the 
appropriate aspects of t he Proje ct beyond those currently t ransferred through tra ditional performance-based 
contracting mechanisms. 
(f) Stakeholder  Engagement: Foster sta keholder understanding and support throughout Proj ect 
development and implementation. 
Project Phasing 
The D&C Work and the Asset Management Work are phased for the Project. This is de picted in the Figure 
below.   
26 
 
Project Phasing 
 
 
Key consideration of the phasing are as follows: 
• The conversi on of the  Existing S treet Light Network is disag gregated into bu ndles (" Bundle"). A bun dle 
consists of a set of streetlights that has been established by the Borrower based on required bundling criteria;  
• Preliminary Work (design work) is required for each Bundle; 
• The Borrower i s requ ired to  perform asset management o n the entire Existing St reet Li ght Network  from 
achievement of NTP3 of the first Bundle; and 
• The Borrower is required to perform asset management of the ent ire Improved Street Light Network from 
Project Final Completion. Project Final Completion is achieved when the Borrower has achieved Street Light 
Bundle Substantial Completion and Smart City Bundle Subs tantial Completion of all Stree t Light B undles 
and Smart City Bundles.

Implementation of the Project 
The Project Agreement governs the relatio nship between the District in connec tion with the de sign, 
development, construction, financ ing and ass et ma nagement of the Pro ject.  For a more detaile d descript ion of t he 
Project Agreement, see "PROJECT AGREEMENT" and "APPENDIX B—SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVIS IONS 
OF THE PROJECT AGREEMENT." 
Construction of the Project is being undertaken by the D&C Contractor, pursuant to a "fixed-price/fixed-date" 
D&C Co ntract.  For a m ore detailed de scription, see "D&C CONTRACT AND D&C GUARANTEE" and 
"APPENDIX C—SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE  D&C CONTRACT." 
Asset management of th e Pro ject is  being undertake n by ENGIE Services Inc. ( the " Asset Management 
Contractor"), pursuant to  a "fixed price s chedule" Asset Management Contract.  For a m ore detailed description, see  
"ASSET MANAGEMENT CONTRACT" and "APPENDIX D—SUMMARY OF  CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE 
ASSET MANAGEMENT CONTRACT."

District-owned Elements included in the Asset  Inventory and Condition Report  and the condition ratings with respect 
to such Elements is set forth below.

included in the i nventory set fort h in the Asset Inventor y and Condition R eport or (ii) in a phy sical condition that is 
worse than the rating for such Element set forth in t he Asset Inventory and Condit ion Report, the B orrower will be

District Change process, see " APPENDIX B —SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PRO VISIONS OF T HE PR OJECT

SECURITY AND SOURCES OF REPAYMENT FOR THE SERIES 2022 BONDS 
Sources of Payment Generally 
Payments due un der th e Series 2 022 B onds are expected  to be m ade from sources including Availability 
Payments and Milestone Payments paid to the Borrower under the Project  Agreement, Equity Contributions under the 
Equity Contribution Agreement and other available amounts, as more fully described herein. Principal repayments due 
under t he S eries 2 022 Bonds are principally expected to be pai d fro m Availability Payments paid t o the Borrower 
under the Project Agreement. 
The Borrower will pay the principal of, and interest on, the loan made to it by the Bond Issuer pursuant to the 
Loan Agreement, so as to enable the Bond Issuer to pay the principal of, and interest on, the Series 2022 Bonds. 
Collateral Generally 
The Secured Obligations will constitute direct, senior secured and unconditional obligations of the Borrower, 
which will rank pari passu and ratably without any preference or priority among themselves and will rank in prio rity 
to all unsecured obligations o f the Borrower and will be secured b y substantially all personal property interests of the 
Borrower ( to th e extent pe rmitted and except  otherwise expres sly provided), as described in fur ther detail at "—
Security Agreement" below. 
The Holdco will pledge its membership interests in t he Borrower and any de bt d ue to the Holdco (as 
creditor) from the Borrower under the terms of the  Holdco Pledge Agreement (as described  in further details at "—
Holdco Pledge Agreement" below).  
The Series 2022  Bonds are Limited Obligations 
The Series 2022 Bonds are special obligations of the Bond Issuer payable solely from the Trust Estate and 
certain other collateral held by the Collateral Agent on behalf of the Secu red Parties and shall be a valid claim of the 
holders thereof only against the Trust Estate  and such other collateral, which is being assigned and pledged under the 
Bond I ndenture and t he other Finance Do cuments for the equal and rat able payment of the Series 2022 Bonds and 
shall be used for no other purpose than to pay the principal of, premium, if any, and interest on the Series 2022 Bonds, 
except a s may be otherwise expressly authorized in the Bond Indentur e.  The princi pal or rede mption price of and 
interest on the Se ries 2022 Bonds are payable  solely and exclusively  from the revenues and funds pledged for their 
payment pursuant to the Bond Indenture.  
 
THE SERIES 2022 B ONDS S HALL BE SPECIAL OBLIGAT IONS OF THE BOND ISSUER.  THE 
SERIES 2022 BONDS  ARE WITH OUT R ECOURSE TO  THE BOND ISSUER.  T HE SERIES 2022 BONDS  
SHALL NOT BE GENER AL OBLIGATIONS OF THE BOND ISSUER, SHALL NOT BE A PLEDGE OF OR 
INVOLVE THE FAITH AND CREDIT OR THE TAXI NG POWER  OF THE BOND IS SUER, SHALL NOT 
CONSTITUTE A DEBT OF THE BOND ISSUER, AND SHALL NOT CONSTITUTE LENDING OF THE P UBLIC 
CREDIT FOR PRIVATE UNDERTAKINGS AS PROHIBITED BY SECTION 6.02(A)(2) OF THE HOME RULE 
ACT.  THE SERIES 2022 BOND S ARE PAYABLE SOLELY FROM THE  LOAN PAYMENTS RECEIVED BY 
THE BOND TRUSTEE FROM THE BORROWER AND ARE S ECURED BY A PLEDGE OF THE TRUST 
ESTATE, ALL OF WHICH SHA LL, EXCEPT AS MAY BE OTHERWISE EXPRESSLY AUTHORIZED IN THE 
BOND INDENTURE, BE USED FOR NO OTHER PURP OSE T HAN TO PAY THE P RINCIPAL OF, 
REDEMPTION PREMIUM, IF ANY, ON, AND INTEREST ON, THE SERIES 2022 B ONDS.  THE SERIES 2022 
BONDS SHALL NOT GIVE RISE TO ANY PECUNIARY LIABILITY OF THE BOND ISSUER AND THE BOND 
ISSUER SHALL HAVE NO OBLIGATION WITH RESPECT TO THE P URCHASE OF  THE SERIES 20 22 
BONDS, UPON ISSUANCE, SALE AND DELIVERY OF THE SERIES 2022 BONDS.

subject to the Collateral Agency Agreement, the Collateral Agent may exercise remedies authorized by Law, as further

(a)  t he ri ght to require the Borrower to a ssemble the Collateral o wned by it at su ch pl ace or pla ces,

to extend the time of payment, arrange for payment in installments, or otherwise modify the terms of all or any part of 
the Collateral;

receive any money or property at any time payable or receivable on account of or in exchange for all or any part of the 
Collateral, but will be under no obligation to do so;  
(d)  the right to, upon fifteen (15) days' prior written notice to th e Borrower of the time and place,  with 
respect to the Collateral or any part thereof that shall then be o r shall thereafter come into the possession, custody or 
control of the Collateral A gent or the S ecured Parties or any of their respective agents, sell, lease, assign or otherwise 
dispose of all or any part of such Collateral, at such place or places as the Collateral Agent deems best, and for cash or 
for credit or for future delivery (without thereby assuming any credit risk) at public or private sale , without demand of 
performance or notice of intention to effect any such disposition or of the time or place thereof (except as required by 
applicable Law);

of a secured party under the UCC and any relevant laws or 
(f)  have a court having juris diction appoint a receiver, which receiver shall tak e charge and p ossession

or concurrently, and such action shall not operate to estop or prevent the Collateral Agent from pursuing any other or 
further remedy which it may have hereunder or by law. 
Holdco Pledge Agreement 
The Collateral Agent, on behalf of the Bondholders of the Series 2022 Bonds and the other Se cured Parties, 
will enter into the Holdco Pledge Agreement, under which the Holdco will pledge: 
(a) its present and future member interests and other equity interests in the Borrower (being 100% of the 
issued and outstanding share or equity interests in the Borrower at the Financial Closing Date);  
(b) all dividends, distributions, return of capital,  cash, instruments and other p roperty from time to tim e 
received, receivable or otherwise distributed in respect of or in exchange for any or all of the equity interest;  
(c) all subscription warrants, rights or options issued thereon or with respect of such equity interests;  
(d) all of its rights, privileges, authority and power under the LLC Agreement;  and 
(e) any I ndebtedness from time t o time owed to  it by the Borrower or any  successor e ntity and the 
instruments, if an y evidencing such Indebtedness, and all interest, cash, instruments and other property from time to 
time received, receivable or otherwise distributed in respect or in exchange thereof. 
The Holdco Pledge Agreemen t w ill provide s ecurity fo r the  obligations of the Borrower under the Loan 
Agreement, the Collateral Agency Agreement and the o ther Finance Documents and will be enforceable upon the 
occurrence of an Event of Default. So long as an Event of Defa ult has not occurred and is not continuing to occur, the 
relevant pledgor will be entitled to exercise voting rights and other consent rights, unless doing so would be reasonably 
expected to have an a dverse ef fect on the value of the collateral pledged und er the Holdco Pledge Agreement (the 
"Pledged Collateral") or the remedies of the Collateral Agent, and to receive and retain any and all dividends, interests, 
income, cash and other amounts payable in cash to the extent permitted by the terms of the Finance Documents.  T he   
34 
 
Holdco, as pledgor, agrees under the Holdco Pledge Agreement that it will not sell, assign or otherwise dispose of any 
of the Pledged Colla teral or create or  a llow any Security I nterest to be cr eated thereon , except f or the pledge, 
assignment and security interest created under the Holdco Pledge Agreement.

(other than the HRA), including the withdrawal of proceeds of the Bonds to pay for or reimburse Project Costs, subject 
to (other t han in res pect of amount  in the HRA) the satisfaction by the Borrower of cer tain requir ements for

FINANCING FOR THE PROJECT 
Series 2022 Bonds 
Upon the issuanc e of the Serie s 20 22 B onds, all  of the proceeds of s uch S eries 2022 Bonds wi ll be 
immediately loaned by the Bond  Issuer to the Borrower in a ccordance with and s ubject to the te rms of the Loan 
Agreement, the Bond Indenture and the Colla teral Agency Agreement, to pay certain costs of the  Project. The Ser ies 
2022A Bond proceeds (after any discount or premium and the Underwriter's discount) will be deposited initially into 
the Tax -Exempt Bonds  Costs of Issuan ce Sub -Account and the Tax-Exempt Bonds Proceeds Sub-Account of  the 
Construction Account and the Ser ies 2 022B Bond proceeds (afte r a ny dis count or premium  and the Un derwriter's 
discount) will b e deposited initially into the Taxable Bonds Proceeds Sub -Account of the Construction Account a nd 
the T axable Bonds Cost s of Issuance Sub -Account o f the C onstruction Account. Am ounts deposited in t he 
Construction Account will be invested in Qualified Investments at the direction of the Borrower and shall be used by 
the Borrower to pay Project Costs, subject to the ter ms of the C ollateral Agency Agreement a nd the ot her Finance 
Documents. For more information, see "PROJECT ACCOUNTS AND FLOW OF FUNDS—Fl ow of Funds." 
 
Loan Agreement 
Generally.  T he Borrower a nd the Bond Is suer will enter into a Loan Agreement (the "Loan Agreement"), 
pursuant to which the proceeds of the issuance of the Series 2022 Bonds will be loaned to Borrower (the "Loan").  The 
proceeds received fr om the sale of the Series 2022 B onds (including, without limitation, any interest earned b y the 
Borrower in respect thereof), net of costs of issuance, will be deposited directly into the Construction Account held by 
the Collateral A gent pursuant to  the Collateral Agency A greement a s required by the Bond Indent ure a nd the 
Collateral Agency Agreement and agreed to by Borrower.  The Borrower will use the  proceeds of the Loan to pay a 
portion of the Project Costs.  In order to secure the repayment of the Series 2022 Bonds, all of the Bond Issuer's right, 
title and interest in and to the Loan  Agreement (except for Reserved Rig hts) will be as signed to, and are sub ject to a 
security interest in favor of, the Bond Trustee pursuant to the Bond Indenture. 
Compliance with  the Bond Indenture.  T o the extent applicable, the Borrower will take all actions required 
to be taken by Borrower in the Bond Indenture as if the Borrower were a party to the Bond Indenture. 
Repayment Terms.  The Borrower will agree to repay the Loan, as follows: on or before any Bonds Payment 
Date for the Series 2022 Bonds or any other date that any payment of principal or Redemption Price of and interest on 
the Series 2022 Bonds is required to be made or provided for in respect of the Series 2022 Bonds pursuant to the Bond 
Indenture (which payments of principal of and interest on the Series 2022 Bonds will be in the respective amounts set 
forth on the debt  service schedule att ached to the Loan Agreement and as amended from time to time pursuant to the  
Bond Indenture), until the payment of principal  or Rede mption Price  of and interest on the S eries 2022 B onds will 
have been full y paid or provision for the paymen t thereof has been ma de in accordance with the Bond Indenture, in 
immediately avail able funds, a sum w hich, together with any other moneys avai lable for such payment i n t he 
applicable Account of the Debt Service Fund, will en able the Bond Trustee  to pay t o the Bondholders of the Series 
2022 Bonds the amount due and payable on such date as interest, principal or Redemption Price on the Series 2022 
Bonds as provided i n the Bond Indenture.  All pay ments made by the Borrower will be made free and clear of (and 
grossed up for) any tax or stamp duty.  
Obligations of  Borro wer Unconditional.  The obligatio ns of  the Borrower to make payments as requi red 
above and to observe and perform all covenants under the Loan Agreement will be absolute and unconditional.
