Tax Covenants of Borrower and Bon d Issuer.   
(a) The Borrower will not take any action, nor fail to take any action or permit an y action to be taken, if 
any such action or inaction would adversely affect the exclusion from gross income for federal income tax p urpose of 
the interest on t he Series 202 2A Bonds. Without limiting the generality  of the foregoing, the Borrower wi ll comply 
with the instructions and re quirements of the Tax Certificate. The Borrower (or any related persons, as defined in 
Section 147(a)(2) of the Code) will not, pursuant to an arrangement, formal or informal, purchase Series 2022A Bonds 
in an amount related to  t he amount of any obligation  to be acquired from the Borrower by the Bond Issuer. 
Notwithstanding any other provision of this Lo an Agreement such covenants will survive the defeasance of  the Series 
2022A Bonds. 
(b) The re quirements of this section will no t apply to the Series 2022A B onds or any portion of the 
Series 2022A Bonds, if all of the proceeds o f the Series 2022A Bonds are fully expended within six (6)  months after 
the Financial Closing Date. To assist and  act on b ehalf o f the Borrower in the perfo rmance of the covenant in 
paragraph (d) of this section, the Borrower will at least thirty (30) days in advance of any calculatio n required pursuant 
to Section 148 of the Code, during the term of the Series 2022A Bonds, the proceeds of which are not expect ed to be 
fully ex pended six ( 6) months from the Financial Closing Date, and at least thirty (30) days thereafter, retain an 
independent Person to act as Rebat e Monitor and to perform the calculations required purs uant to this section. In the 
event that the  R ebate Monitor becomes unable to perform such calculation, the Borrower will appoint another 
independent Person to perform the s ame w ith the approval of the B ond Issuer, which approval shall no t be 
unreasonably withheld, conditioned or delayed, and will g ive prompt notice of such appointment to the Bond Trustee. 
In the event the Borrower does not appoint a successor Rebate Monitor to perform such calculations, the Bond Issuer 
will appoint such a successor Rebate Moni tor, the expe nse of which will be borne by  the Borrower, and will give 
prompt notice of such appointment to the Bond Trustee. The special covenants of the Borrower in th is se ction 
concerning complia nce with Section 148 of the Code wil l survive the defea sance or payment in full of the Series 
2022A Bonds notwithstanding any other provision of this Loan Agreement until the requirements for payment of any 
amounts required to be paid with respect thereto pursuant to Section 148(f) of the Code have been fully satisfied. 
(c) S ubject to the Reserved Righ ts of the Bond Issuer, in the  event that the Bond Issuer is notified in 
writing that the Series 2022A Bonds or any transaction pertain ing thereto is the subje ct of any Internal Revenue 
Service investigation, suit or order, the Bond Issuer will promptly give notice of the same to the B orrower and allow 
the Borrower to participate in all aspects of the response. In the event that the Borrower is notified in writin g that the 
Series 2022A Bonds or any transaction perta ining to the Series 2022A Bonds is the subj ect of any Internal Revenue 
Service investigation, suit or order, the Borrower will p romptly give notice of the same to the Bond Iss uer and allow 
the Bond Issuer to participate in all aspects of the response. 
(d) On or bef ore the  Financial Closing Date, Bo rrower will provide true and accurate information 
sufficient for the Bond Issuer with the assistan ce of Bond Counsel to file, on or within the legally permitte d period 
after the Financial Closing Date, Form 8038 ( Information Return for Private Activity Bon d Issues) with the Internal 
Revenue Service.

Events of Defa ult under the Loan Agreement.  The following events are "Events of Default" under the Loan 
Agreement (subject to certain cure periods, materiality and other qualifications, as applicable).  See "APPENDIX G—
SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE LOAN AGREEMENT—E vents of Default Defined." 
(a) Failure by the Borrower to pay any a mount required t o be paid  u nder the Loa n A greement as 
described in the subsection "—Repaym ent Terms" above and such non-payment remains unremedied for a period of 
three (3) Business Days, provided that it shall not c onstitute an Event of Default if such failure results from a technical 
error or omission caused by a party other than the Borrower in connection with the administration of the accounts from 
which such payments are to be made and continues for a period not greater than two (2) Business Days after notice of 
such non-payment is received by the Borrower from the Bond Trustee. 
(b) Failure by the Borrower to observe and perform any covenant, condition or agreement on its part to 
be observed or performed under the Loan Agreement, other than as re ferred to in ( a) above, and such non-compliance 
shall remain unremedied for a period of thirty (30) days after the earlier of (i) written notice specifying such failure 
shall have been given to th e Bond Trustee by the Borrower or the Bond Issuer, or (ii ) written notice specifying s uch 
failure and requesting that it b e remedied shal l have been given to the Borrower by the Bond Trustee, or such longer 
period as is reasonably neces sary under the circumstances to remedy such failure, such extension not to e xceed ninety 
(90) days. 
(c) The occurrence o f an Insolvency Event with respect to the Borrower. 
(d) Any o f the repres entations, warranties o r cert ifications of th e Borrower made in or d elivered 
pursuant to  the Loan Agr eement (including, but not limited to the in formation furnished by Borrower to the Bon d 
Issuer for u se by the Bond Issuer in its Tax Certificate or information furni shed by the Borrower in the Application 
submitted to the Distr ict of Columbia's Revenue Bond Pro gram) will prove to ha ve been incorrect when made, unless 
such misrepresentation is capable of being cured and is cure d within thirty (30) days after the Borrower's receipt of 
written notice from the Bond Trustee of such misrepresentation. 
(e) An "Event of Default" occurs under the Bond Indenture or the Collateral Agency Agreement. 
(f) Any Security Document ceases, except in accordance w ith its terms or as ex pressly permitted under 
the Finance Documents, to be effective to grant a  perfected Security Interest on any material portion of the C ollateral 
described therein, o ther than as a result of acti ons or f ailure to act by  the Bond Tru stee, the Collateral Agent or any 
other Secured Party. 
(g) Any acceleration occurs of the maturity of any indebtedness authorized under the Collateral Agency 
Agreement pursuant to any Finance Document, or any such indebtedness shall n ot be paid in  full upon the  fin al 
maturity thereof.  
Remedies on E vent of Default   
Whenever any Event of Default referred to in  the section "—Ev ents of Default under the Loan Agreement" 
above will have occurred and be continuing, the Bond Trustee, or the Bond Issuer with the written consent of the Bond 
Trustee, has the right to, in conjunction with its available remedies under the Bond Indenture and subject to the terms   
38 
 
of the Collater al Agency Agreement, take one or any comb ination of the following r emedial steps, by notice to the 
Borrower and the Collateral Agent: 
(a) Declare that all or any part of any amount outstanding under the Loan Agreement is (i) immediately 
due and payable, and/ or (ii) payable on demand  by the Bond Trustee, and any such notice shall take effect  in 
accordance with its terms but only if all amounts payable with respect to the Outstanding  Bonds are being accelerated 
pursuant to the Bond I ndenture, or if all of the Outstan ding Bonds are b eing defeased under the terms of the Bond 
Indenture or otherwise paid in full;  
(b) Take on behalf of the Bondholders whatever other action at law or in equity as may appear necessary 
or desirable to collect the amounts then due and thereafter to b ecome due, or to enforce performance and observance 
of any obligation, agreement or covenant of the Borrower under the Loan Agreement or the rights of the Bondholders;  
(c) Have reasonable access to and inspect, examine and make copies of the books a nd records and any 
and al l accounts, data and income tax and other tax r eturns of  the Borrower during regular b usiness hours of  the 
Borrower and following prior reasonable notice; or 
(d) Pursuant to the terms of the Securit y Documents, direct the Collatera l Agent to take or cause to be 
taken any and all actions necessary to implement any available remedies with respect to the Collateral under any of the 
Security Documents. 
Any amounts collected pur suant to action taken under  this section "Remedies on Event of Defa ult" and the 
Security Documents paid to the Bond Trustee will be applied in accordance with the provisions of the Bond Indenture. 
Any rights and r emedies as are given to the Bond Issuer under the Lo an Agreement will also extend to the 
Bondholders, and the Bond Trustee, subject to the p rovisions of the Bond Indenture, will be e ntitled to the benefit of 
all covenants and agreements contained in the Loan Agreement, subject to the terms of the Security Documents. 
Rescission/Waiv er 
(a)  The Bond Trustee  or the Bond Issuer, as applicable, shall rescind any acceleration and it s 
consequences immediately after the acceleration of the Series 2022 B onds has b een rescinded in accordance with the 
Bond Indenture.  
(b) T he Bond Trustee or the Bond Issuer, as appli cable, shall w aive any Event of Defaul t immediately 
after any such Event of Default has been waived in accordance with the Bond Indenture. 
(c) The Bond Trust ee or th e Bond Issuer, a s applicable, shall have t he right, but shall be unde r no 
obligation (except with respect to clauses (a) and (b) above), to waive any other Event of Default at any time.  
In case o f any such waiver or rescission, then and in every s uch case the B ond Issuer, the Bond Trustee and 
Borrower shall be restored to their former positions and rights, but no such waiver shall  extend to any  subsequent or 
other Event of Default, or impair any right consequent thereon.

subsequent to the issua nce o f the Series 2022 Bonds an d unles s and until all con ditions provided for i n t he Bond 
Indenture for release of  the Bond Indenture h ave been met, the Loan Agreement m ay not be effectively amend ed, 
changed, modified, altered or termi nated except by  an instrument in wr iting signed by the Borrower and the Bond 
Issuer, consented to by the Bond Trustee, and in accordance with the provisions of the Bond Indenture, as applicable.  
No amendment, change or modi fication to the Loan Agreement will be effective without the prior wri tten consent of 
the Borrower and the Bond Issuer.

Collateral Agency Agreement  
General. The Collateral Agency Agreement will be en tered into by the Borrower, the C ollateral Agent, the 
Bond Trustee and the Securities Intermediary. The Collateral Agency Agreement contains a number of common term s 
and covenants binding on the Borrower, and the use of the proceeds of the Se ries 2022 Bonds and all other Revenues 
together with all payments to  be made from or betw een the Proje ct Accounts will be governed by the terms of th e 
Collateral Agency Agreement. 
 
Covenants of the Borrower. In the Collateral Agency Agreement, the Borrower w ill undertake to co mply 
with certain covenants, including, but not limited to the following: 
 
(a) Maintain L egal Structure. Throughout the ter m of th e Collateral Agency Agreement, the Borrower 
will maintain (i) its legal e xistence as  a limited liability company, ( ii) its good standing and qua lification to do 
business in the District of Columbia, and ( iii) all material rights, franchises, privileges and consents  necessary for t he 
maintenance of its ex istence and for the developmen t, construction and operation and maintenance of the Project and 
in order to perform its obligations and exercise its remedies under the Transaction Documents. 
 
(b) No Repu
 diation of Finance Documents. The Borrower will not take any action to repudiate any of its 
obligations under the Finance Documents. 
 
(c) Litig
 ation.  The Borrower will take all necessary action, acting reasonably, to manage a ny litigation 
to which it is a p arty in relation to th e Project. T he Borrower will manage such litigation in a prudent and diligent 
manner and may enter into any settlement of any such litigation, subject, in any case, to the terms and conditions of the 
Project Documents.  
 
(d) Proj
 ect Document Amendments.  
 
 (i) The Borrower will perform all its obligations a nd enforce all i ts rights under each Pr oject 
Document, except to the extent that failure to e nforce such rights could not reasonably be expected to have  a 
Material Adverse Effect.

(including amendments expressly permitted in accordance with the terms of this pa ragraph (d)), the Borrower 
will (A) not amend, modify or waive any default under any material provision of a Proje ct Document; (B) not 
terminate, assign or surrender any Project Document; (C) consent to any of the foregoing in subsections ( A) 
and ( B) in re spect o f a ny Pro ject Do cument; (D) not amend or wa ive any default  under any Per formance 
Support or termi nate, assi gn or surrender any Performanc e Suppor t o r consent to any  of the foregoing in 
respect of a ny Pe rformance Support; and ( E) not amend, modify or  w aive any default unde r or termin ate, 
assign or surrender Borrower's operating agreement or the organizational documents of the Borrower, in each 
case of (A)-(E) above, in a manner which could reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect: 
 
 (iii) Subject to clause ( v) below, the B orrower may enter into Changes, amendme nts or 
modifications, as applicable, to the Project  Agreement if such Change, amendment or modification (A) could 
not reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect, an d (B) is not an a mendment to the Secur ed 
Parties rights pursuant to the Collateral Agent's step-in right under the Project Agreement.

(iii) The Borrower hereby covenants for the benefit of the Bond Issuer and the Bondholders of 
the Series 2022A Bonds and the Tax-Exempt Additional Pa rity Bonds that it will not take any action or omit

Series 2022A Bonds or the Tax-Exempt Additional Parity  Bonds if such action or  omission (A) would cause 
interest paid on the Series 2022A Bonds or the Tax-Ex empt Additional Parity Bonds to be included in the 
gross income of the Bondholders of the Series 2022A B onds or the Tax-Exempt Additional Parity Bonds for 
federal income tax purposes (other than interest paid  to Bondholders of the Series 2022A Bonds or the Tax-
Exempt Additional Parity Bonds that are a "substantial user" of the facilitie s financed or refinanced with the 
Bonds or a "related person" within the meaning of Section 147(a) of the Code) or (B) would cause interest 
paid on the Bonds to be included in the taxable income of the Bondholders for District of Columbia income 
tax purposes, under present District of Columbia law. The foregoing covenant shall remain in full force and 
effect notwithstanding the payment in  full or defeasance of the Bonds un til the date on which all obligations 
of the Borrower in fulfilling the above covenant under the Code and District of Columbia law have been met.

(o) Completion. If  the LTA reasonably determines at any time that the Project  Final Completion Date 
will be delay ed beyond the Long Stop De adline, the Borrower will develop in consu ltation with the LTA a r emedial 
plan (an "I nitial Remedial Plan ") satisfactory to the LTA, acting reasonably, that demonstrates  that the Borrower will 
achieve the Proj ect Final Completion Date by no lat er than t he Long Stop Deadli ne. The Borrower will submit the 
Initial Remedial Plan to the LTA as soon as pos sible and by no la ter than fifteen (15) Business Days after such  
determination. The Borrower will comply in all material respects with the Initial Remedial Plan, provided that a breach 
of the Initial Remedial Plan by the Borrower will not constitute or give rise to a Default or an Event of Default. If there 
is any mate rial breach by t he Borrower of th e Initial Remedial Plan, the Borrower will be requi red to develop, in 
consultation with the LTA, a furthe r remedial plan (a "Final Remedial P lan") s atisfactory t o the LTA,  acting 
reasonably, which will again be required to demonstrate that  the Borrower will achieve the Project Final Completion 
Date by no l ater than the Long Stop Deadline. The B orrower will submit the Final Remedial Plan to the LTA  as soon 
as possible and no later  than ten (10) Business Days after the occurrence of a material breach  of the Initial Remedial 
Plan. The Borrower will comply in a ll material respect s with the Final Rem edial Plan, provided that a breach of the 
Final Remed ial P lan by the Borrower will not constitute or give rise to a Default or an Event of D efault. Any 
amendments t o the I nitial Remedial Plan or the Final Reme dial Pla n must be satisfact ory to the LTA, acting 
reasonably. Such Initial Remedial Plan or Final R emedial Plan may be the same as that which may be required under 
the Pro ject Agreement and such remedial plan re quired under the Project A greement will supersede any  Initial 
Remedial Plan or Final Remedial Plan; pro vided that the remedial plan of  the Borrower under the Project Agreement 
must provide that the Project Final Completion Date is achieved by no later than the Long Stop Deadline.

includes, among other things, a summary of the overall constructio n progress up to such date, description of 
any material delay or material cost overrun, a description of any material change ord ers, and a reasonable 
estimate of the Project Final Completion Date for D&C Work; 
(iv) semi-annually during the Post Project Final Co mpletion Period, no later than (i) ninety (90) 
days after the end of the Fiscal Year and (ii) ninety (90) days after the end of the Fiscal Quarter ending on 
June 30th of each Fiscal Year, an operating report  showing, in each case, with respect t o the Project, (a) an 
operating report (including analysis of deductions in curred) and (b) the variances for such period between the 
actual Revenues and actual  Project Costs incurred, and the projected Revenues and bud geted Project Co sts 
respectively for the same per iod as set forth in the Annual Operating Budget, together with a  brief narrative 
explanation of the reasons for any such varianc e of 10% or more; pr ovided, that a delay in d elivery of such 
report or information shall not be a Default or Event of Default under the Finance Documents.  Such report or 
information shall be prepared by the Borrower, in a form and covering such matters as shall be agreed prior to 
the Project Final Completion Date;  
(v) promptly upon submit ting a remediation plan to the District prepared by the Borrower or 
the D&C Contractor pursuant to the Project Agreement, a copy of such remediation plan; and 
(vi) upon expiry of each insurance policy required to be maintained as set forth in the Finance 
Documents, evidence of renewal of each such insurance policy.

Equity Contributions 
 Pursuant to the terms of the Eq uity C ontribution Agreement , the Holdco will agree to  contribute to  the 
Borrower an amount equal to the aggregate amount of the Equity Commitment in accordance with the ter ms of the 
Equity Contribution Agreement.  Equity Contributions will be deposited into the  Equity Contribution Sub-Account of 
the Construction Account a pplied in acco rdance with t he Collater al Agency Agreement.  For more information, see 
"PROJECT ACCOUNTS AND FLOW OF FUNDS—Project Accou nts."  
The Equity Commitments will be secured by Equity Sup port, which may be drawn if among  other things, the 
Holdco fails to make a payment as required  under the Equity Contribution Agreement.  The  Equity Support will be 
delivered to the Collateral Agent.   
Equity Support  supporting the Equity Commitment obligations under the Equity Contribution Agreement 
shall be in form and substance reasonably s atisfactory to the Collateral Age nt, and will includ e the fol lowing terms 
(among others): (a) it shall be issued by a  Permitted Letter of Credit Provider , (b) the reimbursement obligations will 
be with no r ecourse to the Borrower, and ( c) it will allow dr awings for th e reasons set out in  the definition of 
Acceptable Credit Support.  The Borrower or the Collateral Agent shall be entitled to draw on an Equity Sup port (a) in 
the event that the Holdco fails to make a payment under the Equity Contribution Agreement at any time Project Costs 
are due and the Borro wer has no  other s ource of available funds, ( b) during the ten (10)  day period prior to the 
scheduled expiry thereof (unless otherwise renewed or replaced), ( c) at any time after the date that is twenty 
(20) Business Days after the date on which the Equity  Support c eases to  meet the requirements of an Acceptable 
Credit Support if, prior to such date the Holdco has not arranged for the replacement of the appl icable Equity Support 
with a letter of credit meeting such requirements and the other requirements of the Equity Contribution Agreement,  or 
(d) in the event that Holdco fails to make a payment under the Equity Contribution Agreement following an  Event of 
Default. Following any payment of amounts payable under  the Eq uity Contribution Ag reement by payment into the 
Equity Contribution Sub-Account, the principal amount owing under the face amount of the Equity Support  shall be 
reduced by an amount equal to the amount of such payment.  
Upon the occurrence of an Event of D efault, amounts owing under the  Equity Contribution Agree ment will 
become due and payable within three (3) Business Days after  receipt by the Holdco of a  demand from the Collateral 
Agent or the Borrower or automatically (without any requirement for a demand) and immediately due and payable on 
an Event of Default resulting from the occurrence of a Insolvency Event with respect to the Borrower.  
Milestone Payments and Availability Payments 
Generally, Milestone Payments can be characterized as progress payments f or the Borrower’s performance of 
certain segments of the D&C Work and will, subject to annual appropriation of sufficient amounts, be paid to the 
Borrower as partial consideration for D&C Work and all obligations related thereto that have been carried out by the 
Borrower in accordance with the Project Agreement. The amounts of potential Milestone Payments and the dates for 
payment (the "Milestone Dates") are set out in the table below. 
 
Milestone Payment Milestone Date Maximum 
Amount 
Milestone 1 The Borrower is entitled to  invoice the District for Mil estone 1  
on the final day of the Calendar Quarter in which more than 50% 
of the total number of Lighting Units are in Street Light Bundles 
that have achieved Street Light Substantial Completion. Th e 
Borrower expects to invoi ce the District for  Milestone 1 on 
September 30, 2023, and receipt of Milestone 1 is expected on or 
around November 30, 2023. 
$21,970,000 
Milestone 2 The Borrower is entitled to invoice the District for Milestone 2 
on the final day in the  Calendar Quarter in which Project Fi nal 
Completion is achieved. Th e B orrower expects to invoice the 
District for Milestone  2 on June 30, 2024, and receipt of 
Milestone 2 is expected on or around August 31, 2024. 
$22,220,000

Milestone Payments are made with in sixty (60) days following the District’s receipt of a Milestone Payment 
invoice that is accurate and complete to the reasonable satisfaction of the D istrict. Milestone 1 and Milestone 2 will be 
reduced to the extent the B orrower is unable to p rovide evidence of incorporating Conversion Work with a value at 
least equal to, in the case of Milestone 1, the amount of Milestone 1, or in the case of Milestone 2 , the cumulative 
amount of Milestone 1 and Mil estone 2. Addition ally, Milestone 1 and Miles tone 2 will be reduced by t he amount of 
any Noncompliance Deductions or Unavailability Deductions incurred prior to the relevant Milestone Date, provided 
that any Noncompliance Deductions or U navailability Deductions set off agai nst such Milestone Payments  shall be 
recovered from the D&C Contract or or the Asset Management Contractor, as applicable, pursuant to the terms of the 
D&C Contract, the Asset Management Con tract, and the Interface Agreement. Noncompliance Deductions are 
assessed by the D istrict with respect to certain Noncompliance Events which occur prior to Project Final Completion , 
while Unavailability Deductions are assessed by the District in the event that a publicly-owned street, roadway, park or 
sideway, or access t o private property or publi cly-owned building is fully or partially closed or blocked, and such 
closure or blockage is not permitted pursuant to the Project Agreement.

RISK FACTORS 
THE PURCHASE OF THE SERIES 20 22 BONDS IS SUBJECT TO CERTAI N RISKS.  EACH 
PROSPECTIVE INVESTOR  IN THE S ERIES 20 22 BONDS IS ENCOURAGED  TO REA D T HIS 
OFFICIAL STATEMEN T IN ITS ENTIRETY, INCLUDI NG ALL APPENDICES HERETO.  
PARTICULAR ATTENTION SHOULD BE GIVEN TO THE FACTORS DESCRIBED BELOW, WHICH , 
AMONG OTHERS, COULD AF FECT THE PAYMENT OF PRINCIPAL OF AND INTERES T ON THE 
SERIES 2022 BONDS AND WHICH COULD ALSO AF FECT THE MARKET PRICE OF  THE SERIES 
2022 BONDS TO AN EXTENT THAT CANNOT BE DETERMINED. 
The following discussion is not meant to be an exha ustive list of the risks  and other factors t hat should be 
considered in connection with the pur chase of t he Series 2022 Bonds and does not nec essarily reflect the relative 
importance of the various risks and other factors.  The occurrence of any of the fol lowing risks could materially and 
adversely affect the Borrow er's bu siness, financial conditio n and resul ts of o perations or the District's financial 
condition and results of operations .  In any such event, the Borrower may not have sufficient revenues, after paying 
fees and expenses and operations and maintenance expenses, to make payments  pursuant to the Lo an Agreement 
and, in turn, there may be insufficient monies under the Bond Indenture to be able to pay debt service on the Series 
2022 Bonds.  In an y su ch event, the market price and/or liquidity for the Serie s 20 22 B onds could d ecline an d 
investors could lose all or part of their investment.  There can be no assurance that other risk factors will not become 
material in the future. 
Risks Relating to the District and the Borrower 
Borrower is a Single Purpose Entity 
The Borrower was formed for the purpose of e ntering into the Project Agreement and has no assets o ther 
than the  Equity Contr ibutions and t he Borrower's rights under the Pr oject Documents, including the Borrower's 
rights under the Project Agreement to receive the Milestone Payments and the Avail ability Payments.  Substantially 
all of the Borrower's design and construction-related responsibilities in connection with the Proje ct are being passed 
through to the D&C Contractor; substantially all of the Borrower' s asset management -related responsi bilities in 
connection with the Project are being passed through to th e Asset Management Contractor; and substantially all o f 
the Borrower's rights under the Project Agreement and the other Project Documents are being pledged and assigned 
as security for the Borrower's financial obligations in connection with the Project.   
The Borrower's a bility to m ake payments pursuant to the Loan Agreement, all depe nd upon the Borrower 
successfully (a) devel oping, designing, constructing and financing the Project  and (ii) undertaking the  Asset 
Management Services with respect to the  Project.  No assurance can be given, however, that the funds available to 
the Bond Trustee will be sufficient to make all of the payments to be paid from the Trust Estate, including payments 
to be made of principal of and interest on the Series 2022 Bonds. 
The o bligations of the Sponsors are lim ited to their obligations to m ake capital contributions of cash or 
equity to the capital of PIDC Holdco as required by the PIDC Holdco LLC Agreement.  The obligations of the D&C 
Contractor Guarantor are limited to its obligations under the D&C Guarantee.  Neither the Sponsors, nor any direct 
or indirect owner thereof, nor a ny officer, director, member, agent or employee thereof, is obligated t o make any 
payments in connection with, and none of such parties is guaranteeing the Series 2022 Bonds or any other agreement 
entered into by the Borrower or by any other party in connection with the Series 2022 Bonds or the Project.  
Appropriation Risk 
A failure by the Council or Congress (as applicable) to appropriate adequate funds for the Distric t to fulfill 
its obligations pursuant to the Project Agreeme nt may adversely impact the Borrower's ability to satisfy its payment 
obligations under the Loan Agreement and, in tur n, the repaymen t of t he Se ries 20 22 B onds.  For addi tional 
information on pay ments from t he District an d th e app ropriation proces s of the C ouncil and Congress , see  
"PROJECT AGREEMENT," a nd "SOURCE OF FUNDS FOR P AYMENTS BY THE DISTRICT , DISTRICT 
BUDGETING AND APPROPRIATIONS PROCESS UNDER DISTRICT LAW."   
62 
Political Risk and Community Risk 
As in any commercial arrangement, parties may disagree about the appropriate course of action to be taken, 
particularly if adverse events occur.  The District and the Borrower have different priorities and intere sts and may 
have difficulty in re solving disputes should their interests diverge.  S imilarly, the District and the Bond Trustee, on 
behalf of the B ondholders of the Series 2022 Bonds, may have different interests and priorities foll owing a default 
or other adverse event under the Project Agreement , and no assurance can be given that the District will be willing 
or able to take into account the interests of the Bondholders of the Series 2022 Bonds if an event occurs that would 
entitle the District to terminate or to take other remedial action under the Project Agreement. 
Construction of the Project could have considerable local business and community impacts, such as noise, 
dust, and vibrations during the D&C  Period.  Ad dressing these impacts to the satis faction of loca l re sidents and 
businesses could result in delays to and/or in increased costs of t he Project.  To the ext ent these delays or increased 
costs are not Compensation Events or Relief Events under the Project Agreement for which the Borrower is entitled 
to sc hedule, monetary , or pe rformance relief, a nd to the  extent such risks  are not p assed down  to the D&C 
Contractor, it could result in increased costs or loss of rev enues, which could impact the Borrow er's ability to make 
payments in the amounts a nd at the times required to pay debt service on the Series 2022 Bonds or other payments 
required under the Bond Indenture with respect to the Series 2022 Bonds. 
To the ext ent suc h risks are  passed down  to D &C Contractor, the D&C Con tractor could be delayed  i n 
performing the D&C Work and while the D&C Contractor is required to pay liquidated damages to the Borrower in 
certain circumstances in the event of certain delays, suc h amount is limited by the terms of  the D&C Contract and 
may not be sufficient to cover all of the Borrower's losses in t he event of a delay or failure to complete the work in 
accordance with the Project Agreement.  A direct effect on the Project or on the  Borrower, political pressure 
affecting the District in general, or th e District or the  U.S. District of Tr ansportation in particular, co uld ha ve an 
effect on the Bor rower's ability to make payments pursuant to th e Loan Agreement in the amounts and at the t imes 
required to enable the Bond Issuer to pay debt service on the Series 20 22 Bonds or other payments required under 
the Bond Indenture with respect to the Series 2022 Bonds.  For example, future District and/or federal budget, tax or 
expenditure limitations or otherwise could have direct and substantial effects on the amount of funds available to the 
District for any par ticular project (see "—Appr opriation Risk" above).  No assurance can be given that the District 
will always have sufficient resources to perform its monetary and other obligations under the Project Agreement.   
Risks Relating to the Project 
Pass-Through Risks 
The D&C Contract is structured to pass thr ough, for  fixed comp ensation, sub ject to adjust ment in 
accordance wi th terms of the D&C Contract, to the D&C C ontractor all o f t he Borrower's o bligations and risks 
under the Project Agreement with respect to the design and construction of the Project.  There can b e no assurance, 
however, that in all cases all of such responsibilities and risks have been passed through or that events will not occur 
that w ould result in increases i n the compensat ion payable to the D&C Contra ctor t hat ar e no t reimbursed or 
otherwise provided for under the Pr oject Agreement.  In addition, permitted reductions in payment s by the D istrict 
to the Borrower because of non-performance by the D&C Contractor may not be made up by reductions in Borrower 
payments to  t he D&C Contractor, an d unde r some circumstances th e re ductions in p ayments by the  District may 
exceed the amounts for which the D&C Contractor is responsible.  
The As s et Management Contract is struc tured to pass t hrough, for fixed compensatio n, sub ject to 
adjustment i n accordance with terms of  the Asset Management  Contract, to the Asset Management  Contractor 
substantially all of the Borrower's obligations and risks u nder the Project Agreement with respect to the asset 
management services  for the Pro ject.  There can be no assurance, however, tha t in all cases all of  such 
responsibilities and risks have been passed thr ough or that events will not occur that would res ult in increases in the 
compensation payable to the Asset Management Contractor that are not reimbursed or otherwise provided for under 
the Project Agreem ent.  In addition, permitted r eductions in payments b y the District to the Borrower because of 
non-performance by the Asset Management Contractor may not be made up by reductions in Bo rrower payments to 
the Asset Management Contractor, and under some circu mstances the reductions in pay ments by the District m ay 
exceed the amounts for which the Asset Management Contractor is responsible.   
63 
Construction Risks 
As described in "APPENDIX C —SUM MARY O F CERTAIN P ROVISIONS OF THE D &C 
CONTRACT," the D&C Contr act requires the D&C Co ntractor t o achieve Street  Light Bu ndle Substantial 
Completion of a certain number of Lighting Units by certain specified dates and ultimately Project Final Completion 
by the applicable date required in the  Project Agreement.  This schedule, however, may be extend ed under certain 
circumstances.   
As with any major constr uction effort, the construction of the Project involves many risks that could result 
in cost overruns, in delays or in a f ailure to complete the Project at all.  Increased construction costs (including as a 
result of del ays or over runs) could adversely affect t he Borrower's abil ity to pay debt serv ice on the Series 2022 
Bonds.  Failure to meet any of the construction schedule may result in delayed and/or reduced payment of Milestone 
Payments and/or Availability Payments that could impact the Borrower's ability to pay debt service under the Loan 
Agreement which in tur n would  impact the Bond Issuer 's ability to pay de bt service on the Se ries 20 22 Bonds.  
Failure to achieve Street Light Bundle Substantial Completion of the required number of Lighting Units by the dates 
specified in th e D&C Contract may result in delayed and/or reduced payment of Availability Payme nts and/or 
Milestone Payments by th e District t o the Borrower that could impact the Borrower's ability to  pay debt servic e 
under the Loan Agreement which in turn w ould impact the Bond Issuer's ability to pay debt service on the  Series 
2022 Bonds. 
Some of t he risks to  completing the Project on time and within budget include shor tages of materi als and 
labor, work stoppages, labor disputes, bad weather, floods, earthquakes and other casualties, unforeseen engineering, 
environmental or geological probl ems, changes in law, discovery of unidentified geologic or hazardous mater ials or 
unidentified ut ilities, third -party l itigation, difficulties in ob taining or renewing perm its or other  federal or loc al 
government approval s, chan ges in f ederal or loca l design or bui lding requirements a nd per mit conditio ns, any of 
which could incr ease the cost of and de lay the construction and start -up of the Project.  Despite the fact that th e 
D&C Contract is a fixed-price contract and th at a number of risks have been contractually allocated to the D&C 
Contractor under the D&C Contract or to the D istrict under the Project Agreement, not all of these risks have been 
shifted or can be insured and there can be no assurance that the Project will be completed on the projected timetable 
or in line with the budget and other assumptions described in this Official Statement. 
Lack of coordina tion among the Borrower, t he District, the D& C Con tractor, the  Asset Managemen t 
Contractor, the utility owners, or othe r third parties with respect to compl etion of the Work or their inspections on 
schedule could also re sult in d elays and/or co st overruns.  I ncreased costs th at result from delays or change orders 
caused by action s o f the District, Borrower, utility own ers or other third pa rties or by  a Force Majeure Event, 
changes in applicable laws or other uncontrollable circumstances may not in all circumstances be covered under the 
fixed price and schedule set fort h in the D&C Contract and may not, in such circumstances, require payment by the 
D&C Con tractor of liquidated damages to the ext ent the D&C Contractor is not responsi ble for such matters  
pursuant to the D&C Contract.  Some, but not all, of  these events may be covered by the Borrower's builders' risk 
insurance, b y the District pursuant to provisions of the Project Agreem ent that req uire payment of Com pensation 
Amounts o r other  relief for  Force Maj eure Events and other  Compensation Events and Re lief Even ts under the 
Project Agreement and by availab le contingency funds, bu t no assura nce can b e given t hat if such uncontrollable 
events occur the Borrower would have or w ould be able to obtain suf ficient funds to cause the D&C C ontractor to 
complete construction on time or the Bond Issuer to pay debt service on the Series 2022 Bonds or to make the other 
payments required to be made under the Bond Indenture.  Moreover, while the Borrower may be entitled to receive 
compensation from the D&C Contractor for certain delays or defects unless there is an excep tion under the terms of 
the D&C Contract, th e D&C Contr actor could fail t o com pensate the Borrower for such d elays or def ects or  the 
D&C Contractor Guarantor could fail or re fuse to  honor its payment obligations u nder the D&C Guar antee.  In 
either case, t his w ould result in a delay  in the Borrower' s a bility to  collect the s ame or ot herwise prev ent the 
recovery of any such a mounts a ltogether.  In par ticular, col lection efforts  against the D&C Contractor Guarantor 
could entail substantial delay and expense, and might not be successful.  In such cases, the Borrower may be entitled 
to access the Payment Bond; however, such Payment Bond may be insufficient to ensure the Borrower can fulfill its 
obligations and any such failure or delay m ay adversely impact the Borrower's cash flow, its ab ility to comply w ith 
the Project Agreement and its ability to pay debt service under the Loan Agreement which in turn would impact the 
Bond Issuer's ability to pay debt service on the Series 2022 Bonds.    
64 
The D&C Guarantee is a payment and not a performance guarantee and, as such, there are associate risks in 
respect of  timing of recov ery by the Borrower, a s recovery would only available after the Borrower’s claim  for 
payment under the D&C Contract has crystalized. 
Not all risks  are insured, and it is n ot possible to obtain insurance for all Force Majeure E vents and other 
contingencies described in the D&C Contract or in th e Project Agre ement.  In addi tion, the amount of liquidate d 
damages the D&C Contractor could be required to pay under the D&C Contract for delay is limited by the terms of 
the D&C Contract and may not be sufficient to cover all of the Borro wer's losses in the eve nt of a delay or a failure 
to complete the required Wo rk in accordance with the Project Agreement.  In addition, the D&C Contractor has not 
waived its rights to contest a demand for payment of liquidated damages, and neither the D&C Contractor Guarantor 
nor the Bond Issuer of the D&C Contractor's performance bond is guaranteeing performance by the D&C Contractor 
under all circumstances and may assert as a defense to payment any defenses the D&C Contractor claims or has.  No 
assurance can be given tha t available co ntingency funds, ins urance and/or other fund s will be suffic ient should 
delays occur or should the Borrower h ave payment obligations that are not satisfied by or  the responsibility of t he 
D&C Contractor under the D&C Contract. Further, liquidated damages related to Project  Final Completion under 
the D&C Contract do not become payable until six (6) mont hs following the G uaranteed Project  Final Completion 
Date and therefore the Borrower will be relaying on its capitalized costs to pay any debt service. 
The Indepe ndent  Engineer has retained the ulti mate responsibility for certifying the achievement of Street 
Light Bundle Substant ial Comp letion with r espect to the Street Light Bundle and Smart C ity B undle Substanti al 
Completion with respect to the Smart City Bundle. The Project Agreement sets out the Borrower's obligations with 
respect to achieving each Street Light Bu ndle Substantial Completion  and Smart City B undle Substantial 
Completion, and t hese oblig ations have been la rgely pas sed down t o the  D&C Contra ctor pursuant to the D&C  
Contract. The D& C Contract sets out the  D&C Contractor's obligations with respect to ach ieving certification from 
the Independent Eng ineer t hat the r elevant Street Lig ht Bundle Substant ial Com pletion or Smart City B undle 
Substantial Co mpletion has occurred. If the Indep endent Engineer fails to certify that the applic able Street Lig ht 
Bundle Substantial Completion or Smart City Bundle Substantial Completion has occurred as of the da te the D &C 
Contractor and Borrower have stated that such Street Light Bundl e Substantial Completion or Smart City B undle 
Substantial Completion (as applicable) has o ccurred, t hen the amount and  timing of Availa bility Payments to the 
Borrower could be reduced and/or delayed. Although the Borrower has in most circumstances passed down the risk 
of a failure by the I ndependent Engineer to certify t he achievement of each Street Light Bundle Substantial 
Completion and Smart City Bundle Substantial Completion to the D&C C ontractor, and in such circumstances the 
D&C Con tractor wou ld be obligated to pay liqu idated damages purs uant to  the D&C  Cont ract, th ere can be no 
assurance that in all cases Borrower will be able to c ollect all compensation payable by the D&C Contractor or that 
all costs associated with delays caused b y the Indep endent Engineer's failure to certify a chievement of any Street 
Light Bundle Substantial Completion or Smart City Bundle Substantial Completion can b e passed th rough t o the 
D&C C ontractor or recover ed from t he District through the d ispute resolution process set out in the Project 
Agreement.    
Qualifying Changes i n Law 
Although the change in laws provisions in the Project Agreement are intended to protect the Borrower from 
certain Qualifying Changes in Law, chan ges i n the laws related to the Proje ct may n onetheless impact th e 
Borrower's ability to satisfy its payment obligations u nder t he Loan Agreem ent, thereby negatively affecting the 
repayment of the Series 2022 Bonds. 
The Project and the related financing are subject to various laws, policies and regulations, including, among 
others, laws governing envi ronmental pr otections and  tax po licies.  The Proj ect and t he Borrower's business, 
financial c ondition a nd results of op erations may be adversely affected by ch anges i n such laws, policie s or 
regulations.  To the extent that th e Borrower and/or any other p arties that a re involved i n the  Project requ ire 
expenditures of a dditional funds n ot budgeted for in order to b e in compliance wi th any new or amended policies , 
regulations or laws, and assuming that no compensation or other r elief is provided p ursuant to the terms and

with any new or ame nded policies, regulation s or laws, and as a  result, completion of construction of the Project is

Environmental and Permitting Risks 
The Borrower ma y b e adver sely affected by  environmental contamination tha t c ould require sub stantial 
expenditures and/or delay Project completion.  Environmental laws, including CERCLA, impose liability on owners 
or operators for the clean-up costs associated with remediating contaminated property.  The Borrower could become 
liable for certain claims for remediation of pre- existing contamination existing on or under the Project area, whether 
such contamination is known or unknown, as well as future contamination associated with the Project. 
Under the Pr oject Agreement , th e District has agreed to pay certain Compensation Amounts rel ated to 
environmental remediation for certain di scoveries or sp ills of Hazardous Materials o n or nea r the Pro ject.  In  
addition, at all times prior  to Projec t Final Completion, the D&C Co ntractor is solely responsible under the D&C 
Contract for the management, treatment, handling, storage, monitoring, remediation, removal, transport and disposal 
of any Hazardous Materials in, under or on any Project Site that are enco untered except to the extent s uch release is 
an Asset Management Contractor Hazardous Materials Releas e. At all times from and after Project Final 
Completion, the Asset Management Con tractor is solely responsible under the Asset Managemen t Contract for the 
management, treatment, handling, storage, monitoring, remediation, removal, transport and disposal of  any 
Hazardous Materials in, under or on any Project Site that are encountered except to the extent such release is a D&C 
Contractor Hazardous Mat erials Release.  The presence of hazar dous material contamination at or near the Project 
could cause construction delays in order to permit investig ation and remediation of such conditions.  The Borrower 
receives relief in connection with the remediation o f pre-existing Hazardous Materials that are unknown and are not 
reasonably expected to exist prior to the Commercial Closing Date and of contaminat ion that is due  to certain spills 
of Hazardo us Mater ial by a third par ty, and  t he Borrower provides certain r elief in  respect t hereof to th e D&C 
Contractor pursuant to the D&C Contract.  See "PROJECT AGREEMENT" herein for additional information. 
Changes in e nvironmental laws ma y r esult in increased c onstruction c osts, delays  in c onstruction, or 
increased compliance costs .  The laws and regulations go verning envir onmental protec tion have changed 
significantly over recent years and are expected to  continue to change.  Regulations governing, among other things, 
air p ollution, no ise a batement and control, wetlands mitigation,  hazardous waste, solid waste,  water quality and 
endangered species may become more st ringent in  the future, poss ibly requiring  additional compliance and  
conceivably having a material  and adverse effect on the  design, construction, or Asset Management Services with 
respect to the Proje ct.  The Project Agreement provides relief from adverse cost or schedule effects of Qualifying 
Changes in Law that are Compensation Events.  See "APPENDIX B—SUMM ARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS 
OF THE PROJECT AGREEMENT—Payments to Borrower—Compensation Events ." 
The inability to obta in r equired permits or material ch anges in permitting r equirements may adversely 
affect the Proj ect.  Governmental Approvals of many k inds ar e required to be obta ined for the c onstruction and 
maintenance of each Street Light Bundle and Smart City Bun dle that is a part of the Pro ject.  The issua nce of such 
Governmental Approvals m ay include public notice and comment, hearings, or ad ministrative or judi cial appeal s.  
Governmental Approvals may be appealed if they have not been issued in co mpliance with law, and ther e ar e 
various re medies avai lable to governmental agencies and to the  public if  the P roject is constructed without 
appropriate Governmental Approvals or is constructed other than in compliance with the Governmental Approvals.

As at the Setting Date, the District had secured all District-Provided Approvals.   
The Borrower is to  obtain all o f the other Gov ernmental Approvals (and re newals o r amendme nts, 
including those to the NEPA Document) required for the Pr oject. Under the D&C Con tract, the D&C Contractor is 
assuming the Borrower' s responsibilities under the Project Agreement with respect to obtain ing, furnishing, paying 
the cost of and maintaining in full force and effect Governmental Approvals required for the timely construction of 
the Proje ct and with respect to complying with an d pa ying the  cost of c ompliance with environmental laws 
applicable to t he construction of the P roject.  Unde r the Asset Management  Contract, t he Asset Management 
Contractor is assuming the Borrower' s responsibilities with respect to obtaining and complyin g with Governmental 
Approvals requir ed for ope ration, maintenance an d r enewal of the Project.  The ob ligation to obtai n require d 
Governmental Approvals ex tends to thir d-party consents or other r equired ap provals aris ing under agreements 
between the District and other persons, including municipalities, railroads, and utilities. 
The design of the Project is  not complete and the terms of existing and expected Governmental Approvals 
could change as t he Project specifications or environmental or species conditio ns change during the design process, 
and delays could ensue particularly if any required Governmental  Approvals would require p ublic hearings or are 
otherwise challenged.  The Borrower and the D&C Co ntractor expect that all Gov ernmental Approvals required for 
the Pr oject will be obtained a s required f or t imely construction o f the Project.  In the case of t hese per mits and 
approvals and many other cases, the issuance of these Governmental Approvals, including any terms and conditions, 
is subject to the discretion of the Governmental Authority, and such Governmenta l Approvals are often subject to  
administrative and judicial appeal.  If not timely obtained, or if issued with restrictive terms and conditions, the need 
for these Governmental Approvals or for satisfying any conditions thereunder could cause delays in the construction 
of the Project.  
The construc tion sched ule for  th e P roject assum es tha t G overnmental Approvals will be  obtained in 
accordance with a schedule that an ticipates a conventional  permitting process witho ut significant a ppeals, delays, 
imposition of unexpected conditio ns, unexpected changes in environmental or species conditions, or modifications 
to Project design.  Any material del ay i n obtaining or renewin g Govern mental Approvals, imposition of an 
unexpected m aterial co ndition on a Gov ernmental A pproval, unexpe cted ch anges in e nvironmental or species 
conditions, or modification o f P roject desi gn req uired as a r esult of the Governme ntal A pprovals p rocess cou ld 
increase the costs of constructing the Project or delay its completion.  The Borrower is eligible for schedule and cost 
relief for certa in ty pes of de lays in o btaining Gover nmental Approva ls or  for certain unexpected condition s th at 
constitute Compensation Events under the Project Agreement, but the delays inherent in governmental processing of 
the Governmental Approvals for the Project may not cover all risks relating to delay in the Governmental Approvals 
process.  Much of the ri sk of these additional costs and delays is allocated to the D&C Contractor under the D&C 
Contract, which would limit the impact o n the Borrower of such increas ed costs and delays.  There are pro cedural 
and other limitations on the liability of the D&C Contractor under the D&C Contract, and, if the D&C Contractor is 
not liable or does not pay for such costs or delays, there may be an adverse effect on the Borrower's ability to satisfy 
its obligations under the Loan Agreement and, in turn, the repayment of the Series 2022 Bonds.   
Certain maintenance and lifecycle obligations may  require Government Approvals or  p ermits to be 
obtained pr ior to the performance of such work. While maintenance and lifecycle work is general ly scheduled in 
advance and sufficient time is provided for procuring any necessary Governmental Approvals or permits prior to the 
performance of such work, instances could arise in which unplanned maintenance or lifecycle work is required to be 
performed. In any case where a Gover nmental Ap proval o r permit that is required to  be procured prior to the 
performance of maintenance or lifecycle work is delayed or otherwise not procured when necessary, there may be an 
adverse effect on the Bo rrower's ability to fulfill its obligations p ursuant to the Project Agreement, which in tur n 
could adversely affect the Borrower's abi lity to s atisfy its obligations under the Loan Agreement, and in turn the 
repayment o f the Seri es 20 22 Bon ds. See " PERMITS, ENVIRONMENTAL COMP LIANCE AND

The asset management of the Pr oject in volves various ope rational risks.  The quality of the asset 
management of the  Project, as well as events outsid e of the Borrower's c ontrol, could reduce  the Availability 
Payments received or coul d increase the expense o f operating and maintaining the Project and make it difficult or  
impossible f or th e Borrower to make p ayments pursuant to the Loan Agreement in the amounts and at  the times 
required to pay debt service on the Loan (so as to enable Bond Issuer to pay debt service on the Series 20 22 Bonds 
or other payments required under the Bond Indenture with respect to the Series 2022 Bonds).  In addi tion, any such 
events could cause the Borrower to be in default of its obligations under the Project Agreement and, to the extent not 
otherwise cured or re solved, could r esult in the District having the right  to terminate the Project Agr eement for a 
Borrower D efault.  In the event of a t ermination of the Pr oject A greement, th e Termination Sum payable by the 
District to the Borrower may not be sufficient to pay 100% of the the n outstanding repayment obligations under the 
Series 2022 Bonds.  Despite the fact that the Asset Management Contract is a fixed-price contract and that a number 
of risks have been  c ontractually allocated to  the Asset Management Contrac tor under the Ass et Management 
Contract or to the District under the Project Agreement, not all of these risks have been shifted or can be insured and 
there can be no assuranc e that the asset management obligations will be delivered in accordance with the s pecified 
performance obligations or in line with the budget and other assumptions described in this Official Statement. 
As d es c ribed under " PROJECT AGREEMENT" and in " APPENDIX B —SUMMARY OF  CERTAIN 
PROVISIONS OF THE PR OJECT A GREEMENT," the accum ulation of Noncompliance Ev ents and 
Noncompliance Po ints for  failure to  achieve specified levels of asset management , and the occurrence of 
Unavailability Events for failure to adhere to  permitted closure requirements may result in a reduction in the amount 
of Avai lability P ayments and/or Mi lestone Pa yments paid by the D istrict to the  Borrower under t he Project 
Agreement.  Although certain reductions in Availability Payments and /or Milestone Payments are passed t hrough to 
the Asset Management Contractor and D&C Contra ctor, as applicable, any such reduction in Availability Payments 
and/or Milestone Payments could reduce the amount available to pay Project  Costs and other expenses and to pay  
debt service on the Seri es 2022 Bonds.  Th ere is no  guarantee that the Borrower w ould be able to deliver the asset 
management services subcontracted to t he Asset Manageme nt Contractor fo r the c osts set out in  the Asset 
Management Contract in the e vent that the Asset Management  Contract is te rminated for any reaso n.  The 
performance security p rovided by t he Asset Management Contractor may no t be sufficient t o compensate t he 
Borrower for any or all increased costs or reduced revenues that could be incurred due to the performance failures of 
the Asset Management Contractor.  Moreover, while the Borrower may be entitled to receive compensation from the 
Asset Management Contractor for  certain defaults, breaches, or performance failures, the Asset Management 
Contractor could fail to comp ensate the Borrower for such default, br each, or performance failure.  In  either case, 
this could result in a delay in the Borrower's ability to collect the same or otherwise prevent the recovery of any such 
amounts altogether.  Any such failure or delay may adversely  impact the Borrower's cash flow, its ability to comply 
with the Project Agreement and its ability to  pay debt s ervice on the Loan (so as to enable the Bond Issuer to pay 
debt service on the Series 2022 Bonds). 
As with any  in frastructure project of the s ize and co mplexity of  the Pr oject, asset manag ement of the 
Project could be affected by m any factors, including breakdown or failur e of equipment or processes, performance 
below expected lev els o f availab ility, failure to operate to design specif ications and  in ac cordance with the n-
applicable permit requirements, labor disputes, changes in law and events of force majeure.  Not all of th ese events 
are within the control of the Borrower and not all can be i nsured against.  The District has agreed to bear the risk of 
some, but not all, of th ese events under the C ompensation Event and Relief Event regimes set  forth in the Project 
Agreement w hereby th e District is required to pay Compensation A mounts for certai n of such Compensa tion 
Events, and the Borrower is excused from certain perf ormance obligations for  certain s uch Compensation Events 
and Relief Events.  T he occurrence of any of these types of events could impair Availability Payments to the  extent 
no rel ief is provided under the Project Agreement  by the incur rence of U navailability Event s or  Noncompliance 
Points and, t hus, reduce o r even elimin ate Revenues available for repayment of the Series 2 022 Bonds. The 
occurrence of any of these types  of events, in a ddition to the  failure by the Borrower to perform maintenance in a 
timely manner, could significantly increase Project Costs. The incurrence by the Borr ower of such Noncom pliance 
Points and Unavailability Deductions may also potentially give rise to a right of the District to terminate the Project 
Agreement in certain circumstances.

Risks Relating to The Project Agreement 
Failure to Comply with Project Agreement 
The Borrower's failure to comply with the terms and conditions of the Project Agreement may result in the 
reduction o f Mi lestone Payment s and/or A vailability Payments, as a pplicable, paya ble t o the Borrower or in the 
early ter mination of the  Project Agreement in a manner that woul d l imit th e Borrower's ability to s atisfy its 
obligations under the Loan Agreement and, in turn, negatively impact the repayment of the Series 2022 Bonds. 
The Borrower's principal asset is t he legal right that it has  pursuant to the Project Agreement  to receive 
Milestone Paymen ts, Avai lability P ayments, Compensation  A mounts, and Te rmination Sums related to the 
construction and asset management of the Project for approximately a 15-year period (comprised of an app roximate 
two-year construction period and an approximate 13-y ear asset management period).  In the event o f certain failures 
by the Borrower in the performance of its obligations in a ccordance with the terms of the Project Agreement, the 
Borrower will be assessed Nonco mpliance Points or deductions for  Unavailability E vents in accor dance with the 
Project Agreem ent, and in the case of certain  material or cont inuing d efaults, t he District wi ll ha ve the rig ht to  
terminate the Project Agreement , take possession and a ssume operational control of the Project and take such other 
action as it may deem appropriate in accordance with the Project Agreement. 
In the event o f termination of the Project Agreement, the T ermination Sum payable by the District to the 
Borrower is subject to  appropriation and may not be su fficient to repay 100% of the t hen outstanding Series 20 22 
Bonds.  For  mo re i nformation on t he Termination Sum, see " PROJECT AGREEMENT" and "APPENDIX B —
SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE PROJECT AGREEMENT."    
69 
In addition, since the Borrower's principal asset is its rights under the Project Agreement, there are practical 
limitations on  the exer cise of remedies in respect the reof.  U nder the Project A greement, any t ransfer of the  
Borrower's rights, including pursuant to a foreclosure, is subject to the prior approval of the District.  Moreover , any 
transferee must meet certain requirements establis hed b y the Project Agr eement.  Thus, as a pract ical matter, the 
Borrower's creditors (including the Bondholders of the Serie s 2022 Bonds) will have limitations on thei r ability to 
replace the Borrower as the d eveloper u nder t he Project A greement.  See "PROJECT AGREEMENT" and 
"APPENDIX B—SUMMA RY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE PROJECT AGREEMENT." 
Risk of Set-Off 
The Project Agreement provides that, if the Borrower owes any amounts to the District, the District may set 
off such amounts against amounts payable by the District to the Borrower.  As a resul t, it is possible that amounts 
owed by the  Borrower t o the Di strict, inc luding, without limi tation, a mounts owed in respect of t he Borrower's 
obligation to in demnify the District against certain costs, claims and liabilities, could be set off against the amount 
of Milestone Payments and/or Availability Payments that the District is required to pay to the Borrower.  Depending 
upon the amount  of any such set -off, it is possible that the net  amount payable as one or more Mile stone Payment 
and/or Availability Payment (as applicable) would be insufficient to pay debt service on the Series 2022 Bonds or to 
complete the construction of the Project. 
Risk Related to Chang es in Technical Requirements 
In addition to meeting current requirements of the District, the Borrower must be able to respond if changes 
in federal, state or other requirements mandate changes in the Project's facilities or technical requir ements.  It is not 
possible to predict the kind or cost of changes that could be mandated over the term of the Series 2022 Bonds, and as 
with the risks o f changes in Governmental Ap proval requirements,  no assurance can be given that the Borrower 
and/or the District will always be able to respond.

has agreed to co mply wi th such deadlines as they  relate to th e wo rk requir ed to be unde rtaken by the  D&C 
Contractor under the D&C Contract, except to the extent delays are caused by Borrower.

circumstances, the inability of, the Borrower to receive the Milestone Payments or Availability Payments, thereby 
adversely impacting the repayment of the Series 20 22 B onds.  Const ruction de lays could impact t he amount of 
Milestone Payments  r eceived du ring construction, which co uld l ead to a shor tfall in funds avai lable to p ay for 
Project capital costs and complete cons truction of the Project.  Furthermore, if  the D&C Contractor does not meet 
the applicable co nstruction deadl ines set forth in the D&C Contract, the Borrower may not achieve Project Final 
Completion for th e Proj ect.  If th e long stop construction deadline is not met (this being the Long Stop Deadli ne 
under the Project Agreement), the District may terminate the Project Agreement.  The applicable Termination Sum –
– payable under such circumstance may not be suf ficient to pay in full all obligations under the Ser ies 2022 Bonds.  
See "PROJECT A GREEMENT" and "APPENDIX B —SUMMARY OF C ERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE 
PROJECT AGREEMENT."  In addition, although the Borrower may be entitled to r eceive compensation from the 
D&C Contr actor through paym ents o f l iquidated damages to  use for Proj ect capital costs and/or debt service
