Events of Force Majeure; Limited Insurance Coverage; Relief Deductibles 
Construction and asset management of the Proj ect are at risk from certain ev ents, such as fir e, explosion, 
unusually adverse weather, flood, earthquake,  r iot, and na med windstorms, and Forc e Majeure Events, such as  
terrorism, wars, epidemic, and nuclear, chemical or biological contamination, among other events.  Construction and 
asset management also may be stopped or delayed by n on-casualty events such as disco very o f a rchaeological 
artifacts, changes i n law, delays i n obtaining and re newing G overnmental Approvals, re vocation or rev ision o f 
Governmental Approval requirements and litigation, among other things. 
Although the D&C Contractor, the Asset Management Contractor, and the Borrower are required to o btain 
and maintain certain insurance, the required policies do not cover damage and delay from all events that potentially 
could interrupt con struction and asset management of the Project.  Insuran ce policies may not be maintained or be 
obtainable in amounts that would be sufficient or be paid on time in all events to pay all of the costs required to be 
paid under t he Project Agreement and under the  Bond Indenture and the Collateral Agency Agreement , including 
debt serv ice on the Seri es 2 022 B onds.  The Borrower is ent itled to s chedule and performance relief under t he 
Project Agreement for certain Compensation Events, Re lief Events  and F orce Majeure Events ; however, such 
protection does not cover all events that potentially could interrupt construction and asset management of the Project 
and in certain cases result in a termination of the Project Agreement.  
The Force Maj eure Events include events such as w ar or arme d conflict, nuclear, chemical  or biol ogical 
contamination, ionizing radiation, terrorism and epidemics.  The majority of these events are not insurable (with the 
exception of terrorism).  Addi tional risks  that may not be insurable include u nforeseeable geological conditions, 
discovery of archeological artifacts, criminal or intentional acts by the  insured, and insurer insolvency.  In addition, 
changes in federal or local design, building and environmental requirements and other changes in la w are not ri sks 
that are generally ins urable.  Certai n policies, includin g the D&C Contractor's policies an d Asset Managem ent 
Contractor's policies are blanket policies that cover other facilities as w ell as the Project, and n o assurance can be 
given that suffic ient coverage will be available,  especially if more than one facil ity is damaged at the same time.  
See "ADVISOR REPORTS—Insura nce Advisor Report."  
Inflation Risk 
T
he Availability Payments are the primary source of revenue exp ected to be available to pay debt service 
on the Series 2022 Bonds during the Asset Management Per iod.  Fol lowing Project Final Completion, 43% of the 
MAP is adjusted each year  by the Consu mer Price Ind ex (as co mpared to th e Consumer Price I ndex at  the 
Indexation Base Date), while 57% of the MAP is indexed to a  fixed rate throughout the Term.  While the indexation 
of the M AP is intende d to  closely reflect the cost st ructure of t he Borrower, including cer tain asset management 
costs, there can be no guarantee that this will appropriately match the fixed and variable costs of the Borrower.   
Risks Relating to the Series 2022 Bonds 
Bankruptcy and Insolvency Risks 
General.  The enforceability of the rights and remedies of the Bondholders of the Series 2022 Bonds under 
the Bond Indenture, the enforceability of obligations of th e Borrower, the D&C Contractor, the Asset Management 
Contractor, the District, the D&C Contractor Guarantor, and the Bond Issuers or obligors under the letters of credit 
and perf ormance bonds a nd the  enforceability of th e liens,  security interests and ple dges created by the Bond 
Indenture, Collateral Agency Agreement, Sec urity Documents and other documents may be s ubject to Bankruptcy 
Law, and to other bankr uptcy, inso lvency, reorgan ization, moratorium or similar la ws affecting creditors ' rights 
generally and equitable princ iples tha t m ay limit e nforcement o f cer tain re medies.  Risks associated with a 
bankruptcy of the Borrower include the risks of delay in payment or of nonpayment under  the Loan Agreement and 
the risk that the Collateral Agent or the District may be unable f or an extended time, or at all, to substitute a n ew 
developer.  Certain of these ri sks are risks that are incurred whenever one enters into a contract with an ent ity that 
could become a debtor unde r the Bankruptcy Law, while other s are risks that result fr om the treatment under the 
Bankruptcy Law of unexpired le ases or secured financings.  Potential purchasers of the Series 2022 Bonds should 
consult t heir own att orneys a nd advisors in assessing th e risks and the likelihood of reco very in the event the   
71 
Borrower or any other party to a docu ment described herein be comes a debtor under the  Bankruptcy Law prior to 
the time the Series 2022 Bonds are paid in full.  
Borrower Bankrup tcy Risk.  Most of the assets that comprise the Trust E state and the other collateral held 
by the Collateral Agent on behalf of the Se cured Parties are derived fro m the Project Agreement.  If the Borrower 
becomes a debtor under the B ankruptcy Law, operation of the automatic stay provisions of the Bankruptcy Law 
under certain circumstances may require the Bond Issuer, the Bond Trustee and the Collateral Agent, as applicable, 
to obtain bankruptcy court approval prior to taking any action to e nforce the Project Agreement (or to enforce the 
Loan Agreement, the Collateral Agency Agreement, the Security Documents or any other agreement that contains a 
guarantee or other wise creates a deb t obligation o f t he Borrower or a  S ecurity Interest in favor of the Collateral 
Agent for the be nefit of the Bond Trustee  o n behal f of the Bondholders of the Series  2022 Bon ds), i ncluding 
declaring the Project Agr eement (or such other document s) to b e in de fault, recovering a mounts due but  u npaid, 
terminating the Project Agreement, accelerating the due dates  of any payments due fro m the Borrower, evicting the 
Borrower and t aking possession of the P roject or realizi ng aga inst a ny collateral pr ovided by the Borrower a s 
security f or its payment obligations und er th e Project Agreemen t, the Loan Agreement , t he Co llateral Agency 
Agreement or enforcing any other remedies provided for in the Project Agreement, the Finance Documents or in any 
other documents. 
Other Parties.  T he D&C Contractor and its members, guarantor and sureties, and the Asset Management 
Contractor and sureties are involved in, or are affiliates of companies that are involved in, many businesses and are 
entities that can become debtors under the Ba nkruptcy Law.  If  one of the contractors (or its guaran tor) becomes a 
debtor under the Bankrup tcy Law, the Borrower's or the Collateral Agent's ability to subs titute a new con tractor, to 
obtain funds under the guarantees  or to exercise other remedies may be delayed or un available.  Substantial delays 
or losses could result. 
Limitations on Enforcea bility  
Upon a defau lt u nder t he Project A greement, the Loan A greement, t he Bond I ndenture or any of the  
Security Documents, the remedies available to the Bond Iss uer, the District, the Borrower, the Bond Trustee and the 
Collateral Agent may depen d upon judicial a ctions that may be subject t o sub stantial d iscretion and dela y.  As a 
result, remedies otherwise provided for in these agreements may not be enforceable.  Th e rights of the Bondholders 
of th e Series 2022 Bonds and the enf orceability of  the Borrower's, the Bo nd I ssuer's and the other p arties' 
obligations may be subject to the exercise of judicial discretion under a variety of circumstances.  The enforceability 
of governmental obligations is al so su bject to constitutional,  st atutory and publi c policy limitation s an d to other 
considerations that do not limit e nforcement of obligations of p rivate parties.  The District makes a number of 
agreements, such as its ag reement to make payments during construction and operat ions, its agreements regarding 
additional debt and its agreements to make certain payments in the event the Project Agreement with the Borro wer 
is terminated.  W hile these agreements and others are mad e largely for the benefit of the Bo ndholders of the Series 
2022 Bonds, no assurances can be given t hat a court exercising its j udicial discretion will enforce such agreements 
in all circumstances.  The opinion o f Bond Counsel as to the enforceability of th e Bond I ndenture and the S eries 
2022 Bonds a nd the opinions of other parties' counsel will be qualified  as to a pplicable ban kruptcy, i nsolvency, 
reorganization, moratorium and similar laws affecting creditors' rights and remedies generally, and the application of 
such laws may limit or prevent remedies otherwise available to the Bond Truste e and Collateral Agent, respectively, 
in the Bond Indenture, the Collateral Agen cy Agreement and in the oth er do cuments and/or, in  each case,  to the 
Bondholders of the Series 2022 Bonds, the Bond Issuer, or the District from being exercised by these constituencies. 
The D&C Contra ctor and the D&C Contractor Guarantor, and the Asset Management Contractor are each 
entering into a  "D irect Agreement " with the Borrower and the Collateral Agent f or the be nefit of  the Secured 
Parties.  Each such Direct Agreement is intended to provide the Collateral Agent with notice and time to take action 
following the occu rrence o f an event that would entitle a party  to terminate or to suspend it s agre ement with the 
Borrower, and to obtain a new agreement if the contract to which such Direct Agreement relates is terminated.  Each 
of these Direct Agreement s, however, imposes certai n  terms and conditions  with respect t o the ability of the 
Collateral Agent or any d esignee o r assig nee t o succeed  to the interests of th e B orrower under the agreemen t to  
which such Direct Agreement relates, or to request that the applicable counterparty deliver a replacement agreement.  
Consequently, no assurance can be given that the provisions of these Direct Agreements will always be enforced or 
that the terms and conditions specified therein will be satisfied.     
72 
Upon the Financial Closing Date, the Collateral Agent will beco me a party to the Project Agreement solely 
for the p urposes o f adminis tering ce rtain terms rela ting to the righ ts of Lende rs a s set forth therein , as further 
described in "APPENDIX B —SUMM ARY OF CE RTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE  PROJECT AGREEME NT—
Provision Addressing Rights  of Fi nance Parties ." N o assurance c an be g iven that the prov isions of the Project 
Agreement with respect to  this right will always be enf orced or that the terms and conditions specified therein will 
be satisfied. 
The enforceability of the D&C Contractor's performance bond and payment bond may be l imited not on ly 
for the reasons describe d ab ove, but also by vari ous pr ovisions of su retyship a nd i nsurance law.  The surety 
providing the performance bond  or letter of credit is not waiving  its right to assert the D&C Contractor's or Asset 
Management Contractor's, as applicable, defenses to payment, nor is the surety waiving its suretyship defenses.  The 
obligations of the surety under the D&C Contractor's performance bond to complete construction or to p ay damages 
and the obligations of the sure ty under the Asset Management Constructor's letter of credit, thus are, in each case 
limited, and no assur ances can be given that the s urety will honor a claim under  the performance bond or letter of 
credit, as applicable. 
Insufficient Collateral 
It may be d ifficult to realize t he value of the Collateral pled ged as part of the Tr ust Estate and under the 
other Security Documents, and the pro ceeds received from a sale o f such Collateral may be insufficient to repay the 
Series 2022 B onds.  Foreclos ure on such Co llateral on the B ondholders' behalf may be subject t o p erfection and 
priority issues and to practi cal problems associated with the realizatio n of the Bondholders' security interest in such 
Collateral.  The enfo rcement of the se curity interest w ith respect to any s uch Collateral may not provide sufficient 
funds to repay all amou nts due on the Seri es 2022 Bonds.  Any Co llateral of the Borrower will be share d with the 
holders of other senior debt that the Borrower incurs concurrently with the issuance of the Series 2022 Bonds or in 
the future, which increases the risk that the proceeds of foreclosure on such Collateral will not be sufficient to repay 
the Series 2022 Bonds.  In addition, since the Borrowe r's principal asset is its ri ghts under the Pr oject Agreement, 
there are pr actical limitations on the exe rcise of  remedies in respect thereo f.  Under the  Project Agreement, any 
transfer of the Borrower's rights, including pursuant to a foreclosure, is subj ect to the prior approval of the District.  
Moreover, any transferee must meet certain requirements established by the Project Agreement.  Thus, as a pr actical 
matter, the Borrower's creditors (including the Bond Issue r un der the Loan A greement and indirec tly the 
Bondholders of the Se ries 2022 Bonds) will have limi tations on the ir ability  to repla ce the Borrower as t he 
developer under the Project Agreement. 
Uncertaintie s of Forecasts and Assumptions 
The information in this Official State ment includes  certain assumptions,  forecasts and projections.  
Demonstration of compliance with certain of the co venants contained in the Bond Indenture, the Collateral Agency 
Agreement, the other Finance Documents and in the Project Agreement may also be based upon assumptions and 
projections.  Such assumptions, forecasts and projections and any forecasts and projections that may be contained in 
any future certificate required und er the Project Agreement or the  Loan Agreement or the Bond Ind enture or th e 
Collateral Agency Agreement or any o ther Finance Document are not n ecessarily indicative of futu re performance, 
and actual r esults are likely to differ,  p erhaps materially, from those projected.  None o f the Borrower, the Bond 
Issuer, the Spo nsors or the ir affiliate s o r any oth er party assu mes any respons ibility for th e accuracy of s uch 
projections.  In addition, certain assumptions with respect to future business and financing decisions are subject to 
change.  No representation is made or intended, nor should any representation be inferred, with respect to the likely 
existence of any particular future set of facts or circumstances, and prospective purchasers of the Series 2022 Bonds 
are cauti oned not to place  u ndue relian ce upon the pr ojections co ntained in this Offi cial Stateme nt or upon 
requirements for future project ions.  I f actual results are les s favorable than the results p rojected or if t he 
assumptions used in p reparing the pro jections prove to be inco rrect, the District's ability to m ake the payments  
required by the Project Agreement, the Borrower's ability to make timely payment of a mounts due under the Loan 
Agreement, and the Bond Issuer's ability to make tim ely payment of the principal of and interest on the Series 2022 
Bonds may be materially and adversely affected.   
73 
Ratings of Series 2022 Bonds  
One credit rating agency, Moody's, has assigned a credit rating to the Series 2022 Bonds.  The rating of the 
Series 2022 Bonds is not a recommendation to purchase, hold or sell the Series 2022 Bonds, and the rating does not 
comment on the m arket price or  suitability of th e Series 2022 Bonds for a par ticular investor.  The rating of the 
Series 2022 Bonds may not remain for any  given period of t ime and may be lowered or withdrawn depending on, 
among other things, the rating agency's assessment of the Borrower's and/or the District's financial strength. 
Market Liquidity 
The Series 2022 Bonds constitute a new issue wit h no establ ished tr ading marke t.  Although the 
Underwriters have infor med t he Bond Issuer  and the Borrower that th e Under writers c urrently intend  to make a 
market for the Ser ies 2022 Bonds, the Underwriters are not obligated to d o so, and they may  discontinue any such 
market-making at any time without p rior notice.  No a ssurance can be given as to  the development or l iquidity of 
any market for the Series 2022 Bonds.  If an active public market does not develop, the market price and liquidity of 
the Series 2022 Bonds may be adversely affected. 
No Interest In Real Property 
Under the Project Agreement, the Borrower has not been granted any right, title or interest in or to any real 
property (o ther than contractual rights of access to the subject lands) .  Because of this , t he Co llateral does  not 
include any real property pledged as security to secure repayment of the Series 2022 Bonds.  Note that pursuant t o 
the Project Agreement, Borrower has a contractual right to , subject to the term s and conditions of the Project 
Documents, enter onto (and engage in the ac tivities contemplated in the Proj ect Agreement) each Pro ject Site, for 
the purposes of performing its obligations under the Project Documents only. 
Risks Relating  to COVID-19 (Coronavirus) Pandemic 
The District has taken various emergency measures and other actions to r espond to the spread of COVID-
19 i n the District.  While the Di strict co ntinues to closely monitor and assesses t he effects of th e COVID-19 
pandemic and its impact on  the District's financial position and operat ions, the Di strict also expects COVID-19 to 
have an impact on its de mographic and soci oeconomic condit ions.  While econo mic recovery in the District has 
continued, there remain significant uncertainties and risks to the District's key economic sectors and industries.  It is 
likely that the impact of C OVID-19 on the District, its economy and financial positio n, a nd its de mographic a nd 
socioeconomic conditions w ill continue to c hange as circumsta nces and events evo lve and may not be wholly 
apparent for some time.

THE PROJECT AGREEMENT 
On March 7, 2022, the Borrower entered into the Project Agreement with the District of Columbia (acting 
by and through the District of Columbia Office of Public -Private Partnerships, for and on be half of the District of 
Columbia Department of Transportation  and t he Of fice of the  Ch ief T echnology Officer) (the "District") f or the 
development, design, construction, financing, and asset managemen t of the Project.  Certain terms of the P roject 
Agreement will be amended on or prior to the Financia l Closing Date an d this Official Statement sets out the terms 
of the Project Agreement as will be in effect on the Financial Closing Date. 
 
Principal Rights and Responsibilities of the Borrower and the District 
 
 Pursuant to the Project Agreement, the District has granted to the Borrower the exclusive right to, subject to 
the terms of the Project Agree ment, (a) develop, design, construct and finance the Project, and (b) undertake Asset 
Management Services with respect to the Project . For t he purpose of performing it s obl igations under the Project 
Agreement only, the Borrower will, subject to the te rms of the Project Agreement, have the right to enter onto (and 
engage in the activities contemplated in the Project Agreement) each Project Site. 
 
 Design and Construc tion of the Project.  The Borrower's obligations include, among other things, (a ) (i) 
furnishing the design and other s ervices n ecessary for the construction, conversion, testing, commissioni ng, and 
provisioning of the Improved Street L ight Network , (ii) installing, t esting, and commissi oning t he Sm art C ity 
Improvements and maintaining t he Exi sting Street Light Ne twork during construction, and (iii) achieving Project 
Final Completion by the Gu aranteed Project Final Completion Da te; and  (b) ensuring all Conversi on Work is 
performed in accord ance with t he Released for Construction Documents. The  Borrower is required to perform the 
D&C Work in accordance with ( a) Good Industry Practice, ( b) all applicable Law, (c) all Governmental Approv als, 
and ( d) the Te chnical P rovisions a nd all other r equirements of the P roject Agreem ent. S ee "APPENDIX B —
SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE  PROJECT AGREEMENT—Design and Construction of the 
Project." 
 
 Asse
t Management Services.  The Borrower shall carry out the Asset Management Services with respect to 
each Street Light Bundle from the Street Light Bundl e Substantial Completion D ate for the relevant Street Light 
Bundle until the Termination Date. The Borrower shall carry out the Asset Management Services in accordance with 
Good Industry Practice, all applicable Law, all Governmental Approvals, and the Technical Provisions and all other 
requirements of the Project Agreement. See "APPENDIX B—SUMMARY OF CERTAIN  PROVISIONS OF THE 
PROJECT AGREEMENT—Mai ntenance of the Project." 
 
 Hazardous Mater ials.  Subject to the terms of  the Pr oject Agreement, the Borrower must manage, t reat, 
handle, store, monitor, remediate, remove, transport, and dispose of any Hazardous Materials that are encountered 
in, under, or on any Project Si te during the Term. Othe r t han in connection with certain Co mpensation Events 
described below, the Borrow er will bear all cost s and  expenses of complying with its obligations in respect of 
Hazardous Materials set forth in the Project Agreement. Subject to the terms of the Project Agreement, the Borrower 
will be responsible for obtaining and maintaining all Governmental Approvals relating to any R emedial Action and 
complying with applicable Law concerning or relating to Hazardous Materials in respect thereof. In carrying out any 
Remedial Action required in connection with Hazardous Materials under the terms of t he Project Agreement that is 
the subject of a Compensation Event, the Bor rower will take such steps and actions, as the District may reasonably 
require, in order to protect a nd preserve the District' s pot ential claims of contribution and indemnity, statuto ry or 
otherwise, against potentially responsible parties, provi ded that a ny such steps and actions are not inconsistent with 
applicable Law or the requirements of the Project  Agreement or any relevant Gov ernmental Entiti es o r 
Governmental Approvals. 
 
Project Sites 
 
 Pursuant to the Project Agreement, the District has agreed to obtain and provide access to the Project Sites 
at the District's cost beginning on the Commercial Closing Date.

Payments to the Borrower 
 
 Consideration for the  Work.  As consider ation fo r the Borrower's p erformance of t he Work and all 
obligations related thereto, the District will pay the Borrower (a ) the Milestone Payments and (b) the Availability 
Payments, as described below. 
 M ile stone Payments.  Pursuant to the terms of the Pr oject Agreement, the Borrower is eligible to receive, 
subject to the invoic ing process set f orth there in, t wo Milestone Payments for the Conversion Work and two 
Milestone Payments for the Smart City Work. The two Milestone Payments for the Conversion Work are payable in 
the amounts and at th e times as fo llows: ( a) $21,970,000 the final day of the  Calendar Q uarter in whi ch the 
Borrower has achieved Street Light Bundle Substantial C ompletion f or more than 50% of the  total number o f 
Lighting Units expected to be in the Improved Street Light Network upon Project Final Completion (the "Milestone 
1 Payment ") and ( b) $22,220,000 th e final da y of the Cale ndar Quarte r in which Proj ect Fi nal Completion is 
achieved (the "Milestone 2 Payment "). The two Mile stone Payments for t he Smart City Work are payable in the 
amounts and at the times as follows: (a) $2,075,000 the final day of the Calendar Quarter in which the percentage of 
Smart City Bu ndles t hat ha ve reached Smart City Bundle Substantial Completion fir st exceeds 5 0% and ( b) 
$2,075,000 the final day of the Calendar Quarter in which all Smart C ity Bundles have reached  Smart City Bundle 
Substantial Completion. The Milestone Payments are subject to adjustment under the Project Agreement. 
 
 Availability Payment s.  In ac cordance with the terms of the Project Agreement, and commencing with the 
achievement of Project Final Completion and continuing for the term of the Project Agreement, the District will pay 
to the Borrower the Availability Payments, the proceed s of which a re expected to be used to, among other things, 
pay project capit al costs and sat isfy principal and inte rest payments owed in respect to the Series 20 22 Bonds. The 
calculation of the gross Availability Payment in e ach Calendar Quarter is based on a number of variables. The n et 
Availability Payment is calculated for each  Calendar Quarter and may be adjusted for certain Unavailability Events 
where roadways and other public spaces adjacent to a Project Site do no t meet availability and use requirements, and 
the a ccumulation of Noncomplia nce P oints, which ar e assessed if the  Borrower causes a Noncompliance Event 
(essentially a failure to meet certain performance standards in  the Project Agreement). The Borrower is entitled to  
submit an invoice to the District eac h Calendar Quarter setting out  the net amount o f the Availability Payment due 
for such Calendar Quarter. The Availability Payment s constitute the primary source of revenues from the Project 
and such payments are expected to exceed amounts required to pay scheduled debt service on the Series 2022 Bonds 
and maintenance and renewal costs of the Project during the Term.

Disputes  
 
All Disputes arising under the P roject Ag reement will be re solved pursuant to t he D ispute Resolution 
Procedures, which involve a non- binding dispute resolution  board and claims review process by the Contr acting 
Officer. Submission of a disput e by way of written claim to th e Contracting O fficer is a condition precedent to  the 
Borrower h aving the right to proce ed to litigation of such u nresolved dispute (subject to the jury trial waiver set 
forth in the Project Agreement). During the course of the dispute resolution process, the Borrower will continue with 
the Work ( including any Work t hat is the subjec t to the  Dispute) using reasonable  efforts and without delay or 
otherwise conform to the District's decision or order, and will be governed by all applicable provisions of the Project 
Agreement, and the District will continue to make pay ments of any amount s not in dispute pursuant to the t erms of 
the Project Agreement. Throughout any disputed Work, t he Borrower will keep complete records of extra costs and 
time incurred. The Borrower will provide the District and any dispute review board members access to these and any 
other records needed for evaluating the dispute. 
 
Refinancing Gain 
 
 The District shall be entitled to receive a fifty percent (50%) shar e of any Refinancing Gain arising from a 
Qualifying Refinancing; provide d that the District  shall not with hold or delay its consent to a Q ualifying 
Refinancing in order to obtain a greater than fifty pe rcent (50%) share of the Refinancing Gain. The Borrower shall 
obtain the District's prior written consent to any Qualifying Refinancing and promptly provide the District with full   
76 
details of any proposed Qualifying Refinancing, including a copy of the prop osed financial model relating to it (if 
any) and the b asis for the assum ptions used in the propo sed financial model. The Di strict shall (before , during and 
any time after any Refinancing) have unrestr icted rights of au dit over any financial model and docum entation 
(including any aspect of the calculation of t he Refinancing Gai n) used in connection wi th the Refinancing whether 
the Refinancing is a  Q ualifying Refinancing or not. The District sha ll have t he right to receiv e its share of any 
Refinancing Gain as either a single payment in an amount less than or eq ual to any Distribu tion made on or about 
the date of Refinancing, a reduction in the Availability Payment over the reminder of the Term, or a combi nation of 
these two choices. 
 
 Following the Borrowe r's delivery of th e d etails regarding the proposed Qualif ying Refinancing, t he 
District and the Borrower will work together in good faith to agree on the amount and  timing of the Refinancing 
Gain and the amount of the District's share. 
 
Compensation Events 
 
 T he Project Agreement provides that the Borrower will receive compensation from the District, sub ject to 
certain determinations of the Contracti ng Officer, in ad dition to obtaining relief f rom complying with the dead lines 
set forth therein in the case of the occurrence of certain enumerated Compensation Events. 
 
 In order to obtain com pensation, which i s contemplated to be gene rally equal to the a mount necessary to 
restore t he Borrower to th e same econo mic position it would have been in if the Comp ensation Event had no t 
occurred (except with respect to certain  Compensation Events for w hich the Project Agreement specifies the uni t 
pricing to which the Borrower is entitled to paid), including compensation for Finance Costs which are incurred by 
the Borrower due to delays in receiving Milestone Payments or extensi ons to the Initial Project Final Completion  
Date ari sing from extensio ns of t ime granted b y the District du e to the  oc currence o f a C ompensation Ev ent or 
Uninsured Relief Event the Borrower is required to (among other things): 
 
(a) submit a
  cla im t o the Di strict promptly, and in  any event wit hin thirty ( 30) days, after  the 
Borrower first becomes aware of the relevant Compensation Event; 
(b) promptly, and in any e vent within sixty (60) days, after th e Borrower first becomes aware of the 
relevant Compensation Event, must give full details of th e relevant Compensation Event and the 
extension of time and/or any compensation claimed, including (among other things): 
(i) a Ti me Impact Analysis demonstrating that the relevant Compensation Event will result in 
an identifiable and measurable disruption to the Work affecting a critical path activity; 
(ii) details of the ste ps the Borrower ha s taken  or will take to mitigate the effect of th e 
Compensation Event; 
(iii) the type and amount of i nsurance that may be applicable and amounts that have been or are 
anticipated to be collected under such insurance; and 
(iv) evidence reasonably satisfactory to the District demonstrating that (A) no other concurrent or 
unrelated delay to a critical path activi ty that is the Borrower's re sponsibility has occurred 
that has co ntributed to the delay for whic h relief is being sought and ( B) any Finance Co sts 
claimed by the Borrower will be incurred as a direct result of the Compensation Event; and 
(b) demonstrate to the reasonable satisfaction of the District that: 
(i) the Compensation Event was the direct cause or is reasonably likely to be the direct cause of: (A) a 
delay in achieving Project Final Completion by the G uaranteed Project Final Completion Date or 
(following the Guaranteed Project Final Completion Date) further delay in achieving Project Final 
Completion; (B ) the Borrower' s inabi lity to comply with its obligations und er the Proj ect 
Agreement; and/or (C) the Borrower incurring a Change in Costs and/or Finance Costs; and   
77 
(ii) the Change in C osts, Finance Costs, extension  of time, and/or relief from obligations un der the  
Project Agreement claimed, could not reasonably be mitigated or re covered without material cost 
by the Borrower acting in accordance with G ood Industry  Practice, incl uding by re-sequencing, 
reallocating, or redeploying its forces to other portions of the Conversion Work and/or Smart City 
Work. 
 I n the event that  information is provided after the dates referred to in this section, the Borrower will not be 
entitled to a ny compensation, extension of time, or relief from its ob ligations under the Proje ct Agreement to the 
extent that the quantum thereof was adversel y affected as a result of such not ice or information within the relevant 
time periods. In the event the Borrower fails to provide such information for a period of twelve (12) months or more, 
the rights of the Borrower with respect to the r elevant Compensation Event will be of no further force or effect. If

Borrower's ob ligations und er the Pr oject Ag reement, t he parties will reso lve the matter in accordance wi th the  
dispute resolution procedures set forth in the Project Ag reement. See "APPENDIX B—SUMMA RY OF CERTAIN

 The Project Agreement provides that the Borrower will obtain r elief, subject to certain determina tions of

(a) s
ubmit a claim to the District promptly, a nd in a ny event w ithin thirty ( 30) days, after the 
Borrower first becomes aware of the relevant Relief Event; 
(b) promptly, and in any event within forty-five (45) days, after the Borrower first becomes aware of 
the relevant Relief Event, give full details of th e relevant Relief Event and the extension o f time 
and/or any compensation claimed, including (among other things): 
(i) a Time Impact Analysis demonstratin g that the r elevant Relief E vent will result in an 
identifiable and measurable disruption to the Work affecting a critical path activity; 
(ii) details of the steps the Borrower has taken or will take to mitigate the ef fect of t he Relief 
Event; and 
(iii) evidence reasonably satisfactory to the District demonstrating that ( A) no other concurrent or 
unrelated delay to a critical path activity that is the Borrower's responsibility has occurred that 
has contri buted to the del ay f or which relief is being s ought and ( B) with respect to any  
Uninsured Re lief Event, any Finance  Costs claimed by the Borrower will be incurred as a 
direct result of the Uninsured Relief Event; and 
(c) demonstrate to the reasonable satisfaction of the District that the Relief Event was the direct cause 
or is reasonably likely to be the direct cause of: ( i) a delay in achieving Project F inal Completion 
by the Guaranteed Project Final  Completion Date o r (following the Gu aranteed Project Final 
Completion Date) further delay in achieving Project Final Completion; (ii) the Borrower's inability 
to comply with its obligat ions under the Project A greement; and /or ( iii) with respect to any 
Uninsured Relief Event, the Borrower incurring Finance Costs. 
 In the event that  the Borrower fails to provide any notice or information required to be provided under the 
Project Agreement in connection with any claim relating to a Compensation Event, the Borrower will not be entitled 
to any compensation, extension of time, or relief from its obligations under the Project Agreement to the extent that

the quantum thereof was adversely affected as a result of  such notice or information not being provided to the

Changes 
 
 District Changes.  The District has the right to propose a District Change. In order to request a District 
Change, the District must deliver to the Borrower a change request setting forth (a) the District's proposed change(s) 
to the Work or to the Technical Provisions in sufficient detail to enable the Borrower to calculate and provide an 
estimate of the costs and expenses in connection therewith and (b) the proposed method of compensation for the 
change. 
 
 The Borrower has the right to refuse a change request that would, if implemented: (a) violate applicable 
Law; (b) contravene a Governmental Approval then in full force and effect (provided that such contravention cannot 
be corrected by the issuance of a new or revised Governme ntal Approval that can be obtained by the Borrower); (c) 
materially and adversely affect the h ealth and safety of any Person; (d) cau se an insured risk to become an 
Uninsurable Risk; (e) materially and adversely change the na ture of the Project or the allocation of risk between the 
Parties; (f) be unrelated to the kind of  work initially procured under the RFP a nd to the kind of work that Borrower-
Related Entities provide in the marketplace; or (g) require the Work to be performed in a way that results in a breach 
of the terms of any License Agreement. 
 
 If the District accepts the Borrower Es timate with respect to a District Change, the District shall make a 
payment to the Borrower within sixty (60) days of receiving each invoice in accordance w ith the agreed-upon 
payment schedule, accompanied by the ev idence reasonably acceptable to the Dist rict (where applicable) that the 
relevant part of the District Change has been carried out. If a District Change results in a decrease in the Borrower's 
costs, any payments due from the District under the Project Agreement will be adjusted downwards to reflect such 
reduction in the Borrower's costs. 
 
 Borrower Changes.   If the Borrower wishes to introduce a Bo rrower Change, it must deliver a change 
request to the District setting out (a) the proposed change to the Work in sufficient detail to enable the District to 
evaluate it in full, (b) the Borrower's reasons for proposing the change to the Work, (c) a request to the District to 
consult with the Borrower with a view to deciding whether to agree to the change to the Work and, if so, what 
consequential changes the District requires due to the Borro wer Change, (d) any implications of the change to the 
Work, (e) details regarding proposed changes to the Milestone Payments or the Availability Payments, if any (and, if 
so, giving a detailed cost estimate of such proposed change), (f) any dates by which a decision by the District is 
critical and (g) all of the information required with respect to the Borrower's Estimate as set out in the Project 
Agreement. The District is required to evaluate the Borrower's change request and is required to issue a response to 
the Borrower within fifteen (15) Business Days of meeting with the Borrower to review the change request. Unless 
the District's acceptance specifically agr ees to an increase in the Milestone Pa yments or the Ava ilability Payments 
there shall be no increase in the Milestone Payments or the Availability Payments as a result of a change to the 
Work proposed by the Borrower. While the District is not obligated to provide the reason for rejecting the 
Borrower's change request, the District may not reject a Borrower request that is required in order to conform to a 
change in law. 
 
 If a Borrower Change results in a decrease in the Borrower's costs, an y payments due from the District 
under the Project Agreement will be adjusted downwards to reflect such reduction in the Borrower's costs.

Borrower Indemnity 
 The B orrower must release, defend, indemnify, and hold harmless the Indemnified Parties on demand from 
and against any and a ll liability to thi rd parties for Lo sses arising from (a) death or per sonal injury, (b) loss of o r 
damage to any Indemnified Party's property, and (c) third party claims brought against any Indemnified Party, which 
may arise out of, or in con sequence of, any or all of the fo llowing: (i) any actual or alleged failure by the Borrower 
to comply with, observe or perform any of the covenants, obligations, agreements, terms or conditions in the Project 
Documents or any actu al or alleged breach by the Borrower of its representatio ns or warranti es set forth  i n t he 
Project Agreement; (ii) any violation of any federal or state securities or similar law by any Borrower-Related Entity 
(other than as a direct result of any disclosure statements made by the Di strict or other Indemnified Party); and (iii) 
if applicable, the authorization, issuance, sale, trading, redemption or servicing of any bonds issued to finance  the 
Project (regardless of th e identity of  the Bond I ssuer), or the  B orrower's failure to comply  with any re quirement 
necessary to preserve the tax-exempt status of bonds (other than as a  direct result of any disclosure statements made 
by the District or other Ind emnified Party). How ever, the  Borrower will n ot be responsible or be obliged to 
indemnify any Indemnified Party with  respect to any liability  or losses to the e xtent that the same a rise as a direct 
result of: (a) a Compensation Event, Relief Event , or District Default; ( b) the presence of Hazardous Materials on 
any Project Site for which the District is deemed to be the sole generator and arranger, but only to the extent that the 
relevant Loss does not a rise as a direct result of the negligence of the Borrower or the Borrower failing to comply 
with the terms of the Project Agreement; (c) any non-performance by an Ind emnified Party of its oblig ations under 
the Proj ect Agreement or an y violation of applicable L aw by any Indemnified Party; or ( d) the f raud, negligence, 
recklessness, bad faith or willful misconduct of an Indemn ified Party.  
 
Un
availability Events and Noncompliance Events 
 
The Project Agreement contains a regime pursuant to which Deductions are assessed against the Borrower, 
based on a lis t of breaches or failu res in perfo rmance of obligation s o f the Borrower identified the P roject 
Agreement. Under the  Project Agreement, there are s eparate regimes for determining Deductions relating to D&C 
Work and t he Asset Management Services. Unavailabil ity Events and Noncompli ance Events include, but are n ot 
limited to, failure to provide certain notifications to the District,  failure to observe or comply with various project 
delivery protocols or breach of performance standards. The Borrower has the benefit of certain cure periods and/or 
grace periods for its breaches an d f ailures that are bas ed on the type of  breach o r failure. With r espect to 
Noncompliance Events, the District is permitted to as sess Noncompliance Points after notification to and failur e by 
the Borrower to cure such breach or failure following such notice. 
Each of a Noncompliance Borrower Default Trigger and an Unavailability Default Trigger is the basis for a 
Borrower Default and can, under certain circumstances, serve as a basis for termination of the Project Agreement.

default events include, without limitation, any one or more of the follow ing, among other events: ( a) the Borrower 
fails to commence the D&C Period Asset Management Services within thirty (30) days following the issuance of the 
first NTP3; (b) the Borrower discontinues performance of the Work for sixty (60) or more consecutive days; (c) the 
Borrower fails to pay any amount due  to the District under the Project  Agreement when due, excep t to the exte nt 
such payment is subject to a good faith dispute, or fails to deposit funds in the HRA  in the amount and within the 
time period required by the Project Agreement; (d) a Restricted Change i n Ownership occurs; and (e) an Insolvency 
Event aris es with respect to the Borrower. Se e "APPENDIX B —SUM MARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF

Compensation on Termination 
 
  In the context of a full termination of the Project Agreement, the District is required to pay to the Borrower 
certain termination compensation which may or may not be s ufficient to repay the outstanding amount of the Series 
2022 Bonds. Upon a termination for convenience, termination for a default  by the District, or a termination by court 
ruling, the District will pa y the District Termination Sum, which is equal to (with out double-counting) the sum of: 
(a) all amounts shown in the Base C ase Financial Model as payable by the Borrower from the Early  Termination 
Date, either in div idends or other  distributions on the sha re capital of the Borrower  under the Equity Member 
Funding Agreements, each amount discounted back at the Base Case Equity IRR from the date on which it is shown 
to be payable in the B ase Case Financial Model to the Termination Date; plus (b) Net Lenders ' Liabilities; plus ( c) 
Subcontractor B reakage Cos ts; p lus ( d) accrued  unpaid amou nt due fr om t he D istrict to  the Borrower; plus  (e ) 
Redundancy Payments for employees of the Borrower that have been or will be reasonably incurred by the Borrower 
as a direct result of termination of the Project Agreement; minus (f) any Deductions assessed but not paid; minus (g) 
Deferred Equity Amounts. 
 Upon a termination for Extended Force Majeure, Extend ed Relief Event, or Uninsurability, the District will 
pay the No Fault T ermination Sum, which is equal to (without double-counting) the sum of: ( a) the gr eater of zero 
(0) or the sum of (i) all amounts paid to the Borrower by way of equity to the capita l of the Borrower less dividends 
and other distrib utions paid by t he Borrower to the Holdco  (except to the extent deducted under clause (a) (ii)   
81 
herein); plus ( ii) the p rincipal amoun t of all Equity Mem ber Debt less an amount equal  to the aggregate o f all  
payments made b y the Borrower under the Equity Member Funding Agreement s; plus (b) Net Len ders' Liabilities; 
plus (c) Subcontractor Breakage Costs; plus ( d) accrued unpaid amounts due from the District to the Borrower; plus 
(e) Redundancy Payments for e mployees of the Borrower t hat have been or will be reason ably incurr ed by the 
Borrower as a direct result of termination of the Project Agreement; minus (f) any Deductions assessed but not paid. 
 Finally, in case of termination of the Project Agreement due to any default by the Borrower, the amounts of 
the termination compensation va ry depending o n the timing of the ter mination. If such termination occurs before 
Project Final Completion, the District will pay the Borrower an amount equal to the lower of the D&C Work Value 
and eighty percent (80%) of th e amount of  Net Lenders' Liabilities. If s uch termination occurs on or  after Proj ect 
Final Completion, the District will pay the Borrower Default (Asset Management Period) Term ination Sum, which 
is equal to (without double-counting): (a) eighty percent (80 %) of the a mount of Lenders' Liabilities; minus (b) all 
Losses incurred by the District as a result of such te rmination; minus (c) an amount equal to the sum of (i) Deferred 
Equity Amounts (if any ), (ii) insurance proceeds (excluding proceeds of personal i njury, property damage or other 
third party liability insurance payable to or for the account of a third part y), and (iii) Account Balances calculated at 
the Early Termination Date.  
For more inform ation on the calcul ation of termination compensation in spec ific circumstances, see 
"APPENDIX B—SU MMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE PROJECT AGREEMENT." 
Handback 
 
 Upon the expiration or earlier termination of the Project Agreement, the Borrower is required to transfer the 
Project to the District, at no charge to  the District, in the condition a nd meeting all resid ual l ife require ments 
specified in the Proje ct Agreement. In  c onnection with such handb ack, the Borrower, in conju nction with the 
Independent Engineer, will conduct inspections of a ll of the components of the Project in order to  confirm that the 
residual life requirements have been fulfilled and to determine the renewal work to be performed prior to handback. 
 
Tra
nsfer Restrictions 
 
 Neither th e Borrower nor the Dist rict may ass ign or transfer any of th eir rights or obligations u nder the  
Project Agreement without the consent of the other, with certain exceptions such as allowing the District to assign or 
transfer its rights to other G overnmental Entities th at succeed to the gover nmental powe rs and au thority of the 
District. The re striction set forth in the prior s entence does not  apply to t he grant of any security for any financing 
extended to the Borrower (directly or indirectly) under the Finance Documents or to the enforcement of the same. 
 
Restricted Change in Ownership 
 
 Subject to ce rtain permitted exceptions, a Borrower Default will arise upon the occu rrence of a Restricted 
Change in Ownership. 
 
Provisions Addressing Rights of Lenders 
 
 Right to Cure or Step -In.  As of the Financ ial Closing Date, the Collateral Agent will bec ome a par ty to 
the Project Agreement solely for the purposes of administ ering certain terms relating to the rights of Lenders as set 
forth therein. Provided that all unperformed payment obligations of the Borrower shall have been remedied in full or 
waived by the Distric t, the Project Agreement provides the Collateral Agent, d uring an Event o f Default or during 
the cure periods specified below following the occurrence of a Borrower Default, the right to take certain actions in 
satisfaction of the Borrower's obligations under the Project Agreement or otherwise exercise step in o r substitution 
rights with respect to  the Project Ag reement upon the  issuance o f a step-in notice or sub stitution notice  in 
accordance with the terms of the Project Agreement. The relevant cure periods in respect of Borrower Defaults are: 
 
(a) with respect to any Payment Default, the date falling thirty (30) days after the lat er of ( i) the date 
that the Collateral Agent receives the relevant District CA Termination Notice and ( ii) expiration of any applicable 
cure period granted to the Borrower in accordance with the Project Agreement;   
82 
(b) with respect to any Bankruptcy-Related Default, the date falling sixty (60) days after the lat er of 
(i) the date that the Collateral Agent receives the relevant District CA Termination Notice and (ii) expiration of any 
applicable cure period granted to the Borrower in accordance with the Project Agreement; 
(c) with respect to any  Non-Completion Default, the dat e falling ninety (90) days after the date that 
the C ollateral A gent re ceives the relevant District C A Termina tion Notice ; provi ded that such p eriod will be 
extended by su ch reasonable period of t ime as m ay be required to achieve Project Final Comp letion (subject to a 
maximum extension of one hundred eighty (18 0) days), but only to the extent that: (i) there is a reasonable prospect 
of achieving Pro ject Fi nal Com pletion withi n two hundred seventy ( 270) day s of the r elevant District CA 
Termination Notice and (ii) within the initial ninety (90) -day cure period, the Collateral Agent and the District (each 
acting reasonably) agree to a plan in relation to achieving Project Final Completion; and 
(d) in the event of  any Borrower Default not referred to in cla uses ( a), (b ), or (c ) above, the date 
falling ninety (90) days a fter the  later of ( a) th e date th at the Colla teral Agent receives the relevant District CA 
Termination Notice and (b) expiration of any applicable cure  period granted to the Borrower pursuant to the Project 
Agreement; provided that such perio d will, at the  request of the Collateral Agent, be extended up to a maximum of 
thirty (30) additional days, but only to the extent that: (i) within the initial ninety (90)-day cure period provided for 
in this clause  (d ), the Collateral Agent and the Dis trict (each act ing reasona bly) agree t o a plan s pecifying the 
remedial action to be ta ken with respect to the relevant Borrower Default and ( ii) the extension re quested by the 
Collateral Agent repre sents (in t he reaso nable opinion of the District) a reason able period of time to re medy the 
relevant Borrower Default. 
In addition, under certai n  circumstances where the Project Agreement has been rejected in bankru ptcy, the 
Collateral Agent or the Collateral Agent 's designee or assignee will have th e ability to enter in to a ne w Project  
Agreement with the District. The new Project Agreement shall contain conditions,  a greements, term s, pr ovisions 
and limitations which  are t he same as t hose of the Pr oject Agreement, except for  any obligations that have been 
fulfilled by the Borrower, any party acting on behalf of or stepping in for Borrower, or the C ollateral Agent prior to 
such rejection or termination.

D&C CONTRACT AND D&C GUARANTEE 
 
D&C Contract 
 
On March 7, 2022, the Borrower entered into  the D&C Contra ct with the D&C Con tractor, pursuant to which 
substantially all of the D&C  Work relating to the Project is required to be undert aken by the D&C Contractor, on a 
turnkey lump-sum fixed price basis. Certain terms of the D&C Contract will be amended on or prior to the Financial 
Closing Date and this Official Statement sets out the terms of the D&C Contract as will be in effect on the Financial 
Closing Date.  
 
Principal Rights and Responsibilities of the D&C Contractor and Borrower 
 
 Pursuant to the D&C Contract, the Borrower has granted to the D&C Contractor, the exclusive ri ght to, 
subject to the terms of the D&C Contr act, develop, design and construct the Project in accordance with the terms of 
the D&C Contract. For the purpose of performing its obligations under the D&C Contract only, the D&C Contractor 
will, subject to the  terms of the D &C Con tract, have the right to  enter onto (and  engage in the activities 
contemplated in the D&C Contract) each Project Site. 
 
Design and Constru
 ction of the Project.  The D&C Contractor's obligations include, among other things, (a) 
furnishing the design a nd oth er services necessary fo r the construction, convers ion, testin g, commissioning, and  
provisioning of the Improved Street Light Network  a nd the  installation, testing, and commissioning of t he Smar t 
City Improvements, and achieving Projec t Final Completion by  the Guaranteed Project Final Completion Date; and 
(b) ensuring all Conversion Work i s performed in accordance with the Released for Co nstruction Documents. The 
D&C Contractor is required  to per form the D&C Wo rk in accordance with (a) Good Industry P ractice, (b) all 
applicable Law, (c) all Gover nmental Approvals, and (d) the Technical Pr ovisions and all other requ irements of the 
D&C Contract. See "APPENDIX C—SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE  D&C CONTRACT —
Design and Construction of the Project." 
 
Haza rdous M aterials.  Except as otherwise expressly provided in the D&C Contract, the D&C Contractor is 
solely respons ible for the management, tr eatment, handling, storage, mo nitoring, remediatio n, removal,  t ransport 
and disposal of any Ha zardous Materials in, under or on any Project  Site that are encou ntered at all time s prior to 
Project Final Completion except to the extent such release is an A sset Management Contractor Hazardous Materials 
Release.  At all t imes during the  D&C Term, the D &C Contractor i s sol ely resp onsible for the m anagement, 
treatment, handl ing, storage, m onitoring, remediatio n, removal, tr ansport, and disposal of any D&C Contr actor 
Hazardous Ma terials Release.  The D&C Contract or is (with out accepting or assuming respons ibility under a ny 
applicable Law) responsible  f or obtaining and m aintaining all Gov ernmental Approvals relat ing to any Rem edial 
Action and is solely responsible for compliance with all Governmental Approvals and applicable Law  concerning or 
relating to Remedial Action re lated to Hazardo us Materials f or which it is respo nsible. See "APPENDIX C —
SUMMARY OF CERTAI N PROVISIONS OF THE D&C CONTRACT—Principal Rights an d Responsibilities of 
the D&C Contractor and the Borrower—Hazardous Materials ." 
 
Pa
yments to the D&C Contractor 
 
The lump s um fixed price payable to the D&C Contra ctor for the  performance of the D&C W ork is  
$145,775,589 inclusive of all design fees and costs, insurance , const ruction costs, Governmental Approv als, 
applicable taxes, utilities costs, development, building permits  and a ll other  costs necessary to perfor m the 
obligations of the D&C Contractor und er the D&C Contract.  The D &C Contractor is requ ired to submit monthly  
payment applications, subject to review and audit by the Borrower and the DBC Payment Certifier.  The Borrower 
has the right to withhold certain payments from the D&C Contractor. 
   
84 
The D&C Contract also provides a monthly maximum aggr egate construct ion payment schedule.  Under 
that schedule, the D&C Contractor may not seek to draw more (in a ggregate) than t he amount provided for up to 
such month.  The maximum aggregate amount available to be drawn under the schedule commences at $15,29 4,512 
as of Financial Close and increases each month fo r twenty-three (23) months.  The amount of $7,288,779 is payable 
in r espect of the 23 rd month, and is require d to be paid with in eight (8) Business Days o f the P roject F inal 
Completion Date.   
The DBC Payment Certifier is required to review the invoice submitted by the D&C Contractor each month 
and, subject to certain requirements set out in the D&C Contract, certify an amount that is due to be paid to the D&C 
Contractor in respect of the progress of the work performed as of such month, which in no event will be an amount 
greater than the maximum scheduled amount set out for such month in the D&C Contract.  
 
Disputes 
 
Except for disputes related to equival ent project relief, all disputes arising under the D& C Contract will be 
resolved pursuant to the D ispute Resolutions Procedures.  If  a dispute arises, a party to the dispute sh all have the 
right to req uest a  meeting for resolution of the dispute among offi cers desi gnated f or such p urpose. See 
"APPENDIX C —SUM MARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE D&C CONTRACT —E quivalent Project 
Relief."   
 
In the event the parties fail to resolve the dispute pursuant to the meeting of of ficers, and timely resolution 
of the dispute is required to meet any schedule relevant to the ex ecution by a party of its obligations under the D&C  
Contract, or th e disp ute invol ves a claim the monetary value of which i s e stimated to be les s than or equal to 
$5,000,000, then either party may be wri tten notice to the other require that the dis pute be resolved on an expedited 
basis in accordance with a b inding fast tr ack ar bitration process.  The arbitrator is required to issue a written 
decision as soon as p racticable and in any event within twenty (20) days of the d elivery of the notice of dispute, 
which decision shall be final and binding.  All other disputes shall be determined pursuant to arbitration. 
 
Delay Liquidated Damages 
 
The D&C Contractor is required to pay D elay Liquidated Damages in the event that it fails to comp lete (a) 
Milestone 1 by December 31, 2023 in the amount of $ 81,000 per Calendar Quarter or partial Calendar Quarter; (b) 
Milestone 2 by seven-hundred and thirty (730) days from the Financial Closing Date in the amount of $ 228,271 per 
Calendar Quarter or partial Calendar Quarter; and (c) Project Final Completion by nine-hundred and ten (910) days 
from the Financial Closing Date in the amount of $82,076 per day or partial day. 
 
The m
aximum amount of Delay Liquidated Damages payable b y the D&C Co ntractor is $ 15,392,222. 
Delay Liquidated Damages are payable whether or not the Borrower incurs or mitigates damages, the Borrower does 
not have any obligation to  mitigate any such damages.  Each party to the D&C Co ntract is estopped from alleging 
that, the Delay Liquidated Damages are a penalty, or are otherwise unenforceable for any reason, including that such 
damages are not incurred by the Borrower.   
 
Where Delay Liquidated Damages are incurred in respect of Project Final Completion the D&C Contractor 
is required to pay such amounts to the Borrower on a monthly basis by the last Business Day of the month in which 
Delay Liquidated Damages were incurred.  Where Delay Liquidated Damages are incurred in respect of Milestone 1 
or Milestone 2, the D&C Contractor is required to pay such a mounts on the fi rst day of each Quarter after the date 
by which such Milestone 1 or Mil estone 2 was req uired to  be completed in order  to avoid the pa yment of De lay 
Liquidated Damages. 
   
85 
The dates which trigger the obligation to pay Delay Liquidated Damages may b e extended as a result  of a 
Compensation Event or an Uninsured Relief Event including as a result of a Borrower Act, provided that in the case 
of a Compensation Event or an Uninsured Relief Event and only with respect t o the date by which Project Fin al 
Completion must be co mpleted to avoid  payment of Delay Liquidated Damages, such date will not be exte nded for 
the first 180 days of a ny delay.  In the event a del ay is due to a Relief Event that is n ot an Uninsured Relief Event, 
the applicable scheduled date set will not be extended. 
 
Achievement of Project Final Completion Mechanism 
 
  In certain circums tances, if it is disclosed in  a paymen t applic ation or sh own in an up dated Project 
Baseline Schedule that the D&C Contractor may achieve Project Final Co mpletion more than three weeks after the 
Guaranteed Pr oject Final C ompletion Date, the D&C Contractor m ay be required to pr epare and  deliver to the 
Borrower and the DBC Payment Certifier a plan which is reasonably achievable to accelerate the performance of the 
D&C Work to achieve Project Final Completion on or before the Guaranteed Project Final Completion Date or if the 
Guaranteed Project Final Completion Date has passed, as soon as is reasonably practicable. 
 
By agreement between t he D&C Contractor and the Borrower or a bsent a greement based o n a 
determination of the D BC Pa yment Certifier,  an Actionable Acceleration Plan and cost for acceleration ma y be 
established, in wh ich case the D&C Contracto r is required to , at its cos t and exp ense, carry out the A ctionable 
Acceleration Plan.  In addition, in such circumstance, if directed by the Borrower, the D&C Contractor is required to 
pay to  the Borrower the agreed or estimated cost of acceleration, or perm it such amount  to be set -off again st 
available payments or provide a letter of credit in respect of such amount. The amount so paid, set-off or provided in 
a letter of credit is required to be returned to the D&C Contractor by payment or release of the letter of credit as it is 
determined that the estimated cost of a cceleration has decreased or where the event that t riggered the obligation to 
formulate the Actionable Acceleration Plan has been resolved.  Amounts paid, set-off or provide in a letter of credi t 
erode the D&C Contractor's limit of liability under the D&C Contract. 
 
D&
C Payment Guarantee Requirement 
 
Concurrent with the execution of the D&C Contract, the D&C Contracto r provided a par ent company 
payment guaranty in favor of the Borrower from the D&C Contr actor Guarantor, in the fo rm agreed in the D&C 
Contract, guaranteeing payment all amounts which b ecome owing from D&C C ontractor to the Borrower under the 
D&C Contract and Interface Agreement.  See "D&C CONTRACT AND D&C GUARANTEE—D&C Guarant ee." 
 
Performance Security 
 
As security for the performance by the D& C Contractor under the D&C Contract, the D&C Con tractor is 
required to provide the Borrower on o r before Financial Close, the D &C Security which is require d to name 
Borrower as the obligee and the District and the Collateral Agent as an additional obligee, in  an amount equal to the 
DBC Price, from a sur ety with a credit rating in the top two categories from two of the three  nationally recognized 
rating agencies or that is rated A/VIII or b etter according to the A.M. Best's Financial Strength Rating and Financial 
Size Category and listed on U.S. Treasury C ircular 570 to ensure the completio n of the D&C  Work by the D&C 
Contractor.  S ee "APPENDIX C —SUMMARY OF CERTAI N PROVISIONS OF THE D&C CONT RACT—
Performance Security."

Compensation Events 
 
 The D&C Contract provides that the  D&C Contractor will receive compensation from the B orrower, in 
addition to o btaining relief from complying with the deadlines set forth therein in ca se of the occu rrence of certain 
enumerated Compensation Events. See "APPENDIX C—SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE D&C 
CONTRACT—Payments to the D&C Contract or—Compensation Eve nts" f or a list of applica ble Compensation 
Events. 
 
 In order to obtain compens ation, which is contemplated to be generally equal  to the amount nece ssary to 
restore the D&C Contractor to the same economic position it would have been in if the Compensation Event had not 
occurred (except with respect to certain Compensation Events for which the D&C Contract specifies the unit pricing 
to which the D&C Contractor is entitled to paid), the D&C Contractor is required to (among other things): 
 
(a) submit a claim to the Borrower promptly (a nd i n any event w ithin twe nty (20) days) after the 
D&C Contractor first becomes aware of the relevant Compensation Event; 
 
(b) promptly, and in any event  within fifty (50) days, after the D&C Contractor first be came aware of 
the relevant Compensation Event, gi ve f ull details of the  relevant Compensation Event and th e extension of time 
and/or any compensation claimed, including an enumerated list of items that includes (among other things): 
 
(i) with respect to any claim for an extension of time, a Time Impact Analysis demonstrating 
the rele vant Compensation Event wil l re sult in  an identifiable and measurable disruption to the 
D&C Work affecting a critical path activity; 
 
(ii) d
etails of the steps the D&C Contractor has taken or will take to  mitigate the effect of the 
Compensation Event; 
 
(iii) the type and amo unt of i nsurance that may be applicable and amount s that have b een or 
are anticipated to be collected under such insurance; and 
 
(iv) evidence reasonably sati sfactory to the Borrower demonstrating that no other concurrent 
or unrelated delay to a critical p ath activity that is the Borrower's responsibility has occurred that 
has contributed to the delay for which relief is being sought; and 
 
(c) demonstrate to the reasonable satisfaction of the Borrower that: 
 
(i) the Compensation Event was the direct cause or is reasonably likely to be the direct cause 
of: 
 
(A) a delay  in achi eving P roject Final Completion by the Guaranteed Project  Final 
Completion Date or (follo wing the Guaranteed Proje ct Final Completion Date), f urther 
delay in achieving Project Final Completion;   
87 
(B) the D& C Contractor's inability to comply with its o bligations unde r the D&C 
Contract which are obligations of the Borrower under the Project Agreement; and/or 
 
(C) the D&C Contractor incurring a Change in Costs; and 
 
(ii) the Change in Costs, e xtension of time, and/or relief from obligations u nder the D&C 
Contract claime d, could not reasonably be miti gated or recovered w ithout material cost by the 
D&C Contractor acting in a ccordance with Go od Industry Practice, including by re -sequencing, 
reallocating, or redeploying its forces to other portion s of the Conversion Work and/or Smart City 
Work. 
 
In the event that information is provided after the dates required by this section in respect of Compensation 
Events, then the D& C Contractor will not be entitled to an y compensation, extension of time, or relief f rom its 
obligations under the D&C Contract to the extent that the qu antum thereof was adversely affected as a result of such 
notice or in formation not being provided within the relevant time periods. In the  event the D&C Contractor fails to 
provide such information for a period of eleven (11) months or more, the rights of the  D&C Contractor with respect 
to the relevant Compensation Event will be of no further force or effect. 
 
If the Borrower and the D&C Contractor cannot agree on the extent of any compensat ion, delays incurred, 
or relief from the D&C Contractor's obligatio ns under the D&C Contract, the p arties will resolve the matter in 
accordance with the dispute resolution procedures set forth in t he D&C C ontract, except that any dispute that is a  
Corresponding Project Agreement Claim will be determined in accordance with the provisions of the D&C Contr act 
relating to equivalent project relief . See "APPENDIX C —SUM MARY OF C ERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE 
D&C CONTRACT—P ayments to the D&C Contractor—Compensa tion Events" and "APPENDIX C—SUMMARY 
OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE D&C CONTRACT—Equivalent Project Relief."  
 
Relief Events 
 
The D&C Contract pro vides that the D&C Contractor will obtain relief from complying with the d eadlines 
set forth therein, relief from compliance with the D&C Contractor's obligations th erein, and/or relief from any rights 
of the Bo rrower in connection with termination due to  the occurrence of Relief Events, provided that all claims for 
such relief sha ll be Pass Thr ough Claims, and the D&C Contractor agrees that it shall not be entitled to any relief 
pursuant to the provision s of th e D&C Contract relating to claims in respect o f Relief Events e xcept to the ext ent 
such relief is received b y Borrower in accordance with the Project Agreemen t.  The Relief Events under the D&C 
Contract are the same as the Relief Events under the Project Agreement.  
 
Other than in respect of Finance C osts, whi ch s hall not be a vailable to the D&C Contractor, the relief 
available to the D&C Contractor pursuant to the terms of the D&C Contract in respect of Relief E vents shall be the 
same as the relief available to the Borro wer pursuant to the Project Agr eement.  The procedural requirements which 
condition relief un der the D&C Contract are similar to those in the P roject Agreement, except that certain time 
periods applicable to the D&C Con tractor are sho rter under the D& C Contract, the D&C Contractor is re quired to 
give notices to the Borrower and the Borrower is res ponsible to pro vide such noti ces to the District, a nd the 
Borrower is required to provide certain notices received from the District to the D&C Contractor.  D&C Contractor's 
claims in respect of Relief Events will be Pass Through Claims.

The D&C Contract or may re fuse a District Change Request that would, if implemen ted (a) v iolate 
applicable Law ; (b) contravene a G overnmental Approval the n in full force and effect; provided that such 
contravention cannot be corrected by the issuance of a new or revised Governmental Approval that can be obtained 
by th e D&C Contrac tor; (c) materially adversely affect the health and safety of an y Person; (d) materially and 
adversely change the nature of the Project or the allocation of risk between the Parties; (e) be unrelated to the kind of 
work initially procured unde r the RFP and  to the kind of work that PA Borrower-Related Entities provide i n the 
marketplace; or (f) require the D &C Work to be performed in a way that results in a breach of the terms of an y 
License Agreement. The D& C Con tractor acknowledges and agrees that any refu sal by the D&C Contractor of a 
District Change Request shall be a Pass Through Claim. 
 
If the Borrower accepts the D&C Contractor Estimate the Borrower shall make a payment to the D&C 
Contractor wit hin five  (5) Business Days of receiving payment from the D istrict in re spect of each  invoice in 
accordance with the agreed -upon pay ment schedule, accompanied by evidence reasonably acceptable to the 
Borrower (where applicable) that the relevant part of the Borrower Change has been carried out. If a District Change 
Request results in a decrease in the D&C Contractor costs, any  payments due from the Borrower under the D&C 
Contract will be adjusted downwards to reflect such reduction in the D&C Contractor’s costs.  
 
D&C Cont ractor Changes . If the D&C Con tractor wishes to introduce a D&C Contractor Change it must 
deliver a change request to the Borrower setting out (a) the proposed change to the D&C Work in sufficient detail to 
enable the Borrowe r to evaluate i t in full, (b) the D&C Contractor's reasons fo r proposing t he change to the D&C 
Work, (c) a request to the Borrow er to consult with the D&C Contractor with a view to deciding whether to agree to 
the change to the D&C Work and, if so, what  consequential cha nges the Borrower requires due to the D&C 
Contractor Change, (d) any implications of the change to the D&C Work,  (e) details regarding proposed changes to 
the Milestone Payments, if any (and, if so, giving a de tailed cost estimate of such proposed change), (f) any dates by 
which a decision by the Borrower is critical and (g) all of the information required with respect to a D&C Contractor 
Estimate as set out in th e D&C Contract. Within fifteen (15) Business Days  of meeting with the D&C Co ntractor to 
review the D&C Contractor Change Request the Borrowe r is required to either submit the D&C Contractor Change 
Request to the District pursuant to the terms of the Project Agreement as a Pass Through Claim or reject the D&C 
Contractor Change Requ est.  If Borrower agrees to submit a D&C Contractor Change Request to the Dis trict as a 
Borrower Change Request pursuant to the Pro ject Agreeme nt, and if a s a result the District issues a Borrower 
Change Order, such Borrower Change Order shall be deemed to  be a D&C Contractor Change Order issued under 
the D&C Contract.  The D&C Contractor shall pay all of the Borrower's costs, expenses and Losses associated with 
any D&C Contractor C hange Request submitted to the District together with all of the  Borrower's costs, expenses 
and Losses associated with any deemed D&C Contracto r Change O rder. Unless a D& C Contractor Change Order 
specifically provides for an increase in the D&C Contractor Payments or any other payment to the D&C Contract or 
there shall be no increase in any such payment as a result of a change to the D&C Work pr oposed by th e D&C 
Contractor. If a D&C  Contractor Change Order res ults in a de crease in the D&C Contractor's costs,  any payments 
due from the Borrower under the Project Agreement will be adjusted downwards to reflect 50% of such reduction in 
the D&C Contra ctor's costs. The amount of s uch adjustment will be determined pur suant to the  Project Agreement 
and subject to any Corresponding Project Agreement Claim.
