The D&C Contractor must release, defend, indemnify and hold har mless the Borrower Indemnified Parties 
and the Indemnified Parties on demand f rom and against any and all liability to third  parties for Losses arising from 
(a) death or personal injury, (b) loss of or damage t o any Borrower Indemnified Party or Indemnifie d Party's 
property, and (c) third party claims br ought against any Borrower Indemnified Party or Indemnified Party, which 
may arise out  of, or in consequence  of, any or all of the follow ing: any actua l or a lleged fail ure by the D&C 
Contractor to comply with, observe o r perform any of the covenants, obligation s, agreements, terms or conditions in 
the Project Documents or any actual or alleged breach by the D&C Contractor of its representations or warranties set 
forth in the D&C Contract. 
   
89 
The D&C Contractor will not be respons ible or obliged to indemnify a Borrower Indemnified Party o r 
Indemnified Party with respect to any liability  or Losses to the ext ent that the same arise as a direct  result of: (a) a  
Compensation Event, Relief Event, or Borrower Default (D&C Contract); (b) the presence of Hazardous Mat erials 
on any Project Site for  which the District is, pursuant to the Project A greement, or for whic h the Borrower is 
pursuant to the D&C Contract, deemed to be the sole generator and arranger, but only to the extent tha t the relevant 
Loss does not arise as a di rect result of the negligence of the D&C  Contractor or the D&C Contrac tor failing to 
comply wi th the t erms of the D&C Contract;  (c) any non- performance by a Borrower Indemnified Party of its 
obligations under the D &C Contract, or any non -performance by an I ndemnified Party of its  obligations under the 
Project Agreement or any violation of applicable Law by any Borrower Indemnified Party or Indemnified Party; or 
(d) the fraud,  negligence, recklessness, bad faith or w illful misconduct of a Bo rrower Indemnified Party or 
Indemnified Party. 
 
Limitation on D&C Contractor's Liability 
 
Notwithstanding a ny other provisions of the D&C Contract, the D&C Con tractor's maximum total liability 
under the D&C Contract and the Interfa ce Agreement, inclusive of the capped a mount of Delay Liquidate d 
Damages, shall, subject to the following exc lusions, no t exceed 3 5% o f the DBC Price.  The D&C Contra ctor 
Liability Cap is exclusive of: (a) any insurance proceeds received pursuant  to policies of i nsurance to the extent 
required under the D&C Contract and any liability for Losses that would have be en covered by such insurance up to 
the amount o f the requ ired insurance and deductibles but in respect of whi ch insurance proceeds are no t available 
due to a failure on the part of th e D&C Contractor, or any D&C Contractor-Related Entity, as applicable, to  comply 
with its insu rance obligations as set f orth in the D&C Contract; (b) any claim by the Borrower in respect of fraud, 
gross ne gligence, willful misc onduct and deliberate acts of  wrongdoing by the D&C Cont ractor or any D&C  
Contractor-Related Entity; (c) liabilities arising under any En cumbrance created or caused by the D&C Contractor 
or parties for whom t he D&C Contractor is respo nsible; (d) any claim for personal injury or death;  (e) t hird party 
claims in tort a gainst the D&C Contractor to the extent such claims arise from the negligence or breach of c ontract 
of the D&C Contractor or parties for whom the D&C Contractor is responsible; (f) third party claims in tort against 
the Borrower (excluding claims b y the L enders, the Borrower and Borrower-Related En tity(ies) and t he Asset 
Management Contractor and Asset Management C ontractor-Related E ntity(ies)); (g) third part y claims in tor t 
against the L enders or a Borrower-Related Entity fo r which indemnity is sought a gainst the Borrower to the extent 
such claims arise from the negligence or breach of contract of the D&C Contractor  or parties for whom the D&C 
Contractor is responsible; (h) third party claims re lating to intellectual property to the extent such claims are caused 
by the D &C Contractor;  (i) amounts p aid to the Bo rrower by the D&C C ontractor to the extent that same are 
subsequently recovered b y the D&C  Contractor from the Asset  M anagement Contractor pursuant to the Interface 
Agreement; (j) amounts paid by th e D&C Contractor which are sub sequently repaid to the D&C  Contractor by the 
Borrower, as required pursuant to any  bankruptcy or insolvency legislation; (k) any losses incurred by the Borrower 
in respect of the D&C Contractor ab andoning the D&C Works; (l) expenses incurred by the Borrower in enforcing a 
claim under the D&C  Contract; (m) any Project Cost Overruns;  (n) any amount pa id to the Borrower by the D&C 
Contractor to th e extent that the same is subsequently recovered by t he D&C Co ntractor from th e Borrower or any 
Borrower-Related Entity; (o) any interest, penalties and fees incurred by the Borrower due to unpaid amounts owing 
from the D&C Contractor to the Borrower; and (p) fines and penalties under statue or any reaso nable costs incurred 
by Borrower or any Borrower-Related Entity a nd fo r which D&C Con tractor is liable  to the Borrower or a 
Borrower-Related Entity in complying with statutory obligations or any order of a Governmental Entity.  
 
Neithe
r the D&C Contrac tor nor the Borrower have any liability under the D&C Co ntract, of any nature, 
after th e date when the las t of the D &C Contractor 's obligations under the D&C C ontract have be en d ischarged, 
which shall not be before the expiry of all Latent Defects Periods.

a copy to the Borrower of any Noncompliance Event related to th e D&C Work or the action s or omissions of  the

omission of the D&C Contracto r.  Noncompliance Points,  Noncompliance Deductions and Unava ilability

Agreement. 
 
The D&C Contracto r is liab le for Noncompliance P oints, and associ ated Deductions, resultin g f rom

a fair and equitable sharing of the amounts due to or payable by each party based on their respective contributions to 
the Borrower Default with any disputes to be resolved pursuant to the Dispute Resolu tion Procedure.  If the Project 
Agreement is terminated as a result of a Borrower Default for which the D&C Contractor is not respon sible, the

double-counting, such payment will be without prejudice to any claims  under the D &C Contract that the Borrower 
and D&C Contractor may have against each other unrelated to the termination.  See "APPENDIX C—SUMMARY

Restrictions on Change in Ownership 
 
The D&C Cont ractor shall ensure that there will be no sale, transfer, o r o ther disposal of any direct o r 
indirect legal, b eneficial, equ itable or other ownership int erest in the D&C Contrac tor or the D&C Contrac tor 
Guarantor which, when taken together  with any other such transfer or disposal during t he relevant period gives r ise 
to a Change of Control of th e D&C Contractor or the D&C Contractor Guarantor; grant of any security interest, lien 
or other encumbrance over any legal, beneficial, equitable or other direct or indirect ownership interest in th e D&C 
Contractor or the D&C Contractor Guarantor giving rise to a Change of C ontrol of the D&C Contractor or the D&C 
Contractor Guarantor, as applicable; or agreement, whether or not subject to any condition precedent or su bsequent, 
to do any  of the forego ing, without the prior writte n consent of the Borrower, su ch consent not to b e withheld or 
delayed where ( a) the D&C Con tractor can demonstrate to the satisfaction of  the Borrower, acting reasonably, that  
the D&C Contractor  will have a level of financial c apacity (taking into accou nt the financial strength of the  D&C 
Contractor and the D&C Contr actor Guarantor), resources, and ability to perform t he D&C Work  after the Change 
of C ontrol Transaction that is eq ual to or greater  than the level of financial capacity, resources, and abili ty to 
perform the D&C Work held by the D&C Contractor before the Change of Co ntrol Transaction and (b) the Change 
of Control Transaction will not have a Material Adverse Effect. 
 
Relationship Between District Determination and Borrower Determination 
 
Where, i n accordance with the provisi ons of the D&C Con tract, a deter mination, action,  negotiation, 
decision, discretion, election or other thing is made, taken, done, exercised or elected by the Borrower in respect of a 
matter in accordance with a determinatio n, action, negotiation, decision, discretion, election or other thing made, 
taken, done, exercised or e lected by the D istrict in respect of the same matter pursuant of the Projec t Agreement, to 
the extent that D&C Contractor disa grees with such determination, action, negotiation, decision, discretion, ele ction   
92 
or other  thing, D&C C ontractor's sole remedy, to the extent pe rmitted in accordance with the te rms of the D&C 
Contract is to bring a Pass Through Claim, a claim against the District pursuant to the Project Agreement.  
 
Equivalent Project Relief 
 
Whenever the Borrower is permitted to do so by the terms of the Project Agreement and the D&C Contract, 
the Borrower shall in good faith, unless it determines it would be  manifestly frivolous or vexatious to do so, present 
all of the D&C Contractor's Pass Through Claims to the Distr ict as Corresponding Project Agreement Claims to the 
extent requested by the D&C Contractor and at the D& C Contractor's cost.  See "APPENDIX C—SUMMARY OF 
CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE D&C CONTRACT—Equivalent Project Rel ief." 
 
As between the Borrower and the D&C Co ntractor, the res olution o f a Pass  Through Claim wil l not be 
subject to any dispute resolution procedures under the D&C Contract, and all applicable time periods with respect to 
the resolution of such claim unde r the D&C Contract will be tolled, while the Project Agreement Dispute 
Proceeding with respect to the Corresponding Project Agreement Claim is pending. With respect to its Pass Through 
Claim against the B orrower under the Pr oject Documents, the D&C Contractor is bound by the resol ution or final  
determination of the C orresponding Project Agreement Claim under the Pro ject Agreement. See "APPENDIX C—
SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE D&C CONTRACT—Equivale nt Project Relief." 
 
As b etween Borrower and the D&C C ontractor, the resolution of a Corres ponding Project Agreement 
Claim will not be subject to any dispute resolution procedures under the D&C Contract or otherwise progressed, and 
all applicable time periods with respect to th e resolution of such claim under the D&C Contract will be tolled, while 
the Project Agreement Dispute Proceeding with respect to the Corresponding Project Agreement Claim is pendin g.  
With respect to any District Claim brought by the Borrower under the Project Documents, each of the Borrower  and 
the D&C Contractor shall be bound by the resolution or final determination of the Corresponding Project Agreement 
Claim under the Pro ject A greement.  See "APPENDIX C —SUMMA RY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE 
D&C CONTRACT—Eq uivalent Project Relief." 
 
In the event of an award rendered bet ween the District and the Borrower under the Project Agreement with 
respect t o a  Corresponding Project Ag reement Claim, pursuant to which the Borrower is liable in a ny respect 
whatsoever, D&C Contractor will forthwith on demand indemnify the Borrower in full in respect of such liability to 
the extent D&C Contractor is liable therefor. 
 
See "AP
PENDIX C—SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE D&C CONT RACT" for a more 
detailed summary of selected provisions of the D&C Contract. 
 
D&C Payment Guarantee 
 
Concurrent with the  e xecution of the D &C Contra ct, th e D&C Contractor delivered a parent  company 
payment guarantee from the D&C Contractor Guarantor .  The  D&C Guarantee is in favor of the Borrower and its 
permitted successors and assigns. 
 
Guaranteed Obligations .  D&C Guar antee unconditionally, absolut ely and irrevocably guaran tees the 
prompt payment in f ull when due, o f all pr esent and future obligations a nd liabilities of all kinds of the D&C 
Contractor to the Beneficiary arising o ut of the D&C Contract and the Interface Agre ement in accordance with the 
terms of the D&C Contra ct and the Inte rface Agreement together with all re asonable costs and expenses relating to 
the enforcement of the D&C Guarantee, except that the guarantor is not liable to pay costs and expenses when no 
payment under the guarantee is due.  The guarantee is a payment guarantee and not  a performance guarantee, and 
therefore the D&C Contr actor Guarantor is liable for payment obliga tions of the D&C Contractor under the D&C 
Contract (including liabilities under  the indemnities, delay liquidated damages and termination liabilities) but is n ot 
obligated to perform D&C Work not performed by the D&C Contractor.  
 
Maximum Liability.  T he maximum aggregate liability of the D&C Contractor Guarantor is subject to the 
same liability cap under the D&C Contract, including the exceptions thereto.   
   
93 
Setoff and Counte rclaims.  The D&C Contractor Guarantor will have the benefit of all setoffs, and the 
right to assert all counter claims and other defenses to  which the D&C Contractor Guarantor or the D&C Contractor 
is e ntitled und er the D&C Cont ract or  Interface Agreement, except for defenses ar ising out of t he bankruptcy or 
insolvency of the D&C Contractor. 
 
No Obligation to Pursue  Other Remedies First .  The Borrower is not requ ired to first pursue or ex haust 
other remedies or rig hts against the D&C Contractor or resort to  any other security or collateral bef ore pursuing its 
rights under the D&C Guarantee.   
 
Limitations on Transfer  and Assignment.  The D&C Contractor Guarantor may assign the guarantee to a 
successor whi ch is the upstream North A merican holding company for the D&C  Contractor and other North 
American affiliated companies, provided that such entity has financial strength at least equal to the financial strength 
rating of the Guarantor and th at the assignment complies with any requir ements of the  D&C Contractor Lenders' 
Direct Agreement.  The B orrower may assign the D&C Guarantee to any affiliate, in conjunction with a perm itted 
assignment of the D&C Contra ct, to the Dist rict as security for the obligations of the Borrower under the P roject 
Agreement and to the Collate ral Agent as security or in  connection with the enforcement of such se curity.  In all 
other cases the guarantee may be assigned by a party wi th the conse nt of the other p arty, not  to be unreasonably 
withheld or delayed.

ASSET MANAGEMENT CONTRACT 
 
 
On March 7, 2022, the Borrower e ntered into the Asset Management Cont ract with the Asset Management 
Contractor, pursuant to which substantially all of the D&C Period A sset Management Services, Asset Management 
Services and the Expansion Work are required to be performed by the Asset Management Contractor. 
Principal Rights and Responsibilities of the Asset Management Contractor and Borrower 
 
Pursuant to the Asset Managem ent Co ntract, the Borrower has granted to t he Asse t Management 
Contractor, the exclusive right to, subject to the terms  and conditions of the Asset Management  Contract, undertake 
the Asset Management Services, in accordance with the ter ms of the Asset Management  Contract.  Fo r the purpose 
of perfor ming its obligations under the Asset Management Contract only, the Asset Man agement Contractor will, 
subject to the  terms of the Asset Manag ement Contract, have the right to ent er onto ( and engage in the activitie s 
contemplated in the Asset Management Contract) each Project Site. 
Asset Management Work. The Asset Management Services includes the D&C Period Asset Managem ent 
Services, th e Asset Management Period Ass et M anagement Services and  the Expan sion Work. The  D&C Period 
Asset Management S ervices include asset m anagement, administration, and rehabilitation of the Existing Street 
Light Network required to be performed during the D&C P eriod following the achieveme nt of the first N TP3. The 
Asset Management Period Ass et Management S ervices include any and all as set management, administration, and 
rehabilitation of the Improved Street Light N etwork, as described in the Tech nical Provisions and in the Interface 
Agreement.  The Asset Management Period Asset Management Services are requi red to be provided for each St reet 
Light Bundle from the Street Light Bundle  Substantial Completion Date and fo r each Smart City Bund le from th e 
Smart City Bundle Substantial Completion Da te, in each case until the end of the term.  The Asse t Manag ement 
Contractor's scope of work also includes all Expansion Work required to be per formed pursuant to the terms of the  
Project Agreement and the Asset Management Contractor's obligations pursuant to the Interface Agreement. 
In addition to its other obligations in relation to the performance o f the Asset Management Work, the Asset 
Management Contractor is required to perfo rm services in accordance with the Asset Management Plan from time to 
time ac cepted b y the Dis trict and the Borrower.  The Asset M anagement Contractor is required to carry out  the 
Asset Management Services in accordance w ith Good  Industry Practice, all app licable Law , all Gover nmental 
Approvals, and the Technical Provisions and all other requirements of the Asset Management Contract. 
Hazardous Mate ri
 als. Except as otherw ise expressly provide d in the Asset Man agement Contract, the 
Asset Management Contractor is solely responsible for the management, treatment, h andling, storage, moni toring, 
remediation, removal, transport and disposal of any Hazardous Materials in, under o r on any Project Site that are 
encountered at all tim es from a nd after Project  Final Com pletion except to the extent  such re lease is a D&C 
Contractor Hazardous Mater ials Release.  At  all times during t he Asset Management Term, the As set Management 
Contractor is solely responsible for the management, treatment, handling, storage, monitoring, remediation, removal, 
transport, and disposal of any Asset Management Contractor Hazardous Materials Release.  The Asset Management 
Contractor is (without accepting or assuming responsibility under any applicable Law) responsible for obtaining and 
maintaining all Governmental Approvals relating to any Remedial Action and is solely r esponsible for co mpliance 
with all G overnmental Appro vals an d applicable Law concerning or rela ting to Remedial Action related to 
Hazardous Materials for  which it is res ponsible. See "APPENDIX D—SUMMARY  OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS 
OF THE ASSET MANAGEMENT C ONTRACT—Pr incipal Rights and Responsibilities of the Asset Management 
Contractor and the Borrower—Haz ardous Materials." 
Payments to the Asset Management Contractor 
From the Financial Closing Date to the P roject Final Completion Date, the Borrower is required to pay to 
the Asset Managemen t Contractor the AM Mobiliz ation Payments as calculated and earned by  the Ass et 
Management Contractor.  The AM Mobi lization Payments total $24,4 30,822 su bject to adjustment for 
Unavailability Deductions and Noncompliance Deductions.  T he Asset Man agement C ontractor is required to 
submit invoices to the Borrower on a monthly basis, provided that the Borrower has no obligation to pay the invoice 
earlier than the 5th Business Day of the immediately subsequent month.   
95 
From the later of the P roject Final  C ompletion Date and the Guaranteed Proj ect Final C ompletion Date 
until the Asset Management Termination Date, the Borrower is required to pay to the Ass et Management Contractor 
the Total Post P roject Final Completion Period Payme nts as ca lculated and earn ed by the  Asset Management 
Contractor, provided that if th e Project Final Comp letion Date occurs before Guaranteed Project F inal Completion 
Date, no Total Post Pr oject Final Completion Period P ayment is required to be made in the month in which the 
Project F inal Completion Date occurs if the  Borrower is re quired to pay an AM Mobiliz ation Pa yment in such 
month.  The Total Post P roject Final Completion Period Payments is $839,873, Indexed per month and pro-rated for 
partial months, subject to  adjustment in re spect of Unavailability Deductions, Nonc ompliance D eductions, 
Deductions and/or Energy Performance Charges.  The Asset Manag ement Contractor is required to submit invoices 
monthly in arears, provided that the Borrower is not required to make payment to the Asset Management Contractor 
until the later of the  date that is 45 days after  the Borrower's receipt of a proper invoice an d two Business Days of 
receipt of the corresponding quarterly Availability Payment from the District pursuant to the Project Agreement. 
To the extent the Asset Management C ontractor has incurred a monetary amou nt of de ductions associated 
with Unavailability Deductions, Noncompliance D eductions, D eductions and/or  Energy Performance Charges that 
exceeds the AM Mobilization Payments or Total Post Project Final Completion Pe riod Payments, as applicable, for 
any given month, the Asset Management Contractor is r equired to pay the difference between t he total amount of 
monetary deductions incurred by the Asset Managem ent Contractor in the preced ing month and that month's AM 
Mobilization Payment or Total Post Project Final Completion Period Payments, as applicable, to the Borrower. 
Rectification of Nonconforming Work and Defects  
Subject to certain limitations in the As set Manag ement Contract, the  Asset Management Contractor is 
required to recti fy all Nonconforming Work a nd Defects.  Notwithstanding any provision to the contrary in the 
Asset Management Contract, the Asset Management C ontractor has no obligation with resp ect to an y Defect in a 
Structural Element of the Improved Street Light Network, for which the D&C Contractor is or would be liable under 
the D&C Contract but for the expiry of the Latent Defect Period. 
Energy Performance Charge  
Pursuant to the Asset Management Con tract, the Asset Managemen t Contractor acknowledges and a grees 
that it is liable for the payment of the Energy Performance Charge in respect of the Project. 
Disputes 
Except for di sputes related to e quivalent project relief, all disputes arising un der the A sset Management 
Contract will be resolved pursuant to  the Dispute Resolution Procedures.  If a d ispute arises, a party to the d ispute 
shall have the right to request a meeting for  resolution of the dispute among officers designated for such purp ose. 
See "APPENDIX D—SUMMARY OF CERTA IN PROVISIONS OF THE ASSET MANAGEMENT 
CONTRACT—Eq uivalent Project Relief."   
In the event the parties  f ail to resolve the dispute pursuant to the meeting  of officers, and timely resolution 
of the dispute is required to meet any schedule relevant to the execution by a party of its ob ligations under the Asset 
Management Contract, or the dis pute involves a claim the monetary value of which is estimated to be less than or 
equal to $1,000,000, then either party may  be written notice to the other require that the dispute be resolved on  an 
expedited basis  in accordance with a bindin g fast track arbitration proce ss.  The arbitrator is required to issue a 
written decision as soon as pra cticable an d in any event within twenty (20) days of the  delivery of the notice of  
dispute, which decision shall be final and binding.  All other disputes shall be determined pursuant to arbitration.

Management Contract claimed, could not  reasonably be mitigated or reco vered without material

The Asset Management Contractor must relea se, defend, indemnify and hold harmless the  Borrower 
Indemnified Parties and the Indemnified Par ties on demand from and agai nst any and all liability to third parties fo r 
Losses arising fro m (a) d eath or personal injury, (b ) loss of or damage to any Borrower I ndemnified Party or

Indemnified Party, which may arise out  of, or in consequen ce of, a ny or all of the f ollowing: any actual or alleged  
failure by the Asset Management Contractor to comply  with, observe or perform any of  the covenants, obligat ions, 
agreements, terms or conditions in the Project Documents or any actual or alleged breach by t he Asset Management 
Contractor of its representations or warranties set forth in the Asset Management Contract. 
 
The Asset Management Contractor will not be responsible or obliged to indemnify a Borrower Indemnified 
Party or Ind emnified Party with respect to any liability or Losses to the extent that the  same arise as a direct result 
of: (a) a Compensation  Event, Relief Event, or Borrower Default (Asset Management Contract); (b) the presence of 
Hazardous Materials on any Project Site for which the D istrict is, pursuant to the Project Agreement, or for whi ch 
the Borrower is pursuant to the Asset Management C ontract, deemed to be the sole generator and arranger , but only 
to the extent  that the relevan t Los s do es not arise as a direct result of the negl igence of the Asset Man agement 
Contractor or the Asset Ma nagement Contractor failing to comply with the terms of the Asset Management 
Contract; (c) any non-performance by a Borrower Indemnified Party of its obligations under the Asset M anagement 
Contract, or any non-performance by an Indem nified Party of its obligations u nder the Project Agreement or any 
violation of applicable Law by any Borrower Indemnified Party or Indemnified Party; or  (d) the fraud, negligence, 
recklessness, bad faith or willful misconduct of a Borrower Indemnified Party or Indemnified Party.

Limitation on Asset Management Contractor's Liability 
Notwithstanding a ny other provisions of the As set Management Contract, except in the case of termination 
for an Asset Management Contractor Default, or unless re-set as provided in the Asset Management Contract, to the 
extent permitted by applicable Law , t he Asset M anagement Contractor's maximum total liability under the Asset  
Management Contract and the Interface Agreement, is, subject to the following exclusions, limited to the 75% of the 
annual average forecast ed Asset Management Service Payments (incl usive of the  l ife-cycle costs) (index-linked) 
over the period commencing o n the Financial Closing Date and ending on the Expir y Date as sh own in the i n the 
Base Case Financial Model or the most recent update to the Base Case Financial Model.  In the case of a termination 
of the Asset Management Co ntract for an Asset Management C ontractor Default, the liability o f the A sset 
Management Contractor is, subject to the following exclusions, limited to the 75 % of the a nnual average forecasted 
Asset Management Service Payments (inclusive of the life -cycle costs) (index-linked) over the p eriod commencing 
on the Financial Closing Date and end ing on the Expiry Date as shown in the in the Base Case Financial Model or 
the most re cent update to the Base Case Financial Mod el. The liability caps are excl usive of:  ( A) any insurance 
proceeds received pursuant to policies of insurance to the extent required under the Asset Management Contract and 
any liability for Losses that would have been covered by such insurance up to the  amount of the required insurance 
and deductibles but in respect of which insurance proceeds are not available due to a failure on the part of the Asset 
Management Contractor, or a ny Asset Managem ent Contractor-Related Entity, as applicable, to comply with  its 
insurance obligations as set forth in the Asset Management Contract; (B) any claim by the  Borrower in respect of 
fraud, gross negligence, willful misconduct and deliberate acts of wrongdoing by the Asset Management Contractor 
or any Asset Management Contractor-Related Entity; (C) liabilities arising  under any Encumbrance cr eated o r 
cau
sed by t he Asset Management Contractor or parties for whom the Asset Management Contractor is responsible;  
(D) any claim for personal injury or death; (E) third party claims in tort against the Asset Management Contractor to 
the extent such cla ims arise f rom the n egligence or b reach of contract of  the Asset Management Contractor or 
parties for whom  the Asset Managemen t Contracto r is  responsible; (F) third party claims in tort  against the 
Borrower (excluding claim s by the  Lenders, the Borrower and B orrower-Related E ntity(ies) and the D&C 
Contractor and D&C  Contractor-Related Ent ity(ies)); (G) third party claims in tort against the  Lenders o r a 
Borrower-Related Entity for which indemnity is sought against the Borrower to the extent such claims arise from the 
negligence or breach of contract of th e Asset Management Contractor or parties for whom the Ass et Management 
Contractor is responsible; (H) third party claims relating to intellectual property to the extent such claims are caused 
by the Asset Management Contractor; (I) amounts paid to the Borrower by the Asset Management Contractor to the 
extent tha t same ar e sub sequently reco vered by the Asset Management Contractor from th e D&C Contractor 
pursuant to the Interface Agreement; (J) amounts paid by the Asset Management Contractor which are subsequently 
repaid to the A sset Management Contractor by the Borrower, as required pursuant to any bankruptcy or insolvency 
legislation; (K) e xpenses incurred by the Borro wer in enforcing a claim under the Asset Management Contract; (L) 
any amount paid to t he Borrower by the Asset M anagement Contractor to the extent t hat the same is su bsequently 
recovered by the Asset Management Contractor fr om the Borrower o r any Borrower-Related Entity; (M) a ny 
interest, penalties a nd f ees incurred by the Borrower due to unpaid am ounts owing fr om the Asset M anagement 
Contractor to th e Borrower; and (N) fines a nd p enalties under statue or any reasonable cost s incurred by Borrower  
or any  Borrower-Related En tity and fo r which Asset Manageme nt Contractor is liable to the  Borrower o r a 
Borrower-Related Entity in complying with statutory obligations or any order of a Governmental Entity.  
If at any time, the A sse t Management Contra ctor's a ggregate liability ( except in the case of early 
termination or termination for an Asset Management Co ntractor Default) is equal to or exceeds the amount of  the 
Asset Management Contractor Li ability Cap, the Asset Management Co ntractor may deliver w ritten notice to the 
Borrower advising that it wishes to re -set the Asset Management Contractor Liability Cap, by agreeing that Existing 
Asset Management Liabilities no longer count t owards the Asset Management Contractor Liability C ap. The Asset 
Management Contractor may exercise this right twice as of right and thereafter with the consent of the Borrower. 
Noncompliance and Unavailability Events 
 
The Asset Management Contract contains a regime p ursuant to which Deductions are assessed agai nst the 
Asset Management Contractor, based on a l ist of breaches or failures in p erformance of obligations of the Asset 
Management Contractor identifie d the Asset Management Contract. Unavailability Events and N oncompliance 
Events include, but are not limited  to, failure to provide certa in notifications,  failure to observe or compl y with 
various project del ivery protocols or breach of performanc e standards. The Asset Management Cont ractor has the   
100 
benefit of certain c ure periods and/or grace periods for cert ain breaches and failures which are based on the typ e of 
breach or failure. T he Asset Management Contractor is required to notify the District, with a copy to the Borrower 
of any N oncompliance Event related to t he A sset Management Work or the actions or om issions of the As set 
Management Contractor.  The District is permitted to notify the Borrower o f Noncom pliance Events and 
Unavailability Failures and the Borrower shall notify the Asset Management Contractor of such where related to the 
Asset Management Wo rk or any action or omission of the Asset Managem ent Contractor.  Noncomplian ce Points, 
Noncompliance Deductions and Unavailability Deductions a pplicable under the Asset Management  Contract shall 
be determined in accordance with the terms of the Project Agreement. 
 
The Ass et Management C ontractor is liab le for Noncompliance Points,  and associated D eductions, 
resulting from Noncompliance Event s related to the Asset Management Work or  actions or omissions of the Asset 
Management Contractor.  The number of Non compliance Points assessed under the Asset Management Contract is 
required to be the same as the number of Noncompliance Points assessed pu rsuant to the Project Agreement, to the 
extent related to any Asset Manageme nt Work or actions or omissions of the As set Management Contractor.  The 
Asset Management Contractor is liable for Una vailability Deductions resulting from Unavailability Events related to 
the Asset Management Work or act ions or omissions of the Asset Management Contractor.  The amount of 
Unavailability Deductions assessed under the Asset Manag ement Contract is required to be  the same as the amount 
of Unavailability Deductions asses sed p ursuant to the Project Agreement, to  the extent related to any Asset 
Management Work or actions or omissions of the Asset Management Contractor. 
 
Handback 
Upon the expiration or earlier ter mination of the Project Agreement, the Asset Management Contractor is 
required to transfer the Project to the Borrower or at the direc tion of the Borrower the District, at no char ge to the 
Borrower, in the condition and meeting all residual life requiremen ts s pecified i n t he Project Agreement. In 
connection with such hand back, t he Borrower, in conjunction with the Independent Engineer, w ill conduct 
inspections of all of the compone nts of the Project in order to c onfirm that the r esidual life requirements ha ve been 
fulfilled and to determine the renewal work to be performed prior to handback. 
The Asset Management Contractor is responsible for the funding of the H RA, provided that the  Asset 
Management Contractor may  provide the Hand back Letter of Credit in lie u of  funding t he HRA.  The  Asset 
Management Contractor is also entitled to the benefit of amounts released from the HRA where the aggregate of the 
balance of the H RA and the undra wn value of any H andback Letter of Credit exceeds  the Handback Reserve 
Amount required unde r t he Project Agreement and is entitled in certain circumsta nces to withdrawal of amounts  
from the H RA or reduction of t he H andback Letter of Credit to pay for Renew al Work. See "APPENDIX D—
SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE ASSET MANAGEMENT CONTRACT—Hand back."

Transfer Restrictions 
 
The Asset Management Contractor is not permitted to assign or transfer any of its ri ghts or o bligations 
under the Asset Management Contract without the written consent of the Borrower, which the As set Management 
Contractor acknowledges shall not be granted unless permitted pursuant to the Finance Documents. 
 
Restrictions on Change in Ownership 
 
The Asset Management Contractor shall ensure that there will be no sale, transfer, or other disposal of an y 
direct or indirect legal, benefic ial, equitable or other ownership interest in the Asset Management Contractor which, 
when taken together wit h any other such transfer or d isposal during the relevant period gives rise to a Change of 
Control of the Asset M anagement Contractor; grant of any s ecurity interest, lien or other encumbrance over any 
legal, beneficial, equitable or other direct or indire ct ownership interest in the A sset Management Contractor giving 
rise to  a Change of Control of the Asset Management Cont ractor; or agreement, whether or not sub ject to any 
condition precedent or subsequent, to do any of the foregoing, without the prior wri tten consent of the Borrower, 
such consent no t to be withheld or d elayed where ( a) the Asset Management Contractor can d emonstrate to the 
satisfaction of the Borrower, acting reasonably, that the Asset Management Contractor will have a level of financial 
capacity, resources, and ability to perform the Asset Management Work after the Change of Control Transaction that 
is equal to or greater than the le vel of finan cial capacity, resources, and ability to p erform the Asset Manag ement 
Work held by the Asset Man agement Contractor before the Change of Contr ol Transaction and (b) the Change of 
Control Transaction will not have a Material Adverse Effect. 
 
Re
lationship Between District Determination and Borrower Determination 
 
Where, i n a ccordance with the provisions  o f the Ass et Management Contract, a determin ation, a ction, 
negotiation, decision, discretion, election or other thing is made, taken, done, exercis ed or elected by the Borrower 
in respect of a matter in accordance with a deter mination, action, negotiation, decision, discretion, election or other 
thing made, taken, done, exercised or elected by the District in respect of th e same matter pursuant of th e Project 
Agreement, to the extent that Asset Manag ement Contractor disagrees w ith such determination, actio n, negotiation, 
decision, discretion, electio n or other thing, Asset Management Contractor's sole remedy, to the extent permitted in 
accordance with the terms of the Asset Management Contract is to b ring a Pass Through Claim , a claim against the 
District pursuant to the Project Agreement.

the Project Agreement. See "APPENDIX D—SUMMARY O F CERT AIN PROVISIONS OF THE ASSET

Agreement Claim will not be subject to any dispute resolution procedures under the Asset Management Contract or 
otherwise progressed, and all applicable time pe riods with respect to the r esolution of such cl aim under the Asset

INTERFACE AGREEMENT 
 
The Int erface Agreement was ent ered in to by t he Borrower, the D&C Co ntractor and the Asset  
Management Contractor on March 7, 2022. Certain terms of the Interface Agreement will be amended on or prior to 
the Financial Closing Date and this Official Statement sets out  the terms of the Interface Agreement as will be in 
effect on the Financial Closing Date.  
Pursuant to the I nterface Agreement, the Borrower covenants in favor of each of the D&C Contracto r and 
the Asset M anagement Contractor that it shall carry out i ts obligations under e ach of the D&C Con tract and Asset 
Management Contract in accordance with the provisions the reof. Each of the D&C Contrac tor and the Asset 
Management Contractor covenants i n favor of the ot her an d in  favor of the Borrower that it shall ca rry out i ts 
obligations under its respective subcontract in acco rdance with the provisions thereof . For avoida nce of doubt, a 
breach of  the Int erface Agreement  shall not of itself constitute  a breach of the D&C Co ntract or the Asset 
Management Contract, an d a breach of the D&C Contract or the Asset Management Contract shall not itself 
constitute a breach of the Interface Agreement. 
General  
The Int erface Agreement includes certain commitments  o f t he D &C Contr actor and Asset Management 
Contractor, including, t o consult and coope rate with one  another in the performance of  their activities under the 
D&C Contract or Asset Management Contract, as applicable, to share certain information related to the performance 
of the D&C Contract or Asset Management Contract, as applicable, to coordinate their respective subcontractors and 
employees to work reasonably coo peratively in  carrying out their obligations und er the D&C Con tract or Asset 
Management Contract, as applicable, a nd to not permit any act or omission on the part of th eir subcontractors or 
employees that would cause or constitute a breach by the other contractor of ap plicable permits, licens es and 
approvals required by such other contractor for p erformance of the D&C Contract or Asset Management Contract, 
as applicable. 
Limit of Liab ility 
The limit of liability of the D&C C ontractor under the D&C Contrac t forms part of  and is subject to the  
D&C Contractor's maximum a ggregate liability to the Borro wer under the D&C Contract. T he limit of liability  of 
the Asset Management Contractor under the Asset Management Contract forms part of and is subject to the Asset 
Management Contractor's maximum aggregate liability to the Borrower under the Asset Management Contract. 
Delay Damages 
The I nterface Agreem ent specifi es certain delay damages t hat will accrue to the Asset Management 
Contractor in the event that the  D &C Contractor is r esponsible for a delay in the  achievement of Mile stone 1 , 
Milestone 2 o r Pro ject F inal Compl etion w hich are capped at an amou nt approximately equal to the maximum 
amount of liquidated damages that the D&C Contractor may become liable to pay in the event of  such a delay. The 
Interface Agreement specifies certain del ay damages t hat will a ccrue to the Asset Management Contractor in the 
event that the D&C Contractor fai ls to ach ieve Pro ject Final Completion by the G uaranteed Project Final 
Completion Date capped at the amount of $27,996 (Indexed) per day. 
Construction Period 
The Asset Manag ement Contractor is gra nted cert ain informational rights a nd rights to revi ew certain 
materials and attend certain meetings related to t he construction of the Project.  Th e Asset Management Contractor 
is further granted a right to access the Project Sites Un der Conversion for performance of certain activities under the 
Asset Management Contract a nd, subj ect to the terms of the Interf ace Agreement, shall be permitted to observe, 
document and record the construc tion of the Proje ct.  The Parties acknowledge th at the D&C Contra ctor shall be 
solely responsible to the Borrower for achieving Project Final Completion. 
   
105 
Defects and Latent Defects 
The D&C Contractor is responsibl e, pursuant to the D&C Contract , for fully and timely correcting, at its 
sole cost, any and all Defects and for fully and timely correcting, at its sole cost,  or for the cost of fully and timely 
correcting, Laten t Defects, in eac h case, in accordance with the D&C Contract during the appl icable W arranty 
Period. The  Asset Management Cont ractor shall, for and on behalf of the D&C Contractor, in return for payment 
from the D&C Contractor (subject to a cost split set forth in  the Interface Agreement), fully and timely correct any 
and all Defects and L atent Defects in accordance with the D&C Contract and the Interface Agreement during the 
applicable Warranty Period, s ubject to the provisi ons of a defects p rotocol and work flow process as set out in the 
Interface Agreement .  Th e D&C Contractor s hall als o b e respons ible for all Dedu ctions i ncurred by the  A sset 
Management Contractor as a result of  a Defect or  a Latent Defect.  The D &C Contractor shall h ave no liability to 
the Asset Management Contractor for Defects or Latent  Defects for which a not ice was not received prior to the 
expiration of the Warranty Period. 
Warranties 
The D&C Contractor is required to o btain warranties for the LED Luminaires, drivers, and nodes which are 
required to i nclude a w arranty against Def ects for  a period of  10 yea rs be ginning on t he Street Light Bundle 
Substantial Comple tion Date for the LE D Lumi naires in each of the applicable Street Light Bundles; a nd the 
warranties for the LED Luminaires is require to inclu de a provi sion about the accuracy of th e target lifespan of the 
LED and p ermit an evaluation of  the lighting degr adation r ate, in conf ormance with LM80 – TM21 tests. The 
warranties are required provide for r eplacement if the LED Lum inaires do not meet such lifespan  and degradation 
tests.  T he warranties are required to be stated to be for the additi onal benefit of the District, the Borrower and the 
Asset Management Contractor. 
Adverse Energy Consumption Adjustments 
The D&C Contractor is required to ensu re that the RMCS t o be provi ded in res pect of the Proje ct meets 
certain specified requirements and includes certain reporting obligations in respect of energy consumption.  Once all 
Bundles have been submitted by D&C Contractor and approved by Asset Management Contractor, D&C Contractor 
is req uired to submit a  final report reco nciling certain actual electricity consumption a mounts and expect ed 
electricity consumption amounts. S uch report is required to als o include a  calculation of the Energy Performance 
Charge if on e is owed by D&C Contr actor to Asset Management C ontractor.  Within sixty (60) days of D&C 
Contractor's receipt  of A sset Management C ontractor's written acceptance of the final report, D&C C ontractor is 
required to pay to the Asset Ma nagement Cont ractor the net pr esent value of fifteen (15) years of the Energy 
Performance Charge calculated in accordance with the Interface Agreement using a discount rate of 4% per year. 
Allocation of Liab ility 
The Borrower is required to act reasonably in  allocating responsibility for any damage or loss, including 
Deductions, accrued under the  Project A greement between the Borrower, the A sset Managem ent Co ntractor and 
D&C Contractor.  The determinati on of the Borrower in respect of  such amounts shall be bi nding on the parties 
pending settlement of any dispute related thereto. 
Performance Security and Certain Claims 
Where the D &C Contr actor or the Asse t Management Contracto r has a mat erial, valid, enforceable and 
final non-appealable claim against the other contractor under the Interface Agreement, which also forms the basis of 
a v alid and  en forceable claim by Borrower against such other c ontractor, the  Borrower is required to enforce its 
rights a gainst such other contractor under t he D&C C ontract or Asset  Ma nagement Contra ct, as applicable, and 
subject to the rights of the Collateral Agent, enforce any performance security available to it.   
106 
Disputes 
The I nterface Agreement includes pro visions fo r dispute  resolution by negotiation and arbitration, 
including a fast track arbitration process. 
Interface Committee 
The parties to the Interface Agreement agree to establish an interface committee, which shall be a forum for 
dialog as to issue s and concerns relati ng to any  conflict, potential c onflict and other concerns between the parties  
with respect to the Pr oject A greement, D&C Contra ct an d Asset Management Contr act.  The c ommittee shall 
operate by unanimous consent and shall not have the right to alter the parties righ ts' under the Interface Agreement, 
Project Agreement, D&C Contract or Asset Management Contract. 
Scope Split 
The D&C Contractor and the Asset Management Contractor agree that all  Work required to be performed 
under the Project A greement, including but not limited to t he requi rements s et out in  Technical Provi sions, are 
required to be performe d either by the D&C Contractor or by the Asset Management Contractor in accordance with 
the responsibility matrix  set out in the Interface  A greement. Any Dispute in relation to al location of  Work 
responsibility shall be re solved in accordance with the dispute resolu tion pro cedure set out in  the Interface 
Agreement; provided, however, that the D &C Contractor and the Asset Management Contractor agree that, except 
as explicitly set forth in the D&C Co ntract or the Asset Managemen t Contract, in no event shal l Borrower be 
responsible for any D&C Work or Asset Management Services.

SOURCE OF FUNDS FOR PAYMENTS BY THE DISTRICT, DISTRICT BUDGETING AND 
APPROPRIATIONS PROCESS UNDER DISTRICT LAW 
 
Sources of Funds for Payments under the Project Agreement 
The District has multiple potential sources of funds for payments due by it under the Project  Agreement 
(including the Milestone  P ayments and the Availability Payment s), but currently anticipates that all Milestone 
Payments and Availability Payments will be ma de using a combination of (a ) federal-aid funds made av ailable by 
FHWA through the Federal-Aid Highway Program and ( b) local District funds from DDOT’s capital and operating 
budgets. The District's fiscal year 2022 budget includes a capital budget for DDOT  of approximately $652 million 
and an operating budget of approximately $170 million. Further details on these funding sources are set out below.  
 
 Funds for the payment of amounts due under the Project Agreement in fisc al year 20 22 have been made 
available to DDOT pursuant to the District’s budgeting and allocation processes. 
 
 Federal-Aid Funds & Local Match 
 
 The District r eceives federal rev enues to fund speci fic expenditures incurred by the District, includ ing 
funds administered by FHWA for the Federal- Aid H ighway Program. FHWA  reimburses eligible expenses for 
approved District projects according to cost -sharing formulas established by FHWA and DDOT.  Any federal funds 
received by the District under the Federal -Aid Hig hway Progr am must first be directed  towards repayment of 
existing Grant Anticipation Revenue Vehicle ( "GARVEE") d ebt servi ce relating to  the Eleventh S treet Br idge 
Project and the South Capi tol Street Corridor Project. As detailed in the  table below, the total amount of GARVEE 
debt service currently paid by the District per year is approximately $27.9 million.  
 
DDOT c urrently oversees the District’s existing streetlight network, which is comprised of, among other 
assets, approxi mately 75,000 street an d alley lights.  DDOT intends to direct f ederal-aid funds currently used to 
support its streetligh t c apital maintenance p rogram to support payments due under the Project Agreement. The 
current cost-sharing formula for District streetlight assets is 58% federal-aid funds, with the remaining 42% funded 
via local fu nds from the District. The anticipa ted cost-sharing formula for the Project is 48.49% federal -aid funds, 
with the r emaining 51.51% to be funded via local funds from the District. DDOT and FHWA will enter into a 
project agreement that sets forth the final cost-sharing formula. 
 
The tables bel ow p rovide further details r egarding the level of federal funding the D istrict has received 
from FHWA in recent years and the District’s GARVEE debt service obligations. No assurance can be given that the 
District’s federal highway funding will continue to increase or remain at the same levels in the future. 
 
 Th
e District’s Highway Trust Fund (the "DHTF") may be used to pay the local match for obligated federal -
aid projects. In a ddition, the DHTF may be used to match future federal transportation funds, wi th the remaining 
balance used for capit al improvements to local streets  and roads. Allocations from th e DHTF can only be made to 
individual projects that are included in the District’s approved six-year transportation improvement plan. The DHTF 
consists of, among other things, loc al dedicated tax collections used to support i nvestments in the District’s 
transportation network. The local revenue component of the DHTF is funded from  a variety of local sources which, 
in part, include District funds derived from parking taxes, rights o f way revenues, parking meter revenues, and th e 
District’s tax and local transportation surcharge on motor vehicle fuel (as further described in D.C. Code § 47-2301). 
The total DHTF bud get for fiscal y ear 2022 is $244, 245,248. For fi scal year 2022, the Dis trict anticipates that 
FHWA will make $2 12,539,000 of fede ral-aid funds avail able for DHTF projects, with an approved local match 
budget of $31,705,647 (bas ed on estimates of loc al DHTF  revenues). The District’s Office of the Chief F inancial 
Officer is responsible for forecasting revenues for the D istrict government, which such for ecasts are published on a  
quarterly basis and available at:  https://cfo.dc.gov/page/quarterly-revenue-estimates. The District’s fiscal year 2022 
budget reflects a local match totaling approximately $198 million for fiscal year 2022 through fiscal year 2027.

Overview of Federal-Aid Funding History  
 
Obligation Authority 
 
The table below details the obligation authority made available to the District from federal fiscal year 2012 
through federal fiscal y ear 20 21. The  provision of obligation authority is part of the  federal au thorization and 
appropriation process for the federal -aid highway program. Obligation aut hority, which is apportione d to states on 
an annual basis, sets t he upper limit on the federal gove rnment’s commitment to pay, through reimbursement s, the 
federal shar e of eligible expenditures on approved projects. The obli gation authority in a curr ent year, plus prior 
years’ obligation authority oblig ated but not yet expend ed, determines the maximum am ount of  federal-aid funds 
that a state may recei ve under  the federal- aid highway program. Annual obligation authority is not  a direct 
representation o f the amount of reimbursements a state will receive un der t he federal -aid hig hway program in a 
given year due to lags in spending and special appropriations. Obli gation authority levels will determine over time 
the amount of reimbursements that a state may receive.

     District’s Financial Resources and Annual Budget 
 
The most recent general purpose financial statem ents of the Di strict, which can be  found in the District’s 
Annual Comprehensive Financial Report ( "ACFR") for th e f iscal year ended September  30, 2021. The following 
portion of the A CFR for f iscal year 2021 is incor porated herein by referen ce: the info rmation under the heading 
"Financial Section," from pages 21-192, inclusive (collectively, the "F iscal Year 2021 Financial Statements").  The 
District’s ACFR for fiscal year 2021 and the Fiscal Year 2021 Financial Statements can be found on th e District’s 
investor relations website at www.DCbo nds.com, on EMMA or by registering with and logging onto the websit e of 
Digital Assurance Certification, L.L.C. ("DAC") at www.da cbond.com. DAC is t he disclosure dissemination agent 
for the District.   
The District’s annual budget is comp rised of ( a) the budget for the forthcoming fiscal year, (b ) a five-year 
financial plan (which covers the curren t fiscal year, t he upcoming fiscal year, and three add itional years), and (c) a 
six-year capital improvement plan (which cov ers the upcoming fiscal year and five ad ditional years). The District’s 
fiscal year 2022 budget includes a capital b udget for DDOT o f approximately $652 million and an operating budget 
of approxima tely $ 170 million. The  capital improvement plan incorporated in to the District’ s fiscal year 2022  
budget includes future streetlight capital maintenance  expenditures, includin g t he Project. T he Pro ject is al so 
included in the District’s most recent Statewide Transportation Improvement Program for fiscal years 2021  through 
2024, which identifies transportation programs and projects to be funded with federal dollars. 
 
In addition  to federal-aid funds made avail able through the Federal -Aid Highway P rogram and funds 
available from the DHTF to support the local match fo r eligible federal-aid projects, the D istrict’s funding plan for 
the Project includes contributions from DDOT’s capital budget and o perating budget , as well as  pay-as-you-go 
funds for ca pital proj ect e xpenditures (which are supported by transfers from t he District’s general fund ). The 
Project is included in the Mayor’s proposed capital improvement p lan for fiscal years 2023 through 202 8 and is 
supported by funding from Fund 0330 (Local Transportation Revenue) of approximately $30 million and Fund 0331 
(Local Streets – PAYGO) of approximately $59. 4 million. DDOT will establish a distinct project within its capital 
budget for purposes of a dministering it s payment obligations under the Projec t Agreement . The Project  will be 
funded through the District’s annual budgeting and appropr iation process, as further described below, to match the 
District’s payment obligations under the Project Agreement.  
 
Th
e Mil estone Payments relating to the Smart City Work (th e t otal maximum amoun t of which is 
$4,150,000) will be funded by the District’s Office of the Chief Technology Officer.  
The District’s Budget Process 
The District of Columbia Home Rule Act  (Public Law 93-198; D.C. Official Code § § 1-201.01 et seq.), as 
amended by the Local B udget Autonomy Act of 2012 (D.C. La w 19-321; codified in  various sections of Chapter 2 
of Title I of the D.C. Official Code), proscribes the procedure for  the approval o f the annual budget for the District 
of Columbia government. Under Section 424(d)(5)(A) (D.C. Official Code § 1 -204.24d(5)(A)), the Chief Financ ial 
Officer prepares and submits to the Mayor and the Council annual estimates of all revenues of  the District ( without 
regard to the source of such revenues), including approved  revenues. The revenue estimates are binding on the 
Mayor and the Council for purposes of the annual budget to be submitted to Congress, except that the Mayor and the 
Council may base the bu dget on estima tes of revenues that are lower than those prepared b y the Chief Financial 
Officer. Under Section 442(a) (D.C . Official Code § 1-204(a)), the Mayor prepares and submits an annual budget to 
Council. In preparing the annual budget , the Mayor may  use a budget pre pared by the Chief Financial Officer f or 
this purpose under Section 424(d)(2) (D.C. Official Code § 1 -204.24d(2)). The Mayor m ay also prepare and  submit 
supplemental or deficiency budget recommendations to the Council from t ime to time, pu rsuant to Sect ion 442(c) 
(D.C. Official Code § 1-204.24(c)). A statement of justification must be included. 
 
Under Section  6 03(c) (D.C. Officia l Code § 1- 206.03(c)), the Mayor is required to submit a balanced  
budget and identify any tax incr eases that shall be required to balance the budget. The Council is req uired to adopt 
such tax increases to the extent the budget is appro ved. The annual budge t submitted shall i nclude, among other 
items, a multi- year plan for all agencies of the District g overnment (as r equired under Section 443 (D.C. Official 
Code § 1-204.43)) and multi-year capital improvements plan for all agencies of  the District government (as required 
under Section 444 (D.C. Official Code § 1-204.44)).   
111 
The Council must hold a public hea ring on the b udget submission and adopt a budget by two (2) separate 
acts pursuant to the time perio d required by Section 446 (D.C. Of ficial Code § 1 -204.46). The acts are entitled the 
"Local Budget Act " and the " Federal Portion Budget Request Act. " The Local Budget A ct requires two r eadings 
before the Council and the Fed eral Portio n Budget Request Act r equires only one  reading. Unlike o ther acts 
submitted to th e Mayor for signature, the Mayor may exercise a line- item veto under Section 404(f) (D.C. Offi cial 
Code § 1-204.04(f)). If an item or provision is disapproved, the Mayor  must attach to the act a statement of the item 
or provision that is disapproved and, w ithin the ten (10)-day period for approval or disapproval, return a copy of the 
act and statem ent with her ob jections to th e C ouncil. The Council has thirty ( 30) calendar days  to reenact a 
disapproved item or provis ion by a two -thirds vote of the  members of the Council p resent and voting. If an item or 
provision is reenacted, the Chairman submits it to Congress. If a disapproved item or provision is not returned to the 
Council in a timely manner, the Mayor shall  be deemed to have ap proved the item or provision, and the Ch airman 
will submit it to Congress. 
 
The Local Budget Act is submitted to Congre ss for a thirty  ( 30)-day peri od of review, like all other 
permanent legislation submitted under Home Ru le Act Section 602(c) (D.C. Official Code § 1- 206.02(c)). This act 
shall take effect upon the e xpiration of the 30 -day period (excluding Saturdays, Sunda ys, holidays, and any day on  
which neither House is in session because of an adjo urnment sine die, a re cess of more than  three days, or a n 
adjournment of more than three (3) days) beginning on the day s uch act is transmitted by the Chairman  to the 
Speaker of the House of Representatives and the President of the Senate, unles s during such thirty (30)-day period, 
there has  been enacted into l aw a j oint resolution disapproving such act . In any case in wh ich any  such j oint 
resolution disapproving such an act has, within such thirty (30)-day period, passed both Houses of Congress and has 
been transmitted to the P resident, such resolution, upon be coming law, subsequent to the expiration  of such 30-day 
period, shall be deemed to have repealed such act, as of the date such resolution becomes law. 
 
The Federal Portion Budget Request Act is submitt ed by the Mayor to the President for transmission to  
Congress. Unlike the Local Budget Act, the  Federal Portion Budget Request  Act does no t become effective after a 
period of congressional review; it never becomes District of Columbia local law . Ultimately, Congress appropriates 
all federal funds for the Distr ict by an act. This act may, but is not required to, include some or al l provisions of the 
Federal Portion Budget Request Act as transmitted by the District.  
 
The 
Mayor may prepare a supplemental or deficiency budget plan  under Home Rule Act Section 442(c) 
(D.C. Official Code § 1- 204.42(c)), which authorizes the Mayor to sub mit budget r ecommendations that are, in his 
or h er judgment, ne cessary on account of a law passed after budget transmi ssion or ot herwise justified  as i n the 
public interest. The process  for submitting a supplemental budget is  also included in Home Rule Act  Section 446 
(D.C. Official Code § 1 -204.46) and mirrors the st atutory requirements of submitting the annual  Local Budget Act. 
This includes requirements for a public hearing, submission to Congress, and Congressional review. 
 
The District of Columbia may o bligate and expend an increase in the amount of  funds authorized under  
Adjustments to Appropriations (D.C. Official C ode § 47- 369.01 e t seq.) only whe re the Chief Financial Officer 
certifies (a) the increase in revenue; and (b) that the use of the amounts  is not anticipated to have a negative impact 
on the long- term financial, fis cal, or economic health of the District. These funds  may b e increased  (a ) by an 
aggregate amount of not more  than 25%, in the case of amounts approv ed to be allocated as  "Other-Type Funds" in 
the annual Budget Request Act submi tted to Congress; and (b) by an aggregate amount of not more than 6 %, in the 
case of any other amounts approved to be allocated in the Lo cal Budget Act.  The amounts shall be obl igated and 
expended in accordan ce with laws e nacted by the Council in support o f e ach such obl igation and expen diture, 
consistent with the requirements of this s ubchapter. The amount s may  not be used to fund any agenci es of the 
District government operatin g under court -ordered receivers hip. The  amounts may not be obligated or expende d 
unless the Mayor has notified the Committees on Appropriations of the House of Representatives and the Senate no 
fewer than thirty (30) days in advance of the obligation or expenditure. 
The Federal Anti-Deficiency Act and the District of Columbia Anti-Deficiency Act 
The f ederal Anti -Def iciency Act (31 U.S.C. §§ 1341, 1342,  1349-1351, 1511-1519) and the Di strict of 
Columbia Anti- Deficiency A ct (D.C. Official Code §§ 47- 355.01 – 355.08 ) (collectively, the "Anti-Deficiency 
Acts" or "ADA") are the federal an d l ocal statu tes that ensure tha t District officials comply with feder al 
appropriations requirements governing District expenditu res. These statutes prohibit the District from o bligating or   
112 
expending funds in advance or in the excess of an appropriation available for t he purpose of the e xpenditure. 
Typically, this requires District age ncies to certify the a vailability of the full am ount of appropriated funds needed 
for a project prior to entering into an obligation, with these appropriated funds authorized by Congress in the annual 
federal appropriations act. 
   
Section 451(c ) of the Ho me R ule Act (D.C. Offi cial Code § 1- 204.51(c)) a lso au thorizes the District to 
enter into multi- year contracts where payments for future years’ performance will be made throug h f uture 
appropriations. If the needed ap propriations do not become av ailable, the District must termin ate the contract and 
pay t ermination costs from one of several specified s ources. To avoid violating the Anti- Deficiency Acts with 
respect to multi- year contracts, th e District must hav e appropr iated funds available to pay termination costs for a 
multi-year contract at the time the  contract is entered into, and must maintain fun ds in reserve for the payment of 
these costs throughout the pendency of the contract. 
    
To a ddress the issue of termination costs in the context of  a long-term public-private partn ership, which 
present commercial  and financial complexities that differ fr om the District’s traditional multi- year contracts, the 
District has included p rovisions in the Local Budget Acts for FY s 2019- 2022 that authorize the District t o pay  
termination cos ts for public-private partnershi p pr ojects from appropriations the District h as available for any 
purpose, except the District’s emergency and contingenc y reserve funds. This provision gives the District advance 
authority to reprogram  funds needed to pay t ermination costs f rom other part s of  the District budget. With this 
authorization, the District will have sufficient funds available to cover termination  costs, both at the time the Project 
Agreement is executed and throughout the ter m of the Project Agreement. Congress’s appropriation of the District’s 
budget, as described abov e, means that federal legislative approval is needed to permit the District to mak e funds 
Congress appropriated  for othe r purposes available for term ination cos ts. In the District’s view, Congress has 
provided this  approval by incorporating by reference the D istrict’s Local Budget Act, including the provisions 
relating to termination costs for public-private partnership projects, into the annual federal appropriations act.    
The District’s Debt Ceiling Act 
In 2009, the District passed the Limitation on Bor rowing and Establishment of the Operating Cash Reserve 
Act of 2008, effective March 25, 2009, as amended (D.C. Law 17 -360; D.C. Official Code §47-3 34 et seq.) ( the 
"Debt Ceiling Act" ), which limits the is suance o f any District general obligation bonds, Tre asury capital -project 
loans, income tax-supported revenue bonds, notes or other  debt instruments secured by revenues derived from taxes, 
fees, or other general revenues of the District,  or its agencies and authorities, pursuant to the District’s power to tax 
and impose fees, including tax increment financing bonds and payment -in-lieu o f taxes n otes, certificates o f 
participation and lease purchase f inancing obligations (collectively, with the exceptions noted in the Debt Ceiling 
Act, "Tax-Supported Debt ").  The District’s Debt Ceiling Act restricts the issuance of any Tax -Supported D ebt if 
such issuance would resul t in total debt service in the fisca l year of iss uance, or any of the five succeeding fiscal  
years, o n all outstanding Tax- Supported Debt exceedi ng 12% of annua l District General Fund expenditures and 
transfers in any applicable fiscal year, as contained in the most recently enacted District budget (the "Debt Ceiling").  
The District considers th e portion of t he Availability Payments that is paid f rom local general revenues of the 
District and that are used to pay debt service on the Bonds , to be a tax -supported debt obligation that must be 
included in th e District’s Debt Ceiling calculations.  It is impor tant to distinguish that while a portion of the 
Availability Payments is considered an obligation accounted for in the Dist rict’s Debt Cei ling calculations , such 
payments are not secured by a p ledge of or  involve the faith and  credit or the taxing  power of the District.  Federal 
funding earmarked fo r this portion of the Availability Payments is not derived from local general r evenues of t he 
District, thus that portion of the obligation is not considered tax-supported debt and i s not included in the  District’s 
Debt Ceiling calculations.

Description of Project Accounts 
The following is a description of each of the Project Accounts:  
Revenue A ccount In accordance w ith the terms of the Co llateral A gency Agreement, (a) all amounts 
received by the Borrower from any source (including all Qualified Investments proceeds and all liquidated damages) 
that are not req uired to be paid i nto another Project Account; and (b) all payments by the District under the Project 
Agreement will be paid into the Revenue Account, provided that payment s made by the District during th e D &C 
Period shall be promptly a nd automatically (without further direction from t he Borrower) transferred to the Project 
Revenue Sub -Account (provided that no Event of Default has occurred an d is continuing or would result 
therefrom).Subject to "—Withdra wal and Application of Funds; Priority of Transfers from Project Accounts" below 
and withdrawals relating to the Milestone 2  described below, including the delivery of a Funds T ransfer Certificate 
by the Borrower (to the extent required), beginning with the first Monthly Transfer Date, the Collatera l Agent shall 
make withdrawals, transfers and payments from the Revenue Account and the sub-accounts therein in the amounts, 
at the t imes and only f or the purposes speci fied in the Collateral Agency Agreement. Such withdrawals, transfers 
and payments will be made at  the request of the Borrower in a F unds Transfer Certificate  at the times and in the 
order of priority set forth in "—Flow of Funds" below. 
To the extent tha t on any date of determination amounts on deposit in the Senior Debt DSRA are in excess 
of the Initial Required Senior Debt Se rvice Reserve Level or the Required Senior D ebt Service Reserve Level (as 
applicable), such excess amounts shall be transferred to the Revenue Account. 
Milestone 2. If the Borrower receives the Milestone 2 Payment after the Project Final Completion Date, the

Account in an amount equal t o t he Redempti on P rice due on the Bo nds Milestone 2 Maturities . Any r emaining 
balance of the Milestone 2 Payment after such transfer is fully made shall be retained in the Revenue Account. 
Loss Proceeds Ac countAll insurance or loss proceeds received by the Borrower or to its order (other than in 
respect of (a) soft costs and business interruption proceeds or other anticipated losses of revenue, which are to be 
paid dir ectly to the Revenue A ccount and (b) delayed start-up proceeds, received at any time prior to the Project 
Final Completion Date, which are to be p aid directly to the Construction Account) and all conde mnation proceeds 
received by the Borrower, in e ach case, with respect to physical damage to the Project shall be paid directly into the 
Loss Proceeds Account.  Except in connection with the application o f funds after a n Event of D efault, amounts on 
deposit in the Loss Proceeds Account will be withdrawn and paid to the Borrower to be applied to the restoration of 
the Project or any portion th ereof in accordance with the requirements of the Project Agreement, except that, to the 
extent that ( a) such proceeds e xceed the amount req uired to restore the Proje ct or an y porti on thereof to the 
condition required by the Project Agreement or, if the Project Agreement requires restoration of the Project but does 
not require the condition for suc h r estoration to be  to the condition exi sting p rior t o the event of  loss or (b) the 
affected property cannot be restored or is not req uired pursuant to the ter ms of the Project Agreem ent and the 
Finance Documents to be restored, and the Borrower elects not to do so or the Finance Documents otherwise require 
redemption of the Bonds with such amounts, such proceeds will be applied pro rata  to the applicable sub -account of 
the Mandatory Prepayment Account to c ause the extr aordinary mandatory redemption of the Bonds (on a pro  rata 
basis), and, in t he  case of any remaining moneys thereafter, to the Revenue Account.

the applicable sub-account of the Construction Account all net pr oceeds of the Bonds in accordance with the Bond 
Indenture, all proceeds of Eq uity Contributions, all Milestone  Payments an d any other amounts (inc luding net

Milestone 2 Maturities;

Prepayment Account shall be pledged solely as collateral to secure the Bonds and shall be established solely for the 
benefit of the Bondholders of such Bonds, and will be held by the C ollateral Agent, and the lien thereon maintained, 
for the exclusive benefit of only such Bondholders.

Construction Liquidity Re serve Account 
On the Financial Closing Date, the Borrower will deposit to the Construction Liquidity Reserve Account an 
amount equal to $7,288,779 (the "CLRA Reserve Amount").

continuing under the D&C Contract, the funds on depos it in the Construction Liquidity Reserv e Account may be 
used to cure an y breach or default by the D&C Contractor or in  lieu of payment of amounts due from the D&C 
Contractor under the D&C Contract (provided that the B orrower shall retain its rights to pursue the D&C Contractor 
for payment of the same).  
Funds withdrawn from the Construction Liquidity Reserve Account under this section may be used for any 
purpose to complete the construction of the Project. 
Amounts withdrawn from the Construction Liquidity R eserve Account by the Borrower will be replaced  
with amounts received from, or set-off from payments made to , the D&C Contractor under the D&C Contract (and 
as a pre-condition to payment of amounts to the D& C Contractor), but the Borrower will have no other obligation to 
replenish the Construction Liquidity Reserve Account. 
Upon P roject Final Completion, funds remaining in the Construction Liquidity Reserve Account will be 
transferred to the Construction Acco unt in ord er to fund the D&C Contractor Final Com pletion Pay ment. To the

Qualified Investment 
Funds in t he Project Accounts  may be invested and reinvested only  in Qualified Investments ( at the risk 
and expense of the Borrower) in accordance with written instructions given to the Collateral Agent by the Borrower 
(prior to the occur rence of an Event of Default and, thereafter (so long as such Event of Default will  be continuing), 
as directed by the Bond Trustee ) and, unless an Event of Default has occurred  and is continuing, t he Borrower is 
entitled to i nstruct the  Collateral Agent to liquidat e Qualified Investments for purposes of effecting any such 
investment or reinvestment or for any other purpose permitted under the Collateral Agency Agreement.

made purs uant to an exe cuted Fund s Transfer Certificate, which c ertificate will be provide d an d pr epared by the 
Borrower and will c ontain a certification b y the Borrower that such withdrawal or transfer complies with the 
requirements of the Collateral Agency Agreement.   
Unless a shorter period is acceptab le to the Co llateral Agent, such Funds  Transfer Certificate rela ting to 
each app licable Pr oject Accou nt (other than from the HRA  and the Operating Account ) will be delive red t o the 
Collateral Agent n o later than tw o (2) Business Days prio r to each date on which f unds are propos ed to be 
withdrawn or transferred.  In th e e vent that a cert ificate d oes not compl y with the requirements of the Collateral 
Agency Agreement, the Collateral Agent has the right to reject such certificate and the Borrower will not be entitled 
to cause the proposed withdrawal or transfer until it has submitted a revised and compliant certificate.  
Withdrawal and Application of F unds following an Event of Default

notice of an E vent of Default and during the continuance o f the related Ev ent of Defa ult, the Bond Trustee  may,

AGREEMENT—Ap plication of Proceeds"; provided that at any time prior to the taking of an Enforcement Action, 
proceeds of the  Project Accounts will be  applied in the order set forth i n "—Fl ow of Funds"; provided further that 
prior to the taking of an Enforcement Action, proceeds in the Construction Account and the Senior Debt DSRA may 
only be a pplied in th e manner described in "—C onstruction Account" and the  "—Senior  Debt DSRA " set forth
