Relief Events 
The Project Agreement provides that the Borrower will obtain relief from complying with the deadlines set 
forth therein, relief  from compliance with the Bor rower's obligations t herein, and/or relief from an y rights of th e 
District in connection with termination due to a the occurrence of certain enumerated events, which include: 
(a) any Change in Law that is not a Qualifying Change in Law; 
(b) any Force Majeure Event: 
(c) any flood; 
(d) fire, explosion, explosion, or earthquakes; 
(e) any tornado, named windstorm and any ensuing storm surge, or unusually adverse weather; 
(f) any riot or civil commotion; 
(g) any blockage or embargo; 
(h) any industry -wide of ficial or unof ficial s trike, generally affecting th e lig hting industry or a 
significant sector of it; 
(i) any accidental loss or damage to a Lighting Unit or Project Site not caused by the Borrower; 
(j) any de lay in o btaining any Governmental Approval to the extent that  s uch delay is b eyond t he 
reasonable control of any Borrower-Related Entity; 
(k) any failure by a Utility Owner to cooperate with t he Borrower in relation to a Utility A djustment 
in such manner that would result in a d elay to t he D&C Work (as se t out in the Project Baseline 
Schedule) of more than thirty (30) days; provided that the Borrower shall have continued to satisfy 
the Conditions to Assistance with Ut ility Owner for the duration of such fa ilure to cooperate by  
the Utility Owner; 
(l) the failure of an y Utility Owner having operational jurisdiction in the area in which any Lighting 
Unit is located to provide and  maintain el ectric utility se rvice to the Stree t Light Netw ork 
upstream of the conne ction p oint to the re levant Lighting Uni t requi red f or the Borrower to 
perform its obligations under the Project Agreement; or 
(m) compliance b y the Borrower with an order or direction of a Governmental Entity in an Emergency, 
except, in ea ch case, t o the ext ent attributable to an y breach of the P roject Agreemen t or any negligen t act or 
negligent omission of a Borrower-Related Entity. 
If a Relief Event d irectly causes, or is likely to  directly cause, the Borrower to do any on e or mor e of the 
following: 
(a) fail to ac hieve Pr oject Final  C ompletion by the Guaranteed P roject Fina l Completion Date or, 
following the Guaranteed Project Final Completion Date, in cur further delay i n achieving Proj ect 
Final Completion; 
(b) fail to receive any Milestone Payment on the date shown in the Base Case Financial Model; or 
(c) comply with any of its obligations under the Project Agreement,  
B-17 
then, t he Borrower may claim one or more of th e following: ( i) an ext ension o f the Gu aranteed Pr oject 
Final Completion Date or, following the Guaranteed Proj ect Final Completion Date, the Long St op Deadli ne; (ii) 
relief from compliance with its obligations under the P roject Agreement; rel ief from any rights of the District with 
respect to a Borrower Default; or (iii) comp ensation for  any Finance Costs with re spect to the occurrenc e of an 
Uninsurable Relief Event. 
Procedure  for Relie f. Subject to the late provision of n otice requir ements of the P roject Agreement, any 
request for reli ef made by the Borrower must be submitted to the District promptly (and in any  event within thirty 
(30) days) after the date that the Borrower first became aware that the relevant Relief Event had occurred and would 
have the effect that is  the subject of t he Borrower's claim. Additionally, the Borrower must promptly (and in any 
event within forty-five (45) days) after the date that the Borrower first became aware that the relevant Relief Event 
had occurred and would have the effect that is the subject o f the Borrower's claim, give full details of the relevant 
Relief Event, the extension of time and/or relief requested, including (among other things): 
(a) with r espect to any cla im for an extension of time, a T ime Impact Analysis (based on the Pro ject 
Baseline Schedule most recently ag reed t o pursuant to  the T echnical Pr ovisions) demonstra ting 
that the relevant Relief Event will result in an  identifiable and measurable disruption to the Work, 
which will impact a Critical Path activity (i.e., will consume all available float and will exten d the 
time requir ed to achieve Pro ject Final Completion  or satisfy the  conditions to receive any 
Milestone Payment); 
(b) evidence reas onably satisfact ory to the District tha t no othe r concu rrent unrelated delay to a 
Critical Path activity that is the Borrower's responsibility has occurred that has contributed to the 
delay for which the extension of time or relief from obligations (as applicable) is being sought; 
(c) evidence reasonably satisfactory to the District demon strating that any Fina nce Costs c laimed by 
the Borrower will be incurred during the District Delay Period as a dir ect result of the Uninsure d 
Relief Event or is r easonably like ly to be a di rect result of an Uninsure d Relie f Event  (as 
applicable); and 
(d) details of any steps that the Borrower has taken o r will take to miti gate the effect of the Rel ief 
Event. 
Giving of Re lie f.  If the Borrower satisfies the co nditions described above, t hen the Borrower wil l be 
entitled to an extension of time or relief from obligations (as applicable), as follows: 
(a) in the c ase of a delay, the Guaranteed Project Final Completion Date or, following the Guaranteed 
Project Final Com pletion Da te, the Long S top Deadline. will be e xtended by suc h time as is 
reasonable, based on the Time Impact Analysis, for such a Relief Event; 
(b) if any Borrower Default or breach  of the Project Agreement would not have occur red but for the 
occurrence of t he R elief Eve nt, such Borrower Default or  breach will be deem ed to ha ve not 
occurred for the purposes of the Project Agreement; 
(c) if any Noncompliance Event would not have occurred but for the occurrence of t he Relief Event, 
such Noncompliance Event will be  deemed to hav e not occurred for the p urposes of the P roject 
Agreement and no Noncompliance Points or Deductions will be assessed by the District in respect 
of such event; 
(d) if any Unavailability Event would n ot have occurr ed but for the occurrence of th e Relief Event, 
such Unavailability Event will be de emed t o have not  occurred for purposes of th e Project 
Agreement and no Deductions will be assessed by the District in respect of such event; and  
B-18 
(e) in the case of  any Finance Costs the Borrower will incur during the District D elay Period due to 
the occurrence of an Uninsur ed Relief Event , the Borrower will be entitled to c ompensation in  
accordance with the relevant terms of the Project Agreement.  
Late Provision of N otice or I nformation.  In the event that  information is provided after the dates referred 
to in this section , then the Borrower will not be entitled to any compensation, extension of t ime, or relief from its 
obligations under the Project Agreement to the extent that the quantum thereof was adversely affected as a result of 
such notice or informatio n with the rel evant time pe riods. I n the  event the Borrower fails to provide su ch 
information for a period of twelve (12) months or more, the rights of the Borrower with respect to the relevant Relief 
Event will be of no further force or effect.

relief from the Borrower's obligations under the Project Agreement, the parties will resolve the matter in accordance 
with the Dispute Resolution Procedures set forth in the Project Agreement. 
Changes 
Distr ict Changes. The D istrict has the right to propose District Changes. In ord er to re quest a D istrict 
Change, the District shall deliver to the Borrower a document (a "District Change Request") setting forth: 
(a) the D istrict's proposed change(s) to the Work o r to the Technical Provisions in sufficient detail to 
enable the Borrower to calculate and provide an estimate of the costs  and expenses in connection 
therewith; and 
(b) the method of compensation for the change. 
The Borrower shall be entitled to refuse a District Change Request that would, if implemented: 
(a) require the Work to be performed in a way that violates applicable Law; 
(b) contravene a Go vernmental Ap proval then in full fo rce an d e ffect  (pro vided that such 
contravention cannot be corrected by the issuance of a new or revised Governmental Approval that 
can be obtained by the Borrower); 
(c) materially and adversely affects the health and safety of any Person;  
(d) cause an insured risk to become an Uninsurable Risk; 
(e) materially and adversely  ch ange the nat ure o f the Project or th e alloc ation of risk between  the 
Parties; 
(f) be unrelated t o the kind of work ini tially procured under the  RFP and to the kind  o f work that 
Borrower-Related Entities provide in the marketplace; or 
(g) require the Work to be performed in a way that results in a breach of the terms of a ny License 
Agreement. 
Promptly ( and i n any event within fifteen (15) Bus iness Da ys) after ha ving rece ived a D istrict Change 
Request, the Borrower shall deliver to the D istrict an estimate of costs and expenses and other matters with respect 
to such District Change Request (a "Borrower Estimate"), or confirmation as to when a Borrower Estimate is to be 
provided to the D istrict (provided, t hat the Bo rrower shall u se all Reasonable Ef forts t o prepare  its Borrower 
Estimate within fo rty-five (45) days after having received the D istrict Change Request, unless f urther extended by 
the District acting reasonably). Promptly (and in any event within ten (10) B usiness Days) after the District receives 
the Borrower Es timate, t he Borrower shall meet with th e D istrict to rev iew, discu ss and see k to agr ee upon  the 
Borrower Estimate. During such discussions, the District may modify the District Change Request, and may (to the  
B-19 
extent practicable, give n the  nature of the Extra Work) require the Borrower  to seek an d evalua te competitive 
tenders for  the relevant cap ital w orks in connection with such D istrict Change, as ap plicable. In each case the 
Borrower shall, within fifteen (15)  Business Days or such longer per iod as a greed to b y the District (acting 
reasonably), after receipt o f such modification, no tify the D istrict of any  consequential change s to the Borrower 
Estimate. 
If the D istrict confirms the Borrower Estimate (as may be modified), the Borrower shall use Reasonable 
Efforts to implement the relevant Extra Work in accordance with the Borrower Estimate and the District shall make 
a payment to the Borrower within sixty (60) days of receiving each invoice (co mplete in all mater ial respects) in 
accordance with the  agreed-upon payment schedule, a ccompanied by the  evidence r easonably acceptab le to the 
District (where app licable) tha t the r elevant p art of  the District Cha nge has  b een carried out . I n the event that  a 
District Change Request is withdrawn, the D istrict will reimburse the Borrower for all reasonable and documented 
third-party costs incurred by the Borrower (as shown in an invoice reasonably acceptable to the Contracting Officer) 
in connection with such District Change Request. 
With respect to  District Ch anges relating to expansions of the S treet Light Ne twork, th e installation of 
Metropolitan Police Department CCTV cameras on Poles, the inspection of Lighting Units as part of the installation 
of small cell networking equipment by a third-party, and structural analysis or design work on Poles owned by third-
parties prior t o the inst allation of w ireless acces s poi nts, t he bas is of t he corr esponding Borrower Estimate and 
resulting change order will be unit pricing for such tasks specified in the Project Agreement. 
Borrower Changes.  If the Borrower wishes to introduce a chang e in the Work, it must deliv er a change 
request ("Borrower Change Request") to the District setting o ut (a) the pr oposed change to the Work in  sufficient 
detail to enable the District to evaluate it in full, (b) the Borrower' s reasons for p roposing the change to the Work, 
(c) a request to th e District to consult with the Borrower with a view to d eciding whether to a gree to the change to 
the Work and, if so,  what consequential cha nges the District r equires due to the Borrower Ch ange, ( d) any  
implications of the change to the Work,  (e ) details regarding proposed changes to the Milestone Payments or the 
Availability Payment, if any (and, if so, givi ng a detaile d cost estimate o f such proposed change), (f) any dates by 
which a deci sion by the Di strict is critical an d (g) all of the in formation re quired with respect to a Borrower 
Estimate. 
The District is require d to evaluate the Borrower Change Request and is required to issue a response to the 
Borrower with fifteen (15) Business Days of meeting with the Borrower to review the Borrower Change Request. 
Unless the Di strict's acceptanc e specifically agrees to an in crease in th e Milestone P ayments or the  Availability 
Payments there shall be  no increase in the Milestone Paymen ts or the Availability P ayments as a resul t of a change 
to the  Work proposed by the Borrower. If a Borrower Change results in a decrease in th e Borrower's costs, any 
payments due f rom the Di strict under the Project Agreem ent will be adjusted downwards t o reflect  50% of  such 
reduction in the Borrower's costs.  
While the District is not obligated to provide the reas on for re jecting the Borrower Change Request, the 
District may not reject a Borrower request that is required in order to conform to a Change in Law. 
Asset Inventory and  Condition Report.  The District has provided data, in the form of a report, setting forth 
(a) an inventory of each District-owned Element included in the Existing Street Light Network as of the Setting Date 
and ( b) a description of the condition of, an d a co ndition rating f or, each exis ting Di strict-owned El ement ( such 
report, the "Asset Inventory and Condition Report "). If, during the p erformance o f the  Conversion Work, t he 
Borrower discovers an E lement ( a) t hat is not included in the inventor y set fo rth in t he Asset Invent ory an d 
Condition Report or ( b) in a physi cal condition that is worse than the condition rating for su ch Element set forth in 
the Asset Inventory and Condition Report, the Borrower shall be entitled to request a District Change, pro vided that 
(a) the Borro wer notifies the District pro mptly (and in any event within t en ( 10) Bus iness Days) following such 
discovery (each such notice, a "D iffering Asset Notice") and (b)(i) the physical condition of such element w as not 
known to the Borrower or a ny Borrower-Related En tity as o f the Setting Date, and ( ii) th e existence or physical 
condition of such Element, as applicable, was not reasonably capable of be ing identified on or bef ore the Setting 
Date through review and analysis of the Asset  Inventory and Cond ition Rep ort b y an a ppropriately qualified and 
experienced contractor or engineer exercising due care and sk ill and Good I ndustry Practice. The Borrower shall 
include in each Differing A sset Notice: (a) a description of each component of th e D&C Wo rk impacted by su ch  
B-20 
Element; (b) an opinion on the likely effects of such Element on the DBC Price and the Project B aseline Schedule, 
along with r easonably d etailed suppor ting doc umentation; and (c ) a descr iption of the repair, rehabilitation, or 
replacement work the Borrower believes is required with respect to such Element. 
Within thirty (30) days of the District's receipt of a Differing Asset Notice, the District will make one of the 
following determinations: (a) the Element described in the Differing Asset Notice must be repaired, rehabilitated, or 
replaced in accordance with the recommendations set forth in  the Diff ering Asset Not ice an d the Borrower is 
entitled to requ est a District Change for such repa ir, rehabilitation, or replacem ent work; (b) the Element described 
in the Differing Asset Notic e must be repaired, rehabilitated, or replaced in accordance with the recommendations 
set forth in the Differing Asset Notice and the Borrower is not entitled to request a District Change for  such repair, 
rehabilitation, or replacement work; or (c) the Element described in the Differing Asset Notice is in a condition that 
does not require repair, rehabilitation, or replacement of such Element. 
If in respect of any Differing Asset Notice the Borrower is entitled to request a District Change pursuant to 
the Project Agreement and the Contracting Officer determines that an eq uitable adjustment to the DBC Price and/or 
Project Baseline  Schedule is required to  address the  impact of such District Change, the Parties shall ex ecute a 
mutually-acceptable District Ch arge Order setti ng forth the terms of such equitable adjustment. If in respect of any 
Differing Asset Notice the District determines that that the Borro wer is not entitled to request a District Change , the 
Borrower may refer the matter for resolution pursuant to the Dispute Resolution Procedures. 
The Borrower sha ll not be entitled to submit a Differing Asset Notice or oth erwise be entitled to request a 
District Change pursuant to the  Project Agreement with respect to the rehabilitation, repair, or repl acement of any 
Element undertaken on or after the Project Final Completion Date. 
Tariff Event .  If  a Tariff Event  occurs, t he Borrower shall provide w ritten notice (a  "P reliminary Tariff 
Event Notice") to the District promptly (and in any event within thirty (30) days) following the occurrence of such 
event setting forth: (a) a description of each comp onent of the D&C Work imp acted by the Tariff Event and (b)  an 
opinion on the likely effects of the Tariff  Event on the DB C Price, such op inion to include the Borrowe r's estimate 
of the increase or decrease in the cost of each relevant component of the D&C Work between the Financial Proposal 
Due Dat e and the date on  which the  Borrower expects to install o r purchase each suc h component. Promptly 
following the Borrower's installation or pu rchase of a c omponent of the D&C Wor k that is the su bject of a 
Preliminary Tariff Event Notice ( and in any event within thirty ( 30) days following the Borrower's installation or 
purchase of the relevant compon ent), t he Borrower shall pro vide to the D istrict a fo llow-up notice (a "Detailed 
Tariff Event Notice") that includes a detailed description, along with reasonably detailed supporting documentation, 
of the increase or decrease in the cost of each relevant component of the D&C Work between the Financial Proposal 
Due Date and the date on which the Borrower installed or purchased each such component. 
Within thirty  (30) days foll owing the Project Final Completion Date, th e Contracting Officer shall review 
each Detailed Tar iff Event Notice and determine whether an equit able adjustment to the DBC Price is required to 
address the impact (either po sitive or negative) of all such Tariff Events. Following such determination, the Parties 
shall execute a mutually acceptable District Change Orde r setting forth o f the terms of such equitable adj ustment. 
The Borrower may refer the matter f or resolution pur suant to the Dispute R esolution Proc edures if the Borrower 
disagrees with the Contracting Officer's determination. 
If the e quitable adjustment to  the DBC Price agreed b y the Parties  or finally determined pursuant to the 
Dispute Resolution Procedures, as app licable, includes an increase to the DBC Price, the District shall pa y to the 
Borrower, subject to the C ontracting Officer's confirmation that there are sufficient funds legally available to make 
such payment, the amount of such equitabl e adjustment within thirty (30) da ys of receiving an invoice, in a f orm 
reasonably acceptable to the District, from the Borrower. If the equitable adjustment to the DBC Price agreed by the 
Parties or finally determined pursuant to the Dispute Resolution Procedures, as applicable, includes a decrease to the 
DBC Price, the Borrower shall pay to the District the amount of such equitable adjustment within thirty (30) days of 
receiving an invoice, in a form reasonably acceptable to the Borrower, from the District.

Borrower Indemnity 
To the fullest extent permitted by applicable Law, the B orrower shall release, defend, indemnif y and hold 
harmless the Indemnified Parties on dema nd from and against any and all liability to third parties for Loss es arising 
from (a) d eath or p ersonal injury, (b ) loss of o r da mage to  any Indemnified P arty's property, an d ( c) third party  
claims brought against any Indem nified Party, which may aris e ou t of, o r in cons equence of, any or all of the 
following: (i) any actual or alleged failure by the Borrower to comply with, observe or perform any of the covenants, 
obligations, agreements, terms or condit ions in t he Project Documents or any actual or alle ged breach  by the  
Borrower of its representations or warranties set f orth in the Projec t Agreement; (ii) any violation of a ny federal or 
state se curities or similar law by a ny Borrower-Related Entity (ot her t han as  a direct r esult of any disclosure 
statements made by the District or other Indemnified Party); and (iii) if a pplicable, the authorization, issuance, sale, 
trading, redemption or servicing of any bonds issued to fi nance the Project (r egardless of the identity of the Bond 
Issuer), or the Borrowe r's failure  to comply with any r equirement necessary to preserve the tax-e xempt status o f 
bonds (other than as a direct result of any disclosure statements made by the District or other Indemnified Party). 
The Borrower will not be res ponsible or obliged to indemnify an Indemnified Party with resp ect to any 
liability or Losses to the extent that the same arise as a direct result of: (a) a C ompensation Event, Relief Event, or 
District Default; (b) the presence of H azardous Materials on any Project Site for which the District is deemed to be  
the sole generator and arranger, but o nly to the extent that the relevant loss does  not arise as a direct result of  the 
negligence of the Borrower or the Borrower failing to comply with the terms of the Project Agreement; (c) any non-
performance by an Indemnified Party of its obligations und er the Project A greement or any violation of applicable 
Law b y an y Indemnified P arty; or (d) the fraud, n egligence, recklessness, bad  faith or  willful misconduc t of an 
Indemnified Party. 
Unavailability  Events and Noncompliance Events 
The Project Agreement con tains a regime pursuant to which Deductions are assessed against the Borrower, 
based on a list of bre aches or failures in p erformance of ob ligations of the Borrower identified the  Project 
Agreement. Under the Project Agreement there are separate regim es f or determining Ded uctions before Project 
Final Completion and after Project Final Completion. Unavailability Events and Noncompliance Events include, but 
are not limited to, failure to provide certain notifications to the District, failure to observe o r comply with various 
project delivery protocols or breach of performance standards. The Borrower has the benefit of certain cure periods 
and/or grace periods for its breaches and failures which are b ased on t he type of breach or failure. With respect to 
Noncompliance Events, the District is permitted to assess Noncompliance Points after notification to and failure by 
the Borrower to cure such breach or failure following such notice. 
Each of a Noncompliance Borrower Default Trigger and an Unavailability Default Trigger is the basis for a 
Borrower Default and can, under certain circumstances, serve as a basis for termination of the Project Agreement.

include any one or more of the following (each a "Borrower Default"):

continuous period of more than sixty (60) days;

Warning Notices 
If the Borrower commits a breach of the P roject Agreement (ot her than ( a) any  breach for which a  
Noncompliance Point could have been assessed or (b) any breach that arises as a direct result of the occurrence of a 
Compensation Event or a Re lief Event) that co ntinues for more than thirty (30 ) conse cutive days or occu rs more 
than three (3) tim es in an y six (6)-month period, then the District may serve a notice (an " Initial Warning Notice") 
on the Borrower specifying that it is an Initial Warning Notice, giving reasonable details of th e relevant breach, and 
stating that the relevant breach is a breach which, if it recurs frequently or continues, may result in termination of the 
Project Agreement for Persistent Breach. 
If the breach specified in an Initial Warning Notice continues beyond thirty (30) consecutive days or recurs 
in three (3) or more times within the three (3)-month period after the date of servic e of the Initial Wa rning Notice, 
then the District may serve another not ice (a "Final War ning No tice") on the Borrower (a ) specifying that it is a 
Final Warning Notice, (b) stating that the breach specified has been the subje ct of an Initial Warning Notice served 
within the three (3)-month period prior to the  date of service of the Final Warni ng Notice, and (c) stating that if the 
breach recurs three (3) or more times within the three (3)-month period after the date of service of the Final Warning 
Notice, the Project Agreement may be terminated.

Initial Notice and Cure Periods 
The District will provide wri tten notice to the Borrower of the occurrence of a Borrower Default. U pon 
receipt of the District's notice (if required), the Borrower shall have the following cure periods: 
(a) For a Borrower Default in respect of clauses (a), (b), (k), (l), and (q) of the definition of Borrower 
Default above, a period of t hirty (30) days after the Borrower r eceives written notice from the 
District of such Borrower Default. 
(b) For a Borrower D efault under claus es (m ), (n), (o), (s) a nd ( t) of the definition of Borrowe r 
Default above, a period of thirty (30) days after Borrower receives written notice from the District 
of such Borrower Default or, if despite the Borrower's commencement of meaningful steps to cure 
immediately after receiving the Borrower Default Notice, t he Borrower Default cannot be cured 
within such thirty (30) day-period, the Borrower will have such additional period of time, up to a 
maximum cure period of n inety (9 0) days, as is r easonably neces sary to cur e the  Borrower 
Default. 
For any other Borrower Default, there is no cure period. 
Remedial Plan 
In the event that a Borrower Default occurs and it has not been cure d within any relevant cure period, the 
District m ay, at its so le option an d without pr ejudice to a ny oth er r ight or remedy ava ilable to it,  require t he 
Borrower to prepare and  submit, within thirty (30) days of being notified of such requirement, a remedial plan tha t 
shall set forth a schedule and specific actions to be taken by the Borrower to cure the relevant Borrower Default and 
reduce the likelihood of such defaults occu rring in the future.  Such actions may include changes in organizational 
and man agement stru cture, revising and rest ating m anagement pla ns and procedures, improvemen ts to  quality 
control p ractices, increased mo nitoring and inspections, changes in key personne l and  other important per sonnel, 
and replacement of contractors. 
Within th irty (3 0) days of receiving any such re medial plan, the District will notify the Borrower whether 
or not the re levant re medial plan is, in the D istrict's absolute discretion, acceptable.  If the D istrict n otifies the 
Borrower that the r elevant remedial plan is  acceptable, the Borrower will be required to implement such remedial 
plan in accordance with its terms. 
Right of Termination 
In the event that  a Borrower Default occurs and it has not been cured within a ny relevant cure period set 
out in Initial Notice and Cure Periods section above, or (if relevant) in accordance with any remedial plan a ccepted 
by the D istrict pursuant to the Remedial Plan sect ion above, t he District may terminate the Project Agreemen t in 
accordance with, and subject to the terms of, the term ination for Borrower Default provisions set out in the  Project 
Agreement. 
Borrower Default Termination Sum 
On termination  of t he P roject Agreement as t he resu lt of a Borrower Default prior to  Project Final 
Completion, the District will be r equired to p ay to the Borrower the Borrower Default (D&C Period) T ermination 
Sum in accordance with and subject to the applicable compensation on termination provisions set out in the P roject 
Agreement. The Borrower D efault ( D&C Period) Termination Sum will be an amou nt e qual to th e lower o f t he 
D&C Work Value and eighty percent (80%) of Net Lenders' Liabilities. 
On t ermination of the P roject Agreement as th e result of a Borrower Default on or after  Project Final 
Completion, the D istrict will be required pay to the Borrower the Borrower Default ( Asset Ma nagement Perio d) 
Termination Sum in accord ance wi th and subject to  certain a dditional compensation provisio n described in the   
B-25 
Project Agreement, the Borrower Default (A sset Management Period) Termination Sum wi ll equal t he a mount 
calculated at the Early Termination Date (without double-counting) as follows: 
(a) Eight percent (80%) of Lenders' Liabilities; minus 
(b) all Losses incurred by the District as a result of such termination, including but not limited to  (i) 
the costs to rectify any Nonconforming Work and a ny funds reasonably necessary to ensure th at 
the future costs of Asset Manage ment Work and the fulfillment of the H andback Requ irements 
will not e xceed the costs  shown i n the Base C ase Financial Model; provided,  that if  t he E arly 
Termination Date occurs during the Handback Period, such amount will be reduced by the amount 
of any Handback Letter of C redit or bala nce standing to the  credit of the H RA, which shall be 
drawn or retained (as applicable) by the District and (ii) the costs of procuring a new developer to 
undertake the Work; minus 
(c) Deferred Equity Amounts (if any); minus 
(d) Insurance Proceeds (excluding proceeds of perso nal injury, property damage or other third pa rty 
liability insurance payable to or for the account of a third party); minus 
(e) Account Balances. 
Other Termination Rights; Effect of Termination 
Termination for District Default 
The Project Agreement provides for a number of default events by the District. Such default events include, 
without limitation, any one or more of the following: (a) the District fails to make any payment due to the Borrower 
under the Project Agreement when due, except to the e xtent such payment is subject to a good faith dispute; (b ) any 
District representation or warranty made under the Pr oject Agreement is false, misleading, or inaccurate when made 
in each case in any material respect or omits material information when made; (c) any condemnation or other taking 
by eminent domain of  al l or more t han f ifty percent (50%) of t he Lighting U nits included in the St reet L ight 
Network; (d) the Distr ict fails to perform any of its ob ligations under th e Project Agreement, w hich substantially 
frustrates or  r enders it subst antially impossible fo r the Borrower to p erform i ts obligations under the Pr oject 
Agreement for a co ntinuous period of s ixty (60) d ays or more; and ( e) the Distric t fa ils to comply with  the 
assignment and transfer provisions of the Project Agreement applicable to the District. 
If a default b y the District occurs and is co ntinuing and has not been cured within the relevant cure periods 
set forth in the P roject Agreement, the Borrower will have access to certain remedies, i ncluding, as  and when 
applicable, the right to termin ate the Project Agreement an d to receive the D istrict Termination Sum  from the  
District. T he D istrict w ill pay to the Borrower the D istrict Termination Sum in acc ordance wi th and subject to 
certain additional c ompensation provisions described below.  The D istrict T ermination Sum w ill equal the amount

During the continuance of any Fo rce Majeure Event, th e affected party will be e xcused from performing 
those of its obligations dir ectly affected by such Forc e Majeure Event.  Subject to certain conditions (including the 
passage of certain periods of time), either pa rty may elect to termin ate the P roject Agreement for Extended Force

Majeure or Extended Relief Event o r uninsurability, the D istrict will be require d to pay t he No Fault Termination 
Sum to the Borrower in accordance with and subject to certain additional compensation provisions described below. 
The No Fault Termination Sum will equal the amount calculated at the Early Te rmination Date (without doub le-
counting) as follows: 
(a) the gr eater of zero (0) or the sum of: (i) all amounts paid to th e Borrower by way of equity to the

Members (except to the extent de ducted under clause ( a)(ii)); plus (ii)the principal amount of all

incurred by the Borrower as a direct result of termination of the Project Agreement; minus 
(f) any Deductions assessed but not paid.

Miscellaneous Compensation Provisions 
Set Off on Termination 
The D istrict is not entitled to set off any amount against the  D istrict Termination Sum or the No F ault 
Termination Sum if the e ffect o f ex ercising s uch right of set off woul d be to reduce  the amount payable to  the 
Borrower to less than an amount equal to the Net Lenders' Liabilities. 
Timing of Payment of Termination Sum 
The District shall pay any Te rmination Sum within sixty (60) da ys after such amount is finally agreed or 
determined, and an invoice (in a form reasonably acceptable to the  District) is presented to t he District. Other than 
with respect to any t ermination for Borrower Default, such p ayment shall b e made together with any interest that 
may a ccrue under the Financ e Documents betwe en the Ea rly Termination Date  and the  date of payment o f the  
Termination Sum by the District. 
If the Borrower Default (D&C Period) Termination Sum or Borrower D efault (Asset Management Period) 
Termination Sum is less than zero (0), the Borrower shall pay an amount equal to the absolute value of the Borrower 
Default (D&C Peri od) Termination Sum or Borrower Default (Asset Man agement Period) T ermination Sum (as 
applicable) to the District within sixty (60) days after such amount is finally agreed or determined. 
Transfer of Key Assets 
As a condition precedent to th e payment of any Termination Sum, the District may require the Borrower to 
(a) transfer its rights, title and interest in and to the Key Assets to the District an d (b) make available t o the District 
as of the Early Termination Date the benefit of a ll warranties provided to the Borrower by the D&C Contractor and 
the Asset Management Contractor. 
Exclusivity of Remedy 
Any T e rmination Sum irrevocably paid by the District to the Borrower or the Borrower to the District will 
be in full and final settlement of each Party's rights and claims against the other for breaches and termination of the 
Project Agreement whether under contract, tort, restitution or otherwise, but without prejudice t o: (a) any accrued 
liability of either Party to the other that arose prior to the Early Termination Date, but not from the termination itself 
(including any Milestone Payments or Availability Payments accrued but not yet paid to the Borrower), to the extent 
such liability ha s not  already been tak en into ac count in the calcu lation o f the  Termination Sum ; a nd ( b) any 
liabilities arising with respect to any breach by either Party after the Early Termination Date of any obligation under 
the Pro ject Agreement that su rvives the Ear ly Te rmination Date, t o the extent not t aken into account in th e 
calculation of the Termination Sum. 
District Right to Step-In 
Subject to the step-in rights of th e Collateral Agent set forth in th e Project Agreement (des cribed below), 
the District will be entitled to exercise its step-in rights under the Project Agreement if it reasonably believes that an 
emergency has arisen, a Borrower Default has occurred and has not be en cured within any relevant cure period, or 
the Borrower has failed to meet any safety standard or comply with any safety compliance order within a reasonable 
period of time under the circumstances. 
If any such event ha s occurred a nd the District wishes to take step-in action, the  District must notify th e 
Borrower, in writing , of the actio n the  District wishes to take, the reaso n for such action, the date it w ishes to 
commence such action, t he time per iod that it belie ves will be ne cessary for such  action, a nd, to th e extent 
practicable, the effect such action will h ave on the  Borrower's obligation to car ry out the Work. In the c ase of an 
emergency, the District may take any action it reasonably believ es is necessary in order to mitigate or contain such 
emergency without prior not ice t o the Borrower. Following servi ce of  such notice or the o ccurrence of  an 
emergency, the D istrict may take s uch actions previously described  and a ny consequential additional action as it  
B-29 
reasonably believes is necessary and the Borrower will use reas onable efforts to give all a ssistance to the D istrict 
while it is tak ing such action. The District is required to provide the Borrower with notice of completion of such  
action and shall use reasonable efforts to provide such advance notice as is reasonably practicable of its antic ipated 
completion. 
If the actions of th e District did not result from the Borrower breaching its oblig ations under the Pr oject 
Agreement, to the extent that the District's actions prevents or delays the Borrower's performance the Borrower will 
be relieved from perform ing such obligations and such Required Action will be a Compensation Event.   If the 
actions o f the District resulted from the Borro wer breaching its obligations under the Project Agreemen t, to the 
extent that the District's actions prevents or del ays the Borrower's performance the Borrower will be relieved from 
performing such obligations under the Project Agreem ent, any Noncompliance Events or Unavailability Event that 
would not have occurred but for the occurrence of such Required Action will be deemed to have not occurred and an 
amount equal to all of the reasonable costs incurred by the  District in taking such action will be deduct ed from the 
Availability Payment to be paid by the District to the Borrower for a given calendar quarter. 
Handback 
Upon the expiration or earlier termination of the Project Agreement, the Borrower is required to transfer the 
Project t o th e District, at no charge  to the D istrict, in the condi tion a nd m eeting all residual life r equirements 
specified i n the Project Agr eement. In conne ction with  such handba ck, the  Borrower, i n conjunction w ith the 
Independent Engineer, will conduct inspections of all of the  components of the  Project in order to c onfirm that the 
residual life requirements have been fulfilled and to determine the renewal work to be performed prior to handback. 
HRA 
N
o later than twe nty-four (24) months befor e the s cheduled end of the Project Agreement term, the 
Borrower is required t o establish a reserve account to  which the Borrower will grant to th e District a fir st priority 
security interest. 
No late r t han twenty-four ( 24) mont hs before the scheduled end of the P roject Agreement term, th e 
Borrower, in conjunction with  t he Independe nt E ngineer, shall begin to as sess th e condition  of the Imp roved 
Streetlight Network. Beginning on the date that is sixty (60) days following the Borrower's commencement of such 
assessment, a nd cont inuing ever y sixty (60) days  thereafter unt il the Borrower c ompletes the assessment, t he 
Borrower  is required to deliver to the District a report setting out its calculations of the amount to be funded into the 
HRA in accordance with the Project Agreement. Within fourteen (14) days of any such report being delivered to the 
District, the Parties will seek to a gree upon t he Handback Reserve Amount, and in the ab sence of agreement, the 
Handback Reserve Amount wi ll be finally de termined pursuant to the dispute resolution procedures. In lieu of the  
establishment or ongoing funding of the HRA, the Borrower may deliver to the District one or more letters of credit 
with aggregate value equal to the ongoing Handback Reserve Amount. 
Transfer Restrictions 
Neither th e Borrower nor the District m ay assign or transf er any of th eir rights or obligations  under the 
Project Agreement without the consent of the other, with certain exceptions such as allowing the District to assign or 
transfer its rig hts to o ther Go vernmental E ntities that succeed to  the governm ental power s and author ity of  the 
District. The restr iction set forth in the pr ior sentence does not apply to th e grant of any securit y for any financing 
extended to the Borrower (directly or indirectly) under the Finance Documents or to the enforcement of the same. 
Restrictions on Change in Ownership 
Subject to certain permitted exceptions, a Borrower Default will arise if (a) at any time prior t o the second 
anniversary of t he Project Final  Completion D ate, without the pr ior written consent of the D istrict, in its so le 
discretion, any Permitted Investor ceases t o own (directly or indirectly) the same percentage of the issued sh ares or 
membership interests in the Borrower that it owned (direc tly or indire ctly) o n the date  of the P roject Agreement, 
other than as a resul t of an Ad ditional Equity Investment, ( b) any Change in Ownership oc curs that involves t he  
B-30 
transfer of a ny shares or membership interests, o r (c) any Change in Ow nership occu rs that would be reasonably 
likely t o h ave a material adverse ef fect on the Borrower's ability  to pe rform its obligations  under the Project 
Agreement with respect to th e Asset Ma nagement Period As set Manag ement Services (a "R estricted Change in 
Ownership"). 
Disputes 
All disputes ari sing under the Project Agreem ent will be resolv ed pursuant to the d ispute resolution 
procedures, which involve a non -binding d ispute r esolution board and claims review p rocess by the  C ontracting 
Officer. Submission of a  dispute by way of written claim to the C ontracting Officer is a c ondition precedent to the 
Borrower having the ri ght to p roceed to litigation of su ch unresolved di spute ( subject to the jury trial waiver se t 
forth in the Project Agreement). During the course of the dispute resolution process, the Borrower will continue with 
the Work (i ncluding any Work that is the  subject to  the Dis pute) usin g reasonable effort s  and without delay or 
otherwise confirm to the District's decision or order, and will be governed by all applicable provisions of the Project 
Agreement, and the District will continue to make pay ments of any amounts not in disp ute pursuant to the terms of 
the Project Agreement. Throughout any disputed Work, the Borrower will keep complete records of extra costs an d 
time incurred. The Borrower will provide the District and any dispute review board members access to these and any 
other records needed for evaluating the dispute. 
Governing Law and Jurisdiction 
The Project Agreement will be g overned by, and interpret ed and enforced in accordance with,  the laws of 
the District of Colu mbia. The Borrower has consented to the jurisdiction of any c ourt of the District of Columbia, 
waiving any claim or defense that such f orum is not convenient or pr oper. The Borrower has agreed that any su ch 
court shall have in personam j urisdiction over it, and consents to servic e of process in any mann er authorized by 
applicable Law. 
Provision Ad dressing Rights of Financing Parties 
Right to Cure or Step-In 
As of the Financial Closing Date, the Collateral Agent will become a party to the Pr oject Agreement solely 
for the purposes of administering certain terms relating to the rights of Lenders as set forth therein. Provided that all 
unperformed payment obligations of the  Borrower shall have been remedied in full or  waived by t he District, the 
Project Agreement provides the Collateral Agent, during an Event o f Default o r during the cure perio ds specified 
below following the occurrence  of a Borrower Default, the right to  take certain actions  in satisfaction o f the 
Borrower's obligations under the Project Agreement or otherwise exercise step in or substitution rights with respect 
to the Project Agreement upon the issuance of a step-in notice or substitution notice in accordance with the terms of 
the Project Agreement. The relevant cure periods in respect of Borrower Defaults are: 
(a) with respect to any Payment Default, the dat e falling thirty (30) days after the later of ( i) the date 
that the Collateral Agent r eceives the relevant District CA Te rmination Notice and (ii) expiration 
of any applicable cure period granted to the Borrower in accordance with the Project Agreement; 
(b) with respect to any  Bankruptcy-Related Default, the date falling six ty (60) days after the later of 
(i) the date that the Collateral Agent receives the relevant District CA Termination Notice and (ii) 
expiration of any applicable cu re period grant ed to the Borrower in accordance w ith the Proje ct 
Agreement; 
(c) with respect to a ny Non-Completion Default, the date  falling ninety ( 90) days after the dat e that 
the C ollateral Agent receives the  relevant District CA Te rmination Notic e; pro vided that su ch 
period will b e extended by such re asonable period of time as may be required to achieve Project 
Final Completion (subject to a maximum extension of one hundred eighty (180) days), but only to 
the extent that: (i) there is a reasonable prospect of achieving Project Final Completion within two 
hundred seventy (270) da ys of the relevant District CA Termina tion Notice and ( ii) within the  
B-31 
initial ninety (90)-day cure period, the Collateral Agent and the D istrict (each acting reasonably) 
agree to a plan in relation to achieving Project Final Completion; and 
(d) in the event o f any Borrower Default not referred to in clauses ( a), ( b), or (c ) abov e, t he date 
falling ninety (90) days after the later of ( i) the date that the Collateral Agent receives the relevant 
District CA Termination Notice and ( ii) ex piration o f any applicable  cure period granted to th e 
Borrower pursuant to the Pr oject Agreement; provided that such peri od will, at the request of the 
Collateral A gent, be exten ded up to a maximum of   thirty (30) additional days, b ut o nly to the 
extent that: (A) within the initial ninety (90)-day cure period provided f or in this clau se (ii), the 
Collateral Agent and the Dis trict (each acting re asonably) agree t o a p lan specifying the remedial 
action to be t aken with respect to the relevant Borrower Default and (B) the extension requested 
by the Collateral Agent represents (in the reasonable opinion of the District) a reasonable period of 
time to remedy the relevant Borrower Default. 
In addition, under certain circumstances where the Project Agreement has been rejected in bankruptcy, the 
Collateral Agent or the Collatera l Agent's de signee or assignee will have the a bility to e nter into a new Project  
Agreement with the District. T he new P roject Agreement  shall contain conditions,  a greements, terms, provisions 
and limitations which a re the same as t hose of  the Project Agreeme nt, except  for a ny obligations that have been 
fulfilled by the Borrower, any party acting on behalf of or stepping-in for Borrower, or the Collateral Agent prior to 
such rejection or termination.

Project Final Completion 
The D&C Contrac tor will provide a  notice to the Borrower once it has satisfied all con ditions se t forth

(b) With respect to the Project: 
(i) T he Project is fu lly operational as a single comprehensive system an d capable of bei ng 
operated full-time, uninterrupted, and continuously in accordance with the Technical Provisions. 
(ii) Each of the following components of the Improved Street Light Network has completed a 
period of continuous performance of not less than thirty (30) consecutive days: 
(A) O ne (1) Street Light Bundle in each Ward of the District; 
(B) with respect to each type of network communic ations technology incorporated 
into th e RM CS (i ncluding, as app licable, mesh netwo rk, star net work, and cellular 
network), ten per cent (10%)  of the to tal number of Light ing Units controlle d by each 
such network communications technology; 
(C) Ten percent (1 0%) o f the total nu mber of " Posttop" L ighting U nits incl uded 
within the Improved Street Light Network; and 
(D) Ten percent (10%) o f the tota l number o f "Cobrahead" Lighting Units included 
within the Improved Street Light Network.  
For purposes of the foreg oing, "continuous performance" means that, for a period of not less than 
thirty (3 0) consecutive days, the relevan t c omponent of t he Improve d Street L ight Network 
operates such that no Noncomplian ce Points wo uld accrue if the performance requ irements set 
forth in the Technical Provisions and Payment Mechanism of the Project Agreement were in effect 
during such thirty (30)-day period.   
(c) All establishment and commissioning procedures for the RMCS and AMIS have been successfully 
completed for  the Improved Street Light Network in accordance wit h the requir ements set fo rth in the Technic al 
Provisions. 
(d) All a ncillary items including equipment, supplies, spare parts and manuals are in place. 
(e) The D&C Contractor has del ivered to the Borrower all manufacturer war ranties in accor dance 
with the Technical Provisions. 
(f) A ll demobilization from the relevant Project Site is c omplete, including the removal of temporary 
work and eq uipment used in the perf ormance of t he D&C Work, but not required for the Asset Managemen t 
Services. 
(g) I f any Governmental E ntity with jur isdiction requires  any f orm of cer tification of  design, 
engineering or constructio n with respect to the Project, inc luding any certification from the Engineer of R ecord for 
the Project, the D&C Contractor has caused such c ertificates to be delivered and has concurrently issued identical 
certificates to the Borrower.

Performance Security 
The D&C Contractor is  required t o provide  the Borrower on or bef ore Fi nancial Close, a Performance 
Bond which is req uire to name Bor rower as the o bligee and the District and the C ollateral Agent as an addit ional 
obligee, in an amount equal to the DBC Price, from a surety with a credit r ating in the top two categories from two 
of the three (3) nationally recognized rating agencies or tha t is ra ted A/VIII or bett er according to the A .M. Best's 
Financial S trength Rating  and Fina ncial Size Category  and l isted on U .S. T reasury C ircular 570 to ens ure the 
completion of the D&C Work by the D&C Contractor.   
The D&C Con tractor is required to provide the Borrower on or before the Financial Close, a Payment Bond 
which is required to name Borrower as the obligee and the District and the Collateral Agent as an additional obligee, 
in an amount equal to the DBC Price, from a surety with a credit rating in the t op two categories from two of the 
three nationally recognized rating agencies or t hat is rated A/VIII or better according to the A.M.  Best's Financial 
Strength Rating and Financial Size Category and listed on U.S. Treasury Circular 570.   
Concurrent with the execution of t he D& C Contract, the D &C Contractor provided a parent co mpany 
guaranty in favor of the Borrower from the D&C Contrac tor Guarantor, in the form agreed in the D&C C ontract, 
guaranteeing payment all amounts  which become owi ng from D&C Contrac tor to the Borrower under the D&C 
Contract and Interface Agreement . See  " D&C CON TRACT AN D D&C GUARA NTEE—D& C G uarantee" for 
further details regarding the D&C Guarantee.  
Warranties 
The D&C Contractor warrants that the D&C Work is comple te and conform s to the D&C  Contract an d 
Good Industry Practi ce and the D&C Work, incl uding all materials a nd equipment f urnished a s pa rt of the D&C 
Work, are new (un less otherwise specified in the D&C  Contract) of good quality, and free of Defect s in mater ials 
and workmanship.  The Defects Warranty will be effective for a period of t wenty-four (24) months beginning on the 
Street Light Bundle Substantial Completion Date  for the appl icable Street L ight Bundle or the Smart City Bundle 
Substantial Completion Date for the app licable Smart City Bundl e.  The D&C Contr actor is further liable for the 
correction or rem edy of Latent Defects that are notified to t he D&C Contractor wi thin the applicable Latent Defect 
Period.  The Latent Defect Period is ten (10) years beginning on the  Street Light Bundle Sub stantial Completion 
Date for the applicable Street Light Bundle or the Smart City Bundle Substantial Completion Date for the applicable 
Smart City Bundle.    
C-10 
 
If the D&C Contractor is obliged to carry out work to remedy any Defect of which it was notified within 
the period of six  (6) months prior to the end of the applicable Defects Warranty Period or applicable Latent Defect 
Period, t hen, in respe ct o f such applicab le remedial work only, the relevant Defects Warranty Period or relevan t 
Latent Defect Period s hall be extended for a further period of six (6) months from the dat e such remedial work is 
completed to the satisfaction of the Borrower (acting reasonably). 
Within seven (7) days of receipt by the D&C Contr actor of notice from the Borrower during the applicable 
Defects Warranty Period specifying a De fect, the D&C Contractor and the Borrower shall agree when and how the 
D&C Contractor shall remedy such Defect in such manner and at such times as to minimize di sruption (to the extent 
commercially reasonable) to the operation of t he Project; provided, however, that in case of an emergency requiring 
immediate corrective action, the Borrower shall have the right to pe rform or have performed by third parties any 
immediately necessary Corre ctive Work ( after provid ing the D&C Contractor , if practicable u nder th e 
circumstances, notice of such eme rgency and an opport unity to perform the necessary C orrective Work), and the 
reasonable costs thereof shall be borne by the D& C Contractor . If the D&C  Contractor does not prom ptly and 
diligently c ommence the Correct ive Wo rk wit hin the agreed time, or should t he part ies fail to r each s uch an 
agreement concerning the existence of a Defect and scope of the Corrective Work within such seven (7) -day period, 
the Borrower, after notice to the D&C Contract or, shall have the right to perform or have pe rformed by third parties 
the necessary Corrective Work, and the reasonable costs thereof shall be borne by the D&C Contractor; subject to 
the D&C Contractor's right to contest the actual existence of a Defect an d Borrower's determination related thereto, 
in accordance with the Dispute Resolution Procedure. 
Within seven (7) days of receipt by th e D&C Contractor of notice from the Borrower specifying a Late nt 
Defect, the D&C Contractor and th e Borrower shall agree when and how such Latent  Defect shall be remedied in 
such manner and at such times as to minimize disruption (to the extent commercially reasonable) to the operation of 
the Project. In respect of t he D&C Contractor, the D&C Contractor shall not be re quired to carry out the Corrective 
Work but shall be given a reasonable opportunity to carry out, or cause to be carr ied out, the Corrective Work first 
(before the Borrower carries out, or causes to be c arried out, the Corrective Work in accordance with the provisions 
of the D&C Contract) and shall re main liable for the  cost of any Corrective Work incurred by the Borrowe r if the 
D&C Contractor does not carry out, or cause to be carried out, the Corrective Work. If the D&C Contractor agrees 
to carry out the Corrective Work and does not promptly and diligently do so within the agreed time, the parties agree 
that a person other than the D&C Contractor shall carry out the Corrective Work or the par ties fail to reach such an 
agreement within such seven (7)-day period, the Borrower, after notice to the D&C Contractor, shall have t he right 
to perform, or have performed by third parties, the necessary Corrective Work, and the reasonable costs thereof sh all 
be borne by the D&C Contractor; subject to th e D&C Contractor 's right to contest the actual  existence of a Laten t 
Defect and the Borrower's determination related thereto, in accordance with the Dispute Resolution Procedure. 
The D&C Contractor shall bear all costs in c o nnection wit h Corrective Work r equired to be performe d 
pursuant to the  w arranty provisions of the D &C Contract, a nd shall r eimburse o r pay the District' s and the  
Borrower's expenses made necessary thereby within ten (10) days after the Borrower's demand. 
Delay Liquidated Damages.  
The D&C Contractor is requ ired to pay Delay Liquidated Damages in the eve nt that it fails to complete (i) 
Milestone 1 by December 31 , 2023 in the amount of $81,000 per Calendar Quarter or partial Calendar Quarter; (ii) 
Milestone 2 by seven-hundred and thirty (730) days from the Financial Closing Date in the amount of $ 228,271 per 
Calendar Quarter or partial Calendar Quarter; and (iii) Project Final Completion by nine-hundred and ten (910) days 
from the Financial Closing Date in the amount of $82,076 per day or partial day. 
The ma ximum amount of Delay Liqu idated Damages pay able b y the D&C Contractor is $ 15,392,222. 
Delay Liquidated Damages are payable w hether or not t he Borrower incurs or m itigates damages.  The Borrower 
does no t have any obligation to mitigate any such damag es.  Each party to the D&C Contr act is estopped f rom 
alleging that the Delay Liquidated Damages are a penalty, or are othe rwise unenforceable for any reason , including 
that such damages are not incurred by the Borrower.   
Where Delay Liquidated Damages are incurred in respect of Project Final Completion the D&C Contractor 
is required to pay suc h amounts to the Borrower on a monthly basis by the last Business Day of the month in which  
C-11 
 
Delay Liquidated Damages were incurred.  Where Delay Liquidated Damages are incurred in respect of Milestone 1 
or Milestone 2, the D&C Contractor is required to pay  such amounts on the first d ay of each Quarter after the  date 
by which such Milestone 1 or Milestone 2 was r equired to  be co mpleted in order  to avoi d the payment of Delay 
Liquidated Damages. 
The dates which trigger the obligation to pay Delay Liquidated Da mages may be extended as a result of a 
Compensation Event or an Uninsured Relief Event including as a result of a Borrower Act, provided that in the case 
of a Compensation  Event or an Uninsured Relief Event and  only with respect to  the date by which Project Final 
Completion must be completed to av oid payment of Delay Liquidated Damages, such date will not be ex tended for 
the first 180 days of  any delay.  In the event a delay i s due to a Relief Event that is not an Uninsured Relief E vent, 
the applicable scheduled date set will not be extended. 
Achievement of Project Final Completion Mechanism.   
In certain circumstances, if it is disclosed in a payment application or shown in an updated Project Baseline 
Schedule t hat the D&C Contractor ma y achieve Project Fin al Completion more than th ree weeks after the 
Guaranteed Project Final Comp letion Date, the D&C Contractor may b e requ ired to  prepare and d eliver to the  
Borrower and the DBC Payment Certifier a plan which is reasonably achievable to accelerate the performance of the 
D&C Work to achieve Project Final Completion on or before the Guaranteed Project Final Completion Date or if the 
Guaranteed Project Final Completion Date has passed, as soon as is reasonably practicable. 
By agreement bet ween the D&C Contractor a nd the Borrower or absen t ag reement based  on a 
determination of  the D BC P ayment Certifier, an Actionable Acceleration Pla n and cost for acceleration m ay be 
established, in  which case th e D&C C ontractor is required to, at its cost a nd e xpense, carry out the Actionable 
Acceleration Plan.  In addition, in such circumstance, if directed by the Borrower, the D&C Contractor is required to 
pay to the  Borrower the agreed or estimat ed cost of acceleration, or  permit such amou nt to be set -off against 
available payments or provide a letter  of credit in respect of such amount to  the Borrower. The amount so paid , set-
off or provided in a letter of credit is required to be returned to the  D&C Contractor by payment or release of the 
letter of credi t as  it is determined tha t the estimated cost of acceleratio n has d ecreased or wh ere the event that 
triggered the obligation to  formulate the Actionable Acceleration Plan has been resolved.  Amounts paid, set-off or 
provided in a letter of cr edit erode the D&C Contractor's do not i ncrease the limit of  liability und er the  D&C 
Contract. 
Payments to the  D&C Contractor 
 Payments

obligations of the D&C Contractor under the D&C Contract.  The  D&C Contractor is required to submit mon thly 
payment applications, subject to review and audit b y the Borrower and the DBC Pa yment Certifier.  The Borrower 
has the right to withhold certain payments from the D&C Contractor. 
The D&C Contra ct also provides a monthly maximum aggregate construction payment schedule.  Under 
that schedule, the D&C Contract or may not seek to d raw more (in aggregate) than the amo unt provided for up to 
such month.  The maximum aggregate amount available to be drawn  under the schedule commences at $15,294,512 
as of Financi al Close and increases each during a twenty-three ( 23) month period.  The amount of $7,288,779 is 
payable in respect o f the 2 3rd mo nth, and is require d to be paid  within eight Business Days of the Project Final 
Completion Date.   
The DBC Payment Certifier is required to review the invoice submitted by the D&C Contractor each month 
and, subject to certain requirements set out in the D&C Contract, certify an amount that is due to be paid to the D&C 
Contractor in respect of the progress of the work performed as of such month, which in no event will be an amount 
greater than the maximum scheduled amount set out for such month in the D&C Contract.

Compensation Events 
The D&C Con tract provides that the D&C Contractor will receive compensation  from the Borro wer, i n 
addition to obtaining relief from compl ying with the d eadlines set forth therein in case of the occurrence of certain 
enumerated events, which include, among other things:  
(a) any material breach of an obligation under the Project Agreement by the District; 
(b) any violation of applicable Law by the District; 
(c) any Qualifying Change in Law; 
(d) the issuance by the Borrower of any Directive Letter; 
(e) a ny Required Action taken by the Borrower in the circumstances that is subject of certain District 
step-in rights described in the Project Agreement; 
(f) any suspension of the Conversion Work, the Smart City Work, or the  Asset Management Services 
that constitutes a Compensation Event pursuant to the Project Agreement; 
(g) any mater ial damage or inter ruption to, or interfe rence with, the Work caus ed by any 
Governmental Entity, t he D istrict of Columbia Office of Public- Private Par tnerships, the District o f Col umbia 
Department of Transportation, the Office of the Chie f Technology Officers, or contractors of any of the foregoi ng 
entities (other than the Borrower or any of the Borrower's Contractors), or in the case of any att achments or fixtures 
pursuant to the t erms of the Proj ect Agreement, any Utility Owner or any th ird party, on or i n the vicinity of a ny 
Project Site; 
(h) t he issuance by the District of any Safety Compliance Order that does not arise as a direct res ult of 
any Borrower-Related Entity's failure to comply with one or more Safety Standards; 
(i) the di scovery of any Hazard ous Materials on or under  any Project Site at any tim e after the  
Commercial Closing Date, including any Remedial Action related thereto, but only to the extent that the presence of 
such Hazardous Mat erials ( i) constitutes a Hazardous Environm ental Condi tion and (ii) doe s not const itute a 
Borrower Hazardous Materials Release; 
(j) the iss uance of any pre liminary or permanent i njunction or temporary restraining order or other 
similar order, legal restraint or prohibition by a Governmental Entity of competent jurisdiction under applicable Law 
that materially and adversely affects the District's or the Borrower's performance under the Project Agreement; 
(k) a ny (i) amendment or varia tion to the terms and  conditions of any District- Provided Appr oval 
after the Setting Date not previously agreed to by t he Borrower (including any variation to the terms and conditions 
of any extension or renewal of any final District-Provided Approval), as compared to those versions of such District-
Provided Approvals provided to the Borrower (including in draft form) as part of the Disclosed Information prior to 
the Setting Date or (ii) any invalidation or cancellation, in w hole or in part, of any District-P rovided Approval after 
the Setting Date; 
(l) the exercise by  the District of  its ri ght under th e Project  Agreement to uncover Work i n a 
circumstance considered a Compensation Event pursuant to the terms of the Project Agreement; 
(m) (i) failure by a Lice nsor to comply with its obligations under its License Agreem ent or a ny 
amendment thereto in such a manner that would result in a delay  to the Conve rsion Work (as set out in the Project 
Baseline Schedule) or the Asset Manag ement Se rvices (as set out in the Re newal Work Schedule ) o f more t han 
thirty (30) days; provid ed that the Borrower shall h ave conti nued to s atisfy the Conditions to Ass istance with 
Licensor for the duration of such failure to cooperate by the Licensor or (ii) any a mendment to a License Agreement 
or termination  thereof th at materially and adversely affects  the Borrower's p erformance under the P roject 
Agreement;

(n) the discovery of any Undisclosed Utility during the performance of the Conversion Work; 
(p) Vandalism; 
(q) the District directs th e Borrower to undertake Expansion Work in excess of the Expa nsion Work 
Allowance described in the Project Agreement; 
(r) the discove ry of any Co mbination Pole f or which the Borrower is required to ins tall separate 
conduit pursuant to the Technical Provisions of the Project Agreement;  
(s) an act of Terrorism; or 
(t) a Borrower Act, 
except, in each case, to the extent att ributable to any breach of t he Project A greement, applicable Law or 
any Governmental Approval by, or any negligent act or negligent omission of, a Borrower-Related Entity. 
If a Compe nsation Event directly causes, or is reasonably likely to dir ectly cause, the D&C Con tractor to 
do any one or more of the following: 
(a) fail to achieve  Project Final C ompletion b y t he G uaranteed Project Final Completion Date or, 
following the Guaranteed Project Final Completion Date, incur further delay in achieving Project Final Completion; 
(b) fail to comply with its obligations under the D&C Contract; or 
(c) incur additional or increased costs, 
the D&C Contractor may claim: 
(i) an ex tension of the Guaranteed Project Fi nal Comp letion Date or , following the  
Guaranteed Project Final Completion Date, the D&C Long Stop Deadline; 
(ii) relief from compliance with its obligations under D&C Contract;  
(iii) compensation for any Change in Costs that the D&C Contractor has incurred or will incur 
as a direct result of such Compensation Event; or 
(iv) relief from any rights  of the Borrower with respect to te rmination for D&C Contracto r 
Default. 
Procedure for Rel ief a nd Compensation. Subject t o the late provision o f notice requirements set out in the  
D&C Contract, any claim made by the D&C Contractor for a Compensation Event must: 
(a) be su bmitted to the Borrower promptly (and in any e vent within twenty (20) days ) after the date 
that the D&C Contractor first became aware that the relevant Compensation Event had occurred and would have the 
effect that is the subject of the D&C Contractor's claim; 
(b) promptly, and in any event within fifty (50) days, after the D&C C ontractor first became aware of 
the relevant Compensation Event, the D&C Contrac tor must give ful l details of the  r elevant Compensation Event 
and the extension of time and/or any c ompensation claimed, including an enumerated list of items that include s but 
is not limited to: 
(i) with respect to any claim for an extension of time, a Time Impact Analysis demonstrating 
the relevant Compensation Event will result in an ide ntifiable and  measurable di sruption t o the 
D&C Work affecting a critical path activity;  
C-14 
 
(ii) details of the steps the D&C Contractor has taken or will take to mitigate the effect of the 
Compensation Event; 
(iii) the type and  amount of insurance t hat may be applicable and amounts tha t have been or 
are anticipated to be collected under such insurance;  
(iv) evidence reasonably satisfa ctory to the Borrower demonstrating that no other co ncurrent 
or unrelated de lay to a critical path activity that is the Borrower's responsibility has occurred that 
has contributed to the delay for which relief is being sought; and 
(c) demonstrate to the reasonable satisfaction of the Borrower that: 
(i) the Compensation Event was the direct cause or is reasonably likely to be the direct cause 
of: 
(A) a de lay in ach ieving Project Final Completion by the Guaranteed Projec t Fi nal 
Completion Date or (following t he Guaranteed Project Final Completion Date, further  delay i n 
achieving Project Final Completion; 
(B) the D&C Contrac tor's inab ility to co mply with its  obligations under the D&C 
Contract which are obligations of the Borrower under the Project Agreement; and/or 
(C) the D&C Contractor incurring a Change in Costs; and 
(ii) t he Change in Costs, extensio n of  time, and/or relief from ob ligations under the D&C  
Contract cl aimed, could not reasonably be  mitigated or re covered w ithout material cost by the 
D&C Contractor a cting in accordance with Good Industry Practice , incl uding by re-sequencing, 
reallocating, or redeploying its forces to other portions of the Conversion Work and/or Smart City 
Work. 
Giving of Relief and Compensat io n. If the D&C Contractor sati sfies the conditions described above, then 
the D&C Contractor will b e entitled  to an ex tension of time,  relief from obligatio ns, or com pensation (as 
applicable), as follows: 
(a) in the case of a delay  t he Guaranteed Project F inal Co mpletion Date and the D& C Long S top 
Deadline w ill be extended by such time as is reasonabl e, based on the Time Impact Analysis,  for such a 
Compensation Event; 
(b) in the case of Capi tal E xpenditures or any other Change in Co sts arising prio r to Proj ect Final 
Completion, the Borrower shall, in respect of a Compensation Event claim that is a Pass Through Claim, within five 
(5) Busin ess Days of receipt from the District and  in respect o f a  Compensation Event claim that is not a  Pass 
Through Claim within thirty (30) days of t he Borrower's determination as to the D&C Contractor's entitlement to 
compensation, compensate the D&C Contracto r fo r the relevant Cap ital Expenditure o r Change in C osts (e ach as 
adjusted to reflect the actual Capital Expenditure or Change in Costs incurred by the D&C Contractor) that the D&C 
Contractor incurs as a direct result of the relevant Compensation Event; 
(c) i n the case of any Change in Costs th at is not the subject of clause (b) above, the Borrower shall 
compensate the D&C Contractor in accordance with the financial model adjustment provisions set out in the Project 
Agreement or in such other manner as the parties to the D&C Contract may agree (acting reasonably); 
(d) if any D&C Noncompliance Event  would not have occurred but  for the occurren ce of the 
Compensation Event, such D&C Noncompliance Event will be deemed to ha ve not occurred for the purposes of th e 
D&C Contract and no Noncompliance Poi nts or  Deductions will be assessed by the Borrow er in respect of such 
event, provided that in respect of any Compen sation Event claim that is a Pass Through Claim, such relie f shall be 
limited to the relief received by the Borrower pursuant to the Project Agreement;

T
he D&C Contract provides that the D&C Co ntractor will obtain relief from complying with the d eadlines

Contract are the same as the Relief Events under the Project Agreement.

condition relief unde r th e D&C Co ntract are si milar to those in the  Project Agreement, except that certain time 
periods applicable to the D&C Contractor  are shorter under the D &C Contract, the D&C Contractor is required to

claims in respect of Relief Events will be Pass Through Claims.

Changes 
Borrower C hanges . To the extent applicable to the D&C Work, the B orrower may issue a District Cha nge 
Request received by the Borrower from the District pursuant to the Project Agreement to the D&C Contractor, such 
a change being a Borrower Change under the D&C Contract. 
The D&C Co ntractor may refuse a District Change Request that would, if implemented:  
(a) r equire the D&C Work to be performed in a way that violates applicable Law; 
(b) contravene a  Governmental Ap proval then in  full force an d effe ct; provided th at s uch 
contravention cannot be corrected by the issuance of  a new or revised Gover nmental Approval that can be ob tained 
by the D&C Contractor; 
(c) materially adversely affect the health and safety of any Person; 
(d) materially and adv ersely change the nat ure of the P roject or the allocation of risk b etween the 
Parties;  
(e) be unrelated to the kind of work initially procured under the RFP and to the ki nd of work that PA 
Borrower-Related Entities provide in the marketplace; or 
(f) require t he D&C Work to be  performed in a way  that results in a breach o f the terms of  any 
License Agreement. 
The D&C Contractor acknowledges and agrees that  any refusal by the  D&C C ontractor of a District 
Change Request shall be a Pass Through Claim. 
Promptly (and in any event within ten (10) Business Days) after having received a District Change Request, 
the D&C Contractor shall deliver to the Borrower an estimate of costs and  expenses and other matters with respect 
to such District Change Request (a " D&C Cont ractor Estimate"), or confirmation as to when a D&C Contractor 
Estimate is to be provided to th e Borrower (provided, that the D&C Contractor shall use a ll reasonable efforts to 
prepare its D&C Contractor Estimate within thirty-one (31) days after having received the District Change Request, 
unless further extended by the B orrower acting reasonably). Promptly (and in any event within seven (7) Business 
Days) after the Borrower receives the D&C Contractor Estimate, the D&C Contractor shall meet with the Borrower 
to review, disc uss and seek to agree upon the D&C Contractor Estimate. The D& C Contractor sh all provide all 
assistance reasonably requested by the B orrower in respect of the meetings between the District and the Borrower in 
respect of any Borrower Estimate pursuant to the P roject Agreement, including agreeing upon the D&C Contracto r 
Estimate associated with the applicable Borrower Estimate under the Project Agreement. 
The Borrower  shall deliver notice to the D&C Contractor of any modification to a District Change Request 
received by the Borrower pursuant to the Project Agreement, and, subject to t he terms of the D &C Contract, the 
D&C C ontractor shall c omply with any such notic e an d w ithin te n (10) Bus iness Days or such longer period as 
agreed to by  the Borrower (acting reason ably), after receipt of such modification, not ify the Borro wer of any 
consequential changes to the D&C Contractor Estimate. 
Within f ive (5) Busi ness Days o f re ceipt by the Borrower of a District Cha nge Orde r o r notification  of 
withdrawal of a District C hange Request from the District, the Borrower shall either accept the D &C Contractor 
Estimate by issuing a notice to the D&C Contractor in accordance with a District Change Order received pursuant to 
the Project Agreement or notify the D&C Contractor that the Borrower withdraws the District Change Request. 
If the Borrower accepts the D&C Contractor Estimate (as may be modified), the D&C Contractor shall use 
reasonable efforts to implement the rel evant extra work in accordance with the D&C Contractor Estimate and  the 
Borrower shall make a payment to the D&C Contractor within five (5) Business Days of receiving payment from the 
District in respect of each inv oice (complete in all material respects) in acco rdance with the agreed -upon payment

schedule, accompanied by the evidence reasonably acceptable to the Borrower (where applicable) that the relevant
