D&C Contractor Indemnity 
To the fullest extent permitted by applicable Law, the D&C Contractor shall release, defend, indemnify and 
hold harmless the Borrower Indemnified Parties and the Indemnified Parties on demand from and against any and all 
liability to third parties for  Losses arising from (a) death or perso nal injury, (b) loss of or da mage to any Borrower 
Indemnified Party or Indemnifie d P arty's pro perty, and (c) third party  claims brought agai nst a ny Borrower 
Indemnified Party or Indemnified Party, which may arise  out of, or in consequence of, any or all of the following: 
any actual or  alleged failure by t he D&C Con tractor to comply with, observe or  perform any o f the covena nts, 
obligations, agreements, terms or co nditions in the Project Documents or any actual or alleged breach by the D&C 
Contractor of its representations or warranties set forth in the D&C Contract. 
The D&C  Contractor w ill n ot be responsible or obliged to in demnify a Borrower I ndemnified Party or 
Indemnified Party with respect to any liability or Losses to the extent that the same arise as a dir ect result of: (a) a 
Compensation Event, Relief Event, o r Borrower Default (D&C Contract); (b) the pre sence of Hazar dous Materials 
on any Pro ject Site for wh ich the  District is, pu rsuant to the Project Agre ement, or for wh ich the Borrower i s 
pursuant to the D&C Contract, deemed to be the sole generator and arranger, but only to the extent that the relevant 
loss does not  arise as a direct r esult of the negligence of t he D&C Contracto r or the D&C Contractor f ailing to 
comply with the te rms of the D&C  Contract; ( c) any  non-performance by a Borrower Indemnified Part y of its  
obligations under the D&C Contract, or any non- performance by an Indemnified  Party of its ob ligations under the 
Project Agreement or any vi olation of applicable Law by any Borrower Indemnified Party or Indemnified Party; or 
(d) the  fraud, negligence, reck lessness, bad  faith or wil lful misc onduct of a Borro wer Indemnified Party or 
Indemnified Party. 
Limitation on D&C Contractor's Liability   
Notwithstanding a ny other prov isions of t he D&C Contr act, the D&C Cont ractor's maximum total liability 
under the D&C Contract and the I nterface Agreement, inclusive of t he capped amount of Delay  Liquidated 
Damages, sh all, subject to the following exclusio ns, not exc eed 35% of the DBC P rice.  The D&C Contract or 
Liability Cap is exclusive of:  
(a) any insurance proceeds receiv ed pursuant t o pol icies of  insurance to the extent required under the D& C 
Contract and any li ability for Losses that would have been cover ed by such insurance up to the amou nt of 
the required insurance and deductibles but in respect of which insurance proceeds are not available due to a 
failure on the part of the D&C Contractor, or any D&C Contractor-Related Entity, as applicable, to comply 
with its insurance obligations as set forth in the D&C Contract;  
(b) any claim by the Borrower in respect of frau d, gross negligence, willful misconduct and deliberate acts of 
wrongdoing by the D&C Contractor or any D&C Contractor-Related Entity;  
(c) liabilities arising under any Encumbrance created or caused by the D&C Contractor or parties for whom the 
D&C Contractor is responsible;  
(d) any claim for personal injury or death;  
(e) third party claims in tort against the D&C Contractor to the extent such claims arise from the negligence or 
breach of contract of the D&C Contractor or parties for whom the D&C Contractor is responsible;  
(f) third party c laims in tort against the Borrower (excluding c laims by the  Lenders, the Borrowe r and 
Borrower-Related Entity(ies) and th e A sset Ma nagement Contractor and A sset Management Co ntractor-
Related Entity(ies));  
(g) third party claims in tort against th e Lenders or a Borrower-Related Entity for which indemnity is sought 
against the Borrower to the extent such claims arise from the negligence or breach of contract of the D&C 
Contractor or parties for whom the D&C Contractor is responsible;

(h) third party cl aims relating  to i ntellectual property to the extent such cl aims are cau sed b y t he D&C 
Contractor;  
(i) amounts paid to the Borrower by the D&C C ontractor to the extent that same are subsequent ly recovered 
by the D&C Contractor from the Asset Management Contractor pursuant to the Interface Agreement;  
(j) amounts p aid by t he D&C Contr actor which a re subsequently repaid to the D &C Co ntractor b y t he 
Borrower, as required pursuant to any bankruptcy or insolvency legislation;

Unavailability Events and Noncompliance Events 
The D&C C ontract cont ains a r egime pursuant to wh ich Ded uctions a re assessed against the D&C 
Contractor, based on a list of breaches or failures in performance of obligations of the D&C Contractor identified the 
D&C Contract. Unavailability Events and Noncompliance Events include, but are not limited to,  failure to prov ide 
certain notifications, failure to obse rve or comp ly with various project delivery protocols or breach of performance 
standards. The D&C Contractor has the benefit of certain cure periods and/or grace periods for c ertain breaches and 
failures which are based on the type of breach or failure. Th e D&C Contractor is required to notify the District, with 
a copy to the Borrower of any No ncompliance Event rel ated to the D&C Work or the actions or omissions of the 
D&C Contractor.  The Dis trict is permi tted to n otify the Borrower of Noncompliance Events and Unavai lability 
Failures and the Borrower shall notify the D&C Contractor of such where related to the D&C Work or any action or 
omission of the D &C Contr actor.  Noncompli ance Points, Non compliance Ded uctions and Unavailability 
Deductions applica ble u nder the D&C  Contract shall be dete rmined i n accordance with  the t erms of the Project 
Agreement. 
The D&C C ontractor i s liable  for Noncompliance Points,  and ass ociated Dedu ctions, resulting fro m 
Noncompliance Events related to the D&C Work or actions o r omissions of th e D&C Contractor.  The num ber of 
Noncompliance P oints assessed under the D&C Contract is required to  be t he same as the number of 
Noncompliance Poi nts assessed  pursuant to the Proj ect Agr eement, to the e xtent rel ated to any D&C Work or 
actions or omissions of the D&C Contractor.  The D&C Contractor is liable for Unavailability Deductions res ulting 
from Unavailability Events related to the D&C  Work or actions or omissions of the D&C C ontractor.  T he amount 
of Unava ilability Deductions assess ed und er the D&C Contract is required to  be t he same as t he amount of 
Unavailability Deductions assessed pursuant to the Project Agreement, to the extent related to  any D&C Work or 
actions or omissions of the D&C Contractor.

Rights of the Borrower Following a D&C Contractor Default 
The D&C Contract provides for a number of de fault events by the D&C C ontractor. Such default events 
include any one or more of the following (each a "D&C Contractor Default"): 
(a) the D&C Contractor Abandons the Project;  
(b) the D&C Contractor fails to achieve Project F inal Completion by the date that is ninety (90) days after 
the Guaranteed Project Final Completion Date as extended in accordance with the D&C Contract;  
(c) the D&C Co ntractor assigns or transfer s any of its right s or obli gations und er the D&C C ontract in 
breach of the terms of the D&C Contract;  
(d) during the D&C Period, an I nsolvency Event arises  with respect to the  D&C C ontractor or D&C  
Contractor Guarantor;  
(e) the D&C Contractor fails to pa y any amount due to the Borrower un der the D&C Con tract when due, 
except to the extent such payment is subject to a Good Faith Dispute;  
(f) any representation or warranty ma de by th e D&C C ontractor in the D&C Contr act or any cer tificate, 
schedule, report, instrument or oth er document deliv ered to the Borrower pursuant to the D&C Contract is f alse or 
materially misleading or inaccurate when made, in each case in any material r espect, or omits material information 
when made;  
(g) t he D&C Co ntractor fails to comply in  any material respect w ith any Government al Appr oval or 
applicable Law;  
(h) the D&C Contractor fails to promptly  comply with any written suspension of D&C Work issu ed by the 
Borrower or District in accordan ce with certain terms of the D&C Contrac t, except to the e xtent that such f ailure 
arises as a direct result of a Compensation Event or a Relief Event;  
(i) subjec t to relief granted pursuant to a Relief Event, a n Unavailability D &C Default  Trigger or a 
Noncompliance D&C Default Trigger occurs;  
(j) the D&C Contra ctor fails to obtain, provide an d maint ain the D& C Contractor Insurance s or the 
Performance Bond or the Payment Bond in accordance with the requirements of the D&C Contract;  
(k) a D&C Pe rsistent Breach occurs;  
(l) there occurs any final , non-appealable suspension or d ebarment (distinguished from ineligibility due to 
lack of  f inancial qualifications ), or th ere goes int o effect an agreement for voluntary e xclusion, from bidding, 
proposing or contracting with any federal or District of Columbia department or agency of D&C Contractor;  
(m) without limiting any other specifically  s pecified event of defau lt, the D&C Contractor b reaches any 
other material obliga tion under  the D&C Contract, other than a br each for  which a Noncom pliance Point was or 
could have been  assessed; or a breach that aris es as a  direct re sult of the occurrence o f a Compensation Event or 
Relief Event; or the D&C Contractor makes any written repudiation of the D&C Contract;  
(n) there exists a Borrower Default under the terms of the Project Agreement, the caus e of which is directly 
attributable to any breach by t he D&C Contractor under t he D&C C ontract, or a ny negligent or wrongful ac t or 
omission by the D&C Contractor or any applicable D&C Contractor-Related Entity;  
(o) (a) the D&C Contractor  Guarantor breaches the D&C Guar antee delivered by it or takes the position 
that the D&C Guarantee is invalid or unenforceable or (b) the D&C Guarantee ceases to be in full force and effect or

D&C Contractor Default Termination Sum 
In the event of such termination, subject to the D&C Contractor Liability Cap, if the Project Agreement has 
not been terminated, the D&C Contrac tor is obligated to pa y damages to the Borrower to compensate the Borrower 
for losses incurred by the Borrower for delays to completion c aused by D&C Contractor on the termination of the 
D&C Cont ract, any accr ued liability of the D&C Contr actor remainin g unpaid, the addi tional costs incurred or 
reasonably proj ected to be incur red by t he Borrower in conne ction with procuring one or m ore r eplacement 
Contractors t o c omplete the D &C Work, the costs to  make good any  Defects which are the D&C Con tractor's 
responsibility and any  increase in the costs  of the D&C  Work over the DBC Price which the Borrower incurs in 
securing another contractor to complete the D&C Work and if the Project Agreement has been terminated, the D&C 
Contractor is obligated to pay damages to the Borrower in the amount required to discharge all outstanding liabilities 
of the Borrower under the Finance Documents including but not limited to the Project Debt and the Equ ity Member 
Funding Agreements, to the ex tent that the Borrower is not c ompensated for same by the Dis trict under the  Project 
Agreement.  
Other Termination R ights; Effect of Termination 
Termination for Borrower Default 
Subject to the appl icable cure peri ods, the D&C Contract or is entitled  to term inate the D&C Contract  
following one of the following defaults ("Borrower Default (D&C Contract)"): 
(a) the Borrower fails to pay an amount when due to the D&C C ontractor, provided that (i) such amount is 
not being disputed in good fa ith; (ii) a no tice of default of payment has  been given to the Borrower and the default 
by the Borrower continues during a period of sixty (60) consecutive days following such notice; (iii) the outstanding 
amounts payable to the D&C Contractor, individually or in the aggregate, exceed $250,000 (inde x-linked); (iv) the 
D&C Contractor has no right to exercise (and has not exercised) any set off rights in respect of such amount; and (v) 
such payment is not related to a failure of the Borrower to receive a corresponding payment from the District;   
C-25 
 
(b) any representation or w arranty made by th e Borrower under the D&C Contract is false, misl eading or 
inaccurate when made in each case in any material respect or omits material information when made;  
(c) the Borrower committing a material breach of its obligations under the D&C Contract, which bre ach 
materially adversely affects the ability of the D&C Contractor to perform its obligations under the D&C Contract for 
a continuous period of at least sixty (60) days following written notice from the D&C Contractor to the Borrower of 
such m aterial breach  (or, p rovided that the Borrower  is diligently taking all commercially reasonable s teps to 
remedy the such default, such lo nger period as is  reasonable in the circumstances, but in any c ase not exceeding in 
total 90 consecutive days following such notice), provided that any such brea ch is not due to an act or omi ssion of 
the District;  
(d) any condemnation or other taking by eminent domain by the District or any other Governmental Entity 
of all or any  material portion of the Street Lig ht Network; provided t hat, for purpo se of the fore going, a material 
portion of t he Street Light Network means more than fifty (50%) of t he Lighting Units included in the Str eet Light 
Network; and  
(e) an Insolvency Event arises in respect of the Borrower. 
Notice and Cure P eriods.  The D&C Contractor is required to provide written notice to the Borrower upon 
the occurrence of  a Borrower Default (D&C Contract) as wel l as on the  occurrence of a breach specified i n clause 
(a) or (c) abov e in t he definitio n of Borrower Default (D&C C ontract). Upon receipt of a d efault notic e, the 
Borrower will h ave the following cure periods : for a Borrower Default (D&C Contract) under clause (b) o r (d ) 
above in the definition of Borrower Default (D&C Contract), a period of thirty (30) days after the Borrower receives 
the Borrower Default Notice; or if, despite t he Borrower's commencement of meaningful steps to cure immediately 
after recei ving the B orrower D efault Notice, the Borrowe r Default (D&C Contract)  cannot b e cured wi thin such 
thirty (30) day-period, the Borrower will have such additional period of time, up to a maximum cure period of ninety 
(90) days after the Bor rower receives the Borrower Default Notice, as is reasonably necessa ry to cure the Borrower  
Default (D&C Contract); and for a  Borrower Default (D&C Contract) under clause (e)  above in the d efinition of 
Borrower Default (D&C Co ntract), ther e is no cure period .  A Borrower De fault unde r clause (b) above in the 
definition of Borrower Default (D&C Contract) will be regarded as cured when the adverse effects of such Borrower 
Default (D&C Contract) are cured. 
D&C Contractor Remedies Upon Borro wer Default. In the event a notice of a breach pursuant to clause (a) 
above in the de finition of Borrower De fault ( D&C Contract) has been delivered  by th e D&C C ontractor to the 
Borrower in respect of an amount of $250,000 or more that is not rela ted to the failure of the Borrower to receive a 
corresponding payment from the District; or of an event that woul d have entitled the D&C Contractor to deliver a 
notice for breach pursuant to clause (a) above in the definition of Borrower Default (D&C Contract) in respect of an 
amount of $250,000 or more but for t he fact that the payment default is d ue to a failure o f the District to pay the 
Borrower and pr ovided in such c ase that the Borrower has a right t o suspend i ts perfo rmance of the Work in 
accordance with the Proj ect Agreement, th e D&C Contractor m ay suspend  its per formance of the D&C Work 
subject to the followi ng: the  D&C Contractor shall provide th e Borrower and the  Collateral Agen t with  notice 
regarding its intent to suspend at least thirty -five (35) days before implementing the suspension and t he suspension 
shall be required to be lifted u pon the D&C Contractor's receipt of payment in full of all undisputed amounts it is 
owed or in the case of a su spension where the payment failure is due to a failu re of the Dis trict to make payment to 
the Borrower, the D&C Contra ctor shall lift such suspe nsion in the event the Borrower no longer has the  right to 
suspend. 
The ri ght to suspend may be ex ercised without prejud ice to the D&C Contractor's other rights and  relief 
arising with respect t o a Borrower Act, including its right to terminate for  Borrower D efaults (D&C Contract) in

To the extent related to the D &C Work or  the actions o r omissions of the D&C C ontractor, the District is 
permitted to take action under the D&C Contract to the exte nt that it is permitted to take action in accordance with 
the step -in provisions of t he Proj ect Agreement, a nd a ny claims by the D&C  Contractor in  respect of any such 
actions shall be Pass Through Claim s.  S ee "APPENDIX B—SU MMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE 
PROJECT AGREEMENT—D istrict Right to Step-In" for a summary of the rights of the District to step-in under the 
Project Agreement and the conditions applicable thereto.   
The Borrower is required to pr ovide to the D&C Contr actor any notice received by the Borrower from the 
District relating to the District's intention to step -in, in accordance with the terms of the Project Agreement and the 
District may take such actions under the D&C Contr act and the D &C Contractor is requi red to use  Reasonable 
Efforts to give all assistance requested by the District or the Borrower. 
If the actions  of the District did not result from the Borrower breaching its obli gations under the Project 
Agreement, the D&C Contractor shall be entitled to a ny relief from performance that the Borrower i s e ntitled to 
under the Project Agreement and the D&C Contractor shall be entitled to bring a Pass Through Claim in respect of 
the costs of any reasonable assistance provided to the Distric t.  If the act ions of  the District resulted f rom the

Borrower breaching its obligations under the Project Agreement, to the extent t hat the Borrower is relieved from

Project Ag reement related t o t he D&C Work are deemed to not h ave occu rred, suc h Unavailab ility Event an d 
Noncompliance Event will be deemed to have not occurred under  the D&C Contract and to the extent related to the

Borrower Right to Step-In 
If the Borrower accrues 75% of either of the Noncomplia nce Points or Una vailability Deductions 
thresholds set out in the definitions of Noncompliance Borrower Default Trigger or Unavailability Borrower Default 
Trigger due to N oncompliance Points or Unav ailability Deductions, as app licable, accrued by both of t he D&C 
Contractor and the  Asset Management Contractor under their res pective subcontracts,  Borrower may exercise its 
step-in rights in  accordance with the subcontract of the Subcontractor that accumulated the most Noncompliance 
Points or Unavailability Deductions, as applicable. 
If any such event has occurred and the Borrower wishes to take step-in action under the D&C Contract, the 
Borrower must notify the D&C Contractor, in writing, of the action the Borrower wishes to take, the reason for such 
action, the date it wishes to commence such action, the time period that it believe s will be necessary for such action, 
and, to the e xtent practicable, the effect such act ion will have on the D&C Contractor 's obligation to carry out the  
D&C Work. Follow ing service of such notice, the Borro wer may take such actions previously descri bed or 
otherwise as in the  opinion of the Bo rrower reasonably requi red to address t he matters tha t resulted in the 
entitlement of the Borrower to exercise its st ep-in right.  T he D&C Contractor will use reasonable  efforts to give 
assistance to the Borrower while it is tak ing such action. The Borrower is required to provide t he D&C Contractor 
with no tice of completion of such action and shall use reasonable effor ts to provide suc h advance no tice as is 
reasonably practicable of its anticipated completion. 
The D& C C ont ractor shall pay to the Borrower all of  the reasonable cos ts incurred by t he Borrower in 
taking any such action. 
Transfer Restrictions 
The D&C Contrac tor is not permitted to assign or transfer any of its rights or obligations under the D &C 
Contract without the written consent of the Borrower, which the D&C Contractor acknowledges shall not be granted 
unless permitted pursuant to the Finance Documents. 
Restrictions on Change in Ownership 
The D&C C ontractor shall ensure that there will be no sal e, trans fer, o r o ther di sposal of any direct or 
indirect legal, benefici al, equitable or  other ow nership interest in the D&C Contractor or the D&C Contractor 
Guarantor which, when taken togethe r with any other such tra nsfer or disposal during the rel evant period gives rise 
to a Change of Control of the D&C Contractor or the D&C Contractor Guarantor; grant of any security interest, lien 
or other encumbrance over any l egal, beneficial, equitable or other direct or indirect ownership interest in the D&C 
Contractor or the D&C Contractor Guarantor giving rise to a Change of Control of the D&C Contractor or the D&C 
Contractor Guarantor, as applicable; or agreement, whether or  not subject to any condition precedent or subsequent, 
to do any of the foregoing, ( collectively a "Change of Control Transaction"), without the prior written consent of the 
Borrower, su ch consent not to be withheld or delayed where (a ) the D&C Contracto r can demonstrate to the 
satisfaction of the Borrower, acting reasonably, that the D &C Contractor wi ll have a level of financial capacity 
(taking into account the financial strength of the D&C C ontractor and the D&C Contractor Guarantor), resources, 
and ability to perform  the D&C W ork after the Change of Cont rol Transaction that is equal to or greater th an the  
C-29 
 
level of financial capacity, resources, and ability to perform the D&C Work held by the D&C Contractor before the 
Change of Control Transaction and (b) the Change of Control Transaction will not have a Material Adverse Effect. 
Disputes 
Except for disputes related to equivalent project relief, all disputes arising under the D&C Contract will be 
resolved pursuant to the dispute resolutions procedures.  I f a dispute arises, a party to the dispute shall have the right 
to request a meeting for resolution of the dispute among officers designated for such purpose. See "APPENDIX C—
SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE D&C CONTRACT—Equivalent Project Relief."   
In the event the par ties fail to resolve the dispute pursuant to the meeting of officers, and timely resolution 
of the dispute is required to meet any schedule relevant to the execution by a party of its obligations under the D&C 
Contract, or the dispute inv olves a claim the monetary valu e of which is estimate d to be less than or equal to 
$5,000,000, then either party may be written notice to the other require that the dispute be resolved on an exped ited 
basis i n accordance with a binding fa st trac k arbitration process.  The a rbitrator is required to issue a written 
decision as soon as practicable and in any event within twenty (20) days of the delivery of the noti ce o f dispute, 
which decision shall be final and binding.  All other disputes shall be determined pursuant to arbitration. 
The D &C Contractor is requi red to proceed diligently with performance of the  D&C Work pendi ng 
resolution of any Dispute, including any D& C Work that is t he subject of the  Dispute, except for any performance 
Borrower determines in writing should be de layed, suspended or terminated as  a result of such Dispute. The D&C 
Work shall continue without pr ejudice to t he right  to co ntest, dispute and challenge the re levant matter in 
accordance with the provisions of the D&C Contract. 
Governing Law and Jurisdiction 
The D&C Contract will be gover ned by and construed in accordance with the laws of the  District of 
Columbia.  The D& C Cont ractor and the Borro wer consents t o the jurisdiction of any  court of the District of 
Columbia, waiving any claim or defense that such forum is not convenient or prop er. The D&C Contractor and the 
Borrower agree that such court shall have in personam jurisdiction over it, and consents to service of process in any 
manner authorized by applicable Law. 
Relations hip Be tween District Determination and Borrower Determination 
Where, in accordance with t he provisi ons of t he D&C Contract , a determinatio n, actio n, negotiation, 
decision, discretion, election or other thing is made, taken, done, exercised or elected by the Borrower in respect of a 
matter in ac cordance with a determ ination, action, ne gotiation, decision, discre tion, election  or o ther thing m ade, 
taken, done, exercised or elected by the Dis trict in respect of the same matter pursuant of the Project Agr eement, to 
the extent that D&C Contractor disagrees with such determination, action, negotiation, decision, discretion, election 
or other thing, D&C Contractor's sole reme dy, to  the extent permitted in accor dance w ith the te rms of the D&C 
Contract is to bring a Pass Through Claim, a claim against the District pursuant to the Project Agreement.  
Equivalent Project Relief  
 Certain Defined Terms 
A "Corresponding Project Agreeme nt C laim" means ( a) with respect to a  Pass T hrough Claim, the 
corresponding claim that the Borrower may assert against the District under the Project Agreement, as described in 
in accordance with the D&C Contract, and (b) with respect to a D istrict Claim, the corresponding cl aim that the 
District has asserted against Borrower under the Project Agreement. 
"District Claim" means any claim that the Borrower may assert against the D&C Contractor under D&C 
Contract where the District has asserted a corresponding claim against the Bo rrower under the Project Agreement, 
including a claim by the District tha t the Borrower is in b reach o f the  Project Agreement where such breach is 
caused by a b reach by the D& C Contractor of the D&C Contrac t and a claim by the District inv olving events or   
C-30 
 
circumstances giving rise to a possible claim by Borrower against D&C Contractor under the D&C Contract or other 
remedies, compensation or reimbursement for Borrower under the D&C Contract. 
A "Project Agreement Dispute Proceeding" means with respect to a P ass Through Claim or District Claim, 
the proceeding under the Project Agreement by which the Corresponding Project Agreement Claim is resolved. 
A "Pass Through Claim" means any claim that the D&C Contractor may assert against the Borrower under 
the D&C Co ntract where th e Borrower may assert a corresponding claim against th e Dis trict under the Proj ect 
Agreement, including a claim for which the cause is a Relief Event, Compensati on Event or Force Majeure Event, a 
claim by the D&C Contractor that the Borrower is in breach of the D&C Contract where such breach is caused by a 
breach by the Distric t of the  Project Agr eement, and a claim by  the D&C Contractor involving events or 
circumstances giving ris e to a possible claim  by the Borrower against the District under the Pr oject Agreement or 
other remedies, compensation, relief or reimbursement for the Borrower under the Project Agreement. 
A "Borrower Act" means, with respect to a Pass T hrough Claim, any breach of Borrower's obligations 
under D&C Contract or any negligent  act or omission of Borrower that materially adversely impairs the validity of, 
or the rel ief ava ilable from, the District f or the Corresponding Project Agreement Claim  o r a  Borrower Default 
(D&C Contract). 
Pass Through Claims 
The D&C Contractor is r equired to give written notice to the  Borrower as soon as reasonabl y practicable 
upon becoming aware of any events or circumstances giving rise to a possible Pass Through Claim  and in any event 
within any applicable time periods required under the D&C Contract.  
Whenever the Borrower is permitted to do so by the terms of the Project Agreement and the D&C Contract, 
the Borrower shall in good faith, unless it determines it would b e manifestly frivolous or vexatious to do so,  present 
all D&C Contractor Pass Th rough Claims to the District as Cor responding Project Agree ment Claims to th e extent 
requested by the D &C Contractor and at the D& C Contractor's cost. The Borrower will, as req uired by the D&C 
Contractor, unless it would be frivolous or vexatious to do so, enforce those rights and remedies and claim relief and 
compensation and those defenses under the Project Ag reement, which are for the benefit of the Borrower in respect 
of the D&C Work in accor dance with th e direct ions of the D&C Contractor and at the D& C Contractor's cost or, 
alternatively, the Borrower may consent to the D&C Contractor pursuing such rights, remedies, relief, compensation 
and defenses in the name of the Borrower and at D&C Contractor's cost, in either case, to the extent that the relevant 
rights, remedies, relief, compensation and defenses relate to the D&C Wor k or the D&C C ontract and involve the 
District.  
As between the B orrower and the D&C Contractor, the reso lution of  a Pas s Th rough Claim will not be  
subject to any dispute resolution procedures under the D&C Contract, and all applicable time periods with respect to 
the r esolution of su ch cl aim under the D&C Cont ract will be tolled, while the Project Agreeme nt Dispute 
Proceeding with respect to the Corresponding Project Agreement Claim is pending. With respect to its Pass Through 
Claim against the Borrower under the Project Docume nts, the D&C Contractor is bound by the resolution or final 
determination of the Corresponding Project Agreement Claim under the Project Agreement.  
In respect of all Pass Through Claims: 
(a) the D&C Contractor shall be entitle d to, a nd the Borrower's liability shall be limited to, whateve r 
compensation, monetary relief, schedule relief, or other remedy that the Borrower actually recovers from the District 
under the Project Agreement with respect to  the Corresponding Project Agr eement Claim, less the portio n of su ch 
recovery, if any, not at tributable to the D&C Contracto r's Loss u nder the  D&C Contract . The schedule relief to 
which the D&C Cont ractor is e ntitled shall be calculated subject to the Pr oject Agreement, and the monetar y relief 
to which the  D&C Contractor is e ntitled shall be calculated taking into account D&C C ontractor's obl igations to 
bear the cost of insurance deductibles and the risk of receipt of insurance proceeds pursuant to the D&C Contract;  
C-31 
 
(b) if it is determined thr ough a Project  Agreement Dispute Proceeding that the Dis trict is not liable to the 
Borrower for the Co rresponding Projec t Agreement C laim, th e D&C Contractor sha ll be bound b y s uch 
determination of that matter and the Borrower shall be discharged of all liability to the D&C Contractor in respect of 
such Pass Th rough Claim, except with regards to a Borrower Default (D&C Co ntract) and provided that ( a) the 
Borrower has complied with its obligations under the D&C Contract in respect thereof, and (b) all appeals from such 
determination have been exhausted.  
District Claims  
Each party to the D&C Contract shall give notice to the other party as soon as reasonably practicable upon 
becoming aware of any events or circumstances giving rise to a possible District Claim. 
The Borrower and the D&C  Contractor shall adhere to the procedures, requirements , and limitations set  
forth in the D&C Contract for resolution of District Claims, with respect to  the pursuit of t he Pass Through Claim 
related to any District Cla im. As bet ween Borrower and the  D&C Contractor, the r esolution o f a Corre sponding 
Project Agreement Claim will not be subject to an y dispute r esolution p rocedures under the D &C Contract or 
otherwise progressed, and all applicable time periods with re spect to the resolution of such cl aim under the D&C  
Contract will be tolled, wh ile the Pr oject Agreement Dispute Pro ceeding with respect to the Co rresponding Project 
Agreement Clai m is pending.  Wit h respect  to any Di strict Claim brought by the Borrower under th e Project 
Documents, each of the Borrower and the D&C Contractor shall be bound by the resolution or final determination of 
the Corresponding Project Agreement Claim under the Project Agreement.  
In the event of an award rendered between the District and the Borrower under the Project Agreement with 
respect to a Corres ponding Project Agreement Claim, pursuant to w hich the Borrower is liable in  any resp ect 
whatsoever, D&C Contractor will forthwith on demand indemnify the Borrower in full in respect of such liability to 
the extent D&C Contractor is liable therefor. 
Project Agreement Dispute Pro ceedings  
With respect to any Pass Through Claim  or D istrict C laim, the Borrower in cons ultation with D &C 
Contractor, shall elect whether (a) the D&C Contractor (i n the name of the Borrower) will pursue the Corresponding 
Project Agreement Claim under the Project A greement, or (b ) the Borrower will pursue the  Corresponding Project 
Agreement Claim under the P roject Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing or anything i n the D&C Contract to 
the contrary, the Borrower shall not be obliged to submit to  the District or otherwise pursue  (or permit the D&C 
Contractor to pursue in the name of the Borrower) a Pass Thr ough Claim if (a) such claim is manifestly frivolous or 
vexatious or (b) the Borrower compensates D&C Contractor for i ts claim under the D&C Contract, the amount of 
such compen sation to be reasonably deter mined by t he Borrower and the D&C C ontractor, sub ject to Dispute 
Resolution Procedures if the parties cannot reasonably agree. 
For each Project Agreement Dispute Proceeding the Borrower may agree with the District to a resolution or 
settlement of any Corre sponding P roject Agreement Claim in  the course o f t he Project Agr eement Dispute 
Proceeding, subject to the following with respect to any Project Agreement Dispute Proceeding that re lates solely to 
a Pass Through Claim of the D&C Contractor and does not inv olve any claim by the Borrower, the D&C Contractor 
and the Borrower shall jointly decide and direct the Borrower's resolution or settlement of the Corresponding Project 
Agreement Claim, and the Borrower shall not agree to a res olution or settlement of the Corresponding Pr oject 
Agreement Claim without the D&C Contractor's written consent (which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld, 
conditioned or delayed). With respect to any other Project Agreement Dispute Proceeding the B orrower must obtain 
the D&C C ontractor's prior written consent, which consent shall not be unr easonably withheld o r delayed, befor e 
agreeing with the District to a resolution or settlement of the Corresponding Project Agreement Claim. 
With respect to any P roject Agreement Dispute Proceeding tha t relates solely to a Pas s Through Claim of 
the D&C Con tractor and does not  involve any cla im by the  Borrower, or t hat relates solely to a Dis trict C laim 
caused by a D&C C ontractor-Related Entity, the costs and expens es, including attorneys ' fees a nd exper t fees, 
incurred in relat ion of the Project Agreement Dispute Proceeding, including such reasonable documented costs and 
expenses incurred by the Borrower (provided that the Borrower shall mitigate, and not unnecessar ily duplicate, its 
costs and ex penses), shall be born e solely by  the D&C Contractor. Wi th resp ect to any other Pro ject Agreemen t

Dispute Proceeding not de scribed in the previous sentence, each of th e Borrower and D&C Contractor will bear its 
own costs and expenses, including attorneys' fees and expe rt fees, incurred in relati on of the Project Agreement

APPENDIX D 
SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE ASSET MANAGEMENT CONTRACT 
The f ollowing is a s ummary of  selected provis ions of the Asset Management Contract and is not a full 
statement of the ter ms of  such agreement. Accordingly, the follow ing summ ary is qualified in its entirety by 
reference to the Asset Management  Contract and is  subject to the full text of such a greement. A copy of the Asset 
Management Contract is available, free of charge, upon request from the Borrower or the Trustee. Unless otherwise 
stated, a ny reference i n this Offici al Statement to any agreement sh all mea n such agreemen t and all schedules, 
exhibits and at tachments thereto, as am ended, s upplemented or ot herwise modified an d in e ffect as of the date 
hereof. 
The Borrower has been selected by the District to undertake the Project. Borrower and Asset Management 
Contractor en tered into the Asset M anagement Contract dated March 7 , 2022, whi ch sets out the terms  and 
conditions upon which the Asset Management Contractor shall perform the Asset Management Work. 
Principal Rights and Responsibilities of the Asset Management Contractor and the Borrower 
Pursuant to the  Asset Manag ement Contract, the Borrower has gr anted to the Asset Management  
Contractor, the exclusive right to, subject to the terms and co nditions of the Asset Manage ment Contract, undertake 
the Asset Management Services, in accordance with the terms of the Asset Management Contract. 
Asset Management Contractor 
The Asset Management Contractor is ENGIE Services Inc. ("Asset Management Contractor"). 
Term 
The Asset Management Contract will take ef fect on the C ommercial Closing Date and conti nue until the 
15th anniversar y o f the Comme rcial Closing Date or  the earlier termination of th e Asset Management Co ntract 
pursuant to the terms thereof. 
Government Approvals and Utilities 
Except with respect to District-Provided Approvals, the Asset Management Contractor is solely responsible 
for obtaining all Governmental Approvals required in connection with its performance under the Asset Management 
Contract, subject to the terms of the Asset Management Contract.   
However,  the Asset Management Contractor is responsible  for obtaining amendments or modifications to 
any Di strict-Provided Approvals  necessary for Asset M anagement Contractor 's performance under t he Asset  
Management Contract. In the event that any mo difications are not permitted by the Governmental Entity, the Asset 
Management Contractor will be responsible, at its own risk of delay an d cost, for revising its Final Design or means 
and methods as necessary to satisfy the requirements and conditions of the relevant Governmental Entity.  
The Asset Management Contractor is requ ired to perfor m its obligations und er t he Asset Ma nagement 
Contract in compliance with all Governmental Approvals at all times. 
The Borrower, if reasonabl y requested by the Asset Management Contractor, is required to seek the 
District's cooperation in connect ion with a ny application by the Asse t Management Contractor for a Governmental 
Approval and, including seeking to ha ve the District take certain additi onal actions (such as the execution of certain 
documents, at tending meetings or making a pplications in  the name of t he District) all at the Asset Management 
Contractor's cost with respect to the Borrower's third party expenses. 
The Asset Management Contractor is responsible for car rying out, in accordance with the Technical 
Provisions, all Utility Adjustments necessary to accommodate the Asset Management Work.    
D-2 
The Asset Management Contractor is required to notif y the Borrower if it receives a request from  a Utility 
Owner to undertake a Betterment.  The Asset Management Contractor is prohib ited from undert aking a Bette rment 
without the prio r wr itten conse nt of the Borro wer, th e Borrower is required to grant or withhold consent to a 
Betterment based on the gra nting or withhold ing of the District pursuant to the Project Agreement.  If the Di strict 
requires the Borrower to undertake a Bettermen t it is require d to iss ue a D istrict Change Request pursuant to the 
Project Agreement and the pro visions of the Asset Management Contract related to Borrower Changes will apply.  
See "APPENDIX D—SUMMAR Y OF CERTAIN PROVISI ONS OF THE AS SET MANAGEMENT 
CONTRACT—Bo rrower Changes." 
Prior to the Commercial Closing Date, the District has entered into the License Agreements. The Borrower 
must inform the Asset Management Contractor in writing of any amendment to or early termination  of a Licenses 
Agreement which is recei ved f rom t he District and  provide a copy of an y such no tice with in three (3) days of 
receipt.  The Asset Management Contractor must at all times comply with the terms of each License Agreement. 
If pursuant to certain Lic ense Agreements a Utility  Owner is required to be paid a fee in connection with 
any Asset Management Work, the Asset Management  Contractor is required  to provide written  notice to the 
Borrower describing the relevant Asset Management Work and corresponding fee prior to undertaking such Asset 
Management Work. Following the receipt of written authorization from the Borrower, which is re quired to be based 
on the written author ization of the  District pur suant to the Project Agreement, to proceed with such Asset 
Management Work, the A sset Management Contractor is required  to promptly undertake such As set Management 
Work and pay the correspo nding fee to t he Utility Owner in accordance with the terms of the applicable License 
Agreement and the Asset Management Contractor is entitled to a Borrower Change that is equivalent to any District 
Change issued in respect the Asset Management Contractor's payment of such fee to the Utility Owner. 
Hazardous M aterials   
Except as  otherwise expressly  provided in the Asset Man agement Contract, the Asset Management 
Contractor is solely responsible for the management, treatment, handling, storage, monitoring, remediation, removal, 
transport and disposal of any Ha zardous Materials in, under or on any Proj ect Site that are encounter ed at all times 
from and after Project Final Com pletion except to the exte nt su ch re lease is  an Asset Management Contractor 
Hazardous Materials Release.  A t all times  during the A sset Management Term, the Asset Management Contractor 
is solely responsible f or the management, treatment, handling, storage, monitoring, remediation, removal, transport, 
and disposal of any Asset Management Contractor Hazardous Materials Rele ase.  The Asset Manag ement 
Contractor is (without accepting or assuming responsibility under any applicable Law) responsible for obtaining and 
maintaining all Governmental Approvals relating to any Remedial Action and is so lely responsible for compliance 
with a ll Gover nmental Approvals and  applicable L aw concerning or  relating to Remedial Actio n related t o 
Hazardous Materials for which it is responsible. 
In carrying out any Remedial Action required in connection with Hazardo us Materials under the terms of 
the Asset Management Contract that is the subject of a Compensat ion Event, the Asset Manag ement Contractor will 
take such steps and actions, as the Borrow er may reasonably require, in order to protect and preserve the Distri ct or 
the Borrower's potential claims of contribution and indemnity, statutory or otherwise, against potentially responsib le 
parties, provided that any such steps and actions are not inconsistent with applicable Law or the requirements of the 
Asset Management Contract or any relevant Governmental Entities or Governmental Approvals. 
Maintenance of the Project 
The Asset Managem ent Services includes the D&C  Period As set Management Ser vices, the Asset 
Management Period Asset Management Services and the Expansion Work.  
The D&C Period Asset Management Services include asset management, administration, and rehabilitation 
of the Existing Street Light Network required to be performed during the D&C Period following the achievement of 
the first NTP3 .  The Asset Management Period Asset Man agement Services include any and  all asset management, 
administration, and r ehabilitation of the I mproved Street  Light Network, as described in the Technical Pro visions 
and in the Interface Ag reement.  The  Asset Management Perio d A sset Man agement Services  are requir ed to be  
D-3 
provided for each Street Light Bundle from the Street Light Bundle Substantial Completion Date and for each Smart 
City Bundle from the Smart City Bundle Substantial Completion Date, in each case until the end of the term. 
The Asset Manag ement Cont ractor's scope o f work  also i ncludes all Expansion Work required t o be 
performed pursuan t to the terms  of the Project Agreement and the Asset Managemen t C ontractor's obli gations 
pursuant to the Interface Agreement. 
In addition to its other obligations in relation to the p erformance of the Asset Management Work, the Asset 
Management Contractor is required to perform services in accordance with the Asset Management Plan from time to 
time accepted by the District and the Borrower.   
The Asset Manag ement Contractor is  required to carry out  the Asset Manageme nt Services in acc ordance 
with Good Industry Practice, all applicable Law, all Governmental Approvals, and the Technical P rovisions and all 
other requirements of the Project Agreement. 
The Asset Management Contractor acknowledges that the District may at any time suspend, in whole or in 
part, the Asset Management Ser vices under the Pro ject Agreement by writ ten order to the Bo rrower in which case 
the Borrower shall provide suc h not ice to the Asset  Management Contractor  and such susp ension shall apply to 
suspend the work of the Asset Management Con tractor under the Asse t Management Contract . Where any such 
suspension of the Asset Management Services by the District entitles the Borrower to a C ompensation Event under 
the Project Agreem ent, the Asset Management Contractor shall be permitted to bring a  P ass Through Claim in  
respect of such.  See "APPENDIX B —SUMMARY OF C ERTAIN PROV ISIONS OF THE PROJECT

Services" for a summary of t he circumstances in wh ich no C ompensation Ev ent relief will be availa ble in 
connection with a suspension by the District under the Project Agreement. 
Performance Security 
No later than one (1) month prior to the achievement of the first NTP3 for a Street Light Bundle or a Smart 
City Bundle as notified by the Borrower to the Asset Management Contractor, the Asset Management Contractor is 
required to deliver to the Borrower an Asset Management Letter of Credit in an amount equal to 35% of the Annual 
Average Asset Management Service Paymen ts, Indexed and re placed annually within twenty (20 ) days of Oc tober 
1st of ea ch Calendar Year.  The Asset Management Letter of C redit is required  to at all times be provided by a 
United States or Canadian bank or financial institution.  Such institution m ust have, at the time of issuance of th e 
Asset Management Letter of Credit, a rating on its long term senior unsecured debt of at least at the following levels 
(in each case with stable or positive outlook or the equivalent) by at least two (2) of the following rating agencies A 
by S&P, A2 by M oody's or A by Fitch and must maintain at a ll times th ereafter a r ating on its lo ng term senior 
unsecured debt of at least at the following levels (in each case with stable or positive outlook or the equivalent) by at 
least two of the following rating agencies A- by S&P, A3 by Moody's or A- by Fitch, provided that in the event that 
there is no Unit ed States or Canadian b ank or financial institu tion which meets t he rating requirements, a bank or 
financial institution me eting such rat ing requirements that is not a United  States or Ca nadian bank or f inancial 
institution may be deemed to be  an acceptable letter of credi t provider, provided that con sent from the Borrower is 
obtained by the Asset Management Contractor.

required to r ectify all No nconforming Work and Defects, i ncluding, to the ex tent necessary,  through removal or 
replacement, wh ether discovered by the Asset Management Contractor , t he Borrower or the District.

has n o obl igation with re spect to any Defect i n a Structural Element of the Improved S treet Light Network, for 
which the D&C Co ntractor is or would be liable under th e D&C Contract but for the e xpiry of the Latent Defect 
Period (an "Expired Structural Defect").

be resp onsible f or all Noncompliance Points, Nonc ompliance Deduct ions or Unavailability Deductions that may 
accrue as a direct result from such Expired Structural Defect.

Payments to the Asset Management Contractor 
 Payments 
From the Financial Closing Date to the Project Final Comple tion Date, the Borrower is required to pay to 
the Asset M anagement Contr actor the AM Mobilization Pa yments as  c alculated and e arned b y t he Asset 
Management Contractor. The AM Mobilization Payments total $24,430,822 subje ct to adjustment for Unavailability 
Deductions and Noncompliance Deductions.  The Asset Managemen t Contractor is required to submit invoices to 
the Borrower on a monthly basis, provided that the Borrower has no obligation to pay the invoice earlier than the 5th 
Business Day of the immediately subsequent month. 
From t he l ater of the P roject Final Completion Date and the Guaranteed  P roject Final Comple tion D ate 
until the Asset Management Termination Date, the Borrower is required to pay to the Asset Management Contractor 
the Total Post Project Final Completion Peri od Pa yments a s calculated and earned by the Asset M anagement 
Contractor, provided that if the P roject Final Completion Date occurs before Guaranteed Project F inal Completion 
Date, no Total Post Pro ject Final Completion Period Payment is re quired to  be made in t he month in which the 
Project Final Completion  Date occurs if the Borrower is required to  pay a n AM Mo bilization Payment in suc h 
month.  The Total Post Project Final Completion Period Payments is $839,873, Indexed per month and p ro-rated for 
partial m onths, su bject to adjustment in res pect of Unavailability Deductions, N oncompliance Deduct ions, 
Deductions and/or Energy Performance Charges. The Asset Management Contractor is required to submit  invoices 
monthly in arears, provided that the Borrower is not required to make payment to the Asset Management Contractor 
until th e later of the date that is  forty-five ( 45) da ys after the Borrower's recei pt of a proper invoice  and tw o 
Business Da ys of rec eipt of th e corresponding quar terly Availab ility P ayment f rom th e District pursuant to the 
Project Agreement. 
To the extent the Asset Managemen t Contractor has in curred a monetary amount of d eductions associated 
with Unavailability De ductions, Noncompliance Deductio ns, Deductions and/o r Energy Perf ormance Charges that 
exceeds the AM Mobilization Payments or T otal Post Project Final Completion Per iod Payments, as applicable, for 
any given month, the Asset M anagement Contractor is  required to pay the  difference between the total amount of  
monetary deductions incurred by t he Asset Management Contractor in the preceding month and that month' s AM 
Mobilization Payment or Total Post Project Final Completion Period Payments, as applicable, to the Borrower. 
The Asset Management Co ntractor is not entitled to a ny payment expect as s pecifically and expressly set 
out in the Asset Management Contract.   
Compensation Events 
The Asset Manag ement Contract provid es that the As set M anagement Contractor will receive 
compensation from the Borrower, in addition to obtaining relie f from complying with the deadlines set forth therein 
in case of the occurrence of certain enumerated events, which include, among other things:  
(a) any material breach of an obligation under the Project Agreement by the District; 
(b) any violation of applicable Law by the District; 
(c) any Qualifying Change in Law; 
(d) the issuance by the Borrower of any Directive Letter; 
(e) a ny Required Action taken by t he Borrower in the circumstances that is s ubject of certain Di strict 
step-in rights described in the Project Agreement; 
(f) any suspension of the Conversion W ork, the Smart City Work, or the Ass et Management Services 
that constitutes a Compensation Event pursuant to the Project Agreement;

(g) any material d amage or i nterruption to, or interference with, the Work caused  by a ny 
Governmental Entity, the District o f Co lumbia Office of Public -Private Partnerships, the  D istrict of Columbia

entities (other than the Borrower or any of the Borrower' s Contractors), or in the case of any att achments or fixtures 
pursuant to the terms of the Project Agreement, any Utility Ow ner or any third party, on  or in t he vicinity of any 
Project Site;

any Borrower-Related Entity's failure to comply with one or more Safety Standards; 
(i) the di scovery of an y Hazardous Materials o n or under any Project Site at a ny t ime after the 
Commercial Closing Date, including any Remedial Action related thereto, b ut only to the extent that the presence of 
such Hazardou s Materials (i) constitutes a Hazardous E nvironmental Condition and (ii) does  not constitute a 
Borrower Hazardous Materials Release;

similar order, legal restraint or prohibition by a Governmental Entity of competent jurisdiction under applicable Law 
that materially and adversely affects the District's or the Borrower's performance under the Project Agreement; 
(k) any ( i) amendment or  v ariation to t he terms an d conditions o f any Dist rict-Provided A pproval 
after the Setting Date not pr eviously agreed to by the Borrower (including any variation to the terms and conditions

Provided Approvals provided to the Borrower (including in draft form) as part of th e Disclosed Information prior to 
the Setting Date or (ii) any  invalidation or cancellation, in whole or in part , of any District-Provided Approval after 
the Setting Date; 
(l) the exercise by the District  of its right under t he Project Agreement to uncover Work  in a 
circumstance considered a Compensation Event pursuant to the terms of the Project Agreement; 
(m) (i) fa ilure by a Licensor  to c omply with i ts obligations und er its License Agreem ent or any 
amendment thereto in such a manner that would result in a  delay to the Conversi on Work (as set out in the Project

(p) Vandalism;

conduit pursuant to the Technical Provisions of the Project Agreement;

except, in each  case, to t he extent attributable to any breach of  the Project Agreement, app licable Law or

D-7 
If a Co mpensation Event directly causes, or i s reasonably likely to directly cause, the Asset Management 
Contractor to do any one or more of the following: 
(a) fail to comply with its obligations under the Asset Management Contract; or 
(b) incur additional or increased costs, 
the Asset Management Contractor may claim: 
(i) relief from compliance with its obligations under Asset Management Contract;  
(ii) compensation for any Change in Cost s that the Asset Manag ement Contractor has 
incurred or will incur as a direct result of such Compensation Event; or 
(iii) relief from any rights of the Borrower with respect to termination fo r Asset Management 
Contractor Default. 
 
Procedure for Relief and Compen sation.  Subject to the late provision of not ice requirements set out  in the 
Asset Management Contract, any claim made by the Asset Management Contractor for a Compensation Event must: 
(a) be submitted to  the Borrower promptly (and in any event within twent y (20) days) aft er the date 
that the Asset Management Contractor first became aware that the relevant Compensation Event had occurred a nd 
would have the effect that is the subject of the Asset Management Contractor's claim; 
(b) promptly, and in any event w ithin 50 days, a fter the Asset Management Contractor first became 
aware of the relevant Compensation Event, the Asset Management Contractor must give full details of the relevant 
Compensation Event a nd the extension of time  and/or any compensation claimed,  including an enumerated  list of 
items that includes but is not limited to: 
(i) with respect to any claim for an extension of time, a Time Impact Analysis demonstrating 
the relevant Com pensation Event will result in an iden tifiable and measurable disruptio n to the Asset M anagement 
Work affecting a critical path activity; 
(ii) details of the steps th e Asset Management Contractor has taken or will take to mitigate 
the effect of the Compensation Event; 
(iii) the type and amount  of insurance that may be applicabl e and amounts that h ave been or 
are anticipated to be collected under such insurance;  
(iv) e vidence reasonably satisfactory to th e Borrower demonstrating that no other concurrent 
or unrelated delay to a critical path act ivity that is the Borrower's responsibility has occurred that has contributed to  
the delay for which relief is being sought; and 
(c) demonstrate to the reasonable satisfaction of the Borrower that: 
(i) the Compensation Event was the direct cause or is reasonably likely to be the direct cause 
of: 
(A) the Asset Management Contr actor's inability to comply with its obligations 
under the Asset Management Contract which are obligations of the Borrower under the 
Project Agreement; and/or 
(B) the Asset Management Contractor incurring a Change in Costs; and  
D-8 
(ii) t he Cha nge in Costs, extension of time, an d/or relief  from obligations under  the A sset 
Management Contract claimed, could not reasonably be mitigated or recovered without material cost by the 
Asset Management  Contractor acting in accordance with Good Indust ry Pra ctice, including by re -
sequencing, reallocating, or redeploying its f orces to other portions of the Conversion Work and/or Smart 
City Work. 
Giving of Relief and Co mp ensation. If the Asset Management Contractor satisfies the condition s described 
above, then t he Asset Management Contractor will be entitled  to an extension of time, relief from obligations, or 
compensation (as applicable), as follows: 
(a) in the case of C apital Expen ditures or any other Cha nge in Costs arising prior to Pr oject Final 
Completion, the Borrower shall, in respect of a Compensation Event claim that is a P ass Through Claim, within five 
(5) Bu siness Days of r eceipt from th e District and in re spect of a Compen sation Event claim that i s not a Pass 
Through C laim within thirty (30) da ys of the Borrower's de termination as to th e Asset M anagement Contractor's 
entitlement to compensation, compensate the Asset Management Contractor for the relevant C apital Expenditure or 
Change in Costs (each as adjusted to reflect the actu al Capital Expenditure or Change in Costs incurred by the Asset 
Management Contractor) that the  Asset Management Contractor incurs a s a direct resul t of the relev ant 
Compensation Event; 
(b) in the ca se of any Change in Cost s that is not the subject of clau se (a) above, the Borrower shall 
compensate the Asset Management Contractor in accordance with  the financial model adjustment provisions set out 
in the Project Agreement or in such other manner as the parties to the Asset Management Contract may agree (acting 
reasonably); 
(c) i f any Asset Management Noncompliance Event would not have occurred but for the occurrence 
of the Compensation Event, such Asset Management Noncompliance Event will be deemed to have not occurred for 
the purposes of the Asset Management Contract and no Noncompliance Points or Deductions will be assessed by the 
Borrower in respect of such event, provided that in respect of any Compensation Event claim that is a Pass T hrough 
Claim, such relief shall be limited to the relief received by the Borrower pursuant to the Project Agreement; 
(d) if any Asset Management Unavailability Event would not have occurred but for the occurrence of 
the Compensation Event, such Asse t Manag ement Unavailability Event will be dee med to have not o ccurred for 
purposes of the Asset Management Contract and no Deductions wil l be assessed by the Borrower in respect of such 
event, provided that in respect of any C ompensation event claim that is a Pas s Through Clai m, such relief shall be 
limited to the relief received by the Borrower pursuant to the Project Agreement; 
(e) the Borrow er shall give the Asset Management Contractor such relief from its ongoing obligations 
under the Asset Managemen t Contract as is reasona ble given the nature of t he Compensation Event and the 
Borrower's ongoing obligations, provided that in respect of a ny Compensation Event c laim that is a Pass Throu gh 
Claim, such relief shall be limited to the relief received by the Borrower pursuant to the Project Agreement; 
(f) in the case of any Change in Cos ts claimed by the Asset Management Contractor in respect of any 
Compensation Event set out in clause (i) (H azardous Materials) of the definition thereof arising from the discovery 
of le ad paint o n Po les for which the Borrow er is required to take Remedial A ction pursuant to the Technical 
Provisions, (a) the Borrower shall not be required to compensate the Asset Management Contractor in respect of any 
Change in Costs unless, and to the extent that, the total number of Poles that have lead paint for which the Borrower 
is requ ired to take Remedial Action pursuant to th e Technical Provisions du ring the Asset Management Term 
exceeds four thousand nine hundred forty (4,940) and (b) any such Change in Costs  payable by the District to  the 
Borrower shall be limited to $1,150 (Indexed), and  provided that all such claims shall be Pass Through Claims, and 
in no event shall the Borrower be required to pay and in no event shall the Asset Management Contractor be entitled 
to received more than the amount received by the Borrower pursuant to the Project Agreement;  
(g) in the case of any Change in Costs claimed by the Asset Management Contractor in respect of any 
Compensation Event set out in clause (i) (Hazardous Materi als) of the definition thereof arising from a circumstance 
other than the  circumstance described in (f ) above, ( a) t he Borrower shall be require d to com pensate the Asset 
Management Contractor in respect of  any Ch ange in Costs and ( b) any such Change in Cos ts payable by the

Borrower to the Asset Management Contractor shall be limited to an agreed unit price, and pr ovided that such claim

Late Provision of N otice or Information.  In the event  that  information is provided after the dates r equired 
by the Asset Management Contract in respect of Compensation Events, then the Asset Management Contractor will 
not be entitled  to any compensation, extension of time, or relief fr om its obligations und er the Asset Manag ement 
Contract to the extent that the quantum thereof was adversely affected as a result of such notice or inf ormation not 
being provided within the relevant time periods. In the event the Asset Manag ement Contractor fails to provide such 
information for a period of eleven (11) months or more, the rights of the Asset Management Contractor with respect 
to the relevant Compensation Event will be of no further force or effect 
Failure to Agree.  If the Bor ro wer and the Asset Management Contractor cannot agree on the extent of  any 
compensation, del ays incurred, or r elief from the Asse t Management C ontractor's obligation s under the Asset 
Management Contract, the parties wi ll resolve the matter in accordance w ith the dispute resolution procedures s et 
forth in the Asset Management Contract, except that a ny dispute that is a Corresponding Project Agreement Claim 
will be dete rmined in ac cordance with th e provisi ons of the Asset  Management Contract relating to equivalent 
project relief. See "APPENDIX D—SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF  THE ASSET MANAGEMENT 
CONTRACT—Equivalent Project Relief." 
Sole Remedy . Except  for any rights it may have i n respect of a B orrower De fault ( Asset Management 
Contract), as between the Asset Management Contractor and the Borrower, the Asset Management Contractor's sole 
remedy in relation to any Compensation Event shall be the operation of the relevant  Compensation Event section in 
the Asset Management Contract.

Relief Events 
The Asset Management Contract provides that the Asset Management Contractor will ob tain relief from 
complying w ith the deadlines set fo rth therein, re lief from  compliance with the Asset Man agement Contractor's 
obligations therein,  and/or relief fr om any r ights of  the Borrower in connect ion with termination d ue to a the 
occurrence Relief Ev ents, provided that all claims for s uch relief sha ll be Pass Through Claims, and the Asset 
Management Contractor a grees tha t it sha ll n ot be ent itled to any relie f pursuant to the provisions o f the Asset 
Management Contract relating to claims  in respect of  Relief Events except to the extent such relief is  received by 
Borrower in accordance with the Project A greement.  The Reli ef Events under the Asset Management Contract are 
the same as the Relief Events under the Project Agreement.  
Other than in respect of Finance Costs, which  shall not be available to the Asset Management Contractor, 
the relief available t o the Asset Management Contractor pursuant to the terms of the Asset Management Contract in 
respect of Rel ief Events, to the extent applicable to the obligations of the Asset Management Contractor under the 
Asset Managem ent Contract, shall be the same as t he relief available  to the Borrower pursuant to the Projec t 
Agreement.  The procedural requirement s which condition relief under the Asset Management Contract are similar 
to those in the P roject Agreement, except that certa in time periods applicabl e to the  Asset Management Contractor 
are shorter under the Asset M anagement Contract, the Asset Management  Contractor is require d to give notices to 
the Borrower and the Borrower is responsible to provide such notices to the District and the Borrower is required to 
provide certain notices r eceived from the District to  the Asset Management Contractor. The Asset Ma nagement 
Contractor's claims in respect of Relief Events will be Pass Through Claims. 
See "APPENDIX B —SUMMARY OF CERTAI N PROVISIONS OF THE PROJECT AGRE EMENT—
Payments to the Borrower—Re lief Even ts" for a lis t of R elief E vents and summary of the proced ure and relief 
available and conditions thereto in accordance with the Project Agreement.   
Changes 
Borrower Changes.  To the extent applicable to the As set Management Work, the Borrower may issue a 
District Change Request received by the Borrower from the District pursuant to the Pr oject Agreement to the Asset 
Management Contractor, such a change being a Borrower Change under the Asset Management Contract. 
The Asset M anagement Contractor may refuse a District Change Request that would, if implemented:  
(a) require the Asset Management Work to be performed in a way that violates applicable Law; 
(b) contravene a Govern mental Approval then in full force and effect; provided tha t such 
contravention c annot b e corrected b y t he iss uance o f a new or revised Governmental Ap proval that can be 
obtained by the Asset Management Contractor; 
(c) materially adversely affect the health and safety of any Person; 
(d) materially and adversel y change the  nature o f the  Project or t he allocation o f r isk be tween t he 
Parties;  
(e) be unrelated to the k ind of work initially procured under the RFP and to  the kind of work that PA 
Borrower-Related Entities provide in the marketplace; or 
(f) require the Asset Management Work to be performed in a way that results in a breach of the terms 
of any License Agreement. 
The Asset M anagement Contractor acknowled ges and agrees that any refu sal by the  Asset M anagement 
Contractor of a District Change Request shall be a Pass Through Claim.  
D-11 
Promptly (and in any event within ten (10) Business Days) after having received a District Change Request, 
the Asset Management Contractor shall deliver to the Borrower an estimate of costs and expenses and other matters 
with respect to su ch District Change Request (a "Asset Management Contractor Es timate"), or confirmation as to 
when a n A sset Management Contractor E stimate is to b e provided to the  Borrower (provided, t hat the Asset 
Management Contractor shall use all reasonable efforts to prepare its Asset Management Contractor Estimate within 
thirty-one (3 1) days after having received  the Di strict Chang e Request, unless further extended by t he Borrower 
acting reasonabl y). Promptly (and in any ev ent within seven (7 ) B usiness Days) after the  Borrower receives the 
Asset Management Contractor Estimate, the Asset Management Contractor shall meet with the Borrower to review, 
discuss and seek to agree upo n the Asset Management Contractor Estimate. The Asset Management Contractor shall 
provide all assistance reasonably requested by the Borrower in respect of the me etings between the District a nd the 
Borrower in respect of any Borrower Estimate pursuant to the Project Agreement, including agreeing upon the Asset 
Management Contractor Estimate associated with the applicable Borrower Estimate under the Project Agreement. 
The Borrower shall deliver notice to the Asset Management Contractor of an y modification to  a District 
Change Request received by the Borrow er pursuant to the Project Agreement, and, subject to the terms of the Asset 
Management Contract, t he Asset Manag ement Contractor shall comply with an y s uch notice and  within ten (10) 
Business Days or such l onger period as agreed  to by t he Borrower (acting reasonably), after  receipt of such 
modification, notify the Borrower of any consequential changes to the Asset Management Contractor Estimate. 
Within five (5) Business Days o f receipt by th e Borrower of a D istrict Change Order o r no tification of 
withdrawal of a District Cha nge Request from the District, the Borrower shall either accept the Asset Management 
Contractor Estimate by issuing a notice to th e Asset Management Contractor in accordan ce with a Distric t Change 
Order received pu rsuant to the Project A greement or notify the Asset Management Contractor that the Borrower 
withdraws the District Change Request. 
If th e Borrower accept s the Asset Manag ement Contractor Es timate (a s may be modifi ed), the Asset 
Management Contractor sh all use reasonable effort s to implement the re levant extra work i n accordance with t he 
Asset Management Contractor Es timate an d the Borrower shall make a pay ment to the Asset Ma nagement 
Contractor within five (5) Business Days of receiving payment from the District in respect of each invoice (complete 
in all ma terial respects)  in accordan ce wit h the agreed -upon pa yment schedul e, accompanied by  the evidence 
reasonably acceptable to the Borrower (where applicable) that t he relevant part of the Borrower Change has been 
carried out. In  t he event that a Dis trict Change Request  is wi thdrawn, the  Borrower will reimburse t he Asset 
Management Contractor for all reasonable and documented th ird-party co sts incurred by the Asset Ma nagement 
Contractor (as shown in an inv oice reasonably acceptabl e to the Contracting Officer) in  connection wit h such 
District Change Req uest.  Th e am ount paya ble by  Borrower to Asset Management Contractor in  respect thereof 
shall be determined in accordan ce with the Proje ct Agreement and any dispute by Asset Management Contractor in 
respect of such amount shall be  a Pass Thro ugh Claim and shall be paid by the Borrower to the Asset Management 
Contractor within five (5) Business Days of receipt of payment by the Borrower from the District. 
With r espect to District Change s relating to expansi ons of the Street Light Ne twork, the i nstallation of  
Metropolitan Police Department CCTV cameras on Poles, the inspection of Lighting Units as part of the i nstallation 
of small cell networking equipment by a third-party, and structural analysis or design work on Poles owned by third-
parties pr ior to the ins tallation of  wireless access points, the b asis of the  corresponding Borrower Estimate a nd 
resulting change order will be unit pricing for such tasks specified in the Asset Management Contract.  
If the Dis trict issues an Expans ion Directive u nder the terms of th e P roject Agreement, the Borrower is 
required to provide such notice to th e Asset  Management Contract or and  the As set Management Co ntractor is 
required to perform the associated Expansion Work. In the event the District directs the Borrower, pursuant to one 
(1) or m ore Expansion Directives, to undertake Expansion Work in excess of the Expansion Work All owance 
available f or a given Cal endar Year, then such even t shall be a Compensatio n Event pursuant t o t he Project 
Agreement and the Asset Management Contractor shall be entitled to bring a Pass Through Claim in respect of such 
Compensation Event.

Borrower to evaluate it in full, (b) the Asset Management Contractor's reasons for proposing the change to the Asset

Borrower requires due to the Asset Management Contractor Change, (d) any implications of the change to the Asset 
Management Work, (e) details regarding proposed cha nges to the Milestone Payments, if any (and, i f so, giving a 
detailed cost estimate of such proposed change), (f) any dates by which a decision by the Borrower is critical and (g)

Management Contract. 
Within fifteen (15) Business Days of meeti ng with the As set Management Contractor to re view the Asset 
Management Contractor Change R equest the Bo rrower is required  to either submit the Asset Managem ent 
Contractor Change Request to the District p ursuant to the terms of th e Project Agreement as a P ass Through Claim 
or reject the Asset Management Contractor Change Request.
