Order" issued under the Ass et Management Contract.  T he Asset Management Contractor shall pay all of the 
Borrower's costs, expen ses and Loss es associated w ith a ny Asset Management Contractor Change Request 
submitted to the District together with all of the Borrowers costs, expenses an d Losses associated with any deemed 
Asset Management Contractor Change Order. 
Unless an Asset Management Contr actor Change Or der sp ecifically pr ovides for an increase in the 
payments to the Asset Management Contractor there shall be no increase in any such payment as a result of a change 
to the Asset Management Work proposed by the Asset Management Contractor. If an Asset Management Contractor 
Change Order results in a decrease in t he Asset Management Contractor's costs, any payments  d ue from the 
Borrower under the Project Agreement will be adjusted downwards to refle ct 50% of such reduction plus 20% of 
such amount in the Asset Management Contractor's costs. The amount of the 50% adjustment will be determ ined 
pursuant to the Project Agreement and subject to any Corresponding Project Agreement Claim. 
The District may not  reject a Borrower Change Request that is re quired in order to conform to a Change in 
Law and such provision shall ap ply to any claim by th e Asset Management Contractor that an Asset Management 
Contractor Change Request is re quired in orde r t o conform t o a Change i n Law, but on ly in respect of any P ass 
Through Claim. 
Asset Management Contractor Indemnity 
To the fullest extent permitted by app licable Law, the Asset Management Contractor shall release, defend, 
indemnify and hold harmless the Bor rower Indemnified Parties a nd the Indemnified Par ties on dema nd from and 
against any and all liability to third parties for Losses arising from (a) death or personal injury, (b) loss of or damage 
to any Borrower Indemnified Party or Indemnified Party's property, and (c) third pa rty claims brought against any 
Borrower Indemnified Party or Indemnified Party, which may arise out of, or in consequence of, any or all o f the 
following: any actual or alleged failure  by the Asset Management Contractor to comply wit h, observ e or perform 
any of the covenants, obligations, agreements, terms or conditions in the Project Documents or any actual or alleged 
breach by the Asset Management Contractor of its representations or warr anties set forth in t he Asset Management 
Contract. 
The Asset Management Contractor will not be responsible or obliged to indemnify a Borrower Indemnified 
Party or Indemnified Party with respect to a ny liability or Loss es to the extent that the same arise as a di rect result 
of: (a) a Compensation Event, Relief Event, or Borrower Default (Asset Management Contract); (b) the presence of 
Hazardous Materials on any Project Site for w hich the District is, pursuant  to the Project Agreem ent, or for  which 
the Borrower is pursuant to the Asset Management Contract, deemed to be the s ole generator and arranger, but only 
to the exten t that the relevant loss does not arise as a direct result of the negligence of the Asset Management 
Contractor or the Asset Management Contractor failing to  comply with the terms of  the Asset Management 
Contract; (c) any non-performance by a Bor rower Indemnified Party of its obligations under the Asset Management 
Contract, or an y non-performance by an Indemn ified P arty of its  obligations under t he P roject Agreement or an y  
D-13 
violation of applicable Law by any Bo rrower Indemnified Party or Indemnifie d Party; or (d) t he fraud, negligence, 
recklessness, bad faith or willful misconduct of a Borrower Indemnified Party or Indemnified Party. 
Limitation on Asset Management Contractor's Liability   
Notwithstanding any other provisions of the Asset Management C ontract, except in the case of termination 
for an Asset Management Contractor Default, or unless re-set as provided in th e Asset Management Contract, to the 
extent permitted by applicable Law , t he Asset Management Contractor's maximum total liability unde r t he Asse t 
Management Contract and the Interface Agreement, is, subject to the following exclusions, limited to the 75% o f the 
annual average forecas ted Asset Management Service Payments (inc lusive of the life -cycle c osts) (index -linked) 
over the period c ommencing on the Financial Closing Date and ending on the Expiry Da te as shown in the in the  
Base Case Financial Model or the most recent update to the Base Case Financial Model.  In the case of a termination 
of th e As set Managemen t Contract for an Asset M anagement Contra ctor Default o r termination of the Project 
Agreement as a result of an Asset Management Contractor Default, the liability of the Asset Management Contractor 
is, su bject to the fol lowing exclusions, limited to the 75 % of the annual average f orecasted Asset Management 
Service Payments (inclusive of the life -cycle costs) (index -linked) ov er the period commencing on  the Fi nancial 
Closing Date and ending on the E xpiry Date as shown i n the in the Base Case Financial Model or the most recent 
update to the Base Case Financial Model. The Asset Management Contractor Liability Cap is exclusive of:  
(a) any insurance proceeds received pursuant to policies of insurance to the extent required under the 
Asset Management Contract and any liability for Losses tha t would have been covered by s uch insurance up to the 
amount of the required insurance and deductibles but in respect of which insurance proceeds are not available due to 
a failure on the part  of the Asset Management Contractor, or any Asset Management Contractor-Related Entity, as 
applicable, to comply with its insurance obligations as set forth in the Asset Management Contract;  
(b) any c laim by the Borrower in respect of fraud, gross negligence, willful misconduct and deliberate 
acts of wrongdoing by the Asset Management Contractor or any Asset Management Contractor-Related Entity;  
(c) liabilities arising under any Encumbrance creat ed or caused by the As set Management Contractor 
or parties for whom the Asset Management Contractor is responsible;  
(d) any claim for personal injury or death;  
(e) third party claims in tort against the Asset Management Contractor to the extent such claims arise 
from the negligence o r breach  of contract of t he Asset Management Contractor or parties for w hom the Asset 
Management Contractor is responsible;  
(f) third party claims in tort against the Borrower (excluding claims by the Lenders, the Borrower and 
Borrower-Related Entity(ies) and the D&C Contractor and D&C Contractor-Related Entity(ies));  
(g) third party claims in tort against the Le nders or a Borrower-Related Entity for which indemnity is 
sought against the B orrower to the extent  such claims arise from th e negligence or breach of co ntract of the Asset 
Management Contractor or parties for whom the Asset Management Contractor is responsible;  
(h) third party claims relating to intellectual property to the extent such claims are caused by the Asset 
Management Contractor;  
(i) amounts paid to t he Borrower by the Asset M anagement Contractor  to the extent t hat same ar e 
subsequently recovered by the Asset Management Contractor from the D&C Contractor pursuant to the Interface 
Agreement;  
(j) amounts paid by the Asset Management Contractor which are su bsequently repaid to the Asset 
Management Contractor by the Borrower, as required pursuant to any bankruptcy or insolvency legislation;  
(k) expenses incurred by the Borrower in enforcing a claim under the Asset Management Contract;   
D-14 
(l) any amount paid to the Borrower by the Asset Management Contractor to the extent that the same 
is subsequently recovered by the Asset Management Contractor from the Borrower or any Borrower-Related Entity;  
(m) any interest, penalties  and fees incurred by the Borrow er due to unpa id amounts owing from the 
Asset Management Contractor to the Borrower; and  
(n) fines and pen alties under statue or any r easonable costs i ncurred by Borrowe r or any Borrower-
Related Entity and for which Asset Management Contractor is lia ble to the Borrower or a Borrower-Related Entity 
in complying with statutory obligations or any order of a Governmental Entity.  
If at any t ime, the A sset Ma nagement Contr actor's aggregate liability (except in the case of early 
termination or termination for an Ass et Management Contractor Default) is equal to or exceeds the amount of the 
Asset Management C ontractor Liab ility Cap, the Asset Man agement Co ntractor may deliver written notice to the 
Borrower advising that it wishes to re-set the Asset Management Contractor Liability Cap, by agreeing that Existing 
Asset Management Liabilities no longer co unt towards the Asset Management Contractor Li ability Cap. The A sset 
Management Contractor may exercise this right twice as of right and thereafter with the consent of the Borrower. 
Unavailability Events and Noncompliance Events 
The Asset Management Contract contains a regime p ursuant to which Deductions are assessed against the 
Asset Management Contractor, based on a list of breaches or fail ures in performance of obliga tions of the Asset 
Management Contractor identified  the Asset Management  C ontract. Unavailability Events and N oncompliance 
Events incl ude, but are not l imited to, fa ilure to provide certain notifications,  failure to observe or comply with 
various project d elivery protocols or b reach of performance standards. The Asset Management Contractor has the 
benefit of certain cure periods and/or g race periods for certain breaches and failures which are based on the type of 
breach or failu re. The Asset Management Contractor is required to notify the District, with a copy to the Borrower 
of any Noncompliance Even t related to the Asset Management Work or the actions o r omissions of  the Asset 
Management Contractor.  The District is permitted to notif y the Borrower of Nonc ompliance Events and  
Unavailability Failures and the Borrower shall notify the Asset Management Contractor of such where related to th e 
Asset Management Work or any act ion or omission of the Asset Managem ent Contractor.  Noncom pliance Points, 
Noncompliance Deductions and Unavailability Deductions applica ble under the Asset Management Contract shall 
be determined in accordance with the terms of the Project Agreement. 
The A sset Management Contractor is liable for Noncompliance Poi nts, and associa ted Ded uctions, 
resulting from Noncompliance Events related to  the Asset Management Work or actions or omissi ons of the A sset 
Management Contractor.  T he number of N oncompliance Points assessed under the Asset Management Contract is 
required to be  the same as the number of Noncompliance Points assessed pursuant to the P roject Agreement, to the 
extent related to a ny Asset Management Work or actions or  omissions of the Asset Management Contractor.  The 
Asset Management Contractor is liable for Unavailability Deductions resulting from Unavailability Events related to 
the Asset M anagement Work or  actions or omissions of the Asset M anagement Contractor.  T he amount of 
Unavailability Deductions assessed under the Asset Management  Contract is required to be the same as the amou nt 
of Unavailability Ded uctions assessed pursuant  to the Project Agree ment, to the extent relate d t o any Asset 
Management Work or actions or omissions of the Asset Management Contractor. 
Rights of the Borrower Following an Asset Management Contractor Default 
The Asset Management Cont ract provides for a n umber of  default even ts b y the Asset Management  
Contractor. Such default events includ e any one or more of the following (each an " Asset Management Contrac tor 
Default"): 
(a) the failure by the Asset Management Contractor to commence the D&C Period Asset Management 
Services within twenty-one (21) days following the issuance of the first NTP3;  
(b) the Asset Management Contractor Abandons the Project;   
D-15 
(c) the Asset Management Contr actor assigns or transf ers an y of its righ ts or obligations under the 
Asset Management Contract in breach of the terms of the Asset Management Contract;  
(d) an Insolvency Event arises with respect to the Asset Management Contractor;  
(e) the Asset Man agement Contractor fails to  pay any amount due to the Borrower under the Asset 
Management Contract when due, except to the extent such payment is subject to a Good Faith Dispute;  
(f) t he Asset Managem ent Contractor fails to deposit fund s in the H RA or fails to deliver to the 
District a Handback Letter of Credit in accordance with the requirements of the Asset Management Contract (except 
to the e xtent that the amount of  the required deposit or Handba ck Letter of Credi t is subje ct to a Good Faith 
Dispute);  
(g) a ny repr esentation o r warranty m ade by the Asset Manageme nt Contractor in the Asset 
Management Contract or any certificate, schedule, repor t, instrument or other d ocument delivered to the Borrower 
pursuant to the Asset Management Contract is false or mater ially misleading or inaccurate when made, in each cas e 
in any material respect, or omits material information when made;  
(h) the Asset Management Contractor fails  to comply in any material respect with any Gover nmental 
Approval or applicable Law;  
(i) the Asset Management Contractor fails to promptly comply  with any written suspension o f Asset 
Management Work issued by the Bor rower or District in accordance with cer tain terms of the Asset Management 
Contract, except to the extent that such failure arises as a direct result of a Compensation Event or a Relief Event;  
(j) subject to re lief granted pursuant to  a Relief Event, an U navailability Asset Management Default 
Trigger or a Noncompliance Asset Management Default Trigger occurs;  
(k) the As set Managemen t Contractor fails to obt ain, pr ovide and ma intain the  Asset Management 
Contractor Insurances in accordance with the requirements of the Asset Management Contract;  
(l) an Asset Management Persistent Breach occurs;  
(m) there occurs any final,  non-appealable su spension or de barment (disti nguished from ineligibility 
due to lack of financial qualifications), or there goes into effect an agreemen t for voluntary exclusion, from bidding, 
proposing or c ontracting with a ny federal or District of Colum bia departmen t or agency of Asse t Management 
Contractor;  
(n) without  l imiting any other specif ically specified event  of default, the Asse t Management 
Contractor breaches any ot her material obligation under t he Asset M anagement Contract, other than a breach for 
which a Noncompliance Poin t was or coul d have been assessed; or a  breach that arises as  a direct result of the 
occurrence of a Compensation Event o r Relief Event; or the A sset Management C ontractor makes any writ ten 
repudiation of the Asset Management Contract;  
(o) t here exists a Borrower Default under th e terms of  the Project Agreement, the cause of which is 
directly attributable to any breach by the Asset Management Cont ractor under the Asset Management Contract, or 
any negligent or wrongful act or  omission by t he Asset  Management Contractor  o r any appli cable Asset 
Management Contractor-Related Entity;  
(p) t he Asset Management Contractor Lenders' Direct Agreement ceases t o be in full force and effect 
as a result of an Asset Management Contractor Default;  
(q) the Asse t Management Contractor Lenders' Direct Agreement b ecomes i nvalid, void o r 
unenforceable with respect to the Asset Management Contractor, and such invalidity , voidness or unenforceability 
results in a Borrower Default or event of default (in each case however defined) under the Finance Documents;   
D-16 
(r) the issuer of the Handback Letter of Credit ceases  to be an Eligible Se curity Issuer o r the 
Handback Letter of  Credit o therwise ceases to be in full fo rce and effect and, in  e ach case, is not replaced in  
accordance with the terms of th e Asset M anagement Contract as and when required under  the Asset Manage ment 
Contract;  
(s) t he Asset Manag ement Con tractor terminate s the Asset Management Contra ct on grounds or in  
circumstances b eyond the  Asset Management C ontractor's t ermination rig hts s pecifically s et forth in the  Asset 
Management Contract;  
(t) the As set Management Contractor  fails to prov ide the Asset Management L etter of Credit or 
Handback Letter of Credit when and in the amounts required;  
(u) the Asset Management Letter of Credit ceases to be Acceptable Credit Support and is not replaced 
in accordance with the terms of the  Asset Management Contract or otherwise  ceases to be in full force an d effect 
(without replacement); and  
(v) the aggregate of all amount s accrued or paid to t he Borrower pursuant to th e Asset Management 
Contract by the Asset Management Contractor that count towards the  Asset Management Contractor Liability Cap is 
an amount equivalent to the Asset  Management Contractor Liability Cap, u nless the Asset Management Contractor 
agrees to re-set such liability cap in accordance with the terms of the Asset Management Contract.  
Warning Notices 
If the Borrower receives an Initial Warning Notice from the District pursuant to the Project Agreement , the 
Borrower is required to deliver such Initial Warning Notice to the Asset Management Contractor.  The Borrower is 
required to pro vide any Final Warning Notice received by the Borrower in accordance with the provisions of the 
Project Agreement to Asset  Management Contractor.  Se e "APPENDIX B —SUMMARY OF C ERTAIN 
PROVISIONS OF THE PROJECT AGREEMENT —Rig hts of the Distric t Fo llowing a Borrower Default—
Warning Notices" for a summary of the circumstances in which an Initial Warni ng Notice or Fin al Warning Notice 
can be served by the District to the Borrower. 
An Asset Management Persistent Breach will occur if a Final Warning Notice has been d elivered under the 
Project Agreement and delivered to the Asset Management Contractor and following delivery of such Final Warning 
Notice the breach cont inues for more than twenty-two (22) consecutive days under the Asse t Management Contract; 
or recurs two (2) or more times und er the Asset Management Contract within the three  (3) month period after th e 
date of service of the Final Warning Notice to the Asset Management Contractor. 
Initial Notice and Cure Periods 
The Borrower will provid e written notice to the Asset Manage ment Contractor of t he occurren ce of an 
Asset Management Contractor Default. Upon receipt of  the Borrower's notice (if required ), the Asse t Management 
Contractor shall have the following cure periods: 
(a) For an Asset Management Contractor Default in respect of clauses  (a), (b), (e), (f) and ( k) of the 
definition of  Asset Management Contractor D efault above, a period of twenty -one (21) days after the Asset 
Management Contractor receives written notice from the Borrower of such Asset Management Contractor Default. 
(b) For an Asset Managem ent Contractor De fault under clauses ( g), ( h), ( i), ( m) and (n ) of  the 
definition of Asset Manag ement Contractor Default ab ove, a period of twenty -one (21) days after Asset 
Management Contractor receives wr itten notice from the Borrower of su ch Asset Management Contractor Default 
or, if despite  the Asset Managemen t Contractor's commencement of meanin gful steps  to cure immedia tely a fter 
receiving the Asset Management Contractor Default Notice , the Asset Management Contractor Default cannot be 
cured within such twenty-one (21) day-period, the Asset Management Contractor will have such additional period of 
time, up to a maximum cure pe riod of sixty (60) days, as is reasonably necessar y to cure t he Asset Management 
Contractor Default. 
For any other Asset  Management Contractor Default, there is no cure period.  
D-17 
Remedial Plan 
If the Borrower i s required to de liver a D efault Remedial Plan pursu ant to the terms of the Project 
Agreement, the Borrower may require the Asset Management Contractor to prepar e and submit, within twenty-one 
(21) days, a remedial plan. The remedial plan must set out specific actions and an associated schedule to be followed 
by the Asset Management Contractor to c ure any matter relevant to the B orrower Default that is related to the Asset 
Management Work or the actions or o missions of the Asset Manag ement Contractor to be taken to reduce the 
likelihood of such defaults occurring in the future. 
Within forty (40) days of receiving any such remedial plan, the Borrower will notify the Asset Management 
Contractor whether or not the re levant remedial plan i s, in th e Borrower's absolute discretion, acceptable. If  the 
Borrower no tifies the Asset Management Contractor that th e relevant rem edial plan is acceptable, the Asset 
Management Contractor will be required to implement such remedial plan in accordance with its terms. 
Right of Termination 
In the event that an Asset Management Contractor Default oc curs and it has no t been  cured within a ny 
relevant cure period set out in Initial N otice and Cure Periods section above, or (if relevant) in accordance with any 
remedial plan accepted by the Borrower pu rsuant to th e Remedial Plan sect ion above, the Borrower may terminate 
the Asset M anagement Contract in accordance with, and subject to th e ter ms of, the termin ation for Asset 
Management Contractor Default provisions set out in the Asset Management Contract. 
Asset Management Contractor Default Termination Sum 
In the event of su ch ter mination, subje ct to the Asset Management  Contractor Termin ation Ca p, if the 
Project Agreement has not bee n terminated, the Asset Managemen t Contractor is ob ligated to pay damages to the 
Borrower to compensate t he Borrower for a ny accrued liability o f the Asset Management Contractor remaining 
unpaid, the additiona l c osts incurred or re asonably pr ojected to be  incurred by  the Borrower in conne ction with 
procuring one or more replacement Contractors to complete th e Asset Management Services, the costs to make good 
any Defects which are the Asset Management Contractor 's responsibility and any increase in the costs of the Ass et 
Management Services ov er the Asse t Management Contract price which the Borrower incurs in securing another  
contractor to complete the Asset M anagement Work and if the Project Ag reement has been t erminated, the Asset 
Management Contractor i s o bligated to  pay damages to  the Borrower in the  amount required to discharge all 
outstanding liabilities of the Borrower under the Finance Docume nts including but not  limited to the Project De bt 
and the Equity Member Funding Agreements, to the extent that t he Borrower is not compensat ed for same by t he 
District under the Project Agreement. 
Other Termination Rights; E ffect of Termination 
Termination for Borrower Default 
Subject to the applicab le cure periods, the Asset Management Contractor is e ntitled to terminate the Asset 
Management Contract following one of the following defaults ("Borrower Default (Asset Management Contract)"): 
(a)  the Borrower fails to pay an amount when due to the Asset Management Contractor, provided that 
(i) su ch amount is no t being disputed in good faith; (ii) a n otice of d efault of payment has been given to the 
Borrower and the defau lt by the Borrower continues during a per iod of sixty (60) consecutive days following s uch 
notice; (iii) th e outstanding amounts payable to the Asset Management Contractor, individually or in the aggregate, 
exceed $250,000 (index -linked); (iv) the Asset Man agement Contractor has no right to exercise ( and h as not 
exercised) any set off rights  in respect of such amou nt; a nd (v) s uch payment is not  related to a failure of the 
Borrower to receive a corresponding payment from the District;  
(b) any representation or warranty made by the Borrower under the Asset Management Contract is 
false, misleading or ina ccurate when made in each case in any material respect or omit s material information when 
made;   
D-18 
(c) the Borrower committing a material breach of its obligatio ns under the Asset Management 
Contract, which breach materially adversely affects the ab ility of the Asset Management Contractor to perform its 
obligations und er the Asset Management Contract f or a conti nuous period of at least sixty ( 60) days following 
written notice from the Asset Management Contractor to the Borrower of such material breach (or, provided that t he 
Borrower is diligently taking all commercially reasonable steps to  remedy the such default, such longer period as is 
reasonable in the circumst ances, but in a ny case not exceeding in total  ninety (90) consecutive days following such 
notice), provided that any such breach is not due to an act or omission of the District;  
(d) any condemnation or other taking by eminent domain by the District or an y other Governmental 
Entity o f all or any mat erial portion of the  Street L ight Network ; provided that, for pur pose of the foreg oing, a 
material port ion of the St reet Light Netw ork means mo re than  f ifty ( 50%) of  the Lighting Units inclu ded in the 
Street Light Network; and  
(e) an Insolvency Event arises in respect of the Borrower. 
 
Notice and C ure Periods .  The Asset Management Contractor is re quired to provide written notice to the 
Borrower upon the occurrence of a Borrower Default (Asset Management Contract) as well as on the occurrence of 
a breach s pecified in cla use (a) or (c) above in the definition of Borrower D efault (Asset Management Contract). 
Upon receipt of a default notice, the Borrower will have the following cure periods: for a Borrower Default (Asset 
Management Contract) under  clause (b) or (d ) above in  the d efinition of Borrower Default ( Asset Managem ent 
Contract), a p eriod of thi rty (30) days after the Borrower receives the Bor rower Default Notice; or if, despite  the 
Borrower's commencement of meaningful steps to cur e immediately after re ceiving the Borrower Default Notice, 
the Borrower Default ( Asset M anagement Contract) canno t b e c ured wi thin such thirty (30) day -period, the 
Borrower will h ave such  additional pe riod of ti me, up to a maximum c ure period of ninety (90) days after the 
Borrower receives t he Borrower Default Notice , as is reasonably  necessary to cure the Borrower Default ( Asset 
Management Contract); and  for a Borrower Default ( Asset Management Contract) under c lause (e) above in th e 
definition of Borrower Default (Asset Management Contract), there is no c ure period.   A Borrower Default under 
clause (b) above in the definition of Borrower Default (Asset Management Contract) will be regarded as cured when 
the adverse effects of such Borrower Default (Asset Management Contract) are cured. 
Asset Management Cont ractor Rem edies Upon Borrower Default. In the event  a notice of a breach 
pursuant to clause (a) ab ove in the definition of Borrower Default (Asset Management Contract) has been del ivered

entitled the Asset Ma nagement Contractor to deliver a notice fo r breach pursua nt to clause (a) above in the 
definition of Borro wer Default (Asset Management Contract) in respect of an amou nt of $250,000 o r more but for 
the fact that the payment default is due to a failure o f the District to pay the Borrower and provided in such case that 
the Borrower has a r ight to suspend its perf ormance of the Wo rk in a ccordance with the  Project Agreeme nt, th e 
Asset Ma nagement Contractor may sus pend its performance of the Asset Management Work subject to the 
following: the  Asset Manag ement Contractor sha ll provid e the Bo rrower and the Collateral A gent with notice  
regarding its intent to s uspend at least  thirty-five (35) days before implementing the suspension and the suspension 
shall be req uired to be lifted upon the Asset Management Contractor's receipt of payment in fu ll of all undisputed 
amounts it is owed or in the case of a suspension where the payment failure is due to a failure of the District to make 
payment to the Borrower, the Asset Management Contractor shall lift such suspe nsion in the event th e Borrower no 
longer has the right to suspend. 
The right to suspen d may be exercised without prejudice to the Asset Management Contractor's other rights 
and re lief arising with respect to a Bo rrower Act, including its right to terminate for Borrower Defaults ( Asset 
Management Contract) in accordance with the terms of the Asset Management Contract.

Compensation on Termination, on Termination of the Project Agreement  
Project Agreem ent Terminat ion Asset Management  C ontractor D efault.  If the  Project Agreement is 
terminated as a result  of an Asset Management Contractor Default, the Asset Managemen t Contractor shall p ay an 
amount calculated as if the Asset Managem ent Contract had terminated for a n Asset Management Contractor 
Default. 
If the Project  Agreement is terminated by the District as a result of a Borrower Default, which is partly but 
not wholly a result of a n Asset Management Contractor Default, there shall be a fa ir and equitable  sharing of t he 
amounts due to or payable  by each party based on their r espective contributions to the Borrower Default with any 
disputes to be resolved pursuant to the Dispute Resolution Procedure. 
If the Project Agreement is termi nated as a result of a B orrower Default for which the Asset Management 
Contractor is not responsible, the Borrower is required to pay the Asset Management Contractor its Subcontractor 
Breakage Costs, provided that and without double -counting, such payment will be without prejudice to any claims 
under the Asset Manag ement Contract that the Borrower and Asset Management Contractor may have against each 
other unrelated to the termination. 
Project Agr eement Def ault Trigger Termination.  If the Project Agreement is terminated as a resu lt of an 
Unavailability Default Trigger or a Noncompliance Borrower Default Trigger, the Borrower shall pay to the Asset 
Management Contractor its Subcontractor Break age Costs less an amount which is ca lculated base d on a fair and 
equitable sharing of the amount s due to or payable by each par ty bas ed on their res pective contri butions to the 
Borrower Default under the Project Agreement cal culated based on the percentage of Unavailability Deductions or 
Noncompliance P oints that the Ass et Management Contractor is r esponsible f or relative to the t otal n umber of 
Unavailability Deduc tions or Non compliance Points assessed under the Project Agreem ent, and provided further

that the Asset Management Contractor will not h ave an obligation to share in such responsibility if it is re sponsible 
for less than 10% of such points or deductions.  If such amount is negative, the Asset Management Contractor sha ll 
pay such amount to the Borrower. 
Project Agreement Te rmination no Borrower Default.  If the Pr oject Agreement is terminated other than as 
a result of a n Asset Management Contractor Default and other tha n as a result of an Unavailability Default Trigger  
or a Noncompliance Borrower Default Trigger, the B orrower is required to pay  the Asset Management Contractor 
its Subc ontractor Breakage Co sts, prov ided that a nd wit hout double -counting, such pa yment will b e without 
prejudice to any claims under the Asset Management Contract that the Borrower and Asset Management Contractor 
may have against each other unrelated to the termination.

the Borrower and Asset Management Contractor may have against each other unrelated to the termination. 
Miscellaneous Termination Provisions 
Transfer of Key Assets.   The Asset Management Contractor shall on termination of the  Asset Management 
Contract (a)  transfer i ts rights , title an d i nterest in and to th e K ey Assets to the Borrower , or t he District at the 
direction of the Borrower, and (b) make available to th e Borrower, and the District at the direction of the Borrower, 
as of the Early Termination Date the benefit of all warranties in the possession of the Asset Management Contractor 
and related to the Project. 
Exclusivity of Remedy.  Any Asset Management Termination Sum irrevocably paid by the Borrower to the 
Asset Managemen t Contractor o r the Asset Management Contractor to the Borrower wi ll be in full a nd f inal 
settlement of each party's rights and claims against the other for breaches and termination of t he Asset Management 
Contract whether under contract, tort, restitutio n or otherwise, but with out prejudice to:  (a) any accrued li ability of 
either party to the other that arose prior to the Early Termination Date, but n ot from the termination itself, to th e 
extent such liability has not already been taken into account in the calculation of the Asset Management Termination 
Sum; and (b) any liabilities arising with respect to any breach by either party after the Early Termination Date of any 
obligation under the  Asset Management Contract that survives the Early Termination Date , to the extent not ta ken 
into account in the calculation of the Asset Management Termination Sum.

Agreement and the District may take such actions under the Asset Management Contract and the Asset Management

Borrower Right to Step-In 
If the Borrower accrues 75% of eith er of the Noncomp liance P oints or Un availability Deductions 
thresholds set out in the definitions of Noncompliance Borrower Default Trigger or Unavailability Borrower Default 
Trigger (each as such term is defined in  t he Project Agreement) due to No ncompliance Points or Un availability 
Deductions, as a pplicable, accrued by both of the D& C Contractor a nd the Asset Management Contractor u nder 
their respective su bcontracts,  Borro wer may exercise its step -in rights in accordance with the subcontract of t he 
Subcontractor that accumulated the most Noncompliance Points or Unavailability Deductions, as applicable. 
If any such event has occurred a nd the Borrower wishes to take step-in action under the Asset Management 
Contract, the Borrower must notify the Asset Management Contractor, in writing, of the action the Borrower wishes 
to take, the reason  for such action, the date it wishes to commence such action, the time period that it believes will 
be necessary for such action, and, to the extent practicabl e, the  effect such action will  have on the Asset 
Management Contractor's obligation to carry out the Asset Management Work. Following service of such notice, the 
Borrower may take su ch a ctions previou sly described or otherwise as in the opinion  of the Borrower r easonably 
required to ad dress the matters that resulted in the entitlement of  the Borrower t o exer cise its step -in right.  The 
Asset Management Contractor will use reasonable efforts to give ass istance to the Bo rrower while it is taking such 
action. The  Borrower is required to pr ovide the Asset Management Contractor with notice of completion of  such 
action and shall use reasonable efforts to provide such advance notice as is reasonably practicable of its anticipated 
completion. 
The Asset Management Contractor shall pay to the Borrower all of the  reasonable co sts incurred by the 
Borrower in taking any such action.

Restrictions on Change in Ownership 
The Asset Management Contractor shall ensure that there will be no sale, transfer, or other dis posal of any 
direct or indirect legal, beneficial, equitable or other ownersh ip interest in the Asset Management Contractor which, 
when taken together with any  other such transfer or disposal during the relevant period gives rise to a  Change of 
Control of the Asset Management Contractor; grant of  any security i nterest, lien or othe r e ncumbrance over an y 
legal, beneficial, equitable or other direct or indirect ownership interest in the Asset Management Contractor giving 
rise to a Change  of Control of the Ass et Management Cont ractor; or agr eement, w hether or no t s ubject to any 
condition precedent or subse quent, to  do any of the foregoing, ( collectively a "Change of Control Transaction"), 
without the prior written consent of the Borrower, such consent not to be  withheld or delay ed where (a) the Asset 
Management Contractor can demo nstrate to the satisfaction of t he Borrower, acti ng reasonably, that t he Asset 
Management Contractor will hav e a level o f financial capacity, res ources, and ability to perform the Asset 
Management Work a fter the Change of Cont rol Transaction that is equal to o r grea ter than the leve l of financial 
capacity, resources, and ability to perform the As set Management Work held by the  Asset Management Contractor 
before the Change of Control Tran saction and ( b) t he Change of Co ntrol Transaction will n ot ha ve a Material  
Adverse Effect.

written decision as soo n as practica ble and in any event wi thin twenty (20) d ays of th e delivery of the notice of 
dispute, which decision shall be final and binding.  All other disputes shall be determined pursuant to arbitration.

Management Work pending resolution of any Dispute, including any Asset Management Work that is the subject  of

the Dispute, except for any performance Borrower determines in writing should be delayed, suspended or t erminated

Governing Law and Jurisdiction 
The Asset Management Contract will be g overned b y and  construed in accordance wit h the laws of the 
District of Columbia.  The Asset Management Contractor and the Bor rower consent to the jurisdiction of any court 
of the District of Columbia, waiving  any claim or defense t hat such forum is not co nvenient or proper. The Asset 
Management Contractor and  the Borrower a gree t hat such court shall have  in personam jurisdiction over it, and 
consents to service of process in any manner authorized by applicable Law. 
Relationship Between District Determination and Borrower Determination 
Where, in accordance wi th the  provisions of th e Asset Management Contra ct, a d etermination, action,  
negotiation, decision, discretion, election or other thing is made, taken, done , exercised or elected by the Bo rrower 
in respect of a matter in accordance with a determination, action, negotiation, decision, discretion, electio n or other 
thing made, ta ken, done, exercised or  elected by the District in respect of the same matter purs uant of the Project 
Agreement, to the extent that Asset Management Contractor disagrees with such de termination, action, negotiation, 
decision, discretion, election or other thing,  Asset Management Contractor's sole remedy, to the extent permitted in 
accordance with the terms of the Asset Management Contract is to bring a Pass Through Claim, a claim against the 
District pursuant to the Project Agreement.  
Equivalent Project Relief  
 Certain Defined Terms 
A "Corresponding Pr oject Agreement Claim " means (i) w ith respect to a Pass Th rough C laim, the 
corresponding claim that the Borrower may assert against the Dis trict under the Project Agreement, as descri bed in 
in accordance with  the Asset Management Contract, and (ii) with re spect to a District Claim , the corr esponding 
claim that the District has asserted against Borrower under the Project Agreement. 
"District Claim"  means any claim that the Borrower may assert against the Asset Management Contractor 
under Asset Management Contract where the District has asserted a corresponding claim against the Borrower under 
the Project Agr eement, including a cl aim by the District that th e Borrower is in breach of the P roject Agreement 
where such breach is caused by a breach by th e Asset Management Contractor of the Asset Man agement Contract 
and a claim b y the District in volving events or circumstances giving ris e to a possible claim by Borrower against 
Asset Management Cont ractor under the Asset M anagement Contract or oth er remedies, compens ation or 
reimbursement for Borrower under the Asset Management Contract. 
A "Project Agreement Dispute Proceeding" means with respect to a Pass Through Claim or District Claim, 
the proceeding under the Project Agreement by which the Corresponding Project Agreement Claim is resolved. 
A "Pass Through Claim" means any claim that the Asset  Ma nagement Contractor may assert against t he 
Borrower under the Asset Management Contract where the Bo rrower may assert a corres ponding claim against the 
District under the Pr oject Agreement, including a cla im for which the cause is a Relie f Event, Compensation Event 
or Force Majeure Event, a cl aim by the Asset Management Contractor that the Borrower is in breach of the Asset 
Management Contract where such breach is caused by a breach by the District of the Project Agreement, and a claim 
by the Asset Ma nagement Cont ractor i nvolving events or cir cumstances giving ri se to a pos sible c laim by the 
Borrower against the District under the Project Agreement or other remedies, compensation, relief or reimbursement 
for the Borrower under the Project Agreement. 
A "Borrower Act" means, wit h respect to a Pass Through Claim, any breach of Borrower's obligations 
under the Asset Management Contract o r a ny negligent  act or omission of Borrower th at m aterially adversely  
D-24 
impairs the validity of, or the relief available from, the District for the Corresponding Project Agreement Claim or a 
Borrower Default (Asset Management Contract). 
Pass Through Claims 
The Asset Management Contractor is required to  give written notice to the Borrower as soon as reasonably 
practicable upon becoming aware of an y events or circumstances giving rise to a po ssible Pass Th rough Claim and 
in any event within any applicable time periods required under the Asset Management Contract.  
Whenever the Borrowe r is perm itted t o do so by the t erms of the Project Agreement and the Asset 
Management Contract, t he Borrower shall in good f aith, unle ss it determines  it wo uld be manifestly frivolo us or 
vexatious to do so, present all Asset Management Contractor Pass Through Claims to the District as C orresponding 
Project Agreement Claims  to the  extent r equested b y t he Asset Management Contractor and at  the Asset 
Management Contractor's cost. The Borrower will, as required by the Asset Management Contractor, unless it would 
be frivolous or vexatious to do so, enforce those rights and re medies and claim relief and comp ensation and those 
defenses under the Project Agreement, which are for the benefit of the Borrower in respect of the Asset Management 
Work in accordance with the directio ns o f the Asset Managem ent Contractor an d at the Asset  Management 
Contractor's cost or , alternatively, the Borro wer may consent to th e Asset Management Contractor pursui ng such 
rights, reme dies, relief, compensatio n and defenses in the name of the Borrower and at Asset Managemen t 
Contractor's cost, in either case, to t he extent that the relevant rights, rem edies, relief, compensation and defenses 
relate to the Asset Management Work or the Asset Management Contract and involve the District. 
As between t he Borrower and the Asset Management Contractor, the resolution of a Pass Through Claim 
will not be subject to any dis pute resolution procedures under  the Asset Management Co ntract, and all appl icable 
time periods with respect to the resolution of such claim  under the Asset Management Contract will be tolled, while 
the Project Agreement Dispute Proceeding with respect to th e Corresponding Project Agreem ent Claim is pending. 
With respect to its Pa ss Through Claim against the Borrower under the Project Docu ments, the Asset Management 
Contractor is bo und by the resolution or final  determination of the Corresponding Project Ag reement Claim under 
the Project Agreement.  
In respect of all  Pass Through Claims: 
(a) the Asset Management Contractor shall be entitled  to, and the Borrower's liability shall be limited 
to, whatever compens ation, monetary relief, schedule relief, or other  remedy that the Borrower actually recovers 
from the District under the Proj ect Agreement with respect to the Corresponding Project Ag reement Claim, less the 
portion of su ch reco very, if any, not a ttributable t o the Asset Managem ent Contractor 's Loss under  the Asset 
Management Contract. The schedule relief to which the Asset Management Contractor is entitled shall be calculated 
subject t o the Project Agreement, and th e m onetary re lief to which the A sset Manag ement Contractor is e ntitled 
shall be calculated taking int o account Asset Managemen t Contractor's obligations to bear the co st of insurance 
deductibles and the risk of receipt of insurance proceeds pursuant to the Asset Management Contract; and 
(b)  if it is determined through a Project Agreement Dispute Proceeding that the District is not liable to 
the Borrower for the Corresponding Project Agreement Claim, the Asset Management Contractor shall be bound by 
such det ermination of t hat matter and t he Borrower shall be discharged o f a ll liability to the Asset Management 
Contractor in respect  of such Pass Through Claim, except with regards to a Borrower Default (Asset Management 
Contract) and prov ided that ( a) the Borrow er ha s compl ied w ith its obliga tions under the Asset Management 
Contract in respect thereof, and (b) all appeals from such determination have been exhausted.  
District Claims  
Each party to t he Asset Management Contract shall give n otice to the other Par ty a s soon as reasonably 
practicable upon becoming aware of any events or circumstances giving rise to a possible District Claim. 
The Parties shall adhere to the procedures, requirements, and l imitations set forth in the Asset Management 
Contract for resol ution of Dist rict C laims, with re spect to the pursuit of the Pa ss Th rough Claim related to any  
D-25 
District C laim. As between Borrower and the Asset Man agement Contractor, the resolution of a Corre sponding 
Project Agreem ent Claim will not be subject to any di spute r esolution proc edures under the Asset Man agement 
Contract or otherwise progressed, and all  applicable time periods with re spect to the resolution of such claim und er 
the Asset Management Contract will be tolled,  while the Project  Agreement Dispute Proceeding with respect to the 
Corresponding Project Agreement Cl aim is pending.  With respect to any D istrict Claim brought by th e Borrower 
under the Pro ject Documents, each of the Borro wer and the Asset Management C ontractor shall be b ound by the 
resolution or final determination of the Corresponding Project Agreement Claim under the Project Agreement.  
In the event of an award ren dered between the District and the Borrower under the Project Agree ment with 
respect to a Corr esponding Pr oject Agreement Claim , pu rsuant to which t he Borrower is liable in any respect 
whatsoever, Asset Management Contractor  will forthwith on demand indemnify the Borrower in full in respect of 
such liability to the extent Asset Management Contractor is liable therefor. 
Project Agreement Dispute Proceedings  
With respect to  any Pass Thro ugh Claim or District Claim, the Borrower in consultation with Asset 
Management Contractor, s hall elect whether ( a) the Asset Management Contractor (in  the name of  the Borrower) 
will pursue the  Corresponding Project Agree ment Claim un der the Proj ect Agreem ent, or (b) t he Borrower will 
pursue the Corresponding Project Agreement Claim under the Proj ect Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing or 
anything in t he Asset Management Contr act to the co ntrary, the  B orrower shall n ot be obliged to s ubmit to the 
District or otherwise pursue (or permit th e Asset Management Contractor to pursue in the name of t he Borrower) a 
Pass Through Claim if ( a) such claim is manifestly frivolous or vexatio us or (b ) the Borrower compensates Asset 
Management Contractor for its claim under the Asset Management Contract, the amount of such compensation to be 
reasonably determi ned by the Borrower and the Asset Management Contractor, subject to Dispute Resolution  
Procedures if the parties cannot reasonably agree. 
For each Project Agr eement Dispute Proceeding the Borrower may agree with the District to a resolution or 
settlement o f a ny Correspon ding Project Agreement Claim i n the course of the Project Agreement Dis pute 
Proceeding, subject to the following with respect to any Project Agreement Dispute Proceeding that relates sole ly to 
a Pass Through Claim of the Asset Management Contractor and doe s not involve any claim by the  Borrower, the 
Asset Management Cont ractor a nd the Borrower shall j ointly decide a nd direct the Borrower's r esolution or  
settlement of the Corresponding Proj ect Agreem ent Claim, and the Bor rower shall not agree to a resolution or 
settlement of the Correspo nding Project Agreement Claim without the  Asset Ma nagement Contractor's written 
consent (wh ich c onsent shal l not be unreasonably w ithheld, conditioned or del ayed). Wit h respect to any other  
Project Agreement Dis pute Proceeding the Borrower must obtain the A sset Management Contractor's prior written 
consent, which conse nt shall not be un reasonably withheld or d elayed, before agreeing with the District to a 
resolution or settlement of the Corresponding Project Agreement Claim. 
With respect to any Project Agree ment Dispute Proceeding that relates sol ely to a Pass Through Claim of 
the Asset Management Contractor and does not involve any claim by the Borrower, or that relates solely to a District 
Claim caused by an Asset Management Contractor-Related Entity, the costs and expenses, including attorneys' fees 
and expert fees, i ncurred in relatio n of the  Project Agreement  Dispute Proceeding, i ncluding such r easonable 
documented c osts a nd expenses incur red by the Borrower ( provided that the Bo rrower shall mitigate, an d not 
unnecessarily duplicate, its costs a nd expenses), shall be  borne solely by t he Asset Management Contractor. With 
respect to any other Pro ject Agreement Dispute P roceeding not described in the previous sent ence, except to the 
extent otherwise provided  in t he Asset Management Contract, each o f t he Parties will bear  it s own co sts and  
expenses, i ncluding a ttorneys' fees and expert fees, incurre d in relatio n of the Pro ject Agreement Dispute 
Proceeding.

APPENDIX E 
 
SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE COLLATERAL AGENCY AGREEMENT 
The following is a summary of selec ted provisions o f the Collateral Agency Agreement r elating to the 
Project and is not a full statement of th e terms of such agr eement. Accordingly, the f ollowing summary is qualified 
in its enti rety by refer ence to su ch agreement and i s subject to t he full text o f s uch agr eement. A copy of suc h 
agreement is  av ailable, free of cha rge, upon r equest from the Borrower or the Bond Trust ee. Unless otherwise 
stated, a ny reference in this Officia l State ment to any agre ement wi ll mean such agree ment and al l s chedules, 
exhibits and attac hments thereto,  as ame nded, suppl emented or otherwise modified and  in effect as of  the date 
hereof. 
The Collateral Agent 
Duties and Responsibilities 
The Collateral A gent will administer and enforce the Collateral Agency Agreement and the ot her Security 
Documents, and, among other remedies, foreclose upon, collect and dispose of the Collateral and apply the proceeds 
therefrom, for the b enefit of the Secured Parties , and otherwise perform its duties and obligations as the Collateral 
Agent. In most instances, the Collateral Agent w ill only be required to act or refr ain from act ing upon the written 
instructions of the Bond Trustee (acting in accordance with the Bond Indenture); however, the written instructions of 
all of the Secured Parties will be required where expressly provided in the Collateral Agency Agreement. 
In no event will the Collateral Agent be required to foreclose on, or take possession of, the Collateral, if, in 
its reasonable j udgment, such a ction w ould be in violation of any A pplicable Law p ertaining ther eto, or if the 
Collateral Agent reasonably believes that  such action w ould result in the incurrence of l iability by the Co llateral 
Agent for which it is not fully indemnified by the Borrower. 
Neither the Coll ateral Agent, the Securities Intermediary nor any of their directors, officers, employees or 
agents will be liable or resp onsible for any action taken o r omitted to be ta ken by it o r them under the  Collateral 
Agency Agreement, except for its or their own negligence or willful misconduct. 
Authorization 
The C ollateral A gent i s a uthorized by the Bond Trustee , o n behalf of  each Secured Party, to take the 
appropriate actions ne cessary to perform under the Collateral  Agency Agreement, exercise and enforce any and all 
available rights, powers and remedies, and take any other action which the Bond Trustee (acting in accordance with 
the Bond Indenture) shall direct . The Collateral Agent will deliver copies of all notice s it receives on behalf of an y 
of the Secured Parties or i n connection with the Finance Documents or the Project to the Bond  T rustee promptly 
upon receipt. 
Administrative Actions 
The Collateral Agent may, but is not ob ligated to, take action it deems necessa ry to perfect or contin ue the 
perfection of the Security Interests on the Collateral held for the benefit of the other Secured Parties. The Collateral 
Agent will not release any of the Collater al held for the benefit  of the Secured Parties, except: (a) upon the written 
direction of the Bond Trustee (at the direction of the Majority Bondholders) ; (b) upon payment in full of the Senior 
Secured Obligations; (c) for Co llateral consisting of a debt instrument if the Indebtedness evidenced thereby has 
been paid in full; or (d) where such release is expressly permitted under the Security Documents. 
Reliance o f C ollateral Agent 
In c onnection with the performance of its duties und er the Collateral Agenc y Agreement, each of  the 
Collateral A gent and the Securi ties Inter mediary will b e entitled  t o rely conclusi vely upon, and  will be fully 
protected in  acting or r efraining from act ing i n accordance with, any wr itten c ertification, n otice, instrument,  
E-2 
 
opinion, request, consent, order, approval, direction or other written communication of the Bond Trustee or of any  
other Secured  Party, which the Collatera l Ag ent or t he Se curities Interm ediary, as applicable, in good fait h 
reasonably believes to be genuine and to have been signed o r sent by or on behalf of the proper person or persons, 
and it will be  e ntitled to rely  conclusively upon the due executio n, validity  and ef fectiveness, and the  truth, 
correctness and acceptability of, any  p rovisions co ntained therein.  The Collateral A gent and the S ecurities 
Intermediary will not have any responsibility to make any investigation into the facts or matters stated in any n otice, 
certificate, instrument, demand, request, direction, instruction, or other communication furnished to it. Whenever the 
Collateral Agency Agreement sp ecifies tha t any instruction or consent by the Bond Tr ustee is to be  given i n 
accordance with the terms o f the Bond Inden ture, the Collateral Agent  will be en titled to rely u pon any s uch 
instruction or consent by the Bond Trustee, and the Collateral Agent has the right to presume without investigation 
that any such instruction or consent by  the Bond Trustee has been given in accordance with the terms of the Bond 
Indenture. 
Resignation and Removal; Successor Collateral Agent; Individual Collateral Agent 
Subject to the appoin tment and acceptance of a successor Collateral Agent, the Collateral Agent may resign 
at any time by giv ing at least 30 days ' prior written notice to the oth er Secured Parties and the Borrower, and t he 
Collateral Agent may be removed  at any time with or without cause by the Bond Trustee (at the direction o f the 
Majority Bondholders) upon 30 days ' written notice thereof to the Collater al Age nt, the other Secured P arties 
(including the Bond Trustee ) and t he Borrower. Upon any such res ignation or re moval, the Bond Tru stee (at the  
direction of the Majority Bondholders) will have the right to appoint a successor Collateral Agent which, so long as 
no Event of  Default u nder the C ollateral A gency Agreement has occurred an d is c ontinuing, must be reason ably 
acceptable to the Borrower. If no successor Collateral Agent will have been so appointed by the Bond Trustee within 
30 days after the ret iring Collateral Agent's giving of notice of resignation or the removal of t he retiring Collateral 
Agent by the Bond Trustee, then the retiring Collateral Agent has the right to, on behalf of the Secured Parties, apply 
to a court of competent jurisdiction (with notice to  the Bond Trustee  and t he Borrower) for the ap pointment of a 
successor Collateral Agent. In all such cas es, the successor Collateral Agent must be a bank organized under the 
laws of  the Uni ted States of America or any  state th ereof and which agrees to admin ister the Collateral in 
accordance with the terms of the Collateral Agency Agreement and of the other Security Documents and will have a 
total capital stock and unimpaired sur plus of not le ss than $500,000,000 and, so long as no Event of Default under 
the Collateral Agency Agreement has occurred and is continuing, must be reasonably acceptable to the Borrower. 
Books and Records; Reports 
(a)
 The C ollateral A gent wi ll at al l times ke ep, or cause to be kept, proper  books o f reco rd and  
account in which com plete and accurate entries shall be made of al l transactions of t he Collateral Agent relating to 
the S ecured Obl igations, Revenues  and al l Project Account s established pu rsuant to the Colla teral Ag ency 
Agreement. Such books of record and accounts will be av ailable for i nspection by the Bond Trustee and the other 
Secured P arties, o r t heir authorized agent s or represe ntatives duly authorized in writing, at  reasonable hours and 
under reasonable circumstances and upon reasonable prior written request. 
(b) Within 15 days after the end o f each month, the Collateral Agent will furnish to the Bond Trustee 
and the  Borrower a report that sets forth th e account balances, recei pts, d isbursements, transf ers, inve stment 
transactions, and accruals for each of the Project Accounts during such month. 
(c) Within 90 d ays after the end o f each year, the Collateral Agent will furnish to th e Bond Trustee  
and/or the Borrower a r eport setting forth the account balances, receip ts, disbursements, tra nsfers, i nvestment 
transactions, and accruals for each of the Project Accounts during the preceding year.  
(d) Th e Co llateral Agent will maintain r ecords of all receipts, d isbursements, a nd investments of 
funds with respect to the P roject Accounts until the fifth anniversary of the date on which all of the Senior Secured 
Obligations have been paid in full.

such amounts out of t he following accounts in the following order of priority: (i ) from the Equity Lock-Up Account 
and (ii) (x) prior to the Project Final Completion Date, from the Construction Account (to t he extent permitted by 
applicable Law, including  th e Code and Treasury  Regulations), and (y ) upon a nd following the Proje ct Final 
Completion Date from th e Revenue Account. T hese provisions sur vive the termination of the F inance Do cuments 
and the resignation or removal of the Collateral Agent.

the Collateral Agency  Agreement to each Bon dholder that requests  such reports, documents  and information and 
that has established its status as a Beneficial Bondholder. 
Special Covenants 
The Collateral Agency Agreement includes, but is not limited to, the following covenants: 
Maintain Legal Structure. T hroughout the term of th e Collateral Age ncy Agreement, the Borrower shall 
maintain (a) its legal existence as a limited liability company, (b) its good standing and quali fication to do business 
in t he D istrict of Colum bia, and (c) all ma terial rights, fr anchises, privileges  and consents  necessary for t he 
maintenance of its existence and for the development, construction and operation and maintenance of the Project and 
in order to perform its obligations and exercise its remedies under the Transaction Documents. 
 
No 
Repudiation of Finance Do cuments. Th e Borrower shall not take any action to repudiate  any of its 
obligations under the Finance Documents. 
 
Litigation.  The Borrower shall take all necessary  a ction, acting reas onably, to m anage an y litigation to 
which it is a party in relation to  the Project. The Borrower shall manage such litigation in  a prudent an d diligent 
manner and may enter into any settlement of any such litigation, subject, in any cas e, to the terms and condit ions of 
the Project Documents.

and shall ensure that the intere sts of the Collateral Agent are included  in any "lender clauses" identified in the final

with thirty (3 0) days after receipt of s uch no tice by the Collate ral Agent to make s uch payment , and an y such

Collateral Agent, and that appropriate cer tification shall  be ma de t o the Borrower by each i nsurer with respe ct 
thereto.

to audit its an nual financi al stateme nts. Concurrent with suc h appointment, th e Borrower shall authorize such 
accountants to communicate directly with the Bond Trustee or the Col lateral Agent and t o respond to queries of the 
Bond Tru stee or the Colla teral Agent regarding the Borrower's accounts and operations. The B orrower shall be 
given a reasonabl e opportunity to participate i n such communications of the Bo nd Trustee or the Collateral Agent 
when they are by phone or in person, and shall be copied on such communications when they are in writing.   
(c) The Bo rrower shall, as soon as practicable upon becomi ng aware of the occurren ce of any of the 
following (and, where applicable, any actions taken or proposed in resp ect of the event), but in any event by no later 
than the time period specified (if any), deliver the following information to the Collateral Agent: 
(i) Incorrect  Repres entation or Warra nty: If any re presentation or warranty made by the 
Borrower under the Finance Documents is incorrect or misleading in any material respect. 
(ii) Default: I f a Default or Event of Default has occurred under the Finance Documents. 
(iii) Litigation and Proceed ings: If any claim, litigation, suit, proceeding or action is pen ding 
or threatened against the  Borrower that, if resolved, coul d r easonably be expected to have a Material 
Adverse Effect or which involves potentially liability in excess of $5,000,000. 
(iv) Authorizations: A ny complaint, action or threat under any applicable Law or in respect of 
any material permit, license, approval or consent which is likely to have a Material Adverse Effect. 
(v) Deductions: If a ny Noncompliance R emedial Plan Trigger or U navailability Remedial 
Plan Trigger occurs under the Project Agreement. 
(vi) Breaches:  Any material default or any event of d efault under the Project Agreement or 
any other Project Document which is likely to have a Material Adverse Effect; 
(vii) Compulsory A cquisition: A ny proposal by a Governmental Authority to acquire b y 
compulsion any  material part of the Project. 
(viii) Unbudgeted  Expenditure or Change in Business: Any event which is reasonably likely to 
result in a r equirement of exp enditure (including, for certai nty, major maintenance cost) in respect o f the  
E-6 
 
Project which takes the total amount of the Borrower's unbudgeted expenditures for a Fisca l Year beyond 
10% of the budgeted amount or a material alteration to the business of the Borrower. 
(ix) Th ir d Party Claims: Any adverse third party claim in respect of the Proj ect that may have 
a Material Adverse Effect. 
(x) Insurance : A cancellation, material change or reductio n in an insurance policy taken out 
on behalf of the Borrower, Asset Management Contractor or the D&C Contractor, in respect of the Project, 
any such poli cy becoming void or void able or any o ther material circumstance or correspondence relating 
to it, in each case, which is reasonably likely to have a Material Adverse Effect.

insurance policy in excess of $5,000,000. 
(xii) O fficers/Dir ectors: Any change in the Borrower's Authorized Officers. 
(xiii) Change in Ownership: Any Change in Ownership. 
(xiv) Asset Management Plan: If any material changes occur to the Asset Management Plan. 
(xv) Environmental Claim: Within five (5)  Business Days after recei pt thereof, copies of al l 
environmental notices from Governmental Authorities relating to the Project. 
(xvi) Auditors: Within  ten (10) Business Days after the occurrence thereof, any changing of its 
auditors.

accordance with t he Pro ject Agreement f or any failure in r elation to appropriation or upon the Borrower

Borrower Continuing Disclosure Agreement.

Use of Proceeds; Tax Covenant 
(a) The Borrower shall use the pro ceeds of the Senior Debt, any  Permitted Indebtedness subordinate 
to the Senior Debt and any Equity Commitment for the purposes of paying Project Costs, including all debt service 
and other costs and expenses of the Borrower consistent with the Financial Model. 
(b) The Borrower shall not permi t any use of  the proceeds of t he Series 2 022A Bonds o r the Tax -
Exempt Additional Parity Bonds which would cause the Series 202 2A Bonds or the Tax -Exempt Additional Parity 
Bonds to be classified as "arbitrage bonds" within the meaning of section 148 of the Code. 
(c) The B orrower hereby covenants f or the be nefit of the Bond Issu er an d the Bondholders  of the 
Series 2022A Bonds and the Tax-Exempt Additional Parity Bonds that it will not take any action or omit to take any 
action with respect to the Series 2022A Bonds or the Tax-Exempt Additional Parity Bonds and the proceeds thereof, 
any other funds of the Borrower o r any of the facilities financed with the proceeds of the S eries 2022A Bonds or the 
Tax-Exempt Additional Parity Bo nds if such action or omission (i) woul d cause interest paid on  the Series 2022A 
Bonds or the Tax -Exempt Additional Parity Bond s to be includ ed in the gross income of the Bondholders of the 
Series 2022A Bonds or the Tax-Exempt Additional Parity Bonds for federal income tax purposes (other than interest 
paid to Bondhol ders of the Series 2022A Bonds or the Tax -Exempt Additional Parity Bonds that are a "substantial 
user" of the facilities financed or r efinanced with the Bonds or a "related person" within the m eaning of Section 
147(a) of the Code) or (ii)  would cause interest paid on  the Bond s t o be included in th e tax able income of th e 
Bondholders for District of Co lumbia income tax purposes, under pres ent District of Columb ia law. The foregoing 
covenant shall remain in full force and effect notwithstanding the payment in full or defeasance of the Bonds until 
the date on which all oblig ations of the  Borrower in fulfi lling the above covenant under the Cod e and District o f 
Columbia law have been met. 
(d) The Bo rrower f urther covenants , re presents and warrant s t o comply with the r equirements and 
covenants applicable to it, as the case may be, set forth in the Tax Certificate to the extent necessary to comply with 
the covenants set forth in paragraph c above. The Borrower shall (i) comply at al l times with the  requirements and 
covenants set forth in the Tax Certificate and (ii) enforce relevant provisions of the Project Agreement and the other 
Project Documents, in each case, as necessary to maintain the tax-exempt status of the Series 2022A Bonds and the 
Tax-Exempt Additional Parity Bonds, and the Borrower will not take any action, or omit to take any action , which, 
if taken o r omitted, would violate the Tax Certificate or c ause the  Series 2 022A Bonds or the  Tax-Exempt 
Additional Parity Bonds to lose their tax exempt status.

Completion. If the LTA reas onably determines at any time that th e Project Final Completion Date w ill be 
delayed b eyond the  Long St op De adline, the Borrower shall develop in consultation with the L TA an I nitial 
Remedial Plan satisfactory to th e LTA, acting reasonably , that demonstrat es that th e Borrower will achieve the 
Project Final Completion D ate by no later  than t he Long Stop Deadline . The Borrower shall submit the  Initial 
Remedial Plan t o the LTA as  soon as possible and by no later than fi fteen (15) Business Days  after such 
determination. The Borrower shall comply in all mater ial respects with the Initial Remedial Plan, provided t hat a 
breach of the Initial Remedial Plan by the Borrower shall not con stitute or give ri se to a Default or an Event of 
Default. If there is any material breach by the Borrower of the Initial Remedial Plan, the Borrower shall be required 
to develop, in con sultation with the LTA, a Fina l Remedial Plan  satisfactory to the LTA, acting reasonably, which 
will again be required to demonstrate th at the Borrower will achieve the Project Final Completion Date by no l ater 
than the Long Stop Deadline. The Borrowe r shall submit the Fin al Remedial Plan  to the LTA as soon  as possible 
and no later than ten (10) Business Days after the o ccurrence of a mater ial breach of the Initial Remedial Plan. The 
Borrower shall comply in all material respects with the Final Reme dial Pla n, provided that  a breach of the Final 
Remedial Plan  by the Borrower shall not constitute or give rise to a Default o r an Event of Defau lt. Any 
amendments to the I nitial Remedial Plan or the Final Remedial Plan must be satisfacto ry to the LTA, act ing 
reasonably. Such Ini tial Remedial Plan or Final Remedial Plan may b e th e same as that which may be required 
under the  Project Agreement and s uch remedial plan required under t he Proj ect Agreement shall supersede any 
Initial Remedial P lan or Final Remed ial Plan; provided that the remedial pla n of the Borrower under the Pro ject 
Agreement must provide that the Pr oject Final  Completion Date is achie ved by no l ater than the Long Stop 
Deadline.

Event) on the Project is performed for a continuous period of more than forty-five (45) days.

Blocked Person) a Blocked Pers on; or ( b) directly or  indirectly have any investment in or engage in any de aling or 
transaction (including,  without limitatio n, any investment, dealing or tran saction invo lving the proceeds o f t he 
Bonds) with any Person if such investment, dealing or tran saction ( i) would caus e any Secur ed P arty to  be in 
violation of or subject to sanctions under any Sanctions laws or (ii) is prohibited by or subject to sanctions under any 
Sanctions laws.

use the proceeds of the Bonds for any purpose which would violate the Anti-Corruption Laws. 
Entity. The Borrower shall not file an election pursuant to Treasury Regulation 301.7701-3(c) to be treated 
as an association taxable as a corporation or otherwise subject to taxa tion as a corp oration for U.S. federal in come 
tax purposes. 
Joint Ventures and Partnerships. The Borrower shall not enter into any partnership, joint venture, profit 
sharing or similar arrangement whereby the Borrower's income or profits are shared with any Person.

Additional Parity Bonds 
(a) Upon request by the Borrower, the Bond Issuer may issue Additional Parity Bonds pursuant to the 
terms of the Bond Indenture if any of the following conditions in clauses (i), (ii) or (iii) below are met: 
(i) Completion Bonds .  Additional Parity Bonds may be issued if necessary to complete 
construction of the Project after expendi ture of all proceeds of the Series 2022 Bonds and al l Equity 
Commitment, in an aggregate principal amount not to exce ed 10% of the original principal amount of the 
Series 2022 Bonds.  Such Additional Parity Bonds may be issued if:  
(A) the LTA certifies to the Collateral Agent and the Bond Trustee that the proceeds of 
such Additional Parity Bon ds are n ecessary and suff icient to comple te the Project; 
and 
(B) the Borrower certifies to the Bond Trust ee and the Collateral Agent that the 
Projected Senior DSCR shall not be less than 1.00:1.00. 
(ii) Refunding Bonds.  Additional Parity Bonds may be issued for the purpose of refinancing, 
replacement or refunding any Outstand ing Bonds so long as the Borrower provides written certification to 
the Collateral Agent and the Bond Trustee that: 
(A) the debt service payable i n each Bond Ye ar on all Outstanding Bond s af ter the 
issuance of such Additional Parity Bonds (excluding any Bonds that will be refunded 
or defeased with a portion of the proceeds of the Additional Parity Bonds) does not 
exceed the debt service payable on all Bonds Outstanding prior to the  issuance o f 
such Additional Parity Bonds in each Bond Year through final maturity of all Bonds 
(which maturity shall not be extended beyond the final maturity of the B onds 
Outstanding prior to such refunding or defeasance); or   
(B) the Projected Senior D SCR shall n ot be less than 1.1 0:1.00, and t he Borrower 
provides written c onfirmation to the Collateral Agent and the Bond Trustee that the 
issuance of such Additional Parity Bonds will not result in an Adverse Ratings Effect 
resulting in a rating assigned to the Bonds below investment grade. 
(iii) Other Additional Parity Bonds .  Additional Parity Bonds may be issued if necessary to 
fund the costs of (A) completing the Handback Requirements in accordance with the Project Agreement, 
(B) Safety Compliance requi red by  t he Project A greement, or (C) any Changes or Relief Events arising 
under the Project Agreement; in each case after expenditure of all proceeds of the Series 2022 Bonds and 
the Equity Commitment and application of all funds available for such purposes from the Project Accounts, 
in an aggrega te principal amount not to exceed the amount of funds necessary to fund the costs set out in 
clauses (A) through (C), and related reserves and costs of issuance for s uch Additional Parity Bonds.  Such 
Additional Parity Bonds may only be issued if the Borrower provides a certificate to the Collateral Agent 
and the Bond Trustee that:  
E-16 
 
(A) the Projected Senior DSCR shall not be less than 1.10:1.00; 
(B) the issuance of s uch Additional Pa rity Bonds will not res ult in an  Adverse Ratings Effect 
resulting in a rating assigned to the Bonds below investment grade; and 
(C) with resp ect to clause (C), the LTA confirms that the Borrower will continue to meet its 
asset management requirements. 
(b) All Additional Parity Bonds must be issu ed on the s ame terms and conditions then applicable to 
the then Outstanding Bonds, unless otherwise approved by the Bond Issuer and the Borrower, except that the interest 
rate on such Additional Parity Bonds must be fixed and the amortization applicable to  any such Additional Parity 
Bonds would be subject to then-current market conditions and on terms acceptable to the Borrower. 
(c) To the extent that any or all of the Bonds are Outstanding at the time the Additional Parity Bonds 
are proposed to be incurred, the additional financing documents entered into in con nection therewith (i) shall not 
prohibit the Borrower from incurring new Indebtedness to refinance such Bonds (at  least to the e xtent permitted 
hereunder and under the Loan Agreement), ( ii) shall provide that all pr incipal and interest paym ent dates with 
respect to such Additional Parity Bonds will be the same principal and interest payment dates as for the Bonds t o 
remain Outstanding through maturity of such Bonds and (iii) shall require that the proceeds of the Additional Parity 
Bonds, and  any payments du e thereunder, are paid directly to the Construction Account on the same terms and 
conditions as the Outstanding Bonds  as set out in  "PROJECT ACCOUNTS AND FLOW OF FUNDS —Project 
Accounts-Description of Project Accounts—Construction Account ." 
(d) Prior to t he issuance of any Additional Parity Bonds, in addition to those items required to be 
delivered by the Borrower pursuant to paragraph (b) of " APPENDIX F—SUMMARY OF CERTAIN TERMS OF 
THE BOND INDENTURE —Additional Parity Bonds," the Borrower must deliver to the Bond Trustee , the Bond  
Issuer and the Collateral Agent the following: 
(i)  a certificate from the Borrower, signed by a Borrower Representative, dated as of the date 
of issuance of such propos ed Additional Parity Bonds stating that no Default or Event of Default has 
occurred and is continuing or will result from the issuance of such Additional Parity Bonds;   
(ii) an updated Fin ancial Model upon which the Projected Senior DS CR shall be based 
(showing a Pro jected Senior DSCR of at least 1.10:1.00 or 1.00:1. 00 (as applicable) ) which contains 
current projects as of the time of the issuance of the Additional Parity Bonds and which ha s been reviewed 
and certified to be correct and accurate in all material respects by the Borrower and the LMA; 
(iii) executed counterparts of all financing documents related to the Additional Parity Bonds 
including, without limitation, ( A) a certified copy  of the executed counterpart of the Supplemental Loan 
Agreement, under which the Bond Issuer a grees to loan th e proceeds of the Addi tional Parity Bonds to the 
Borrower, and (B) an original e xecuted counterpart of the Supplemental Bond Indenture under which the 
Additional Parity Bonds have been issued; and 
(iv) consultant reports in final form from each of the LTA and the LIA that, after giving effect 
to the issuance of such Additi onal Parity Bonds (and the refunding, prepayment, or defeasance of Bonds in 
connection therewith), th e information contained in the r espective consultant report is consistent with the 
Financial Model as updated for the Additional Parity Bonds.
