Loss Proceeds Account 
All insurance or loss proceeds received by the Borrower or to its order (other than in respect of (a) sof t 
costs and business interruption proceeds or other anticipated losses of revenue, which are to be paid directly to the 
Revenue Account and (b) delayed start-up proceeds, received at any time prior to the Project Final Completion Date, 
which are to be paid directly to the Construction Account) and all condemnation proceeds received by the Borrower, 
in each case, with respect to physical damage to the Project shall be paid directly into the Loss Proceeds Account.  
Except in connection with  t he application o f funds after an Event of Default, amount s o n deposit in the  Loss 
Proceeds Account will be withdrawn and paid to the Borrower to be applied to th e restoration of the Project or any 
portion thereof in accordance with the requirements of t he Project Agreement, except that, to the extent th at (i) such 
proceeds exceed t he amount required to restore th e Project or any p ortion thereof to the condition re quired by the 
Project Agreement or, if the Project Agreement requires restoration of the Pro ject but does not require the condition 
for such re storation to be to the condi tion existing prior to the event of loss or (ii) th e affected property cannot be 
restored or is not required pursuant to the terms of the Project Agreement and the Finance Document s to be restored, 
and the Borrower elects not to do so or the Finance Documents otherwise require redemption of the Bonds with such 
amounts, suc h proceeds will be applied pro rata to the applicable sub -account of the Mandatory Prepayment 
Account to cause the extraordinary mandatory redemption of the Bonds (on a pro  rata basis), and, in the case of any 
remaining moneys thereafter, to the Revenue Account.  
If (and to the extent) moneys in the Reven ue Account are used for the restoration o f the P roject or any 
portion ther eof in accordance with the requirements of the P roject Agreement and proceeds of I nsurance or other 
monies in connection with such restoration have been deposited into or credited to  the Loss Proceeds Account, the 
Borrower may cause the transfer of such moneys from the Loss Proceeds Account to the Revenue Account.

shall be reduced by the aggregate amount o f the principal and interest that are scheduled to be paid f rom the Senior 
Debt DSRA. For greater certai nty and subject to the Restricted Payment Conditions being satisfied, th e Borrower 
shall not be entitled to any Restricted Payment until the Senior Debt DSRA is fully funded.

Senior Debt DSRA.

Investments on deposit  or to be deposited in the Senior De bt DSRA, an Acceptable Credit Suppor t in favor of the 
Collateral Agent with  a face value not less  (when aggregated with amounts on deposit in the Senior De bt DSRA) 
than the Required Senior Debt Service Reserve Level or t he Initial Required Senior Debt Serv ice Reserve Level, as

with such Acceptable Credi t Support and del ivers any such Acceptable Credit Support to  the Collateral Agent, the 
cash or Qualified Investments so replaced will be transferred to the Revenue Account.

used for any purpose to complete the construction of the Project.

Contract (and as a pre- condition to payment of amounts to the D&C Contractor), but the Borrower will have no 
other obligation to replenish the Construction Liquidity Reserve Account.

will be transferred to the Construction Account in order to fund the D&C Contractor Final Completion Pay ment. To 
the extent that any amounts are owed to the Borrower by the D&C Contractor as at the date of release, such amounts 
will be set-off from the D&C Contractor Final Completion Payment.

the terms of the Project Agreement. 
Qualified Investment  
Funds in the Project Accounts may be invested and reinvested only in Qualified Investments (at the risk 
and expense of the Borrower) in accordance with wri tten instructions given to the Collater al Agent by the Borrower 
(prior to the occurrence of an Event of Default and, thereafter (so long as such Event of Default will be continuing),

as directed by the Bond Trustee)  and, unless an Event of Default has occurred and is continuing, the Borrower is 
entitled to instruct the Collateral Agent to liquidate Qualified Investments for purposes of effecting any such 
investment or reinvestment or for any other purpose permitted under the Collateral Agency Agreement. 
Withdrawal and Application of Funds; Priority of Transfers from Project Accounts; Event of Default 
(a) Except for withdrawals by the Collateral Agent as provided in "—Construction Account " and  "—
Senior Debt DSRA " ab ove and elsewhere i n this section, each withdrawal o r transfer of funds from the  Project 
Accounts (other than from the HRA and the Operating Account)  by the Collateral Agent on behalf of the Borrower 
will be made pursuant to an executed Funds Tran sfer Certificate, which certificate will be provided and prepared by 
the Borrower and will contain a certification by the Borrower that such withdrawal or transfer complies with the 
requirements of the Collateral Agency Agreement. 
(b) Unless a shorter period is acceptable to the C ollateral Agent, such Funds  Transfer Certificate 
relating to each applicable Project Accounts (other than from the Excluded Accounts and the Operating 
Account) will be delivered to the Collateral Agent no later th an two (2) Business Days prior to e ach date on which 
funds are propose d to be w ithdrawn or transferred. In the event that a certificate does not comply with the 
requirements of the Collateral Agency Agreement, the Collateral Agent has the right to reject s uch certificate and 
the Borrower will not b e entitled to cause the pro posed withdrawal or transfer unti l it has submitted a revised and 
compliant certificate. 
(c) Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in the Collateral Agency Agreeme nt, upon 
receipt of a notice of an Event of Default a nd during the continuance o f the related Event of Default, t he Bond 
Trustee may, following the taking of an Enforcement Action, without consent of the Borrower, instruct the 
Collateral Agent in w riting to apply proceeds of the Project Accounts (other than the Excluded Accounts or the 
Operating Account) to the paym ent of Secured Obligations, in accordance with the terms of the Collateral Agency 
Agreement and in the order set forth in the section b elow titled "—Application of Proceeds "; provided that at any 
time prior to the taking of an Enforcement Action, proceeds of the Project Accounts will be applied in the order set 
forth in the Collateral Agency Agreement; provided further  that prior to the ta king of an Enforcement Action, 
proceeds in the Construction A ccount and the Senior Debt DSRA may only be applied in accordance with the 
provisions of the Collateral Agency Agreement. 
(d) For the avoidance of doubt, subject to the following paragraph, the Borrower and/or the Sponsors, 
as applicable, will have the rig ht to withdraw or cause to be transferred funds from the O perating Account at any 
time by direction to the Collateral Agent, so long as such withdrawal is effected in accordance with th e terms of the 
Collateral Agency Agreement. Withdrawals from the HRA shall be performed in accordance with the terms of the 
Project Agreement. 
(e) Notwithstanding any other provision of the  Collateral Agenc y Agreement, the Collateral Agent 
will not be obligated to monitor or verify (i)  the accuracy of any Funds Transfer Certificate or Construction Account 
Withdrawal Certificate or other written instructions provided to the Collateral Agent f or the transfer o r deposit of 
funds with respect to any Project Account, or (ii)  the use of amounts withdrawn from the Project Ac counts pursuant 
to written instructions given by the Borrower. 
Termination of Project Accounts 
(a) Upon the satisfaction in full of the Secured Obl igations as confirmed in writing by the Bond 
Trustee, the Collateral Agency Agreement will terminate, and the Co llateral Agent will, within 30 days of receipt of 
a request from the Borrower and at the expense of the Borrower, close the Project Accounts ( than (i) the Operating 
Account, which shall remain at the full discretion of the Borrower, and ( ii) the HRA,  from which funds shall be 
withdrawn in accordance with the terms of the Project Agreement) and/or liquidate any investments credited thereto 
and/or transfer the funds deposited therein or credited thereto, as directed by the Borrower.  
(b) Thereafter, the Collateral Agent will be releas ed from  any further obligation to  (i ) comply with 
entitlement orders originated by the Bond Trustee to the extent that any of the Project Accounts (other than the HRA  
E-29 
 
or the Operating Account) is a " securities account" under the applicable  provision of the  UCC or (ii) comply with 
instructions originated by the Bond Trustee, t o the extent that any of the Project Accounts (other than the Operating 
Account and the HRA) is a "deposit account" under the applicable provision of the UCC or (iii) c omply with any 
obligation under any Finance Document except as specifically provided therein. Nothing contained in this paragraph 
will be constr ued to modify or otherwise affect the Collateral Agent 's security interest in and lien on the Project 
Accounts and the funds therein, prior to such transfer. 
Funds as Collateral 
Any deposit made into the Project Accounts (except through clerical or other manifest error or in a manner 
that is otherwise inconsistent with the Collateral Agency Agreement)  will be irr evocable and all cas h, cash  
equivalents, instruments,  investments and other securities on d eposit in the Project Accounts (other than the 
HRA) will be subject to the Security Interest of the Security Agreement and will be held by the Collateral Agent as 
collateral for the benefit of the Secured Parties as provided in the Collateral Agency Agreement. 
Securities Intermediary 
(a) The Project Accounts will be established and maintained as securities accounts with a securities 
intermediary. Each of the parties t o the Collateral Age ncy Agr eement, have agreed that  U .S. Bank National 
Association (or any successor  thereto) will act as the securities intermediary ( the "Securities Intermediary") under 
and for the purposes of the Collateral Agency Agreement and fo r so l ong as the Collatera l Agent (or any successor 
thereto) is the Collateral Agent. The Securities Intermediary agrees that any financial assets credited to such Project 
Accounts, or any "securities entitlement" (as defined in Section  8-102(a)(17) of the UCC or, with respect to b ook-
entry securities, in the applicable Federal Book -Entry Regulations) with respect thereto, will not be subject to a ny 
security interest, lien, encumbrance, or right of setoff in favor of the Securities Intermediary or anyone cl aiming 
through the Securities Intermediary (other than the Collateral Agent). 
(b) It is the intent of the Collateral Agent and the Borrower that the Collateral Agent (for the benefit 
of the Secured Parties)  be the entitlement holder with respect to the Pr oject Accounts. In any event, the Se curities 
Intermediary has agreed that it will comply with entitlement orders with respect to the Project Acc ounts originated 
by the Collateral Agent without further consent by the Borrower or any other Person. Except as  otherwise provided 
in the C ollateral Agency Agreement, the S ecurities Intermediary has covenanted that it will not agree with any 
Person other than the Collateral Agent to comply with entitlement orders with respect to the Project Accounts 
originated by any Person or entity other than the Collateral Agent. 
(c) Each Project Account will remain at all times with a " securities intermediary" (within the meaning 
of Section 8-102(a)(14) of the UCC or, with respect to book -entry securities, in the applicable Federal Book -Entry 
Regulations) that is a bank organized under the laws of the United States of America or any state thereof that has 
offices in the State of New York that has a total capital stock and unimpaired surplu s of not less than $500,000,000. 
The Securities I ntermediary will give notice to the Collateral Agent and the Borrower of the location of the Project 
Accounts and of any change thereof prior to the use or change thereof. 
(d) A ny income received by  the Collateral Agent with respect to the balance from tim e t o time on 
deposit in each Project Account, includi ng any interest or capital gains on investments in overnight securities made 
with amounts on deposit in each Project Account, will be credited to  the applicable Project Account. All right, title 
and inte rest in and to the c ash amounts on deposit from time to time in each Project Account together with any 
investments in overnight securi ties made from time to time will constitute part of the Collater al for the Secured 
Obligations and will be held for the be nefit of the Secured  Parties and the Borrower as their interests appear under 
the Collateral Agency Agreement and will not constitute  payment of the Senior Secured Obligations (or any other  
obligations to which such funds are provided in the Collateral Agency Agreement to be applied) until applied thereto 
as provided in the Collateral Agency Agreement. 
(e) In the event that , notwithstan ding the last sentence of paragraph (a) above, the Secur ities 
Intermediary has or subsequently obtains by agreement, operat ion of law or otherw ise a Security Interest in any of  
the Project Accounts, or any financial asset credited thereto, or any "securities entitlement" (as defined in Section 8-
102(a)(17) of t he UCC or, with respect  to book -entry securities, in the applicable  Federal Book -Entry  
E-30 
 
Regulations) with respect thereto, the Securities Intermediary agrees that such security interest will be subordi nate to 
the security interest of the Collateral Agent. 
(f) Terms used in this S ection "—Securities Intermediary" that are defined in the UCC shal l have the 
meaning set forth in the UCC.  Without limiting the foregoing, the term "securities intermediary" shall, with respect 
to book-entry securities, have the meaning given to it under 31 C.F.R. Part 357 (regulations governing b ook-entry 
Treasury bo nds, no tes and bills held in Trea sury/reserve automated debt entry system (TRADES) and legacy 
Treasury direct); 12 C.F.R. Part 615 (book -entry procedures for Farm Credit Securities); 12 C.F.R.  Part 1511 (book-
entry procedure); 31 C.F.R . Pa rt 354 (regulatio ns governing book- entry securities of the Student Loan Marketing 
Association (SALLIE MAE)); and  18 C.F.R. Part 1314 (book -entry procedures for TVA power securities issued 
through the federal reserve banks). 
(g) To the extent that the P roject Accounts are n ot cons idered "securities account s" (within the 
meaning of Section 8-501(a) of the UCC), the Project Accounts shall be deemed to be "deposit accounts" (as defined 
in Section 9 -102(a)(29) of th e UCC), which the Collateral Agent shall ma intain with the Secur ities I ntermediary 
acting not as a securities intermediary but as a " bank" (within the meaning of Section 9-102(a)(8) of the UCC).  The 
Securities Intermediary has agreed  to compl y with any and all instructions originated by the Collateral  Agent 
directing disposition of funds in the Project Accounts without any further consent of the Borrower. 
Collateral and Remedies 
Administration of Collateral 
The Account Collateral will be held by the Collateral Agent for the benefit of the Secured P arties pursuant 
to the te rms of the Collateral Agen cy Agreement and will be administered by the Collateral Agent in the manner 
contemplated thereby and by the other Security Documents. 
Notice of Event of Default 
Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contain ed in the Collate ral Agency Agreement or any do cument 
executed in connection with any of the Secured Obligations, the Collateral Ag ent, unless a Responsible Officer of 
the Collateral Agent shall h ave actual knowledge thereof, shall not be deemed to have an y knowledge of any Ev ent 
of Default unless and until a Responsible Officer shall have received written notice from the Borrower, the Bond 
Trustee or any other Secured Party describing such Event of Default in reasonable detail. If the Collateral Agent 
receives any such notice from a Person other than the Bond Trustee, the Collateral Agent shall deliver a copy thereof 
to the Bond Trustee. Upon the occur rence and during the continuance of an E vent of Default, any of the Secured 
Parties may deliver to the Collateral Agent a wr itten notice, with a copy to the Agents and the Bond Issuer (if 
required), that an Event of Default has occurred and is continuing. 
Enforcement of Remedies 
At any time during which an Event of Default has occurred and is continuing com mencing on the date o f 
delivery to the Collateral Agent of the notice described in "—Notice  of Event of Default" above (except with respect 
to an Insolvency Event of the Borrower, in which case no no tice shall be required), the Majority Bondholders shall  
have the right to giv e the C ollateral Agent directions to take on behalf o f the Secured Parties any Enforcement 
Action in respect of such Event of Default. 
Upon the occurrence and during the continuance of an Event of Default, if so instructed by Majority  
Bondholders (except with respect to an Insolvency E vent of the Bo rrower, in which c ase such acceleration shall be 
automatic), the Collateral Agent shall take one or more of the following actions: (i) declare all Secured Obligations, 
all interest accrued and unpaid thereon, an d all other amounts payable in respect of the Secured Obligations to be 
due and payable, whereupon the same shall become immediately due and payable without presentment, demand, 
protest or further notice of any kind, all of which are w aived by the Bond Iss uer, provided that the underlying loans 
under the Loan Agreement may be accelerated pursuant to this paragraph only to the extent the Bonds shall have 
been accelerated or are subject to mandatory redemption; and/or (ii) foreclose on any or all of the Secu rity or  
E-31 
 
proceed to enforce all remedies available to the Bond Trustee or the Collateral Agent pursuant to the F inance 
Documents. The Bond Trustee may or shall at the direction of Majority Bondholders rescind an acceleration of the 
Bonds and its consequence s if (A) all payment defaults with respect thereto (except nonpayment of principal or 
interest that has become due solely because of the acceleration) have been cured or waived and (B) all reasonable 
fees and expenses of the Bond Trustee and the Collateral Agent with respect thereto have been paid. 
Remedies of the Secured Parties 
Unless otherwise consented to in wr iting by the Bond Trustee (acting at the direction of the Majority 
Bondholders), no Secured Party, individually or together with any other Secured Party, will have the right, nor will 
it, exercise or enforce any of the rights, powers or remedies which the Collateral Agent is authorized to exercise or 
enforce under the Collateral Agency Agreement or any Security Document. 
Application of Proceeds 
(a) Subject to paragraphs (c)  and (d), below, after an acceleration of the Bonds pursuant to t he Bond 
Indenture and the Loan Agreement, and following the taking of an Enforcement Action, moneys received by the 
Bond Trustee under the Bond I ndenture or the Collateral Agent under the Collateral Agency Agreement and the 
other Security Documents pursuant to an exercise of remedies will be applied as follows:  
First, to any payments then due and payable by the Borrower t
 o the Rebate Fund established under 
the Bond Indenture;  
Second, ratably, to the payment of Bond Trustee and Collateral Agent fees and  expenses;  
Third, to the payment of any other Transaction Expenses;  
Fourth, to the payment of (i) all Project Costs due and payable as set out in the Financial Model 
not already paid under Second and Third above, excluding (A) amounts owed to the Secured Partie s, (B) 
amounts t o fund the Reserve Accounts, (C) costs under the Asset Management Contract set out in (ii) 
below, and (D) Borrower's Finance Services Fees and Borrower's Management Services Fees; and (ii) costs 
under the Asset Management Contract, less  the amounts p ermitted or agreed to be set off, held back, 
deducted or claimed back by the Borrower, whether because of a service or performance failure on the part 
of the Asset Management Contractor or otherwise, in each case, if an d to the extent the Bon d Trustee  
determines that the payment of such expenses is in the best interest of the Secured Parties;  
Fifth, ratably, to the payment of all accrued and unpaid interest due on the Senior Debt; 
Sixth, ratably, to the payment of the outstanding principal ob ligations due in respect of the Senior 
Debt and any Redemption Price (if applicable); 
Seventh, ratably, to the payment of all other amounts, if any, due and payable under the Finance 
Documents with respect to the Senior Debt; and 
Eighth, to the Bo rrower, upon termination, expiration or payment in full of all commitments and 
outstanding amounts under the Senior Debt, any surplus to be applied at the Borrower' s discretion. 
(b) All proceeds received by the Collateral Ag en t or the other Finance Parties in connection with the  
enforcement of the Security shall be deposited into the C onstruction Account or other account established by the 
Collateral Agent for the purpose of distribution by the Collateral Agent. 
(c) Notwithstanding the foregoing, in no event shall m oneys in the ( i) Taxable Bonds Proceeds Sub-
Account of the Construction A ccount or other proceeds of the Series 2022B Bonds be used for any purpose other 
than the payment of the Series 2022B Bonds; and (ii) Tax-Exempt Bonds Proceeds Sub-Account of the Construction 
Account or other proceeds of the Series 2022A  Bonds or the Tax -Exempt Additional Parity Bonds be used for any  
E-32 
 
purpose other than the payment of rebate or payment of the Series 2022A  Bonds or the Tax -Exempt Additional 
Parity Bonds. 
(c) If at any time any Secured  Party will for any reason obtain any payment  or distribution upon or 
with respect to the Secured Obligations contrary to the terms of the Collateral Agency A greement, whether as a 
result of the Collateral Agent' s exercise of any Enforcement Action in resp ect of the Collateral or otherwise, such 
Secured Party agrees that it will have received such  amounts in trust, and will promptly remit such amount so 
received in error to the Collateral Agent to be applied in accordance with the terms of the  Collateral Ag ency 
Agreement. 
Events of Default and Termination 
Events of Default  
Any one or more of the f ollowing events will constitute an " Event of Default" under the Collateral Agency 
Agreement: 
(a) Payment Default.  If the Borrower shall fail or refus e to make or d efault in the payment of (i) any 
principal or interest, as applicable owing to the Secured Parties or (ii) any other amount due and, in each case under 
clauses (i) and (ii), such non- payment remains unremedied for a period of three (3) Business Days, provi ded that it 
shall not constitute an Event of Default under clauses (i) or (ii) if such failu re results from a technical error or 
omission in connection with the administration of the accounts from which such payments are to be made and 
continues for a peri od not greater than three (3) Busine ss Days after notice of such non -payment is received by the 
Borrower from the applicable Agent; 
(b) Covenant Default. I f any party other than the Finance Parties shall fail, refuse or default in the 
performance or observance o f any of the covenants on its part contained in any Finance Document other than 
covenants with respect to the payment of any amount owing in respect of any Senior Debt or otherwise specifically 
dealt with in this section "—Events of Def ault Under the Collateral Agency Agreement" and such failure, refusal or 
default (if capable of being rem edied) continues for a period of forty -five (45) days after the earlier of (i) notice to 
the Borrower of such failure, refusal or default from an Agent  or (ii) deliv ery of a notice of  default of such failure, 
refusal or default from the Borrower; provide d that, if any such failure cannot reasonably be cured within for ty-five 
(45) days and the Borrower is acting diligently to cure such failure, the Borrower may have such longer period as the 
Collateral Agent may agree (acting on the instructions of the Majority Bondholders); 
(c) Default in Respect of Representations and Warranties. If any representation or warranty made or 
deemed to be made by the Borrower in any Financ e Document shall prove to have been materially incorrect or 
misleading when made or deemed made unless the event or condition giving rise to such misre presentation is 
capable of being cured and is cured within thirty (30) days after the earlier of (i) not ice to the Borrower of such 
misrepresentation from the Collateral Agent or (ii) delive ry of a notice of default of such misrepresentation from the 
Borrower as required in section (g)(iii) of "—Covenants of the Borrower"; 
(d) Replacement of Acceptable Cred it S upport. If the Borrower fails to cause the issuance of 
replacement Acceptable Credit Support for (i) any expiring Acceptable Credit Support that continues to be required 
under the terms of the Finance  Documents, five (5) Business Days prior  to the date  of such expiration; or (ii) any 
provider of Acceptable Credit Support ceases to meet the criteria of a Permitted Letter of Credit Provider and is no t 
replaced within twenty (20) Business Days as provided  in the definition of " Acceptable Credit Support," unless, in 
each case, prior to such time the Borrower has drawn down in full such Acceptable Credit Support (or has requested 
the beneficiary thereof to do so) or the Borrower has provided cash collateral in lieu thereof that is satisfactory to the 
Collateral Agent; 
(e) Expropriation. I
 f all or any material part of the Security or the as sets of the Borrower are seized, 
expropriated or otherwise appropriated by, or custody thereof is assumed by any Governmental Authority or the 
Borrower sells or d ivests itself of all or a material part of its property because it is required to do so by a bi nding 
order from a Governmental Authority or the Borrower is otherwise prevented f rom exercising normal control over  
E-33 
 
all or a material part of its assets or lose s any of the  rights or privileges necessary to maintain its existence or to 
carry on its business, unless such expropriation constitutes a Supervening Event  under the Project Agreement or the 
Borrower has demonstrated to the satisfaction of the Collateral Agent (actin g on the  instructions of the Majority 
Bondholders) within ten (10) Business Days of such seizure or expropriation will not have a Material Adverse 
Effect;  
(f) Abandonment or termination of Project .  If the Project, or any material part thereof,  is abandoned 
by the Borrower; 
(g) Execution or judgment. I f distress or execution is levied or  a judgment, order or security interest is 
enforced or beco mes e nforceable, against the Borrower or the assets of the Borrower for an amount exceeding 
$2,500,000 unless the action o r the judgment or other claim giving rise to such enforcement action is s tayed, 
withdrawn or satisfied within thirty (30)  days of th e date the right to enforce arises and, during such period, the 
Borrower is diligently contesting the action or matter and the matter or enforcement action does not adversely affect 
the Project and its operation;  
(h) Termination of Tra nsa ction Documents. If (i) any Finance Document is terminated for any reason 
without the consent of the Collateral Agent (ac ting on the in structions of the Majority Bondholders) and such 
termination would reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse E ffect or (ii) any P roject Document (other 
than the Project Agreement, the D&C Contract, the D&C Guarantee, the Asset Managem ent Contract , the 
Borrower's Management Services Agreement and the Borrower' s Finance Services Agreement) is terminated for any 
reason without the consent of the Collateral Agent (acting on the instructions of the Majority Bondholders) and such 
termination would reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect or (iii) the D&C Contract,  the D&C 
Guarantee or th e Asset Management Contract is term inated without the consent of the Collateral Agent (acting on 
the instructions of the Majority Bondholders) , unless (A) it is replaced with a contractor or guarantor, as applicable, 
meeting the req uirements of an Acceptab le Substitute, (B) the Borrower has certified that such termination and 
replacement is not reasonably likely to have a Material Adverse Effect , (C) the Co llateral Agent has received 
confirmation from the Rating Agency rating the Bonds that such te rmination and replacement will not cause an  
Adverse Rating Effect and (D) the other conditions in section (kk)(i) of "—Covenants of the Borrower" are satisfied; 
(i) Insolvency. 
 The occurrence of an Insolvency Event in respect of: 
(i) the Borrower or the Holdco;  
(ii) the D&C Contractor or the D&C  Contractor Guarantor, until the expiry of the Defects 
Warranty Period, provided that no Event of Default shall occur her eunder if Borrower replaces the D&C 
Contractor or D&C Contractor Gu arantor in accordance with section (kk)(i) of "—Covenants of the 
Borrower"  within ninety (90) days of such Insolvency Event occurring; or 
(iii) the Asset Management Contractor, provided t hat the occurrence of an Insolvency Event 
in respect of the  Asset Management Contractor shall not constitute an Event of Default if the  Borrower 
replaces the Asset Management Contractor in accordance with in section (kk)(i) of "—Covenants of the 
Borrower" within ninety (90) days of such Insolvency Event occurring so that there is no material 
interruption in the delivery of Asset Management Services 
(j) Security Interest Ceases to Be Perfected. If any Security ceases to be a valid and perfected fir st  
priority Security Interest, subject only to Permitted Security Interests and suc h event continues for a period of ten 
(10) days;  
(k) Funding Shortfall. I f at any time prior to the AP Commencement Date there shall exist a Funding 
Shortfall and such Funding Shortfall has not been remedied within fifteen (15) days after written notice thereof from 
the Collateral Agent is received by the Borrower;  
E-34 
 
(l) Liens on B orrower Assets. If the Borrower creates or permits to exist any Security Interest over 
any of its  property, oth er tha n a Perm itted Security Interest, and such Security Interest is not discharged to the 
satisfaction of the Collateral Agent within ten (10) days;  
(m) Invalidity of Project Documents. I f any material provision of a Project Document (i) do es not have 
effect or ceases to have effect in accordance with its terms; (ii) is or becomes void, voidable, illegal or unenforceable 
or (iii) the Borrower claims any of the matters referred to in (i) and (ii) to be the case and, in each case, such Project  
Document is not replaced on terms s atisfactory to the Collateral Agent within thir ty (30) days of earlier of notice or 
knowledge of such invalidity, unless (A) such Project Document is capable of replacement, but not within thirty (30) 
days, in which case, an addi tional six ty (60) days is permitted to replace such Project Document so lo ng as the 
Borrower has initiated efforts to replace such Project Document within the original thirty (30) day period and is 
diligently proceeding to replace such Project Document and  (B) the invalidity of suc h Project Document does not 
trigger a Borrower Default under the Project Agreement; 
(n) Obligations Unenforceable. I f the obligations of the Borrower under any Finance Document shall 
cease to constitute the legal,  valid a nd binding obl igations of the Borrower or shall cease to be in full force and 
effect or becomes void or voidable or the Borrower shall have contested the validity of any Finance Document or 
denied that it has any liability thereun der or the Security ceases  to create a valid and enforceable Security Interest in 
any material asset forming part of the Security, subject to Permitted Security Interests; 
(o) Failure to Maintain Debt Service Coverage Ratios . S ubject to the Right to Cure, if, on any Bonds 
Payment Date following the Project Final Completion Date, the Senior DSCR or Projected Senior DSCR is less than 
1.00 to 1.00; 
(p) Termination of Project Agreement. The Project Agreement is rescinded or the District terminates 
or becomes en titled to terminate the Pr oject Agr eement by r eason of a Borrower Default under the Project 
Agreement, taking into account any grace period or cure period permitted under the Project Agreement, including 
Article 61 of the Project Agreement; 
(q) Proje ct Final Completion Default. Project Final Completion does not occur on or prior to the Long 
Stop Deadline; 
(r) Enforceability. Any Performance Support (other than Acceptable Credit Support) is, or becomes, 
wholly or partially void, voidable, u nenforceable or illegal and not rep laced wit hin t hirty (30) days  or any 
Acceptable Credit Support is, or becomes , wholly or partially void, voidable, unenforceable or illegal and not 
replaced within twenty (20) days, in each case with a substantially similar Performance Support or Acceptabl e 
Credit Support or otherwise  on terms acceptable to the Collateral Agent (acting on the instructions of the Majority 
Bondholders); 
(s) Failure to maintain Equity Support. Any failure to maintain any of the Equity Support us ed to 
secure the Equity Commitment and such failure remains unremedied for a period of ten (10) days; 
(t) Cross D efault. The occurrence of any " Event of Default " (howsoever described) under any 
agreements or instruments evidencing Permitted Indebtedness specified in clauses (c) or (e) of  the definition thereof, 
so long as such Permitted Indebtedness is payable on  a parity basis with the Senior Debt, or any Person becomes 
entitled to accelerate such Permitted Indebtedness; 
(u) Equity Commitment. Subject t o "PR OJECT ACCOUNTS AND FLOW OF FUNDS —Proje ct  
Accounts-Description of Project Accounts —Construction Account -Equity Contribution Sub -Account," the Holdco 
shall fail to make in full any equity contributions when required in accordance with the terms of the Equity 
Contribution Agreement; and 
(v) Insuffi cient Insurance. If any part of the Project is damaged and the Borrower does not, and will 
not, have or have available to it funds, including, without limitation, the proceeds of I nsurance, sufficient to make 
arrangements for the repair of the Project in a ma nner that ensur es there is no Material Adverse Effect; provided

that, the Borrower shall have a period of fifteen (15) Business Days from the date of occurrence of the damage for

Termination  
Upon termination of the Collateral Agency Agreement, including pursuant  to "—T ermination of Projec t 
Accounts" above, all rights to the Collateral as will not have been sold or otherwise applied, in each case, pursuant 
to the terms of the Collateral Agency A greement and the Security Documents will revert to the Borrower, its 
successors or as signs, or o therwise as a court of competent jurisdiction m ay direct. Upon any such termination, the 
Collateral Agent will, at the Borrower's direction and expense, execute and deliver to the Borrower such documents 
as the Borrower shall  request to evidence such termination. 
Miscellaneous Provisions 
Amendments; Waivers 
(a) Any term, covenant, agreement or condition of the Collateral Agency Agreement or any of the 
Security Documents may be amended or waived only by an instrument in writing signed by each o f the Collateral 
Agent (acting on the direction of the Bond Trustee), the Borrower and the Bond Trustee (acting in accordance with 
the terms of the Bond Indenture); provided that: 
(i) only the Bond Trustee ma y waive any rights of the Bond Trustee und er any provision of 
the Collateral Agency Agreement; no consent to any depart ure by the Borrower from the Collateral Agency 
Agreement (or the Security Documents)  will be effective unless in writing signed by the a pplicable parties 
specified in the Collateral Agency Agreement, and each such waiver or consent will be effective only in the 
specific instance and for the specific purpose for which given; and 
(ii) the consen t of the Securities Intermediary will be required for any amendment to the 
"Securities Intermediary" section of the Collateral Agency Agreement or any other amendment that would 
modify the rights or obligations of the Securities Intermediary. 
(b) The waiver (whether express or implied) by the Collate ral Ag ent of any breach of the terms or 
conditions of the Collateral Agency Agreement, and the cons ent (whether express or implied)  of any Secured Party 
will not prejudice any remedy of the Collateral Agent or any Secured Party in respect of any continuing  or ot her 
breach of the terms and conditions of  the Collateral Agency Agreement, and will not be co nstrued as a  bar to any 
right or remedy which the Collateral Agent or any other Secured Party would otherwise have on any future occasion 
under the Collateral Agency Agreement. 
(c) No failure to exercis e nor any delay in exer cising, on the part of the Collateral Agent  or any other 
Secured Party, of any right, power or privilege under the Collateral Agency Agreement  will operate as a waiver 
thereof; further, no single or partial exercise of any right, pow er or privilege under the Collateral Agency Agreement 
will preclude any other or further exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power or privilege avai lable to 
it. All remedies under the Collateral Agency Agreement and under the other Security Documents are cumulative and 
are not exclusive of any other remedies that may be available to the Collateral Agent, whether at law, in equity or 
otherwise. 
Governing Law 
The Collateral Agency Agreement will be governed by and construed in accordance with  the substantive 
laws of the District of Columbia without giving effect to the application of conflict of law principles.  
E-36 
 
Collateral Agent's Rights 
(a) If at any time the Collateral Agent is served with any judicial or administrative order, judgment, 
decree, writ or other form of judicial or administrative process which in any way affects the Collateral (including but 
not limited to orders of attachment or garnishment or other forms of levies or injunctions or stays re lating to the 
transfer of such property ), the C ollateral Agent is authorized to comply therew ith in any manner it o r legal counsel 
of its own choosing reasonably deems appropriate ; and if  the Collateral Agent complies with any such judicial or 
administrative order, judgment, decree, writ or oth er form of judicial or admini strative process, the Col lateral Agent 
will not be liable to any of the parties hereto or to any other Person o r entity even though such order, judgment, 
decree, writ or process may be subsequently modified or vacated or otherwise determined to have been without legal 
force or effect. 
(b) In the event of any dispute between or conflicting claims by or among the Bo rrower, the Secured 
Parties and/or any other Person or entity with respect to any property being held by the Collater al Agent  in 
connection with the Collateral Agency Agreement or the Security Documents, the Collateral Agent will be entitled,  
in its sole discretion, to refuse to comply with any and all claims, demands or instructions with respect to such 
property so long  as such dispute or con flict will continue, and the Collateral Agent will not be or become liable in 
any way to the Borrower, the Secured Parties or any other party for failure or refusal to comply with such conflicti ng 
claims, demands or instructions. The  Collateral Agent will be entitled to refuse to act until, in its sole discretion, 
either (i)  such conflicting or ad verse claims or demands will have been determined by a final order, judgment or 
decree of a court of competent jurisdiction, which order, ju dgment or decree is n ot subject to appeal, or settled by 
agreement between the conflicting parties as evidenced in a writing reasonably satisfactory to the Collateral Agent 
or (ii) the Collateral Agent will have received security or an indemnity reasonably  satisfactory to it sufficient to hold 
it harmless from and against any and all losses which it may incur by reason of so ac ting. Any court order, judgment 
or decree will be accompanied by a legal opinion by counsel f or the presenting party, reasonably sat isfactory to the 
Collateral Agent, to the effect that said order, ju dgment or decree represents a final adjudication  of the rights of the 
parties by a court of competent jurisdiction, and that the time for appeal from  such order, judgment or decree has 
expired without an appea l having been perfected. The Collateral Agent will act on such court order and legal 
opinions without further question. 
(c) To help the government fight the funding of terrorism and money launderi ng activities, federal law 
requires all financial institutions to obtain, verify, and record information th at identifies each Person who opens an 
account. When any account or sub -account is opened, the Collateral Agent will be entitled to such information that 
will allow it to identify the individual o r entity who is establishing the relationship or opening the account and may 
also ask for formation documents such as articles of incorporation or other identifying documents to be provided.

APPENDIX F 
 
SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE BOND INDENTURE 
The following is a summary of selected provisions of the Bond Indenture  and is not a full statement of the 
terms of such agreement. Accordingly, the following summ ary is qualified in its entirety by refe rence to  such 
agreement and is subject to the full tex t of such agreement.  A copy of such agreement is available, free of charge, 
upon request from the Borrower or the Bond Trustee. Unless otherwise stated, any reference in this Official 
Statement to any agreement  will mean such  agreement and all schedules, exhibits and attachments thereto, as 
amended, supplemented or otherwise modified and in effect as of the date hereof.  
The parties have entered into the Bond Indenture  to provide for the issuance of bonds  for the purpose of 
providing the Loan pursuant to the L oan Agreement to the Borrower, which will be used by the Borrower to finance 
a portion of the cost of the Project. 
Grant of Trust Estate 
The Bond Issuer, in consideration for the purchase of the Bonds  by the Bondholders and other good and 
valuable consider ation, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, in order to secure the 
payment of the Bonds, to secure the performance and observance of all the covenants and conditions set forth  in the 
Bonds and the Bond Indenture, the Bond Issuer ha s executed and delivered the Bond Indenture  and, except for its 
Reserved Rights of the Bond Is suer, has pledged, transferred and assigned or has required t o be pledged, transferred 
and assigned, and by these presents does pledge, transfer and assign unto the Bond Trustee and to its successors and 
assigns forever and, for the benefit of the Bondholders, the Trust Estate. 
Time of Pledge; Delivery of Trust Estate 
The creation, perfection, enforcement, and priority of the pl edge of the Trust Estate by the Bond Issuer to 
secure or pay  the Bonds as provided herein shall be governed b y the Home Rule Act, as amended, the Bond 
Resolution and the Bond Indenture . The Trust Estate  pledged for the payment of the Bonds, as received by or 
otherwise credited to the Bond Issuer, shall imme diately be subject to the lien of such pledge without an y physical 
delivery, filing, or further act. The lien of such pledge shall be valid, binding, and enforceable as against all Persons 
having claims of any kind in tort, contract, or otherwise against the Bond Issuer irrespective of whether such Persons 
have notice of such liens. 
Amounts Received Pursuant to the Collateral Agency Agreement 
All funds provided pursuant to the Collateral Agency Agreement for d eposit into any Fund or Account of 
the Bond Inde nture will be available together with other moneys then o n deposit in such Funds and Accounts to be 
used for the applicable purposes as set forth in the Bond Indenture and any Supplemental Bond Indenture. 
Discharge of Bond Indenture 
If the Bond Indenture is discharged in accordance with the terms of the Bond Ind enture, the right, title and 
interest of each Bondholder and the Bond Trustee in an d to the T rust Estate shall terminate and be discharged 
(except thos e rights, titles and interest that by their term s shall survive the discharge of the Bond Indenture ); 
otherwise the Bond Indenture is to be and remain in full force and effect. 
Bonds Secured on Equal and Proportionate Basis 
The Trust Estate shall be held b y the Bond Trustee for the equal and proportiona te benef it of the 
Bondholders and any of them, without preference, priority or distinction as to lien or otherwise.  
F-2 
 
Special Obligations 
The Bonds shall be special obligations of the Bond Issuer.  The Bonds ar e without recourse to the Bond 
Issuer.  The Bond s shall not be general obligations of the Bond Issuer, s hall not be a pledge of or involve the faith 
and credit or the taxing power of the B ond Issuer , shall not constitute a debt of the Bond Issuer, and shal l not 
constitute lending of the public credit fo r private undertakings as prohibited by section 6.02(a)( 2) of the Home Rule 
Act.  The Bonds are payable solely from the loan payments received by the Bond Trustee from the Borrower and are 
secured by a pledge of the Trust Estate, all of which shall, except as may be otherwise expressly authorized in the 
Bond In denture, be used for no other purpose than to pay the principal of, redemption premi um, if any, on, and 
interest on, the Bonds.  The Bonds shall not give rise to any pecuniary liability of the Bond Is suer and the Bond 
Issuer shall have no obligation with r espect to the purchase of the Bonds, upon issuance, sale and delivery of the 
Bonds. 
No Personal Liability 
No covenant, stipulation, obligation or agreem ent of the Bond Issuer in the Bond Indenture , the Bonds, the 
Loan Agreement or any other Bond Issuer Doc ument shall be deemed to be a covenant, stipulation, obligation or 
agreement of any present or future elected or appointed official, officer, employee o r agent of the Bond Issuer in his 
or her individ ual capacity, and neither the members of the Council nor  any official executing the Bonds shall be 
liable personally on the Bonds or be subj ect to any  personal liability or accountability by reason of the iss uance of 
the Bonds or by reason of the covenants , stipulations, obligations or agreements of the Bond Iss uer contained in the 
Bond Indenture, the Bonds or the Loan Agreement. 
Method and Place of Payment.  
The Bond Trustee shall act as paying agent for the purpose of effecting payment of the principal of , 
redemption premium, if any, and interest on the Bonds.  The principal or Redemption Price of, and interest on, the 
Bonds shall be payable in any coin  or currency of the United States of America which on the respective dates of 
payment thereof is legal tender for the payment of public and private debts. The principal or Redemption Price of all 
Bonds shall be payable by check or draft or by such other me thod as mutually agreed in writing between the 
Bondholder of the Bond and the Bond Trustee at maturity or upon ear lier redemption to the Bondholders in whose 
names such Bonds are registered on the bond register maintained by the Bond Tru stee at the maturity date or 
redemption date thereof, upon the presentation and surrender of such Bonds at the Designated Pay ment Office of the 
Bond Trustee. The interest payable on  each Bond on any Bonds Payment Date shall be paid ( a) by check or draft  
sent on or prior to the appropriate date of payment, by the Bond Trustee to the  address of the Bondholder appearing 
in the registration books on the Record Date or (b ) by such othe r method as mutually agreed in writing between the 
Bondholder of the Bond and the Bond Trustee. 
Application of Proceeds of Bonds. 
The net proceeds of the s ale of the Bonds shall be delivered to  the Bond Trustee on behalf of the Bond 
Issuer, and the Bond Trustee shall transfer the net proceeds to the Collateral Agent for deposit in the following funds 
and accounts established under the Collateral Agency Agreement (the Bond Trustee may establish a temporary fund 
or account in its records to facilitate and record such transfer of the net proceeds):  
(a) (i ) the relevant amount in connection with the net proceeds of the sale of the Series  2022A Bonds 
shall be de posited into the Tax -Exempt Bonds Proce eds Sub -Account of the Construction Account and ( ii) the 
relevant amount in connection with the net proceeds of the sale of the Series 2022B Bonds shall be deposited into 
the Taxable Bonds Proceeds Sub-Account of the Construction Account; and 
(b) (i ) the relevant amount in connection with the net proceeds of the sale of the Series 2022A Bonds 
shall be deposited into the Tax -Exempt Bonds Costs of Issuance Sub -Account of the Construction Account and ( ii) 
the relevant amo unt in connection with the net proceeds  of the sale of the Series 2022B Bonds shall be deposited 
into the Taxable Bonds Costs of Issuance Sub-Account of the Construction Account.

Registration of Bonds; Persons Treated as Bondholders; Transfer and Exchange of Bonds. 
Records for the registration  and transfer of the Bonds shall be kept by t he Bond Trustee which is hereby 
appointed the registrar for the Bonds.  Th e principal of and interest on and Redemption Price of any Bond shall be 
payable only to or upon th e order of the Bondholder or his legal  representative (except as otherwise herein provided 
with respect to Record Dates and Special Record Dates for the paym ent of interest).  Upon surrender for transfer of 
any Bond at the Designated Payment Office of the B ond Trustee, duly endorsed for transf er or accompanied by an 
assignment duly executed by the Bondholder or his attorney duly authorized in writing, the Bond  Trustee shall enter 
such transfer on the registration records and shall execute and deliver in the name of the transferee or transferees  a 
new f ully registered Bond or Bonds of a l ike maturity, aggregate principal amount and interest rate, bearing a 
number or numbers not previously assigned. 
Fully registered Bonds may be exchanged at the Designated Pay ment Office of the Bond Trustee for an  
equal aggregate principal amount of Bonds o f the same maturity and interest rate but of other Authorized 
Denominations.  The Bond Trustee  shall execute and deliver Bonds which the Bondholder making the exchange is 
entitled to receive, bearing numbers not previously assigned. 
All Bonds issued upon a ny transfer or exchange of Bonds shall be the valid obligations of the Bond  Issuer, 
evidencing the same debt, and entitled to the same benefits under the Bond Indenture , as t he Bonds surrendered 
upon transfer or exchange.  
The Bond Trustee may require the p ayment, by the Bondholder of any Bond requesting exchange or 
transfer, of any reasonable charges as well as any taxes, transfer fees or other governmental charges required t o be 
paid with respect to such exchange or transfer. 
The Bond Trustee shall not be required to transfer or exchange (i) all or any portion of any Bond during the 
period beginning at the opening of business 15 days before the day of the mailing by the Bond T rustee of notice 
calling any of the B onds for prior redemption and ending at the close of business on the day of such mailing or (ii) 
all or any portion of a Bond after the mailing of notice calling such Bond or any portion thereof for prior 
redemption. 
Except as otherwise herein provided with respect to Record Dates and Special Record  Dates for the 
payment of interest, the Person in whose name any Bond shall  be registered shall be deemed and regarded as the 
absolute owner thereof for all purposes, and pay ment of or on account of the principal  of and interest on or 
Redemption Price of any Bond shall be made only to or upon the written order of the Bondholder t hereof or his legal 
representative, but such registration may be changed as herein provided.  All s uch payments shall be valid and 
effectual to satisfy and discharge such Bond to the extent of the sum or sums paid. 
Payment of Debt Service and Redemption Price 
(a) The principal and Redemption Price of any Bond shall be paid to the Bondholder thereof as s hown on 
the registration records of th e Bond Trustee upon maturity or prior redemption thereof in accordance with the  Bond 
Indenture and upon presentation and surrender at the Designated Payment Office of the Bond Trustee.  
(b) Interest on the Bonds (other than interest paid as part of the Redemption P rice of a Bond) shall be paid 
(i) by check or draft of the Bond Trustee mailed, on or before each Bonds  Payment Date, to the Bondholder thereof 
at his address as it last appears on the r egistration records of the Bond Trustee at the close of business on th e Record 
Date or (ii) by such other method as mutually agreed in writing between the Bondholder of the Bond and the Bond 
Trustee.  Any such interest not so timely paid shall cease to be payable to the person wh o is the Bondholder thereof 
at the close of business on the Record Date and shall be payable to the person who is the Bondholder thereof at the 
close of business on a Special Record Date for the payment of such defaulted interest.  Such Special Record Date 
shall be fixed by the Bond Trustee whenever moneys become available for payment of the defaulted interest, and 
notice of the Special Record Date shall be given by the Bond Trustee to the Bondholders, not less than 10 days prior 
to the Special Record Date,  by certified or first-class mail to each such Bondholder as shown on the Bond Trustee’s 
registration records on a date selected by the Bond Trustee, stating the date of the Special Record Date and the date

fixed for the payment of such defaulted interest.  Alternative means of payment of inte rest may be used if mutually

 Unspent Tax -Exempt Bond Proceeds.   The Series 202 2A Bonds shall be subjec t to extraordinary 
mandatory redemption,  in whol e or in part (selected in the manner set forth in the Bond Indenture ) at the 
Redemption Price on the redemption date (which will be set by the Bond Trustee on a Business Day that is no earlier 
than the date t hat is the earlier of (A) the date that is ninet y (90) days after the five (5) year anniversary of the 
Closing Date, or (B) sixty (60) days after the Project Final Completion Date) at a redemption price equal to par pl us 
accrued interest thereon up to but no t including the redemption date, in th e principal amount of (rounded upward to 
a multiple of $5,000) such Series 202 2A Bonds being redeemed, and to the extent of any remaining unspent 
proceeds from the Series 2022A Bonds on such date, unless as of such d ate, at least 95% of the net proceeds of the 
Series 202 2A Bonds are expended for the Project; provided that no such redemption shall be required if the 
Borrower has obtained an Opinion of Bond Counsel stating that the failur e to redeem the Series 202 2A Bonds will 
not adversely affect the exclusion of in terest on such Series 2022A Bonds from gross income for federal income tax 
purposes  and furnished such opinion to the Bond Issuer and the Bond Trustee.  
Termination Under Proj ect Agreement.  The Bonds shall b e subject to  extraordinary mandatory 
redemption, i n whole (but not in part) at the Redemption Price on the redemption date upon termination of the 
Project Agreement by either the Borrower or the District (with such redempti on date to be set by the Bond Tru stee 
on a Business Day that is within three Busine ss Days after the Bond Trustee receives notice from the Borrower of 
the receipt by the Borrower of any Termination Compensation payable by the District pursuant to the Proje ct 
Agreement).  Upon the terminat ion of the Project Agreement, the Borrower shall p rovide notice of such termination 
to the Bond Trustee within three Business Days of such termination. 
Insurance Proceeds.  The Bonds shall be subject to extraordinary mandatory redemption, in whole or in 
part, at the Redemption Price on the redemption date (which will be set by the Bond Trustee on a Business Day that 
is no later than sixty (60) days after the receipt by the Borrower of net amounts of insurance or loss p roceeds 
(excluding delayed start -up, so ft costs, bu siness interruption insurance proceeds  and oth er anticipated losses of 
revenue) or condemnation proceeds (i) to the extent that the affected property cannot be restored or is not required to 
be restored pu rsuant to the terms of the Project Agre ement and the Finance Documents and the Borrower elects no t 
to do so and to the extent otherwise permitted under the Project Agreement) in the principal amount of (rounded 
downward to a multiple of $5,000) and (ii) to the extent of any such insurance or lo ss proceeds or condemnation 
proceeds transferred from the Loss Proceeds Account to the Mandatory Prepayment Account on such date, sufficient 
to effectuate such redemption.  If the Bonds are redeemed in part, the Bonds  will be redeemed in the manner 
described in the Bo nd Indenture  (provided that a portion of a Bon d may be redeemed only in Authorized 
Denominations). 
Determination of Taxability.   The Series 202 2A Bonds shall be subject to extraordinary mandatory 
redemption not later than 120 days after a Deter mination of Taxability.  Such redemption shall be in  whole, or in 
part to the extent that, an Opinion of Bond Counsel is delivered to the effect that interest on the Series 2022A Bonds 
which would remain Outstanding after such partial redemption will be ex cludable from gross income for federal 
income tax purp oses.  T he Series 2022A Bonds will be redeemed at the Redemption Price on the redemption date 
and such Redemption Price shall be paid from prepayments made by the Borrower pursuant to the Loan Agreement . 
Other Extraordin ary Mandatory Redemptions.   The Bonds shall be  subject to extraordinary mandatory 
redemption prior to maturity thereof, in whole or in part, at the Redemption Price on the redemption date (which will 
be set by the Bond Trustee on a Busine ss Day that is no later than sixty (60) days after the r eceipt by the Borrower 
of (A) any proceeds from the total or material partial condemn ation of the Project subject to the provisions of the 
Finance Documents and the Project Documents, (B) any proceeds  in respect of the  expropriation or compulsory

principal amount of (rounded upward to a multiple of $5,000) and to the extent of any such proceeds on deposit in 
the Mandatory Prepayment Account on such date, sufficient to effec tuate su ch redemption.  If the Bonds are

Establishment of Funds and Accounts 
There is hereby created and established the following Funds and Accounts: 
(a) The "Debt Service Fund " (the " Debt Service F und"), and within the  Debt Service F und, three 
accounts designated as follows:  (i) the "Interest Account " (the "Interest Account "), including segregated sub-
accounts therein for the Series 2022A  Bonds and the Series 2022B Bonds, (ii) the "Principal Accoun t" (the 
"Principal Account"), including segr egated sub-accounts for the Series 2022A  Bonds and the Series 2022B Bonds; 
and (iii) the "Redemption Account" (the "Redemption Account"), including segregated sub-accounts therein for the 
Series 2022A Bonds and the Series 2022B Bonds; and 
(b) The DC Smart Street Lighting Project Rebate Fund (the "Rebate Fund"). 
Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, the Bo nd T rustee may from time to time hereafte r 
establish and maintain additional funds, acco unts or subaccount s necessary or useful in connection with any other 
provision of the Bond Indenture or any Supplemental Bond Indenture or to the extent deemed necessary by the Bond 
Trustee.  
Debt Service Fund  
(a) There shall be deposited into the appropriate  Account of the Debt Service Fund: (i) amounts 
remitted or transferred to  such Account from the Revenue Account pursuant to the Collateral Agency Agreement; 
(ii) any moneys paid to the Bond Trustee pursuant  to the Bond Indenture and the Collateral Agency Agreement with 
respect to the Redemption Price of the Bonds; (iii) any amo unts remitted or moneys transferred to such Account 
from the Senior Debt DSRA pursuant to the C ollateral Agency Agreement; (iv) any amou nts remitted or moneys 
transferred to such Acco unt from the Const ruction Account pursuant to the Bond Indenture  and the Collateral 
Agency Agreement; (v) any moneys deposited into such Account pursuant to the Bond Indent ure and the Collateral 
Agency Agreement; and (vi) all other moneys received by the B ond Trustee that  a re accompanied by written 
directions that such moneys are to be deposited into such Account. 
(b) If on any Bonds Payment Date the funds on deposit in t he Interest Account are not sufficient  to 
pay the Interest Payment in full on such B onds Payment Date, the Bo nd Trustee shall transfer moneys from the 
Principal Account to the Interest Account sufficient to make such payment.  If on any Bonds Payment Date there 
exists both (i) funds on depos it in the  Interest Account in excess of the amount necessary to pay th e Interest 
Payment due on such date, and (ii ) insufficient funds on deposit in the  Principal Account to make the principal 
payment due on such date in full, the Bond Trustee shall tra nsfer such excess funds on deposit in the  Interest 
Account to the  Principal Account as necessary to provide for such principal payment in full.  All such transfers shall 
be made from the Interest Account and t he Principal Account, as applicable, p rior to transferring any amounts from 
the Senior Debt DSRA to make debt service payments. 
(c) Moneys in each Account of the Deb t Service Fund shall be used solely for the payment (within 
each Account) of the principal and Red emption Price of and interest on th e Bonds; provided, that (A) moneys paid 
pursuant to the Bond Indenture  and the Collateral Agency Agreement shall be used to  pay the Redemption Price of 
the Bonds and (B) moneys held in such Account of the  Debt Service  Fund following an acceleration of the Bonds 
upon an Event of Default shall be used as provided in the Bond Indenture and in the Collateral Agency Agreement. 
Rebate Fund 
The Rebate Fund shall be for the sole benefit of the United States of America and shal l not be subject to the 
claim of any o ther Person, including without limitation, the Bondholders.  Th e Rebate Fund is  established for the 
purpose of complying wi th Section 148 of the Code and the Treasury Regulations promulgated pursuant thereto. 
There sha ll be deposited i nto the Rebate Fund a ll amou nts transferred to such Fund pursuant to the Collateral 
Agency Agreement.  The money deposited in the Rebate Fund, t ogether with all investments thereof and investment 
income therefrom, shall be held in trust an d applied solely as provided in the Ta x Certificate.  The Rebate Fund is 
not a portion of the Trust Estate and is not s ubject to any lien under the Bond Indentur e.  Notwithstanding the  
F-7 
 
foregoing, the Bond Trustee with respect to the Rebate Fund is afforded  all the rights, protections and immun ities 
otherwise accorded to it hereunder.  
Moneys to be Held in Trust 
The Debt Service Fund and any other Fund or Account c reated hereunder (excluding the Rebate Fund and 
any Defeasance Escrow Account), shall be held b y the Bond Trustee, for the benefit of the Bondholders as specified 
in the Bond Indenture.  The Rebat e Fund shall be held by the Bond Trustee for the purpose of making payments to 
the United States pursuant to the Bond Indenture .  Any Defeasance Escrow Account shall be hel d solely for the 
benefit of t he Bondholders to be paid therefrom as provided in the agreement governin g such Defeasance Escrow 
Account. 
Investment of Moneys 
(a) All moneys held as part of any Fund or Account shall be deposited or invested and reinvested by 
the Bond Trustee, at the  written direction of the Borrower, in Qualified Investments; p rovided, however , that 
moneys in any Defeasance Escrow Account may only be invested in Defeasance Securities. 
(b) Earnings from the investment of moneys held in any Fund or Acc ount and losses from the 
investment of moneys held in any Fund or Account shall be charged against the Fund or Account in which they were 
realized. 
(c) The Bond Trustee shall sell and reduce to cash a sufficient amount of the investments held in any 
Fund or Ac count whenever the cash bala nce therein is insufficient to make any payment to be m ade therefrom and  
the Bond Trustee shall not be liable or responsible for any loss or tax resulting from such sale.

The Bond Issuer has made represent ations and warrant ies under the Bond Indenture, including , but not 
limited to the following: 
(a) It is a body corporate a nd politic existing under the Constitution and laws of the United States of 
America. 
(b) The Home Rule Act is in full force and effect as of the Financial Closing Date. 
(c) It is duly authorized by the Constitution of th e United States of America and the applicable Laws 
of the District of Columbia, including the Home Rule Act, and has all necessary power and authorit y to is sue the 
Bonds, to execute and d eliver the Bond Indenture, the Loan Agreement and the  Tax Certificate  ( collectively, the 
"Bond Issuer Documents"), and to assign its interest in the Trust Estate to the Bond Trustee, in the manner and to the 
extent set forth in the Bond Indenture. 
(d) When executed and delivered by the Bond Issuer and the other parties thereto,  each of the Bond 
Issuer Documents will constitute the  legal, valid and binding obligations of the Bond Issuer enforceable against the 
Bond Issuer in accordance with its terms (except as enforcement thereof may be limited by bankruptcy, ins olvency, 
reorganization, moratorium, fraudulent transfer, liquidation an d other similar matters now or hereafter enacted 
relating to creditors' rights generally or by principles of equity which permit the exercise of judicial discretion). 
(e) The execution and  delivery of and  p erformance under the Bond Issuer Documents by the Bo nd 
Issuer will not conflict with or constitute a breach of or default under the Home Rule Act  o r any a pplicable 
judgment, order or decree or any agreement, mortgage, indenture, lease, resolution or other instrument relating to the 
Bond Issuer to which the Bo nd Issuer is a party or by which the Bond Issuer or any of its revenues or assets is 
otherwise bound or subject, and nothing has oc curred or is continuing which, with the passage of time or the giving 
of notice, or both, would constitute a default or an event of default thereunder.

Bond Issuer Documents.  The Bond Resolution has not  been repealed, revoked or rescinded.
