Events of Default 
Any of the events described "THE SERIES 2022 BONDS —Bond Indenture—Even ts of Default under the 
Bond Indenture" will be an "Event of Default" under the Bond Indenture. 
Remedies Following and During the Continuance of an Event of Default 
(a) Upon the occurrence and during the c ontinuance of an Event of Defa ult, any Bondholder or the 
Bond Issuer may deliver to the Bond Trustee a written notice, with a copy to the Bond Issuer, th e Collateral Agent 
and the Borrower, that an Event of Default has occurred and is continuing.  The Bond Trustee shall not be deemed to 
have any knowledge of the occurrence of an Event of Default, except with respect to an "event of default" described 
in the Bond Indenture, unless and until a Responsible Officer has received such a notice from the relevant party. 
(b) At any time during which an Event of Default has occurred and is continuing commencing on the 
date of delivery to the Bond Trustee of the notice desc ribed in subsection (a) above (except with respect to an Event 
of Default described i n clause (a) or (b) of the "Events of Defau lt" section above in wh ich no notice shall be 
required), the Bondholders of a majority of the aggregate principal amount of Outst anding Bonds  shall have the 
right to give the Bond Trustee one or more enforcement d irections directing the Bond Truste e to tak e on behalf of 
the Bondholders, subject to the terms of the Collateral Agency Agreement and  clause (c) below, whatever action at 
law or in equity may appear necessary or desirable to enforce the rights of the Bondholders. 
(c) Upon the occurrence an d during the continuance of an Event of Default, if so instructed by the 
Bondholders of a majority of the aggregate principal amount of the t hen Outs tanding Bonds (or 100% of the 
Bondholders in the case of an Event of Default  described in clause (c) of the "Events of Default" section above), the 
Bond Trustee, subject to the i mmediately succeeding provisos, shall (i)  declare all Bonds, all inter est accrued and 
unpaid thereon, and all other am ounts payable in respect of the Bond s to be due and pa yable, whereupon the same 
shall become immediately due and payable without presentment, demand, protest or further notice of any kind, all of 
which are waived by the Bond Issuer; provided that the Bonds may be accelerated pursuant to this  clause (c) only to 
the extent the underlying  Loan under  the Loan Agreement (or any other loan pursuant to any Supplemental Loan 
Agreement) shall have been accelerated and/or (ii) instruct the Collateral Agent to fore close on any or all of the 
Collateral or proceed to en force all remedies available to the Bond Trustee or the  Collateral Agent pursuant to the 
Finance Documents.  
(d) The Majority Bondholders may, by written notice  to the Bond Trustee, on behalf of all of the 
Bondholders, rescind any acceleration and its c onsequences, if the rescission would not conflic t with any jud gment 
or decree and if all exis ting Events of Default (except nonpayment of principal, interest or pr emium that has become 
due solely because of th e acceleration) have been cured or wai ved and t he Bond  Issuer has paid or deposited, or 
caused to be paid or deposited, with the Bond Truste e a sum sufficient to pay all sums paid or advanced by the Bond 
Trustee hereunder and the reasonable compensation, exp enses, disbursements and advances of the Bond Trustee and 
the Collateral Agent, as applicable , their respective agents and counsel.  In case of any such rescission, then and in  
F-9 
 
every such case the Bond Issuer, the Bond Trustee, the Collateral Agent and t he Bondholders shall be restored to 
their former positions and rights. 
(e) If any Event of Default has occurred and is continuing, the Bond Trus tee in its discretion may, and 
upon the written request of Bondholders  of a majority in principal amount of all Bond s Outstanding and receipt of 
indemnity to its satisfaction shall, in its own name  (i) by mandamus, or other s uit, action or proceeding at la w or in 
equity, enforce all rights of the Bondholders, including the right to require the Bond Issuer to enforce any  r ights 
under the Loan Agreement and t o require the Bond Issuer to carry out any other provisio ns of the Bond  Indenture 
for the benefit of the Bondholders and to perform its duties under the Home Rule Act; (ii) bring suit upon the Bonds; 
(iii) by action or  suit in equity require the Bond Issue r to account as if it were the trustee of an express trus t for the 
Bondholders; and (iv) by action or suit in equity enjoin any acts or things which may be unlawful or in violation of 
the rights of the Bondholders. 
(f) All rights and actions and claims under the Bond Indenture may be prosecuted and enforced by the 
Bond Trustee on  behalf of the Bondholders.  In  the case of pendency of any receivership, insolvency, liquidation, 
bankruptcy, reorganization or other similar judi cial proceeding relative to the Bond I ssuer or the Trust Estate, the 
Bond Trustee, subject to t he Collateral Agency Agreement, shall be entitled to file and prove a claim for the amount 
of the Bond Issuer’s and  the Borrower’s obligations to the Bondholders  o wing and unpaid and to file such othe r 
papers or documents as may be necessary in order to have the claims of the Bondholders allowed in s uch judicial 
proceeding and, to the extent permitted by applicable Law , to collect and receive any moneys or other pro perty 
payable or deliverable on any s uch clai ms and to distribute the same in accordance with  the terms of the Bond 
Indenture and of the Collateral Agency Agreement. 
(g) The exercise of the foregoing remedies upo n an Event of Default herein is subject to the  
provisions of the Collateral Agency A greement.  Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, in the event of 
any conflict between the remedial provisions of the Bond Indenture  and the remedial provisions of the Collateral 
Agency Agreement, the remedial provisions of the Collateral Agency Agreement shall prevail. 
Use of Moneys Received from Exercise of Remedies 
After an acceleration pursuant to  clause (c) of "Remedies Following and During the Continuance of an 
Event of Default " directly above, moneys re ceived by the Bond Trustee from the Co llateral Agent pursuant to the 
Collateral Agency Agreement, the Bond Indenture and the other Security Documents in respect of the Bond Issuer’s 
obligations hereunder shall be applied promptly by the Bond Trustee as follows:  
First, to pay the reasonable an d  proper fees and expenses (including the reasonable fees  and expenses of 
counsel) of and indemnificat ion payments owing to the Bond Trustee and the Bond Issuer pursuant to  the 
Finance Documents, including those incurr ed in connection with the exercise of r emedies following such 
Event of Default;  
Second, to the pay ments then due and payable by the Borrower to the Rebate Fund; 
Third, ratably, to all accrued and unpaid interest on the Bonds; 
Fourth, ratably, to the outstanding principal amount on the Bonds; and  
Fifth, to the Borrower, upon termination, expiration  or payment in full of all commitments, any s urplus to 
be applied at the Borrower’s discretion. 
Limitations on Rights  of Bondholders Acting Individually 
Subject to the Collateral Agency Agreement, no Bondholder shall have any right to institute any suit, action 
or proceeding at law or in equity for the enforcement of any remedy hereunder or for the enforcement of the terms of 
the Bond Indenture, unless an Event of  Default under the Bond Indenture  has occurred and is continuing and the 
Bondholder of such Bonds has made a written request to the B ond Trustee, and has given the Bond Trustee 60 days,  
F-10 
 
to take such action in i ts capacity as Bond Trustee.  Nothing in this se ction shall affect or impair the rig ht of the  
Bondholder to enforce the payment of the princi pal of and interest on or Redemption P rice of any Bond at and after 
the date such payment is due, subject, however,  to the limitations on remedies set forth in t he Bond Indenture.  In 
addition, any action by any Bondholder taken with respect to the Trust E state shall only be taken in accordance with 
the provisions of the Bond Indenture. 
Waivers of Events of Default 
The Bond Trustee, notwithstanding anything else t o the contrary contained in the Bond I ndenture, shall 
waive any Event of Default upon the writt en direction of Bondholders of not les s than a majority of the aggregate 
principal amount of Outstanding Bonds; pro vided, however, that any Event of Default in t he payment of the 
principal of or inte rest on, or the Re demption Price of, any Bond when due sh all not be waived (except as 
contemplated in clause (d) of "Remedies Following and During the Continuance of an Eve nt of Default " above) 
without the consent of t he Bondholders of 100% of the Outstandi ng Bonds, unles s, prior to such waiver, all such 
amounts (with interest on amounts past due on  any Bond at the interest rate on such Bond) and all expenses of the 
Bond Trustee in connection with such Event of Default ha ve been paid or provided for.  In ca se of any such waiver, 
then and in every such case the Bo nd Issuer, the Bond Trustee and the Bo ndholders shall be restored to their former 
positions and rights hereunder, b ut no such waiver shall extend to any subseque nt or other Event of Default, or 
impair any right consequent thereon. 
Bond Trustee to Notify Parties of Default and Disclose Information Relating to Default 
The Bond Trustee shall promptly notify in writing the  District, the Bond Issuer and the Borrower of any 
default hereunder or the occurrence of any Event of Default of which a Responsible Officer of the Bond Trustee has 
knowledge. The Bond Trustee shall  notify as soon as pra cticable in writing all Bondholders of the Bonds of the 
occurrence of any Event of Defaul t and shall make available to such Bondholders information reasonably requested 
of the Bond Trustee concerning the Event of Default, the Bonds, the  Borrower and any other information relevant to 
the Event of Default. 
Resignation or Replacement of Bond Trustee. 
(a) The present or any future Bond Trustee may resign by giving written notice to the Bond Issuer and 
the Collateral Agent (with a copy to the Bor rower) not less than 60 days before such resi gnation is to take effect.  
Such resignation shall take effect only upon the appointment of an d acceptance by a successor qualified as provided 
in subsection (c) below in this section.  If no successor is appoin ted within 60 days following the date designated in 
the notice for the Bond Trustee’s resignation to ta ke effect, the resigning Bond Truste e may petition a  c ourt o f 
competent jurisdiction for the appointment of a successor.  The present or any future Bond Trustee may be removed 
at any time by the Bon d Issuer in the event the Bond Issuer reasonably determines that the Bond Trustee is not duly 
performing its o bligations hereunder or that such removal is in the best interests of the Bond Issuer or the 
Bondholders. 
(b) In case the present or an y future Bond Trustee shall at any time resign or be removed or otherwise  
become incapable of acting, a successor may be appoin ted by the Bond Issuer, with the written consent of the 
Borrower (such consent not to be unreasonab ly withheld, delayed or conditioned).  Upon m aking any such 
appointment, the Bond Issuer shall forthwith give notice thereof to each Bondho lder, which noti ce may b e given 
concurrently with the notice of resignation given by any resigning Bond Trustee.  
(c) Every successor Bond Trustee (i)  shall be a bank or trust c ompany in good standing, (ii)  shall be 
qualified to do business in the District of C olumbia, (iii)  shall b e duly authorized to exercise trust powers and 
subject to examination by federal or state autho rity, (iv)  shall be qualified to act hereunder, and (v)  shall have a 
capital and surplus of not less th an $500,000,000.  Any successor Bond Trustee appointed hereunder shall execute, 
acknowledge and deliver to the Bond Issuer (with a copy to the Borrower and Collateral Agent) an instru ment 
accepting such appoi ntment hereunder, and thereupon such successor s hall, without any further act, deed or 
conveyance, become vested with all the estates, properties, rights, powers and trusts of its predecessor in the tr ust 
hereunder with like effect  as if originally named as Bond Trustee herein; but the Bond Trustee retiring sha ll, 
nevertheless, on the wri tten demand of i ts successor, execute and deliver an instrument conveying and transferring  
F-11 
 
to such successor, upon the trusts herein expressed, all  the estates, p roperties, rights, powers and trusts of the 
predecessor, which shall duly assign, transfer and deliver to the successor all properties and moneys held by it under 
the Bond Indenture.  Should any instrument in writing from the Bond Issuer be required by any  successor for more 
fully and certainly vesting in and confirming to it, such instrument in writing shall, at th e reasonable discretion of 
the Bond Issuer, be made, executed, acknowledged and delivered by the Bond Issuer on request of such s uccessor. 
(d) The instruments evidencing the resignation or removal of the Bond Trust ee and the appointment 
of a successor hereun der, t ogether with all other instruments provided for in this section shall be filed and/or 
recorded by the successor Bond Trustee in each recordi ng office, if any, where the Bond Indenture  shall have been 
filed and/or recorded.

following purposes: 
(a) to provide for the issuance by the Bond Issuer of the Additional Parity Bonds in  accordance with 
Bond Indenture; 
(b) to add to th e covenants, agreements and obligations of the Bond Issuer  under the Bond Indenture, 
other covenants, agreements and obligations to be observed for the protection of the Bondholders, or to surrender or 
limit any right, power or authority reserved to or conferred upon the Bond Issuer in the Bond Indenture; 
(c) to add additional revenues, properties or collateral to the Trust Estate; 
(d) to cure any ambiguity, or to cure, correct or supplement any defect, omission or incon sistent 
provision contained herein, provided that such action shall not have a material adverse effect on the Bondholders;  
(e) to amend any ex isting provision of the Bond Indenture or to add additional provisions which, in 
the Opinion of Bond Counsel , are necessary or advisable (i) to qualify, or to pre serve the qualification of, the 
interest on any Series 2022A Bonds for exclusion from gross income for federal income tax purposes; (ii) to qualify, 
or to preserve the qualification of, the Bond Indenture or any Supplemental Bond Indenture under the federa l Trust 
Indenture Act of 1939; or (iii) to qualify, or pre serve the qualification of, any Series 2022A Bonds for an exemption 
from registration or other limitations under the laws of any state or territory of the Un ited States and under any 
federal law of the United States; 
(f) to amend any provision of the Bond Inde nture relating to the Rebate Fund if, in the Opinion  o f 
Bond C ounsel, such amendment does not adversely affect the excludability of the interest on the Series 20 22A 
Bonds from gross income for federal income tax purposes; 
(g) to provide for or eliminate book-entry registration of any of the Bonds; 
(h) to obtain or maintain a rating (but not a particular rating level) of the Bonds by a Rating Agency; 
(i) to facilitate the receipt of moneys; 
(j) to grant to or confer upon the B ond Trustee for the benefit of the Bondholders a ny addit ional 
rights, remedies, powers or authority; 
(k) to permit the Bond Trustee to comply with any obligations imposed upon it by applicable Law; 
(l) to establish additional funds, accounts or subaccoun ts necessary or useful in connection with any 
other provision of this section; or

no Supplemental Bond  I ndenture modifying the Bond Indenture  in the way described below may be entered into

Conditions to Effectiveness of Supplemental Bond Indentures. 
(a) No Supple mental Bond I ndenture shall be effective until (i) it has been execute d by the Bond 
Issuer and the Bond Trustee and consented to in writing by the Borrower and (ii) Bond Counsel has delivered a 
written opinion to the effect that the Supplemental Bond Inden ture complies  with the provisions of the Bond 
Indenture and w ill not a dversely affect the excludability from gros s income for federal income tax purposes of 
interest on any series of Outstanding Bond s where the interest on such Bonds was intended to be exc ludable from 
gross income for federal income tax purposes on the original date of issuance of such Bonds. 
(b) No Supplemental Bond Indenture entered into as described in above under " Supplemental Bond 
Indentures Requiring Consent of Bondholders " shall be effective until, in addition to the conditions set forth in 
clause (a) of this section, (i) a notice has been mailed to each Bondholder, which notice describes the nature of the 
proposed Supplemental Bond Indenture and states that copies of it are on file at the office of the Bond Trustee for 
inspection by the Bondholders and (ii) subject to the provision s of any Supplem ental Bond Indenture, Bondholders 
of the required percentage of the Bonds have consented to the Supplemental Bond Indenture.  
(c) Anything herein to the contrary  notwithstanding, if an Bondhol der does not respond (in  any way) 
to a request with r espect to any Su pplemental Bond Indenture requiring consent of a majority of the Bondholders, 
but not requiring consent from greater than a majority of Bondh olders, as described in above under "Supplemental 
Bond Indentures Requiring Consent of Bondholders ", within twenty (20) Business Days of delivery of such request, 
then any Bonds registered to such Bondholder shall not  be counted for the purpose of calculat ing the consent  of a 
majority of Bondholders.  For the  avoidance of do ubt, this provision shall not apply to  clauses (a)-(d) of the 
"Supplemental Bond Indentures Requiring Consent of Bondholders" section above.

adversely affect the excludability of the interest on the Bonds from gross income for federal income tax purposes.

Bond Indenture, and the Bond Issuer or the Borrower, as the case may be, shall  not be entitled with respect to such

or other action taken under the Bond Indenture , the Bond Issuer or the Borrower, as the case may be, sh all furnish 
the Bond Trustee a certif icate of an Autho rized Officer, upon which the Bond Tr ustee may rely, de scribing all 
Bonds so to be excluded.

Amendments to Loan Agreement Requiring Consent of Bondholders 
Except for the amendments, changes or modifications as provided the Bond Indenture, the Bond Issuer may 
consent to any other amendment, change or modification of the Loan Agreement with t he prior written consent of 
the Maj ority Bondholders and with th e wri tten consent of the Borrower; provided, however, that no  amendment, 
change or modification o f the Loan Agreement may be entered into in respect of the matters contemplated below 
unless the prior written consent of the Bond holder of each Bond adversely  affected thereby and the Borrower has 
been obtained: 
(a) a reduction of the interest rate, principal of or interest on the Loan, a change in the maturity date of 
the  Loan, a change in the Bonds Payment Date for the  Loan or a change in the prepayment provisions applicable to 
the  Loan; or 
(b) the deprivation of the Bond Trustee of the Security Interest granted by the Security Documents. 
The Bond Trustee shall, upon notice of the same from the Bond  Issuer and upon being satisfactori ly 
indemnified with respect to expenses, cause notice of such proposed amendment, change or m odification to be given 
in the same manner as provided in the Bond Indenture  with respect to Supplemental Bond Indentures; provided, that 
prior to the delivery of such noti ce or request, the Bond Trustee shall be furnished an Opinion of Bond Counsel  to 
the effect that such amendment, change or modification complies with the provisions of the Bond Indenture  and will 
not adversely af fect the excludability of interest on the Series 2022A Bonds from g ross income for federal income 
tax purposes.  Such notice shall briefly set forth the nature of such proposed amendment, change or modification and 
shall state that copies of the instrument e mbodying the same are on file at the Designated Payment O ffice of the 
Bond Trustee for inspection by all Bondholders.  For the  avoidance of doubt, no amendment, change or modification 
of the Loan Agreement shall be effective without the prior written consent of the Borrower.

from gross inco me for federal income tax purposes and is  authorized by the Bond Indenture  and (ii) the receipt of 
the written consent of the Borrower. 
Discharge of Bond Indenture 
If (a) 100% of the principal of and interest on and Redemption Price due, or  to become due, on all the 
Bonds, the fees and expenses d ue to the Bond Trustee and all other amounts payable hereunder have been paid , or 
provision sha ll have been made fo r the payment thereof in accordance with the Bond Indenture  (b) all rebate 
payments payable to the U nited States with respect to the Bonds ha ve been paid, or provision shall have been made 
for the payment thereof, (c) all other amounts payable hereunder have  been paid, or provision shall have been made 
for the pay ment thereof, and (d) the  Opinion of Bond C ounsel required by the Bond Indenture has been delivered, 
then, (i) the right, title and interest of the Bond Trustee in and to the Trust Estate shall ter minate and be d ischarged 
(referred to herein as the " discharge" of the Bond Indenture); (ii) the Bond Trustee shall transfer and convey to or  to 
the order of the Bond Issuer all property that was part of the Trust Estate, including but not limited to any m oneys 
held in any Fund or Account hereunder, except any Def easance Escrow Account created pursuant t o the Bond 
Indenture (which Defeasance Escrow Account shall continue to be held in accordance with the agreement governing 
the administration thereof, subje ct to the Bond Indenture); and (iii) the Bond Trustee shall execute any instrument 
requested by the Bond Issuer to evidence such discharge, transfer and conveyance.  
F-16 
 
Defeasance of Bonds 
(a) All or any portion of the Outstanding Bonds shall b e deemed to have been paid (referred to herein 
as "defeased") prior to their maturity or redemption if: 
(i) if the defeased Bo nds are to be redeemed prior to their m aturity, the Borrower has irrevocably 
instructed the Bond Trustee in writing to give notice of redemption of such B onds in accordance 
with the Bond Indenture and any Supplemental Bond Indenture, a copy of which the Bo nd Trustee 
shall mail to each Rating Agency then maintaining a rating on the Bonds;  
(ii) there has been deposited in trust in a Defeasance Escrow Account eith er moneys or De feasance 
Securities which shall be sufficient, without any reinvestment thereof, to p ay when due the 
principal of and interest o n or Redemption Price, as applicable, due and to become due on the 
defeased Bonds on and prior to the redemption date or maturity date thereof, as the case may be; 
(iii) a verification agent, reasonably acceptable to th e Bond Issuer, has delivered a verificati on report 
verifying the deposit described in paragraph (ii) of this subsection in connection with any defeased 
Bonds; and 
(iv) the Opinion of Bond Counsel, if any, required by the Bond Indenture has been delivered. 
(b) The D efeasance Securities and moneys deposited i n a Defeasance Escrow Account pursuant to 
this section and the principal and interest payments on such Defeasance Se curities shall not b e withdrawn or used 
for any purpose other than, and shall be held in trust so lely for, the payment of the principal of  and interest on and 
Redemption Price of the defeased Bo nds; provided, however, that (i) any mo neys received from p rincipal and 
interest p ayments on such Defeasance Securities that are not required t o pay the princi pal of and interest on or 
Redemption Pric e of the defeased Bonds on the date of receipt shall, to  the extent practicable, be reinvested in 
Defeasance Securities maturing at the times and in amounts sufficient to pay when due the principal o f and interest 
on and Redemption Price to become due on t he defeased Bonds on or prior to the redemption date or maturity date 
thereof, as the case may be; and (ii) any mon eys or Defeasance Secur ities may be withdrawn from a Defeasance 
Escrow Account if (A ) the moneys and  D efeasance Securities that are on deposi t in the Defeasance Escrow 
Account, including any moneys  or Defeasance Securities that are subs tituted for the mone ys or Defeasance 
Securities that are withdrawn from the Defeasance Escrow Account, satisfy the conditions stated in subsection (a)(ii) 
of this section and (B) a verification report and Bond Counsel  opinion are delivered that comply with  subsections 
(a)(iii) and (a)(iv) of this section. 
(c) Any Bonds that are defeased as provided in Bond Inden ture shall no lo nger be secured by or 
entitled to any rig ht, title or interest in or to the Trust Estate, and the  principal of and interest on and Redem ption 
Price thereof shall be paid s olely fr om the Defeasance Securities and money held in the Defeasance Escrow 
Account. 
Opinion of Bond Counsel 
Prior to any dis charge of the Bond Indenture  pursuant to the Bond Indent ure or the defeasance of any 
Bonds pursuant to the Bond Indenture, Bond Counsel must have delivered to the Bond Issuer and the Bond Trustee 
a written opinion to the effect that all requirements of the B ond Indenture  for such discharge or defeasance have 
been complied with and that such discharge or defeasance will  not adversely affect the tax -exempt status of interest 
on any series of Bonds where  the interest on  s uch Bonds was intended to be excludable f rom gross income for 
federal income tax purposes on the original date of issuance of such Bonds. 
Defeasance of Less than all Bonds 
If less than all of the Bonds, any particular maturity or any Bond s with a particu lar interest rate within a 
maturity are d efeased, the Bond Trustee shall institute a system to preserve the identity of the individual B onds or  
F-17 
 
portions thereof that are d efeased, regardless of changes in Bond numbers attributable to transf ers and exchanges of 
Bonds. 
Additional Parity Bonds 
Subject to the restrictions set forth in the Bond Indenture  and the Collateral Agency Agreement, and  upon 
request by the  Bo rrower, the Bond Iss uer may issue Additional Parity Bonds, which shall be ratably  and equally 
secured by the Trust Estate, upon execution of  a Supplemental Bond Indenture and a Supplemental Loan Agreement 
without consent of the Bondholders pursuant to t he Bond Indenture.  Exc ept to the extent inconsistent with the 
express terms of the Additional Parity Bonds issued and the related Supplement al Bond Indenture and Supplemental 
Loan Agreement executed pursuant to the Bond Indenture, all of the provisions, t erms, covenants and conditions of 
this Bond Indenture shall be applicable to any Additional Parity Bonds issued hereunder. 
(a) The Bond Issuer ma y issue Additional Parity Bonds in accordance with  the Bond Indenture and 
the Collateral Agency Agreement.  Such Ad ditional Parity Bonds may be issued if the Borrower provides evidence 
reasonably acceptable to the B ond Trustee and the Bond Issuer that the in debtedness of the Borrower evidenced by 
Additional Parity Bonds is permitted under the Bond I ndenture, the Project Agreement and the Collateral Agency 
Agreement. 
(b) Prior to the issuance of any Additional Parity Bond s, the Borrower must deliver to the Bond 
Trustee and the Collateral Agent the following: 
(i) A certificate of the Borrower dated as of the date of issuance of s uch proposed Additional Parity 
Bonds stating that no  Default or Event of Default has  occurred and is  c ontinuing or will re sult 
from the issuance of such Additional Parity Bonds; 
(ii) A certified copy of a resolution or res olutions of the Council  authorizing the iss uance of such 
Additional Parity Bonds, stating the purpose or purposes for the i ssuance of such Additional Parity 
Bonds and authorizing the execution and delivery of a Supplemental Bond Indenture and such 
Additional Parity Bonds; 
(iii) The ap plicable Sup plemental Bond Indenture, which shall provide for the application of the 
proceeds of such Additional Parity Bonds; 
(iv) An opinion or  opinions of Bond Counsel to the effect that: (A ) all conditions precedent to the 
issuance of the Additional Parity Bonds pursuant to the Home Rule Act , the Bond Indenture  and 
any relevant Supplemental Bond Indenture  have been satisfied, and such issuance is authorized by 
the Bond Indenture ; (B)  the Add itional Parity Bonds, when issued, will be va lid and binding 
obligations of the Bond Iss uer in accordance with their terms; and (C) an opinion as to the tax 
status of the interest on the Additional Parity Bonds under the Code; 
(v) A certification of a Bond Iss uer Representative directing the application of the proceeds of such  
Additional Parity B onds and directing the Bond Trustee to authenticate such Additional Parity 
Bonds.  The Bond Trus tee may rely upon such  written direction of the Bond Issuer as conclusiv e 
evidence that any and all conditions to the  issuance of such Addit ional Parity Bonds by the Bond 
Issuer have been satisfied and shall be under no duty to make any ind ependent investi gation 
thereof; 
(vi) Executed counterparts of all financing documents related to the Additional Parity Bonds including, 
without limitation, (i) a  certified c opy of  t he executed counterpart of the Supplemental Loan 
Agreement, under which the Bond  Issuer agrees t o loan the proceeds of  t he Additional Parity 
Bonds to the Borrower, and (ii) an original executed counterpart of the Supplemental Bond 
Indenture under which the Additional Parity Bonds have been issued; and   
F-18 
 
(vii)  such other opinions, certifications and consult ants’ reports as may re asonably be required by the 
Bond Issuer shall have  been delivered to the Bond Issuer, the Bond Trustee and the Collat eral 
Agent i ncluding consultant reports in final form from each of the LTA and the LIA and a 
certificate from each o ther advisor that, afte r giving effect to the issuance of such Additional  
Parity Bonds (and the refunding, prepayment , or defeasance of Bond s in connect ion therewith), 
the information contained in the respective consultant report is consistent with the Fin ancial 
Model as updated for the Additional Parity Bonds. 
Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in the Bond Indenture , any issuance of Additional Parity Bonds is 
subject to the further condition that it must comply with any applicable requirements set fo rth in the Project 
Agreement. 
No Recourse; No Individual Liability 
No recourse shall be had for the payment of, or premium if any, or intere st on any of  the Bonds or for any 
claim based thereon or upon any obligation, covenant or agreement in the Bond Inde nture contained, against any 
past, present or future elected and appoi nted officer , member, trustee, employee, attorn ey or agent of the Bond  
Issuer or any elected and appointed officer , director, member, trustee, employee, attorney or agent  or any successor 
entity, as such, eith er directly or through the Bond Issuer or any s uccessor entity, under any rule of law or equit y, 
statute or constitution or by en forcement by any assessment or penalty or otherwise.  The members of the Bond 
Issuer, the elected and ap pointed officers and em ployees of the Bond Issuer, attorneys or any ot her agents of the 
Bond Issuer are not subject t o personal liability or  accountability by reason of any action authorized by the Home 
Rule Act, including without limitation, the issuance of bonds, the failure t o issue bonds, the execution of bonds and 
the making of guarantees.  All covenants, stipulatio ns, promises, agreement s and obliga tions of the Bond Issuer or 
the Bond Trustee, as the case may be, contained herein, in any Supplemental Bond Indenture or in the Bonds shall 
be deemed to be the covenants, st ipulations, promises, agreements and obligation s of the Bond Issuer or the Bond 
Trustee, a s the case may be, and not of any member, director, elected and appointed officer , employee, att orney, 
servant or other agent of the Bond Issuer or the Bond Trustee in his or her individual capacity, and no recour se shall 
be had on acco unt of any s uch covenant, stipulation, promise, agreement or obligation, or for any claim based 
thereon or hereunder , against any member, d irector, elected and appointed officer, employe e, attorney, servant or 
other agent of the Bond  Issuer or the Bond Tru stee or any natura l person executing the Bond Indenture , any 
Supplemental Bond Indenture, the Bonds or any related document or instrument.  
Events Occurring on Days that are not Business Days 
If the date for making any payment or the  last day for performance of any act or  the exercising of any right 
under the Bond Indenture  or the Bonds is a day that is not a Business D ay, such payment may be made, such act 
may be performed or such right may be exercised on the next succeeding Business Day, with the same fo rce and 
effect as if done on the nominal date provided in such instrument. 
Actions of the Bond Trustee Required Pursuant to the Collateral Agency Agreement   
The Collateral Agency Agreement requires certain actions of the Collateral Agent, at the direction of the 
Bond Trustee, and the Bond Trustee is hereby authorized to act if (a ) so instructed by the B ondholders of a majority 
of the aggregate principal amount of the then Outstanding Bonds as prescribed in the Collateral Agency Agreement 
or (b ) in the judgement of the Bond Trustee, such action is not materially adverse to the interests of the Bond 
Trustee or any of the Bondholders.  If any one of the required actions set out in ( b) above is deemed material, the 
Bond Trustee shall act only if so instructed by the Bondholder s of a majority of the aggregate principal amount of 
the then Outstanding Bonds. 
Governing Law 
The s ubstantive laws of the District of Columbia s hall govern the construction and enforcement of the 
Bond Indenture and the Bonds without giving effect to the application of conflict of law principles.

APPENDIX G 
 
SUMMARY OF CERTAIN PROVISIONS OF THE LOAN AGREEMENT 
The following is a summary of selected provisions of the Loan Agreement and is not a full statement of t he 
terms of such  agreement. Accordingly, the following summary is qualified in its e ntirety by refer ence to such 
agreement and is subject to the full text of such agreement.  A copy of such agreement is available, free of charge, 
upon request from the Borrower or the Bond Trustee . Unless otherwise stated, any reference in this Official 
Statement to any agre ement will mean such agreement and all schedules, exhibits and attachments thereto, as 
amended, supplemented or otherwise modified and in effect as of the  date hereof. 
Agreement to Issue the Bonds; Loan of Proceeds 
The Bond Issuer  agrees to issue,  sell a nd deliver the Bonds in accordance with the terms of the Bond 
Indenture to provide for the financing of th e Project.  Upon the terms and conditions of the Loan Agree ment and the 
Bond Indenture, the Bond Issuer  agrees to lend to the Borrower  o n the Financia l Closing Date the Loan.  Upon 
written instructions from the Borrower, the Bond Issuer hereby agrees to instruct the Bond Tru stee to deliver to the 
Collateral Agent the proceeds received from the sale of the Bonds with instruction to deposit such proceeds directly 
in the Construction Account held by the Collate ral Agent pursuant to the Collateral Agency Agreement and as 
required by the Collateral Agency Agreement and agreed to by the Borrower.  Neither the Bond Issuer nor the Bond 
Trustee in any way warrants or represents that amounts on deposit in the Construct ion Account will be sufficient to 
fund, in whole or in part, any disbursement requested by the Borrower. 
Borrower to Provide Funds to Repay Loan 
The Borrower unconditionally promises t o repay the Loan .  In the event that proceeds derived from the 
Loan, or any other available (or to be available) funds as described i n the Co llateral Agency Agreement are n ot 
sufficient to financ e the Project Costs on  the Fina ncial Closing Date, the Borrower shall not be entitled to any 
reimbursement from the Bond Issuer or the Bond Trust ee for the payment of such costs nor shall the Borrower be 
entitled to any abatement, diminution or postponement of its payments hereunder. 
Loan to Finance Project Costs 
The Borrower agrees to cause requisitions to be directed to the Bond Trustee as may be necessary to effect 
disbursements out of the Construction Account or to pay or reimburse the Borrower for the financing, refinancing or 
reimbursing of the Project Costs.  The Borrower s hall use the proceeds of the Loan to finance a portion of the 
Project Costs.  
Security for Repayment of Loan; Issuance of Note 
(a) Prior to or simultaneously with the delivery of the Loan Agreement, the Borrower shall deliver the 
Security Documents and the C ollateral to the Bond Trustee, after assignment by the Bond Issuer, and the Collateral 
Agent (as applicable) as security for the payments and obligations of the Borrower hereunder. 
(b) In consideration of the issuance o f the Bonds by the Bond Issue r and the ap plication of the 
proceeds thereof as provided in the Bond Indenture, the Borrower agrees to execute the N otes and deliver the Notes  
to the Bond Issuer concurrent with the issuance and  delivery of the Bonds.  The Loa n shall be evidenced by the 
Notes.  The N otes shall be assigned and delivered by, or on behalf of, the Bond Issuer to the Bond Trustee.  The 
assignment of the Notes shall be without recourse to, or warranty by, the Bond Issuer.

To the extent applicable , the Borrow er shall take all actions required to be taken by the Borrower in the 
Bond Indenture as if the Borrower were a party to the Bond Indenture.  
Amounts Payable 
(a) The Borrower covenants and agrees to repay the Loan, as follows: on or bef ore any Bonds  
Payment Date for the Bonds or any other date that any payment of principal or Redemption Price of and interest on 
the Bonds is require d to  be made or provided for in respect of the Bonds pursuant to the Bond Indentur e (which 
payments of principal of and i nterest on the Bon ds will be in the respective  amounts set forth on the debt service 
schedule attached hereto as Att achment A and as am ended from time to time pursuant to the Bond Indenture), until 
the payment of prin cipal or Redemption Price of and interest on th e Bonds shall have been fully paid or provisio n 
for the payment thereof shall have been made in accordance w ith the Bond Indent ure, in immediately available 
funds, a sum which, together with any other moneys a vailable for such payment in the applic able Account of  the 
Debt Service Fund will enable the Bond Trustee to pay to the Bondholders the amount due and paya ble on such date 
as interest, principal or Redemption Price on  the Bonds as provided in the Bond Inde nture. All payments made by 
the Borrower shall be made free and clear of (and grossed up for) any tax or stamp duty.  
(b) The Bond Issuer hereby directs the Bo rrower and, subject to the Bond Indenture o r the Collateral 
Agency Agreement, as applicable, the Borr ower hereby agrees to pay or direct the Collateral Agent to pay to the 
Bond Trustee a t the De signated Payment Office of the Bond Trustee all payments payab le by the Borrower in 
respect of the Loan pursuant to this subsection. 
(c) Concurrently with the issuance  of the Bonds, the Borrower also shall pay or direct the Collateral 
Agent to pay to the Bond Issuer the Program Fee directly related to the issuance of the Bonds and all agre ements 
related thereto, in an aggregate amount not to exceed the threshold specified in the Loan Agreement. 
(d) The Borrower will also pay or direct the Collateral Agent to pay th e reasonable fees and expenses 
of the Bond Trustee, including without limitation any fees or expenses incurred pursuant to the Bond Indenture, and 
all other amounts which may be payable to the Bond Trustee under the terms of the Bond Indenture or in accordance 
with any contractual arrangement between the Borrower and the Bond Trustee with respect thereto. 
(e) In the event that the Borrower should fail to make any of the  payments required in this section, the 
amount so in default shall continue as an obligation of the Borrower until the amount in default s hall have been fully 
paid, and the Borrower agrees to pay the same with interest thereon, to the extent provided under t he Bond Indenture 
or under the fee agreement between the Borrower and the Bond Trus tee or as permitted by applicable Law, from the 
date when such payment was due, at the rate of interest borne by the Bonds. 
(e) To the extent any moneys have been deposited by the Borrower, or on t he Borrower’s behalf, into 
the Interest Account, Principal Account or Mandatory Redemption Account of t he Debt S ervice Fund for t he 
purpose of paying in terest on and principal and Redemption Price of the Bonds when due, the Borrow er’s payment 
obligations pursuant this section with respect to the applicable Interes t Payment, principal payment or redemption of 
such Bonds will be deemed satisfied.  
G-3 
Obligations of the Borrower Unconditional 
The obligations of the Borrower to make the pa yments required in the Loan Agreement and to perform and 
observe the other agreemen ts contained herein shall be absolute and unconditional  and shall not be  subject to any 
defense or any right of setoff, counterclaim or recoupment arising out of (a) any breac h by the Bond Issuer, the 
Collateral Agent or the Bond Trustee of any obligation to the B orrower, whether hereunder or otherwise, or (b) any 
indebtedness or liability at any time owing to the Borrower by the Bond Issuer, the Collateral Agent or the Bond 
Trustee, and, until such time as the principal or Redemption Price of, and interest on the Bonds shall have been fully 
paid or provision f or the payment th ereof shall have been made in accordance with the Bond Indenture, the 
Borrower (1) will not suspend or discontinue any payments  provided for  in the Loan Agreement, (2) will perform 
and observe all other agreements contained in the Loan Agr eement and the Se curity Documents and (3 ) except as 
otherwise provided herein, will not terminate the Loan Agreement  or any of the Security Documents to which it is a 
party fo r any cause, or any failur e of the  Bond Issuer, the Collateral Agent or the Bond Trustee to perfor m and 
observe any agreement, whether express or implied, or any duty, liability or obligation arising o ut of or connected 
with the Loan Agreement.  Nothing contained in this section  shall be c onstrued to release the Bond Issuer from the 
performance of any o f the agreements on it s part herein contained, and in the event the Bond Issuer should fail to 
perform any such agreement on i ts part, the Borrow er may institute such action against the Bond Issuer as the 
Borrower may deem necessary to compel performance so long as such action does not abrogate the obligations of 
the Borrower contained in the first sentence of this section. 
Prepayment and Redemption 
The Borrower shall have the option t o prepay its obligations hereunder at the times and in the amounts as 
necessary to cause the Bond Issuer to redeem the Bonds in accordance with the terms of the Bond Indentu re and the 
Bonds.  The Bon d Issuer, at the re quest of the Borrower, if applicable, shall forthwith take all steps (other than the 
payment of funds necessary to effect such redemption) necessary under the applicable redemption provisions of the 
Bond Indenture to effect redemption of  all or part of the Outstanding Bonds, as may be specified by the  Borrower 
and required by the Bond Indenture, o n the date established for such redemption.  Upon any such redemption in full 
and payment of all amounts required by t he Bond Indenture, the Loa n Agreement shall terminate as provided in the 
Loan Agreement.  
Mandatory Prepayment 
The Borrower shall deliver or cause to be del ivered to the Bond Tru stee the moneys needed to redeem the 
Bonds in accordance with the mandatory red emption provisions set forth in the Bo nds and the Bond I ndenture.  
Whenever the Bo nds are subject to mandatory redemption pursuant to the Bond Indenture, t he Borrower will 
cooperate with the Bond Issuer and the Bond Trustee in effecting such redemption in accordance with the terms of 
the Bond Indenture.

obligations under the Collateral Age ncy Agreement.  Without limiting the gen erality of the for egoing, the Borrower 
covenants and agrees that (a) it shall comply with all ap plicable provisions of the Collateral Agency Agreement, (b) 
it shall not take or cause or permit to be taken any action ex cept as permitted by t he Collateral Agency Agreement, 
whether or not specif ically permitted hereunder, (c) the covenants and agreement s of the Borrower set forth in the 
Collateral Agency Agreement are incorporated herein by reference and affirmed as if f ully set forth herein; 
provided, however  that the Bond Iss uer acknowledges so  long as  the Bond Issuer is a Secured Party under the 
Collateral Agency Agreemen t that such covenants and agreements shall be enforced pursuant to the Collateral 
Agency Agreement and (d) at the request o f the Bond Issue r or the Bond Trus tee, it shall furnish to ei ther or both 
parties such documents, certificates and reports as it may  be required under the terms of the Collateral Agency 
Agreement to deliver to the Collateral Agent f rom time to time, whethe r or not otherwi se specifically re quired 
hereunder.

accordance with the requirements set forth in the Collateral Agency Agreement.  
Tax Covenants of the Borrower and Bond Issuer 
(a) The Borrower shall not take any action, nor fail to take any action or permit any action to be taken, 
if any such action or inactio n would adversely affect  the exclusion f rom gross income f or federal income t ax 
purpose of the interest on the Ser ies 2022A Bonds. Without limiting the gene rality of the foregoing , the Borrower 
shall comply with the instructions and requirements of the Tax  Certificate. The Borrower (or any related persons, as 
defined in Section 147 (a)(2) of the Code) shall not, pursuant to an arrangement, fo rmal or informal, purchase Series 
2022A Bonds in an amount related to the amount of any obligation to be acquired from  the Borrower by the Bond 
Issuer. Notwithstanding any other provision of the Loan Agreement such covenants shall survive the defe asance of 
the Series 2022A Bonds. 
(b) The requirements of this section shall not apply to the Ser ies 2022A Bond s or any portion of the 
Series 2022A Bonds, if all of the proceeds of the Series 2022A Bonds are fully expended within six (6) months after 
the Financial Closing Date. To assis t and act on behalf of the Borrower in the performance of the covenant in 
subsection (d) of this sect ion, the Borrower shall at least thirty  (30) days in adva nce of any calculation required 
pursuant to Section  148 of the Code, during the term of the Series 2022A Bond s, the proceeds of which are not 
expected to be fully expended six (6) months from the  Financial Closing Date, and at least 30 days thereafter, ret ain 
an independent Person  to act as Rebate Mon itor and to perform the calculations required pursu ant to this section. In 
the event that the Rebate Monitor becomes unable to perform such calculation, the Borrower shall  appoint another 
independent Person to  perform the same w ith the approval of the Bond  Issuer, which approval shall not be 
unreasonably w ithheld, condi tioned or  delayed, and shall give prompt notice of su ch appointment to the Bond 
Trustee. In the event the Borrower does not appoint a successor Rebate Monitor to p erform such calculat ions, the 
Bond Issuer shall appoint such a successor Rebate Monitor, the expense o f which shall be borne by the Borrower, 
and shall give prompt notice of such appointment to the Bond Trustee. The special covenants of the Borrower in this 
section concerning compliance with Section 148 of the Code shall survive the d efeasance or payment i n full of the 
Series 2022A Bonds notwithstanding any other provision of the Loan Agreement until the requirements for payment 
of any am ounts required to be paid with respect thereto pursuant to Section 148(f) of the Code have been fu lly 
satisfied. 
(c) Subject to the Reserved Rights of the Bond Issuer, in the event that the Bond Issuer i s notified in 
writing that the Series 2 022A Bond s or any transaction pertaining thereto is the s ubject o f any Internal Revenue 
Service investigation, suit or order, the Bond  Issuer shall promptly give notice of the same to the  Borrower and 
allow the Borrower to participate in all aspects of the  response. In the even t that the Borrower  is notified in writing 
that the Series 2022A Bond s or any transaction pert aining to the Ser ies 2022A Bonds is the subject of any Internal 
Revenue Service investigation, suit or order, the Borrower shall promptly give notice of the same to t he Bond Issuer 
and allow the Bond Issuer to participate in all aspects of the response. 
(d) On or before the F inancial Closing Date, Borrower shall provide true and accurate infor mation 
sufficient for the Bond Issuer with the assistance of Bond Counsel  to file, on or within the legally pe rmitted period 
after the Financial Closing Date, Form 8038 (I nformation Return for P rivate Activity Bond Issues) with the Internal 
Revenue Service. 
(e) The Borro wer further covenants, represents and warrants to comply with the procedures applicable 
to the Borrower set f orth in the Tax Certificate to the extent necessary  to comply with the cov enant set forth in the 
Loan Agreement.

(a) Except as expressly contemplated herein, in the Bond Indenture, the Collateral Agency Agreement 
and the Securit y Documents, the Borrower shall not assign the  Loan Agreement or any int erest of the Borrower

Borrower hereunder; (B) the Bond Trustee has received an Opinion o f Bond Counsel to the effect that neither the 
validity nor the e nforceability of the Loan Agreement  shall be adversely affected by such assignment; (C ) the 
Project shall continue in the Opinion of Bond Counsel to be a "project" as such term is defined in  Section 490 of the 
Home Rule Act  after such as signment; (D) such assignment shall not, in  the Opinion of Bon d Counsel, have an 
adverse effect on the exclusion  from gross income for federal income tax p urposes of interest on the Ser ies 2022A 
Bonds or has an advers e effect on any applicable tax exemptio n with respect to the Series 2022A Bond s provided 
under applicable Law; (E) the assignment complies with the pro visions of the Collateral Agency Agreement; ( F) the 
Bond Issuer shall have consented to such assi gnment, which consent shall not be unreasonably  withheld; and (G) a 
written confirmation from any Rating Agency then providing a rating on the Bonds is provide d to the Bond Trustee 
stating that there wi ll not be an Adverse Rating Effect as a result of th e proposed assignment, or ( ii) the Bond 
Trustee shall have received instruction by Majority B ondholders to provi de such consent.  Subject to the foregoing,  
G-6 
the terms "Bond Issuer," " Borrower" and "Bond Tru stee" shall, where the context requires, include the respective 
successors and assigns of such persons.   
(b) Except as expressly contemplated herei n, in the Bond Inde nture, the Loan Agreement , the 
Collateral Agency Agreement and the Security  Documents, the Bond Issuer shall not assign the Loan Agreement  or 
any interest of Bond Issuer herein, either in whole  or in part, other  than to any other Governm ental Entity, and such 
other Governmental Entity shall thereupon become vested with all the benefits in respect thereof granted to the Bond 
Issuer herein or otherwise. 
Indemnification 
(a) The Borrower agrees to indem nify and hold harm less each Bond Issuer Indemnitee against any 
and all claims, losses, damages or liabilities, j oint and several, to which  the Bond Issuer I ndemnitee may become 
subject, insofar as such losses, claims, damages or liabilities (or actions in respect of such losses, claims, damages or 
liabilities) arise o ut of, or are based on: (i) the financing or refi nancing of, or reimbursing  of the Borrower f or, 
Project Costs, including, but not limited to, th e authori zation, issuance, sale and delivery of t he Bonds and (ii)  
misrepresentation by the Borrower or breach by the Borrower of any warranty or covenant containe d in the Loan 
Agreement or any certificat e, document or instrument delivered in connection he rewith (including, but not limited 
to, the Tax Certificate and the Application  submitted by the Borrower to the Bond Issuer). 
(b) The Borrower agrees to release th e Bond Issuer Indemnitees from any and all l iability for any loss 
or damage that may be caused to the per sons or property of the Borrower or any  of its members, d irectors, trustees, 
employees, agents, or inv itees due to any act arising out of the construct ion o r o peration of the Pr oject.  
Additionally, the Borrower agrees to release the Bond Issuer I ndemnitees from any and all liability for any lo ss or 
damage that may be caused to the Borro wer or any of its members, directors, trustees, employees, agents, or invitees 
arising out of (i) its performance of or failure to perform any term, provision or covenant co ntained in any of the 
Bond Issuer Documents or (ii) any of the transactions provide d for in any of the  Bond Issuer Documents, provided, 
however, that nothin g contained in the Lo an Agreement shall be construed to preclude in any way any action or 
proceeding against any Bond Issuer Indemnitee, in any court or befo re any governmental body, agency or 
instrumentality to enforce the Bond Issuer’s compliance wi th the p rovisions of the Bond Issuer Documents other 
than that element of any action or proceeding involving a monetary claim.   
(c) In the exercise of the powers of the Bond Issuer and its el ected and appointed  officials, officers, 
employees, and agents involved in the authorization, issuance, sale or delivery of the Bonds (excluding independent 
counsel), including (without limiting the foregoing) th e application of moneys, the investment of funds, and actions 
upon the occurrence of an Event of Default by the Bor rower, no Bond Issuer I ndemnitee shall be accountable to the 
Borrower for any action taken or  omitted by any Bond Issuer Indemnitee in good f aith and reasonably believed by it 
or them to be authorized or within the discretion or rights or powers conferr ed.  Each Bond Issuer Inde mnitee shall 
be protected in acting upon any paper or document believed to be g enuine, and each Bond Issuer Indemnitee  may 
conclusively rely upon the advice of independent counsel and may (but need not) require further evidence of  any 
fact or matter before taking any action.  No recourse shall be had by the Borrower for any claims based on the Loan 
Agreement or on the Bon d Indenture against any Bond Issuer Indemnit ee alleging personal liability unless such 
claims are based upon the negligence or willful or fraudulent misconduct of such Bond Issuer Indemnitee. 
(d) If a Bond Issuer  Indemnitee receives notice of any claim or acti on against such Bo nd Issuer 
Indemnitee with respect to which indemnification is to be sought from the Borrower u nder the Loan Agreement, 
such Bond Issuer Indemnitee will timely notify the Borrower of the claim or acti on in writing; timely notice shall 
mean at such a time so as to enable the Borrower  to promptly and meaningfully participate in a defense against such 
a claim, and in the event of an untimely not ice, the Borrower’s obligation hereunder shall abate with respect to such 
notified Bond Issuer Indem nitee only to the extent such untimeliness w as a proximate cause of a loss as to which 
indemnity is sought hereunde r.  I n the event any cl aim is made or act ion brought against any Bond Issuer 
Indemnitee and s uch Bond I ssuer Indemnitee is entitled to indemn ification under th e Loan Agreement , such Bond 
Issuer Indemnitee shall immediately notify the Borrower, and the B orrower may elect to assume the defense of t he 
claim and any action brought on the claim (with c ounsel reasonably satisfactory to such Bond Issue r Indemnitee) 
and shall pay all reasonable expenses incurred as a result of the claim.  If the Borrower shall n ot have employed 
counsel t o have charge of t he defense of any such action (within forty -five (45 ) days fo llowing the notice and  
G-7 
direction specif ied above), the Bo nd Issuer Indemnitee shall  have the right to engage its own counsel, and the 
reasonable legal and other expenses incurred by such Bond Issuer Indemnitee shall be borne by the Borrower.  If t he 
Borrower and a Bond Issuer Indemnitee shall have reasonably concluded that there may be defenses available to that 
Bond Issuer Indemnitee which are different  from or additional to those available to th e Borrower (in which case the 
Borrower shall not have the right to direct the defense of such action  on behalf of such Bond Issuer Indemnitee), the 
Bond Issuer Ind emnitee shall have the right to engage its own co unsel, and the reasonable legal and other ex penses 
reasonably incurred by such Bond Issuer Indem nitee shall be borne by the Borrower.  No party will be liable in 
respect of any settlement effected without its prior written consent.  The defense of any such  claim or action shall 
include the taking of all actions necessary or appropriate to the defense.  Subjec t to the notice requirements of clause 
(b) of this section, the Borrower agrees to reimburse any reaso nable legal and other expenses reasonably incurred by 
any Bond Issuer Indemnitee in connection with investigating or defending any such loss, cla im, damage, liability or 
action. 
(e) The Borrower wi ll indemnify and hold harmless the Bond Trustee for and against all liabilities, 
claims, costs and expenses incurred on account of the acceptance and administration of the Bond Indenture, 
including any action taken by the Bond Trustee in accordance with the terms of the Loan Agreement, the Bonds, the 
Collateral Agency Agreement or the Bond Indenture or any action taken  at the request of or with the consent of the 
Borrower, including the reasonable costs and expenses  incurred by the Bond Trustee in defending themselves 
against any such claims. In case any action or proceeding is brought against the Bond Trustee in respect of which 
indemnity may be sought hereunder, the party seeking indemnity promptly shall give notic e o f that action or 
proceeding to the Borr ower, and the Borr ower upon receipt of that notice shall have the right to assume the defense 
of the action or proc eeding (represented by counsel reasonably acceptable to the indemnified party); provided that 
such indemnified party shall cooperate with rea sonable requests o f the Borrower in respect of such defense; and 
provided further that failure of a party to give tha t notice shall not relieve the Borrower from any of its obligations 
under this section unless (and t hen only to the extent) that failure pr ejudices the defense of the action or proceeding 
by the Borrower.  If the Borrower shall not have employed counsel to  have charge of the defense of any such action 
(within forty-five (45) days following the notice and  direction specified above), the indemn ified party shall have the 
right to engage its own counsel, and the reasonable legal and other expenses incurred by  s uch indemnified party 
shall be borne by the Borrower.  If the Borrower and an indemnified party s hall have reasonably concluded that 
there may be defenses available to that indemnified party which are different from or additional to those available to 
the Borrower (in which case the Borrower shall not have the right to direct the defense of such actio n on behalf of 
such indemnified party), th e indemnified party may employ separate counsel and participate in the defense and the 
Borrower shall pay the reaso nable fees and expenses of such separate counsel. The Borrower shall not be liable for 
any settlement made without its consent, which shall n ot be unreasonably withheld.  The Borrower shall not consent 
to any settlement with respect to any action affecting  the Bond Trustee without the Bond Trustee’s consent, which 
shall not be unreasonably withheld.  The i ndemnification set forth in this paragraph for the Bond T rustee is intended 
to and shall include the indemnification of all affected directors, officers, agents and employees of the Bond Trustee.  
(f) The indemnification set forth in this section is intended to and shall (i) be enforceable by the B ond 
Issuer and the  Bond Trus tee (as applicable) to the  full extent permitted by applicable Law, and (ii) survive th e 
resignation or removal of the Bond Trustee and the termin ation of the Loan Agreement or the discharge of the Bond 
Indenture; provided that the relevant indemnity shall not, as to any Bond Issuer Indemnitee and the Bond Trustee, be 
available to the extent  that such losses, cl aims, damages, liabilities or related expenses are determined by a court of 
competent jurisdiction b y final and non- appealable judgment to  have resu lted fro m the negligence o r willful 
misconduct of such Bond Issuer Indemnitee  and the Bond Trustee (as applicable) or any indemnified party relat ed 
to it. 
Events of Default Defined 
Any one or more of the following events shall constitute "Events of Default" under the Loan Agreement: 
(a) Failure by the Borrower to pay any amount required to be pai d under  the Loan A greement as 
described in the "Amounts Payable" section above and such non-payment remains unremedied for a perio d of three 
(3) Business Day s, provided that it shall not constitute an Event of Default if su ch failure results from a techn ical 
error or omission, caused by a party other than the Borrower, in connection with the administratio n of the a ccounts  
G-8 
from which such paym ents are to be made and con tinues for a period not gre ater than two (2) Business Day s after 
notice of such non-payment is received by the Borrower from the Bond Trustee;  
(b) Failure by the Borrower to observe and perform a ny covenant, condition or agr eement on its part 
to be observed or  performed under the Loan A greement, other than as otherw ise provided for in the Loan 
Agreement, and such non- compliance shall remain unremedied for a period of thirty (30) days after the ea rlier of 
(i) written notice sp ecifying such failure shall  have been given to the Bond Trustee  by the Borrower or the Bond 
Issuer, or (ii) written notice spe cifying such failure and requesting that it be remedied shall have b een given to the 
Borrower by the Bond Trustee, or such longer period as  is reasonably necessary under the circumstances to remedy 
such failure, such extension not to exceed ninety (90) days; 
(c) The occurrence of a Insolvency Event with respect to the Borrower; 
(d) Any of the representations, war ranties or  certifications of the Borro wer made in or del ivered 
pursuant to the Loan Agreemen t (including, but not limited to, the information furnished b y the Borrower to the 
Bond Issuer for use by the Bond I ssuer in its Ta x Certificate or information  fu rnished by the Borrower in the  
Application submitted to the District of Co lumbia’s Revenue Bond Program), shall prove to ha ve been incorrect 
when made, unle ss such misrepresentation is capable of being cured and is cured wit hin thirty (30) days after the 
Borrower’s receipt of written notice from the Bond Trustee of such misrepresentation; 
(e) An "Event of Default" occurs under the Bond Indenture or the Collateral Agency Agreement; 
(f) Any Security Document ceases, except in accordance with its terms or as expressly  permitted 
under the Finance D ocuments, to be effective t o grant a  perfected Security Interes t on any material portion of the 
Collateral described therein , other than as a result of actions or failure to act b y the Bond Trus tee, the Collateral 
Agent or any other Secured Party; and 
(g) Any acceleration occurs of the maturity of any i ndebtedness authori zed under the Collateral 
Agency Agreement pursuant to any Finan ce Document, or any such indebtedness shall not be paid in full upon  the 
final maturity thereof.  
Remedies on Event of Default 
Whenever any Event of Default hereunder  shall have occurred and be continuing, the Bond Trustee,  or the 
Bond Issuer with the wr itten consent of the Bond Trustee, have the right to, in conjunction w ith its available 
remedies under the Bond Indenture and subjec t to the terms of the Collateral Age ncy Agreement, take one or any 
combination of the following remedial steps, by notice to the Borrower and the Collateral Agent: 
(a) Declare th at all or any part o f any amount outstanding under the Loan Agr eement is (1) 
immediately due and payable, and /or (2) p ayable on demand by the Bon d Trustee, and any such notice  shall take 
effect in accordance with its terms but only if all amounts payable with respect to the O utstanding Bonds are being 
accelerated pursuant to the Bond Indenture, or if all of the O utstanding Bonds are being defeased pursuant to the 
Bond Indenture or otherwise paid in full; 
(b) Pursuant to the terms of the Security Documents, direct the Collateral Ag ent to take or cause to be 
taken any and all actions necessary  to implement any available remedies with respect to the Collateral under any of 
the Security Documents; 
(c) Have reasonable access to and inspect, examine and make copies of the books and records and any 
and all accounts, data an d income tax and other tax returns of t he Borrower during  regular business hours of the 
Borrower and following prior reasonable notice; or 
(d) Take on behalf of the Bondholders whatever other action at law or  in equity as may ap pear 
necessary or desirable to co llect the amounts then due and thereafter to become due,  or to enforce performance and  
G-9 
observance of any obligations, agre ement or covenant of the Borrow er under the Loan Agreement  or the rights of 
the Bondholders. 
Any amounts collected pursuant to action  taken under this section and the Secur ity Documents paid to the 
Bond Trustee shall be applied in accordance with the Bond Indenture. 
Any rights and remedies as are given to the Bond Issuer under the Loan Agreement will also extend to the 
Bondholders, and the Bond Trustee, subject to the provisions of the Bond Indenture, will be entitled to the benefit of 
all covenants and agreements contained in the Loan Agreement, subject to the terms of the Security Documents. 
Rescission and Waiver 
(a) The Bond Trustee or the Bond Issu er, as applicable, shall res cind any accelerat ion and i ts 
consequences immediately  after the acceleration of the Bonds has been rescinded in acco rdance with the Bond 
Indenture. 
(b) The Bond Trustee or the Bond Issuer,  as applicable, shall waive any Event of Default immediately 
after any such Event of Default has been waived in accordance with the Bond Indenture. 
(c) The Bond Trustee or the Bond Issuer, a s applicable, shall have the right to, but shall be under no 
obligation to (except with respect to cla uses (a) an d (b) of this section), waive any other Event of Default at any  
time. 
(d) In case of any such waiver or rescission, then and in every such case the  Bond Issuer, the Bond 
Trustee and the Borrower shall be restored to t heir former positions and rights, but no such waiver shall ext end to 
any subsequent or other Event of Default, or impair any right consequent thereon. 
No Remedy Exclusive 
Subject to Bond I ndenture, no remedy hereunder is intended  to be exclusive of any other available remedy 
or remedies, but each and every such remed y shall b e cumulative and s hall be in addition to every other r emedy 
given under the Loan Agr eement or now or hereafter exis ting at law or in equity.  No delay or omission to exercise 
any right or power accruing upon any Event  of Default shall impair any such right or power or shall be construed to 
be a waiver thereof, but any such right or power may be exercised from time to t ime and as often as may be deemed 
expedient.  In order to entitle the Bond Issuer or the Bond Trustee t o exercise any remedy reserved to it in this Loan 
Agreement, it shall not be necessary to give any notice, other than such notice as may be required by applicable Law 
or in this  Loan Agreement.  A ny such rights and remedies  as are given to the Bond Issuer  hereunder shall also 
extend t o the Bondholders, the Coll ateral Ag ent and the Bond Trustee, s ubject to the provisions of th e Bond 
Indenture, shall be enti tled to the benefit of all covenants and ag reements herein contained, subject to the terms of 
the Security Documents. 
Term of Agreement 
Except to the extent otherwise p rovided herein, the Loan Agreement  shall be effective upo n its execution 
and delivery and shall expire at such time as all of the B onds and the fees and expen ses of the Bond Issuer and the 
Bond Trustee  shall have been fu lly paid or provision made for such payments , whichever is late r; provided, 
however, that the Loan Agreement may be terminat ed prior to such date pursuant to the Loan Agreement and the 
Bond Indenture, but in no event before al l of the obligations and dutie s of the Borrower hereunder have been fully 
performed, including, without limitation, the pay ments of all costs and fees man dated hereunder or under any other 
Finance Document to which the Borrower is a party. 
Amendments, Changes and Modifications 
Except as otherwise expressly provided in the Bond Indenture, subsequent to the issuance of the Bonds  and 
unless and until all condi tions provided for in the Bond Indentu re for release of the Bond Inden ture have been met,  
G-10 
the Loan Agr eement may not be effectively amended, changed, modified, altered or termi nated except by an 
instrument in writing signed b y the Borrower and the Bond Iss uer, consented to by the Bond Trustee,  and in 
accordance with the prov isions of the Bond Indenture, as  applicable.  For the avoidanc e of doubt, no amendment, 
change or modification of the Loan Agreement shall be effective wit hout the prior written consent of the Borrower 
and the Bond Issuer. 
Governing Law 
The substantive laws of the District of Columbia shall govern the construction and enforcement of the Loan 
Agreement and the Notes without giving effect to the application of conflict of law principles. 
Limitation of Liability 
(a) No covenant, agreement or obligation contained herein shall be deemed to be a co venant, 
agreement or obligation of any present or future director , officer, employee, member, shareholder, sponsor or agent 
of the Bond Issuer or the Bor rower in his or her ind ividual capacity so long as such person does not  act in bad faith, 
and no such d irector, officer, employee or agent the reof shall be subj ect to any liability under the Loan Agreement 
or with respect to any other action taken by such person provided that they do not act in bad faith. 
(b) Except as otherwise expressly set forth in the Finan ce Documents, the Secured Parti es will have 
full recourse to the Borrower and all of its assets and properties for the liabilities and obligations of the  Borrower 
under the Finance Documents, but in no event will any Affilia tes of the Borrower, or any offi cer, director or holder 
of any interest in the Borrower o r any Af filiates of the Borrower, b e liable or obligated for such liabilities and 
obligations of the Borrower. For the avoidance of doubt, the foregoing sentence shall  not limit the obligations of th e 
D&C Contractor Guarantor under the D&C Guarantee. 
(c) Notwithstanding anything in clause (b) o f this section , nothing in s aid clause (b) shall limit or 
affect or be const rued to limit or affect the obligations and liabilities of any Affiliate of the Borrower  (1) arising 
under any Finance Document or Project Do cument to which such Affiliate of the B orrower is a party, or (2) arising 
from any liability pursuant to any applic able Law for such Affiliate of the Borrower’s fr audulent actions, bad faith 
or willful misconduct.
